En: Your hands, however skilful, can't manage this task on their own. You should get your chaincutters out.
Zh: 你的手，不管多么灵巧，也不能独自完成这个任务。你应该把切链钳拿出来。

En: A mirror hangs above a bent and broken sink. In a fierce discharge of masculine energy someone has ripped half the faucet off.
Zh: 一面镜子挂在一个扭曲破损的盥洗盆上。某人爆发男子气概的时候，把半边水龙头扯下来了。

En: Hot water sprays from the faucet's base and steam covers the mirror. You cannot see yourself, just a vague impression of a man.
Zh: 热水从水龙头基座中喷涌而出，蒸汽糊住了整面镜子。你看不见自己，只有一个模糊的男性形象。

En: Hot water sprays from the base and steam covers the mirror. You cannot see yourself, just the outline of a man.
Zh: 热水从基座里喷涌而出，蒸汽糊住了整面镜子。你看不见自己，只能辨认出一个男性的轮廓。

En: Wipe the mirror.
Zh: 擦拭镜子。

En: No way, I'm not doing this. [Back off.]
Zh: 不可能，我不会这么做的。[后退。]

En: [Better not to know -- Leave.]
Zh: [现在最好还是不要了——离开。]

En: I don't care, I hurt myself, it's what I do. (Wipe the mirror.)
Zh: 我不在乎，我就要伤害自己，我就这么做。（擦拭镜子。）

En: I don't care. (Still wipe the mirror.)
Zh: 我不在乎。（继续擦拭镜子。）

En: Yeah, there is definitely something wrong with it.
Zh: 是啊，它肯定有问题。

En: What? What's wrong?
Zh: 什么？什么问题？

En: Where to even begin? There's the bloatedness, then the swollenness... It's like there's an upholstery of alcohol underneath your skin.
Zh: 到底该从哪里开始呢？有些发胖，然后是浮肿……感觉就像你的皮肤下面垫衬着一层酒精。

En: I'm scared. I want to stop doing this.
Zh: 我害怕。我不想这么做了。

En: At least my tongue is okay. (Touch your tongue.)
Zh: 至少我的舌头还好。（摸摸你的舌头。）

En: It's not. It's swollen and snail-like, wriggling between your fingers.
Zh: 它不好。它有些浮肿，像一只蜗牛在你的手指间蠕动。

En: Don't be scared. It's only your face. It's not like anyone is going to *see* it.
Zh: 别害怕。这只是你的脸而已。又不是说还有其他人打算*看见*它。

En: I'm sure everything is fine. (Touch your nose.)
Zh: 我确定一切都很好。（摸摸你的鼻子。）

En: I'm sorry. (Touch your nose.)
Zh: 很抱歉。（摸摸你的鼻子。）

En: It's not, your nose feels like a balloon in the middle of your face. It hurts when you honk it -- doesn't appear to be a particularly small nose either. Not with all the drinks it's *absorbed* for you.\n
Zh: 并没有，你的鼻子像是脸庞中央的一个小气球。你按动它的时候会痛，而且看起来并不像是个特别小的鼻子。毕竟它为你*吸收*了那么多酒精。

En: Bet you are. Your nose feels like a small balloon in the middle of your face. It hurts when you honk it, and it doesn't appear to be a particularly tiny nose either. Not with all the drinks it's *absorbed* for you.
Zh: 我敢打赌确实如此。你的鼻子像是脸庞中央的一个小气球。你按动它的时候会痛，而且似乎并不是个特别小的鼻子。毕竟它为你*吸收*了那么多酒精。

En: Wipe the mirror now.
Zh: 现在擦拭镜子。

En: Behold!
Zh: 看好了！

En: Dear lord help me, what is this?!
Zh: 老天啊，救救我吧，这是什么？！

En: Of course I do. It's... uhm... is it some kind of superstar? I think I'm a superstar.
Zh: 我当然知道。这是……呃……某个超级巨星？我觉得我是个超级巨星。

En: I'm not making it, the face is making it itself.
Zh: 不是我做的，是脸自己做的。

En: I think it might be because I'm a superstar.
Zh: 我觉得可能因为我是个超级巨星。

En: Please stop, it's horrible! You're scaring yourself.
Zh: 快停下，真是太可怕了！你把自己吓到了。

En: Okay. (Try to stop.)
Zh: 好吧。（试着停下来。）

En: No, this is what superstars do. (Keep making the face.)
Zh: 不，超级巨星就要这么做。（继续做鬼脸。）

En: You *can't*, can you? It's like it's not even voluntary anymore. You have worn that grin into your face and now it won't come *off*...
Zh: 你*不能*，对吗？甚至都不再像是自愿的了。你把笑容挂在了自己的脸上，现在它没办法*消退*了……

En: What does it even *mean*? What is the emotion you're trying to convey?
Zh: 这到底是什么*意思*？你想表达什么样的情绪？

En: God, I don't know -- it's indescribable.
Zh: 天，我不知道——很难用言语表达。

En: I think it's supposed to look *suggestive*. I'm afraid it's meant *for the ladies*.
Zh: 我觉得它应该看起来很*暧昧*。恐怕这是为*女士们*准备的。

En: I'm insinuating that I'm vaguely sympathetic. I think I'm sort of pulling it off too -- in a *sad has-been* kind of way. There is some charm to it.
Zh: 我在暗示自己隐约能感到共鸣。我觉得我也算是成功了——以某种*悲伤的过气人物*的方式。这里面隐含着某种魅力。

En: It's an expression of pain.
Zh: 这是痛苦的表现。

En: Superstardom.
Zh: 超级巨星。

En: You are correct.
Zh: 你是对的。

En: There might have been. Ten years ago. It's little more than a cadaverous spasm now.
Zh: 可能有吧，十年之前。现在更像是惨白的痉挛。

En: Please promise yourself you are not going to try it on any of the *ladies*, whatever happens.
Zh: 请向自己保证，无论发生什么，也不要对任何*女士*尝试这种表情。

En: I promise.
Zh: 我保证。

En: I promise. (Lie.)
Zh: 我保证。（撒谎。）

En: I'm outta here. [Leave.]
Zh: 我走了。[离开。]

En: You should check yourself for a pulse, superstar. From here it looks like a cadaverous spasm.
Zh: 你应该检查一下自己的脉搏，超级巨星。在这里看起来像是尸体的痉挛。

En: You should check yourself for a pulse, because from here it looks like a cadaverous spasm.
Zh: 你应该检查一下自己的脉搏，因为在这里看起来像是尸体的痉挛。

En: You liar. You can't even tell the truth to yourself. Go ahead, try something!
Zh: 你这个骗子。你甚至都不能跟自己讲真话。来吧，试试看！

En: [Let the mirror be for now.]
Zh: [先不管这面镜子。]

En: Was this not the same *élan* that founds empires and lays waste to cities, virile, uncaring towards *the little things*?
Zh: 这跟建立帝国、摧毁城市的*锐气*不是一样的吗，充满男子气概，做事*不拘小节*？

En: Probably not, no.
Zh: 也许不是，不是的。

En: Abort! You clearly have not thought this through. You won't like what you will see there -- and you will never *un-become* it.
Zh: 停下！你显然没想清楚。你不会喜欢自己将要看到的一切——而且你永远无法*摆脱*它。

En: Dig deep into your mind to locate the source of *The Expression*.
Zh: 深入挖掘你的思想，找出这个*表情*的源头。

En: Heard about the band OO.
Zh: 听说过招摇乐团。

En: Joyce told you about The New.
Zh: 跟乔伊斯聊过新时代。

En: Attempt to stop *The Expression* from happening.
Zh: 尝试阻止*表情*的出现。

En: Know the origin.
Zh: 了解源头。

En: No more steam.
Zh: 没有蒸汽了。

En: Recited the poem, Tommy.
Zh: 背诵那首诗歌，汤米。

En: Standard Wirrâl die.
Zh: 标准威勒尔骰子。

En: Shaved.
Zh: 剃过胡子。

En: Ruby killed herself.
Zh: 露比自杀了。

En: Really? I don't remember *anything*?
Zh: 真的？我*什么*都记不起来了？

En: Really? Nothing?
Zh: 真的吗？什么也不记得？

En: A mirror hangs on the wall, covered in steam. You cannot see yourself, just the outlines of a man. You have no memory of the face that awaits you there, underneath the soft vapour.
Zh: 一面镜子挂在墙上，上面满是水汽。你无法看清自己，只能辨认出一个男性的轮廓。你完全不记得那柔和的水蒸气下，是怎样的一张脸在等待着你。

En: Really -- all recollection of the person you are, the people in your life and even the world you're in has drowned in a sea of blood alcohol. This was no mere night of drinking, it was a deluge. Of world-ending proportions.
Zh: 真的——所有关于你是谁，你的生活，甚至是这个世界的记忆都已经被血液中的酒精海给淹没。这不仅仅是一场普通的豪饮，这是一场洪水，规模足以终结整个世界。

En: You have no idea who this *thing* is, do you?
Zh: 你不知道这个*东西*是谁，对吗？

En: How bad could it be?
Zh: 能有多糟糕？

En: This is the face of a late-stage alcoholic.
Zh: 这是一副晚期酒鬼的脸孔。

En: No. (Keep making the face.)
Zh: 不要。（继续做鬼脸。）

En: No one tells me what to do.
Zh: 没人能对我指手画脚。

En: Check for pulse.
Zh: 检查自己的脉搏。

En: I don't want to know.
Zh: 我不想知道。

En: Good call. Go ahead and try something, if you like. Or -- you know -- stop staring into the mirror.
Zh: 好主意。如果你喜欢的话，那就尽管试试吧。或者——你知道的——就别再盯着镜子看了。

En: That's right! Not even yourself. Go ahead, try something, it's not an order.
Zh: 没错！甚至你自己也不行。来吧，试试看，这不是个命令。

En: You find no sign of life on your swollen neck. However, putting your hand on your chest reveals an irregular heartbeat. You appear to be alive -- for now.
Zh: 你没有在浮肿的脖子上发现任何生命的迹象。不过，放在胸口的双手揭露出不规则的心跳。你好像还活着——暂时而已。

En: I don't need to. I almost died trying to get that tie, but I heard my heart beat.
Zh: 我不需要。想拿到那条领带差点让我把命都搭上了，不过我还能听见自己的心跳声。

En: Getting clever appears to make *The Expression* even worse. Now it's mixed with smugness.
Zh: 变聪明似乎让那个*表情*更加糟糕了。现在还混杂着一种自视甚高的感觉。

En: A mirror hangs on the bathroom wall. In it -- your face, adorned with *The Expression*.
Zh: 一面镜子挂在墙上。在那里面——是你的脸，脸上还挂着那个*表情*。

En: It's too late. Like an image on film, *The Expression* belongs to your primary motor cortex. It would take a minor neurological miracle for you to cease producing it.
Zh: 太迟了。就像电影里的影像。那个*表情*属于你的主要运动皮层。只有发生一个小小的神经性奇迹才能阻止它的发生。

En: Like the rest of you, it comes from a bad place somewhere in the past. That's all you know for now.
Zh: 就像你的其他部分一样，它来自于过去某个糟糕的地方。你暂时只知道这么多了。

En: It belongs in The New -- the third decade of the current century. Enough time had passed from the failure of Revolution that, for a fleeting moment, free market economy seemed like the ultimate, uncontested way of life for our species.
Zh: 它属于新时代——本世纪的第三个十年。自大革命失败已经过去很久了，弹指一挥间，自由市场经济似乎成了我们人类最终的、无可争议的生活方式。

En: Things were good. It was *smooth sailing*. People made gold-and-champagne-tinted interiors and façades to suit the times, calling this *The New Style*. But, more importantly -- disco happened.
Zh: 一切都很好。可以说是*一帆风顺*。为了跟上时代，金色和香槟色的内外饰风格开始在人群中流行起来，谓之*新时代风*。不过，更重要的是——迪斯科出现了。

En: Forget about Ostentatious Orchestrations. For Revachol -- your city -- that meant only one thing: Guillaume Le Million!\n
Zh: 忘掉招摇乐团吧。对于瑞瓦肖来说——你的城市——那只意味着一件事：纪尧姆·列米利翁！

En: For Revachol -- your city -- that meant only one thing: Guillaume Le Million!
Zh: 对于你的城市瑞瓦肖来说——那只意味着一件事：纪尧姆·列米利翁！

En: If it doesn't rhyme, you're not pronouncing it right.
Zh: 如果它不能押韵，那一定是你发音错了。

En: Out of the dazzling swirl of disco music, in an open air *boîte de nuit* somewhere in Revachol West, Guillaume's blond mane appeared on the screen. He sang some bullshit. Then he made *The Expression*.
Zh: 在迪斯科音乐那让人眼花缭乱的漩涡中，在瑞瓦肖西部某处的露天*夜总会*里，纪尧姆的金发出现在了银幕上。他歌唱着胡编乱造的歌词。然后做出了那个*表情*。

En: So I adopted it? Why?
Zh: 所以我接受那表情了吗？为什么？

En: I feel the need to add a clicking sound when I make it: *Click-click*.
Zh: 在我做那个表情的时候，感觉需要加上一个短声：*咔哒*。

En: How long ago was The New?
Zh: 新时代是多久之前的事？

En: Everyone loved it. Maybe you thought some of the stardust would rub off on you? Maybe it did? Either way, it's all gone now. Only the grimace remains.
Zh: 没人不爱那个表情。也许你觉得有些明星的*魔力*会沾染在你身上？也许真的沾上了？不管怎么说，现在全都消失了。只剩下一副鬼脸。

En: The click is used to spur on a horse. It also features heavily in Guillaume Le Million's regional mega-hit "Don't Worry (Your Pretty Little Head)".
Zh: 这个咔哒声以前是用来鞭策马匹的。它在纪尧姆·列米利翁的那首地区大热歌曲《别担心（美丽的小脑袋）》里面也占据了很重要的地位。

En: Sometimes you like to add finger pistols to the mix, because unlike Guillaume le Million you are a police officer. It's your nifty little way to say: I'm armed and dangerous.
Zh: 有时候你想在混音中加入一点手指开枪拟声，因为不像纪尧姆·列米利翁，你是一个警察。这是你在俏皮地暗示：我有武器，我很危险。

En: Anything else? Like who am I? Why did I become a cop? Why did I drink myself into oblivion?
Zh: 还有别的吗？比如我是谁？为什么我会成为一个警察？为什么要把自己喝到烂醉如泥？

En: Anything else? Like who am I? Why did I drink myself into oblivion?
Zh: 还有别的吗？比如我是谁？为什么要把自己喝到烂醉如泥？

En: There is a vast ocean of time between right now and *The Expression* looking good on you -- or anyone. Two decades, if the calendar is to be trusted. Humanity has run aground in that time, it's a different world now. The Expression is a relic.
Zh: 现在和你脸上——或者任何人脸上那个看起来不错的*表情*之间，隔着一片浩瀚的时间之海。如果相信日历的话，已经相隔20余年了。人类曾经在那个时代四处奔忙过，现在已经是一个完全不同的世界了。那个表情本身就是个遗迹。

En: Some twenty odd years. There is a vast ocean of time between right now and *The Expression* looking good on you -- or anyone. Humanity has run aground in that time, it's a different world now. The Expression is a relic.
Zh: 大约20多年。现在和你脸上——或者任何人脸上那个看起来不错的*表情*之间，隔着一片浩瀚的时间之海。人类曾经在那个时代四处奔忙过，现在已经是一个完全不同的世界。那个表情本身就是个纪念物。

En: I guess that's it then. (Conclude.)
Zh: 我猜那就这样吧。（总结。）

En: You have some understanding of the near history of disco. The rest is darkness, aside from the useless fact that the motor carriage outside was a Coupris Kineema.
Zh: 你对迪斯科的近代史有一些理解。其余的都是一片黑暗，除了一个无用的事实：外面那辆汽車是库普瑞斯锐影。

En: You have some understanding of the recent history of disco. The rest is darkness.
Zh: 你对迪斯科的近代史有一些理解，其余的都是一片空白。

En: You have some understanding of the near history of disco, plus the trivia you've picked up along the way. Episodic memory, however, remains in the dark. It may never return. You should prepare yourself for that.
Zh: 你对迪斯科的近代史有一些理解，再加上沿途了解到的一些琐事。但是，成片段的记忆，还停留在一片黑暗中。它可能永远也不会回来了。你应该做好准备。

En: It doesn't have to be. You can swoon over Guillaume and his champagne-cork smile whenever you want to... Maybe some of the stardust will return?
Zh: 不一定非得这样。只要你想，随时都可以为纪尧姆和他那有如香槟酒瓶软木塞一般的微笑而神魂颠倒……也许有些明星的*魔力*会回来呢？

En: With all the synapses you extinguished, it's a miracle you can even think this sentence. You've interrupted a true act of self-annulment here.
Zh: 你熄灭了那么多的神经突触，能想出这个句子已经算是个奇迹了。你刚刚打断了一个货真价实的自我否定。

En: Still not happening. It won't come off that easy.
Zh: 还是不行。它不会那么容易成功的。

En: Okay...
Zh: 好吧……

En: You should check yourself for a pulse, superstar. From here it looks like a cadaverous spasm.
Zh: 你应该检查一下自己的脉搏，超级巨星。在这里看起来像是尸体的痉挛。

En: Suddenly you realise you have no memory of the face that awaits you there, underneath the soft vapour.
Zh: 突然间，你意识到自己完全不记得那柔和水蒸气下即将浮现的脸庞。

En: As you slowly reach your hand toward the surface of the mirror...
Zh: 你慢慢伸出手去碰触镜子的表面……

En: It appears you're also dead. There's clearly rigor mortis on your face, or wait... is that an *expression*? Are you trying to make an expression with that face? Why?
Zh: 看来你也和死了一样，脸上了无生气，或者，等一下……这是一个*表情*吗？你想试着用这张脸做表情吗？为什么？

En: *Too* late. You clearly have rigor mortis on your face, or wait... is that an *expression*? Are you trying to make an expression with that face? Why?
Zh: 晚期*过头*了。你的脸上有着明显的尸僵现象，或者，等一下……这是一个*表情*吗？你想试着用这张脸做表情吗？为什么？

En: Whatever it is, at least it's dead now. There's clearly rigor mortis on your face. Or wait... is that an *expression*? Are you trying to make an expression with that face?
Zh: 不管是什么，至少它现在已经死了。你的脸上有着明显的尸僵现象，或者，等一下……这是一个*表情*吗？你想试着用这张脸做表情吗？为什么？

En: I have no idea why it's there. It just is.
Zh: 我不知道为什么会这样。它就是这样了。

En: Actually, I'm not sure I *can* stop.
Zh: 其实，我不确定自己*能够*停下来。

En: Oh my god, you *can't* stop! It's like it's not even voluntary anymore! You have worn that grin into your face and now it won't come off...
Zh: 天啦，你*不能*停下来！就好像它甚至已经不是自发的了！你把笑容挂在了自己的脸上，现在它没办法*消退*了……

En: No. That would be too insane.
Zh: 不。那样就太疯狂了。

En: Maybe that's how it started? And now it doesn't even matter anymore -- you've worn that grin into your face and it will never come *off*...
Zh: 也许以前就是这样开始的？不过现在已经不再重要——你已经把笑容挂在了自己的脸上，现在它没办法*消退*了……

En: I'm pretty sure I can't stop.
Zh: 我很肯定我停不下来了。

En: I can try to. (Try to stop.)
Zh: 我可以试试。（尝试停下来。）

En: If I can't stop, I wouldn't want to know. Better to just keep making it.
Zh: 如果不能停下来，我也不想知道。最好还是先这样吧。

En: Lets just get it over with. Wipe the mirror.
Zh: 我们还是克服一下吧。擦拭镜子。

En: Maybe I should touch it first, make sure there's nothing *wrong* with my face.
Zh: 也许我应该先碰碰它，确定我的脸没有任何*毛病*。

En: You couldn't stop if you wanted to. It's neurological.
Zh: 你想停也停不下来。这是神经性的。

En: Oh my god, you're right! It's like it's not even voluntary anymore. You have worn that grin into your face and now it won't come *off*...
Zh: 天啦，你是对的！就好像它都不再是自发的了。你把笑容挂在了自己的脸上，现在它没办法*消退*了……

En: Does this have anything to do with Ostentatious Orchestrations?
Zh: 这跟招摇乐团有什么关系吗？

En: Not really. OO must have just stirred your mind. They're more like a disco rock band anyway...
Zh: 不见得吧。一定是招摇乐团激起了你的想法。不过他们更像是一只迪斯科摇滚乐队……

En: Goddamnit...
Zh: 该死…

En: Hold on, my cocksmanship is on par with...!
Zh: 等下，我的床技高得和……！

En: What's that? Can't hear yourself over the moans of your wömen in the hands of foreigners who play them like instruments. Be careful, or they'll wear you like a sock, Harry-boy.
Zh: 什么？你的女人在外国人手中被当成乐器一样玩弄和呻吟呢，我听不见你在说什么。当心点，否则他们会叫你好看的，小哈里。

En: Can't hear you over the moans of your wömen. They writhe in pleasure as the foreigners are playing them like instruments. Be careful, or they'll wear you like a sock, Harry.
Zh: 你的女人在呻吟呢，我听不见你说什么。她们被外国人当作乐器一般玩弄，痛苦并欢愉着。当心点，否则他们会叫你好看的，小哈里。

En: *Never*. May this serve as a constant reminder of your sacred, un-put-downable burthen.
Zh: *不。*希望这个能时刻提醒你别忘记自己神圣的、无法放下的重担。

En: Will I ever be able to make another face again?
Zh: 我还能再换张脸吗？

En: That's one way of saying that the nerve damage is *quite* severe. The only way you'll be able to change your face now is with your fingers...
Zh: 这种说法是用来形容*相当*严重的神经损伤。要改变自己的样貌，就只能用手指了……

En: You step back from the mirror. A loose board creaks.
Zh: 你看着镜子向后退了一步。松动的木板嘎吱作响。

En: In a rundown shack on the far coast, a middle-aged man pounds his fist and looses an animal cry as he stares into a sliver of clouded glass...
Zh: 在遥远海岸边的一个破旧的棚屋里，一个中年男人握紧拳头，看着镜子的碎片发出了野兽般的哭喊声……

En: A cold damp seeps in through the gaps between the wall planks. There is no one to bear witness to your vow...
Zh: 一股寒气从墙板的裂缝中渗进来。没有人能为你的誓言作证……

En: From above the Bay of Revachol, a cold wind gathers in response. The people of the fishing village pull their coats and blankets tight about themselves...
Zh: 一阵冷风从瑞瓦肖海湾吹来。渔村的村民们把身上的外套和毯子裹得更紧了……

En: Fine... The three of you, all together, ride an aerostatic into the sunset. The question remains: Will you sacrifice love to keep Revachol safe?
Zh: 很好……你们三个一起乘坐静风舰驶向夕阳。可问题是，你会牺牲爱情来保护瑞瓦肖的安全吗？

En: It's time you knew the truth, bröther.
Zh: 你该知道真相了，兄弟。

En: I am ready.
Zh: 我准备好了。

En: This is a horrible allegory for... whatever is on the other end.
Zh: 这是一个很糟糕的比喻……不管它到底是在比喻什么。

En: Love... time... Revachol...
Zh: 爱……时间……瑞瓦肖……

En: Maybe it will make you temptation-proof, so something good will come out of this at least?
Zh: 这样也许会让你抵挡住诱惑，所以至少结果会是好的吧？

En: This is the face worn by the first law-bringers, the noble tyrants. Today you join their number...
Zh: 这是张一流执法者的脸，贵族暴君的脸。你今天也成为了他们中的一员……

En: You are a terror even to yourself!
Zh: 你自己都觉得自己可怕！

En: Wait, can Kim be in the aerostatic too?
Zh: 等等，金也能乘坐静风舰吗？

En: Pull the imaginary lever.
Zh: 推动想象的拉杆。

En: It's all shit.
Zh: 全都是狗屁。

En: (Bow down.) Yes. My heart forever beats for Revachol, I am all she has left. The constant kingsman.
Zh: （鞠躬。）是的。我的心脏永远为瑞瓦肖跳动。我是她唯一剩下的王下骑士，永远的王下骑士。

En: Face it, bröther. No one's got the *willpower* to build her up again. They won't do what has to be done.
Zh: 面对现实吧，兄弟。没人有那个*意志力*帮她复兴。该做的事情人们都不会去做。

En: *Ahem*. It's a *lot* like the face on that rather unfortunate muscle-man t-shirt you just so happen to be wearing...
Zh: *嗯哼*。这样子*很*像你碰巧穿着的那件倒霉肌肉男T恤上的脸。

En: *Ahem*. It's a *lot* like the face a certain muscle-man is making on that t-shirt you insist on carrying around...
Zh: *嗯哼*。这样子*很*像你坚持要一直带在身上的那件倒霉肌肉男T恤上的脸。

En: *Ahem*. It's *very* similar to the face Hjelmdallermann is always making on the cover of those books...
Zh: *嗯哼*。这和那张经常用来作《赫姆达尔传奇》封面的脸*非常*相似……

En: It can't be.
Zh: 不可能。

En: All of it?
Zh: 所有？

En: An involuntary twitch runs through your hand, almost like reflex.
Zh: 你的手如同条件反射一般，不自觉地抽动着。

En: We keep it buried like a horrible secret. We feed it through the cracks in the floorboards out of a sickening sense of nostalgia. Just enough to keep it alive. And yet it still comes to us in moments of weakness, unbidden, a source of shame and panic...
Zh: 我们把它当作可怕的秘密一般埋葬起来。我们怀着病态的怀旧感把它埋入地板的裂缝中。仅能让它足以存活。但每当我们脆弱的时候，它就会作为羞耻与恐惧的源头，不请自来……

En: Wait, why do I need to do that?
Zh: 等下，我为什么要这么做呢？

En: The bloated whores, schizophrenic anarchists and deformed moneylenders tremble, trying to shield their eyes, as he -- *Le gendarme en diamant* -- walks by!
Zh: 当他——*光芒万丈的警察*——经过时，那些臃肿的婊子、精神分裂的无政府主义者和畸形的放债者都颤抖着，想要遮住他们的眼睛。

En: A day will come when you need to take a shotgun to that cellar, shoot whatever is down there in the head, and walk away, never looking back.
Zh: 总有一天你会拿着猎枪去地下室，一枪把底下那个家伙给爆头，然后头也不回地走掉。

En: No-no, not the voice! Make it stop...
Zh: 不不，不想听到那个声音！快让它停下……

En: You haven't yet banished all weakness, but you will. We know you will...
Zh: 你还没弃掉身上的所有弱点，但终有一天的。我们知道你会的……

En: Remember: Kingsmen are not born, they are made. And the only thing that can unmake us is that which drove us to rise above in the first place. We keep that thing locked in the deepest cellars of our mind. So deep we forget what it is.
Zh: 记住：王下骑士不是天生的，而是历练出来的。唯有催使我们奋起努力的东西方能摧毁我们。我们把这东西藏在内心深处，深到自己都忘记了究竟是什么。

En: You'll plough your way toward the frigid lands of Katla and Vaasa, smashing every ice sheet in your path. Nothing, *nothing* can stop an icebreaker...
Zh: 你将向着卡特拉和瓦萨城的寒冷冰原前行，粉碎沿途每一块冰盖。*没有任何东西*能够阻挡一名破冰者……

En: While the world burns, the two of you, together again, ride an aerostatic into the sunset. Maybe it will work this time? Spare Revachol and you'll never hear from her again. Will you sacrifice a second chance at love to keep Revachol safe?
Zh: 当整个世界熊熊燃烧的时候，你们两人再次一起乘坐静风舰驶向夕阳。也许这一次能成功？你再也不会听到瑞瓦肖的消息了。你会再一次牺牲爱情，保护瑞瓦肖的安全吗？

En: It can, and it *is*. Just a mountain of shit, every bit of it.
Zh: 不仅可以，而且*就是*如此。完完全全就是一坨狗屎。

En: It was time to banish that ridiculous *Expression* from your face -- and replace it with a perfect reflection of your unyielding resolve, your noble suffering.
Zh: 是时候让你脸上那可笑的*表情*消失了——换上一副能完美体现你不屈决心和崇高苦难的模样吧。

En: It was time to banish that pitiful, *sad* look from your face -- and replace it with a perfect reflection of your unyielding resolve, your noble suffering.
Zh: 是时候让你脸上那可怜的*悲伤*表情消失了——换上一副能完美体现你不屈决心和崇高苦难的模样吧。

En: How dare you speak of Revachol that way...
Zh: 你竟敢那么说瑞瓦肖……

En: You're wrong. There's still a way to turn back time. To make things *richtig*.
Zh: 你错了。时间可以倒流。一切都可以*挽回*。

En: He sets down the paper and quickly scribbles the date and time in his notebook, along with a note: 'HDB possible relapse? Check in first thing.'
Zh: 他放下报纸，在笔记本上迅速写下日期和时间，并附上一条笔记：‘HDB可能复发？先做检查。’

En: You step back from the mirror. A bead of water drips from the pipe beneath the washbasin.
Zh: 你看着镜子向后退了一步。一颗水珠从洗手池下面的水管滴落。

En: I will never forsake my vöws. (Conclude.)
Zh: 我永远不会背弃誓言。（总结。）

En: Now shout from the top of your lungs: YOU ARE THE NOBLE SUFFERER.
Zh: 大声呼喊出来吧：你是高贵的受难者。

En: Even love?
Zh: 爱也是？

En: *All* of it.
Zh: *全*都是。

En: Does this thing *unmake* you, or does it heal you?
Zh: 它究竟是*摧毁*了你，还是治愈了你？

En: Yes... you truly are. (Tightness -- in gaseous form -- leaves your body.) She's lucky to have you, watching her sleep.
Zh: 是的……一点没错。（紧绷感离开了你的身体——仿佛气体被排了出去）。能有你伴她入眠，她很幸运。

En: What happens to me then?
Zh: 那我会怎样？

En: I don't think I can do that.
Zh: 我觉得自己做不到。

En: You're a hard motherfucker... (Tightness -- in gaseous form -- leaves your body.) If we didn't know better, we'd say you *enjoyed* consigning your city to the flames.
Zh: 你真是个又臭又硬的混账……（紧绷感离开了你的身体——仿佛气体被排了出去）。要不是我们了解你，还觉得你*很享受*把自己的城市一把火烧掉呢。

En: Wait, what happened to my face?
Zh: 等等，我的脸怎么了？

En: What's going on? I thought I *succeeded*!
Zh: 怎么回事？我还以为我*成功*了！

En: Face it, bröther. No one's got the *willpower* to set things right. They won't do what it takes.
Zh: 面对现实吧，兄弟。没人有*意志力*去拨乱反正。他们不会这么做的。

En: Meanwhile, his colleague is filling in the last clues of his nightly crossword when he hears what seems to be some kind of animalistic howling from the adjoining room. He directs his eyes toward the door of their shared bathroom...
Zh: 当他听到隔壁房间传来一种动物般的咆哮声时，他的同事正在填写着猜词游戏的最后一栏。他把目光投向了公共浴室的门……

En: Especially love, bröther. Face it, no one's willing to make the sacrifices that are necessary, not in this day and age.
Zh: 特别是爱，兄弟。面对现实吧，这年头没人愿意做出必要的牺牲。

En: Look deep inside yourself. You know it's true, because it *hurts*.
Zh: 好好自省一下吧。你知道这是事实，因为它很*伤人*。

En: Nine million souls flicker out of existence. Sixteen if you include the greater metropolitan area.
Zh: 一共九百万人消失了。要是算上郊区的话就是一千六百万。

En: You will be free to love Revachol like no one has ever loved her. You will be her champion, and she will be *faithful* to you. If you can just make this last sacrifice, you will *finally* know happiness.
Zh: 你可以随意爱着瑞瓦肖，就好像之前从没有人爱过她一样。你是她的守护者，她也将对你*忠诚*。如果你愿意做出最后的牺牲，你*终*将获得幸福。

En: He sighs and returns to his crossword. He is a police officer, not a baby-sitter.
Zh: 他叹了口气，继续做着猜词游戏。他是个警察，不是保姆。

En: In a mould-covered bathroom inside a trashed hostel near the edge of Martinaise, a middle-aged man grips the sides of a cold sink in white-knuckled fury...
Zh: 在马丁内斯一间破败不堪的酒店浴室里，一个中年男人怒不可遏地抓住冰冷水槽的边缘……

En: (Rack the imaginary shotgun.) Whatever it takes. For *Revachol*.
Zh: （摆出装填霰弹枪的姿势。）为了*瑞瓦肖*，我可以付出一切。

En: I may as well give up then...
Zh: 那我还是放弃的好……

En: So ask yourself, when the time comes, can you shoot the past in the head, walk away, and never look back on its rotting remains?
Zh: 问问你自己吧，当时机来到，你会杀死过去的自己，留下腐烂的尸骸，头也不回地走掉吗？

En: I won't give up. I *can't* give up.
Zh: 我不会放弃，我*不能*放弃。

En: Now open your eyes and witness the (small) wonder! The HMS Modest Kingsman has left the docks. You are neither the greatest nor the strongest icebreaker on the seas, but you are what you are.
Zh: 睁开你的眼睛，见证（小小的）奇迹吧！皇家谦逊骑士号已经扬帆起航。你并不是海上最强大、最坚固的破冰船，可你就是你。

En: Now open your eyes and witness the wonder! The HMS Last Kingsman has left the docks. The world will tremble as you smash all the ice in the world.
Zh: 睁开你的眼睛，见证奇迹吧！皇家最后的王下骑士号已经扬帆起航。等你粉碎世上所有的坚冰时，世界都会为你颤抖。

En: That voice... so warm and sweet...
Zh: 这声音……好温暖好悦耳……

En: Your heart can belong to Revachol or it can belong to darkness. As long as it's torn between them it's broken and useless.
Zh: 你的心可以属于瑞瓦肖，也可以属于黑暗。如果犹豫不决，你的心将会破碎，变得毫无用处。

En: Despite doubt, fear, sentimentality and weakness, you plough ever forward, like an icebreaker, tearing through the frigid North Sea.
Zh: 哪怕怀疑、害怕、感伤、软弱，你依然像破冰船一般乘风破浪，穿越寒冷的北海。

En: *Nothing* -- not pain, fatigue, hunger, fear or desire -- will ever deviate you from the path. Like an icebreaker you crush all opposition, ploughing forward *no matter what it takes*.
Zh: 痛苦、疲劳、饥饿、恐惧或是欲望都无法令你偏航。你就像破冰船一般粉碎你所有的对手，*不惜一切代价*地乘风前行。

En: You're tired, afraid, sad, hungry and in pain. But most of all: confused. Yet *still*, like a tiny icebreaker -- the tiniest boat in the fleet -- you plough ever forward...
Zh: 你疲乏、害怕、悲伤、饥饿、痛苦。但更重要的是，你困惑了。然而你*仍*像一艘小小的破冰船——舰队中最小的那只——乘风前行……

En: It takes a true kingsman to summon the *will* to face reality in all its darkness. To stare straight into the abyss and do *what must be done*.
Zh: 只有真正的王下骑士才能靠*意志*去直面黑暗中的现实。才能直视深渊，去做*应该做的事*。

En: Bröther. You *have* succeeded...
Zh: 兄弟，你*已经*成功了……

En: Who said anything about giving up?
Zh: 谁说要放弃？

En: You used to believe you came from a large family with many half-brothers and sisters, but where are they now? However you shout, they can't hear you...
Zh: 你一直觉得自己来自一个兄弟姐妹们众多的大家庭，可他们现在在哪儿呢？不管你怎么呼喊，他们都听不见……

En: Enough. The Franconigerian Court of Honour has held your naked soul up against the Sevenfold Sun Miracle and looked right through it. Close your eyes and *know*, this is who you are:
Zh: 够了。弗兰考格斯荣誉法庭将你赤裸的灵魂放在七日奇迹下暴晒。闭上你的眼睛，你要*知道*这就是真正的你：

En: I will bear any burthen.
Zh: 我愿意承受任何重担。

En: There's a lever in front of you. Pull it, and atomic fire blankets your beloved Revachol. Millions die in an instant. But you are spared. You, and the voice in the payphone.
Zh: 你面前便有一根控制杆。拉动它，你心爱的瑞瓦肖便会付之一炬。数百万人会立刻死去。可是你，还有公共电话另一端的那个人会存活下来。

En: How do I know I'm a true kingsman?
Zh: 我怎么知道自己是真的王下骑士呢？

En: Face yourself.
Zh: 直面自己。

En: Stopped obsessing over sexuality.
Zh: 不再对性着迷。

En: Advanced Race Theory
Zh: 着装规范：强势象征。

En: Read about Devil Woman.
Zh: 阅读《魔女》。

En: Self-proclaimed authoritarian.
Zh: 自我标榜独裁。

En: Been working out.
Zh: 锻炼。

En: Moustache gave you strength.
Zh: 胡子给予你力量。

En: Contemptibly apologetic.
Zh: 卑躬屈膝地道歉。

En: Actively obsessing about sexuality.
Zh: 对性十分着迷。

En: Dresscode: True man.
Zh: 着装规范：毫无保留。

En: Dresscode: Potent symbol.
Zh: 着装规范：真男人。

En: The problem is obvious: You simply can't hack it. You're a failure. It's all *too much* for you.
Zh: 问题显而易见：你根本应付不来。你就是个失败者，这对你来说*太过*了。

En: It's time. Time to see yourself as you truly are. It's going to be absolutely *devastating*, but there's no way to avoid it.
Zh: 是时候了，是时候面对真正的自己了。虽然非常*可怕*，但却无可逃避。

En: This idiocy has gone on too long. It's time to wipe that decadent homo-sexual grin off your face.
Zh: 这种蠢事已经做了太久。该收起你颓废的同性恋笑容了。

En: It's time. Time to see yourself as you truly are. It's going to be absolutely *devastating*, but there's no way to avoid it.
Zh: 是时候了，是时候面对真正的自己了。虽然非常*可怕*，但却无可逃避。

En: This idiocy has gone on far too long. It's time to wipe that decadent homo-sexual grin off your face. Time to see yourself as you truly are.
Zh: 这种蠢事已经做了太久。是时候收起你颓废的同性恋笑容，认识真正的自己了。

En: It's time. Time to see yourself as you truly are. It's going to be absolutely *devastating*, but there's no way to avoid it.
Zh: 是时候了，是时候面对真正的自己了。虽然非常*可怕*，但却无可逃避。

En: This idiocy has gone on too long. It's time to wipe that decadent homo-sexual grin off your face.
Zh: 这种蠢事已经做了太久。该收起你颓废的同性恋笑容了。

En: This idiocy has gone on too long. It's time to wipe that decadent homo-sexual grin off your face.
Zh: 这种蠢事已经做了太久。该收起你颓废的同性恋笑容了。

En: It's time. Time to see yourself as you truly are. It's going to be absolutely *devastating*, but there's no way to avoid it.
Zh: 是时候了，是时候面对真正的自己了。虽然非常*可怕*，但却无可逃避。

En: It's time. Your efforts at avoiding this moment have been truly *heroic*, but now there's nothing left but for you to confront yourself as you *truly* are, no matter the consequences...
Zh: 是时候了。你为了回避这一刻所做的努力真的很*英勇*，但现在已经别无选择，只能直面*真实*的自己，不论结果如何……

En: Have you really been wearing that decadent homo-sexual grin this whole time? It's disgusting. No wonder you haven't been able to *truly face yourself*.
Zh: 你真的一直展露着一副颓废的同性恋笑容吗？真恶心。难怪你不能*真正直面自己*。

En: What's too much?
Zh: 什么东西太过了？

En: Love... time... Revachol... all of it. The pressure is putting you on your knees.
Zh: 爱……时间……瑞瓦肖……所有的一切。这些压力无一不让你屈服。

En: Now, every night, while you masturbate in the ruins of your not-so-great-anymore hall, they're getting pleasured by foreign men with superior testosterone levels and *far* more advanced sexual practices than your feeble grunt-and-thrust.
Zh: 每天夜里，当你在断壁残垣中手淫时，她们就在浑身睾酮的外国男人那儿承欢，那些男人的床技也比你有气无力的撸管要高明得*多*。

En: Look at yourself: You go about with this mopey look on your face like a dog who's been beaten with an old stick.
Zh: 瞧瞧你自己：你像只被棍棒招呼过的狗一样无精打采地到处走……

En: That stupid *Expression* was your last line of defence, but now even that has collapsed. The combined armies of Pain and Shit are streaming through your gates, pillaging your stores, carrying off your wömen...
Zh: 那个愚蠢的*表情*原本是你最后的防线，可是现在也已经崩塌。痛苦与挫折的联合部队破门而入，抢劫你的商店，夺走你的女人……

En: You've let your guard down. Now the combined armies of Pain and Shit are streaming through your gates, pillaging your stores, carrying off your wömen...
Zh: 你放松了警惕。如今痛苦与挫折的联合部队破门而入，抢劫你的商店，夺走你的女人……

En: Hey! It took a minor neurological *miracle* to get your face to this point!
Zh: 嘿！你的脸会露出这幅表情可真是神经学上的小小*奇迹*！

En: No, not the stores...
Zh: 不，不要抢劫我的商店……

En: Not my wömen...
Zh: 不要夺走我的女人……

En: Oh yes, they're all *wiped out*. Your bushels of grain, your salted meats, your hogsheads brimming with bitter ale. All gone. Your fields have been put to the torch, your great-hall is a ravaged husk. You are bereft.
Zh: 哦，是啊，都*没有*了。你大斗的粮食、腌肉，还有那些装满苦啤酒的大桶子。全都没有了。你的田地被一把烧尽，你繁华的大厅也惨遭蹂躏。你一无所有……

En: Oh yes, they're all gone. Carried off by foreign invaders because you failed to uphold your most ancient and noble vöws. The worst part is they might even *prefer* it this way...
Zh: 哦，是啊，都没有了。因为你没有守住最古老而神圣的誓言，所以她们被外国入侵者夺走了。最糟糕的是，她们也许更*乐意*如此……

En: And that doesn't even *begin* to cover what's happened to your wömen...
Zh: 而发生在你女人身上的事情*根本*不止这些……

En: God, does it have to be so awful?
Zh: 天啊，非得这么可怕吗？

En: It's never not too late! I can *still* turn it back...
Zh: 还不晚！我*还*能挽回一切……

En: Accept your fate with quiet resignation.
Zh: 平静而顺从地接受你的命运吧。

En: Yes. It *should* be awful, because you failed. Failing is weakness and weakness is awful. That is the way of the world...
Zh: 是的，你失败了，所以*本来*就该可怕。失败就是软弱，软弱就很可怕。这个世界就是这样……

En: No, it's *far* too late. All that remains is for you to feel bad, because you failed, and there's nothing you can do about it. That is the way of the world...
Zh: 不，已经*太*迟了。你失败了，所以你什么都做不了，只能暗自难过。这个世界就是这样……

En: You don't even have anything to say for yourself, do you? Just gonna take it all lying down? Why not grab your ankles while you're at it? That's why you never stood a chance.
Zh: 你甚至都没法为自己辩解，对不对？就只能躺下来承受这一切？还有挽回余地的时候为什么不抓住？所以你才永远都没机会。

En: Let this new face be a reminder, an unalterable mark of shame. This is the face of The Loser, the face of He Who Looks at Himself and Despairs. Now, get out of your sight.
Zh: 就让这张新面孔成为一个提醒吧，这是无法抹去的耻辱。这是一张失败者的脸，一张对自己丧失希望之人的脸。好了，离开自己的视线吧……

En: It turns out you've already been wearing your *true* face. The face of The Loser. The face of He Who Could Not Handle It. Now, get out of your sight.
Zh: 原来这就是你的*真*面孔。这是一张失败者的脸，一张无能为力之人的脸。好了，离开自己的视线吧……

En: But I was trying so hard! I was making progress!
Zh: 可我已经很努力了！我已经在进步了！

En: It wasn't enough. You simply don't have what it *takes*...
Zh: 这样不够。你就是不具有*成功*的必备条件……

En: Oh no...
Zh: 哦不…

En: What was *that*? Did you just *flinch* before your own reflection?
Zh: *什么*？你在自己的倒影面前*退缩*了？

En: I did not.
Zh: 我没有。

En: It was just a reflex!
Zh: 这只是条件反射！

En: Yes, you did. There's no use denying it...
Zh: 不，你有。否认也没用……

En: Use your chaincutters to fix the faucet. Stop steam from fogging up the mirror.
Zh: 用你的切链钳修好水龙头。防止镜子上沾染雾气。

En: Tool not in hand.
Zh: 手里没工具。

En: Dresscode: Home improver.
Zh: 着装要求：家庭改良者。

En: The chaincutters slip out of your hands as you attempt to twist the faucet into place. Well, you know one thing for sure -- you've probably never been a plumber.
Zh: 你尝试把水龙头拧回原位，但切链钳却从你手中滑了出来。有一件事你很肯定——你可能从来没当过水管工。

En: The faucet is quite terribly mangled, but you just might be able to twist its parts into place.
Zh: 水龙头损坏得很严重，不过你还是可能把一部分拧回原位。

En: You handle the chaincutters deftly, applying just enough pressure. The faucet regains a recognizable shape. The steam stops.
Zh: 你熟练地操作着切链钳，施加的压力也刚刚好。水龙头恢复了可以辨认的形状。蒸汽停止了。

En: The mirror begins to clear slowly without you having to wipe it.
Zh: 你不需要擦拭，镜子也开始慢慢变得清晰。

En: It's like something snaps in you -- a nerve ending, a thought, a sadness. Your face in the mirror is suddenly clean of the leer that had distorted it for god knows how long.
Zh: 就好像有什么东西突然在你身体里折断——一个神经末梢，一种想法，一种悲伤。镜子中映照出你的脸——那副不知道让它扭曲了多久的奸笑模样突然间被清除的一干二净。

En: It's like something snaps in you -- a nerve ending, a thought, a sadness. Your face in the mirror is suddenly clean of the leer that had distorted it. For god knows how long...
Zh: 就好像有什么东西突然在你身体里折断——一个神经末梢，一种想法，一种悲伤。镜子中映照出你的脸——那副让它扭曲的奸笑模样突然间被清除的一干二净。天知道你已经这样多久了……

En: Just like that it's over -- the running gag that your life had become. A sad old man looks back at you.
Zh: 它就这样结束了——你那如笑话一般的生活。一个悲伤的老男人回望着你。

En: Told you that you *needed* those chaincutters. Everything is connected. Everything has a purpose.
Zh: 早说你*需要*那些切链钳吧。一切都是有联系的。一切都是有目的的。

En: Told you that you *needed* those chaincutters. Everything is connected. Everything has a purpose.
Zh: 早说你*需要*那些切链钳吧。一切都是有联系的。一切都是有目的的。

En: The bathroom mirror has been wiped completely clean. You see the reflection of your face in the mirror -- even now adorned with *The Expression*.
Zh: 浴室里的镜子已经被完全擦拭干净。你从镜子里看见了自己脸庞的倒影——即使到了现在还是挂着那副*表情*。

En: A mirror hangs on the bathroom wall. It's barely covered in steam anymore. You see your face, such as it is. A regular, clean-shaven police officer.
Zh: 浴室墙上有一面镜子。上面几乎已经没有水蒸气了。你看清了自己的脸，即使并不怎么好。一个没有蓄胡子的普通警察。

En: The bathroom mirror has been wiped completely clean. You see the reflection of your face in the mirror -- such as it is. A regular, clean-shaven police officer.
Zh: 浴室里的镜子已经被完全擦拭干净。你从镜子里看见了自己脸庞的倒影——即使并不怎么好。一个没有蓄胡子的普通警察。

En: The bathroom mirror has been wiped completely clean. You see the reflection of your face in the mirror -- such as it is without *The Expression*. It's just hairy.
Zh: 浴室里的镜子已经被完全擦拭干净。你从镜子里看见了自己脸庞的倒影——即使并不怎么好，也没有那个*表情*。只是感觉毛发有点多。

En: The bathroom mirror has been wiped completely clean. You see the reflection of yourself in the mirror -- *The Expression* fixed to your clean-shaven face. You're still not accustomed to it.
Zh: 浴室里的镜子已经被完全擦拭干净。你从镜子里看见了自己脸庞的倒影——那个*表情*固定在一张没蓄胡子的脸上。你还是觉得不太习惯。

En: A mirror hangs on the bathroom wall. It's barely covered in steam anymore. You see your face, such as it is without *The Expression*. It's just hairy.
Zh: 浴室墙上有一面镜子。上面几乎已经没有水蒸气了。你看清了自己的脸，即使并不怎么好，也没有那个*表情*。只是感觉毛发有点多。

En: A mirror hangs on the bathroom wall. It's barely covered in steam anymore. You see yourself -- *The Expression* fixed to your clean-shaven face. You're still not accustomed to it.
Zh: 浴室墙上有一面镜子。上面几乎已经没有水蒸气了。你看到了自己——那个*表情*固定在一张没蓄胡子的脸上。你还是觉得不太习惯。

En: A mirror hangs on the bathroom wall. It's barely covered in steam anymore. It's your face in the mirror -- adorned with *The Expression*.
Zh: 浴室墙上有一面镜子。上面几乎已经没有水蒸气了。镜子里倒影出你的脸——上面还挂着那个*表情*。

En: A mirror hangs on the bathroom wall. In it -- your clean-shaven face, adorned with *The Expression*. You're still not accustomed to it.
Zh: 浴室墙上有一面镜子。在那里面——是你没有蓄胡子的脸，还有那个*表情*。你还是觉得不太习惯。

En: A mirror hangs on the bathroom wall. In it -- your face, now without *The Expression*. It's just hairy.
Zh: 浴室墙上有一面镜子。在那里面——是你的脸，现在那个*表情*已经不见。只是感觉毛发有点多。

En: A mirror hangs on the bathroom wall. In it -- your face, a regular, clean-shaven police officer.
Zh: 浴室墙上有一面镜子。在那里面——是你的脸，一个没有蓄胡子的普通警察。

En: An old mirror hangs on the wall, half-covered in shadows. You cannot see yourself fully, just the outline of a man. You have no memory of the face that awaits you there.
Zh: 墙上挂着一面陈旧的镜子，有一半都隐藏在阴影中。你不能完全看清自己，只有一个男人的轮廓。你不记得等待你的是一副怎样的面孔。

En: An old mirror hangs on the wall. You see the reflection of your face in it -- adorned with *The Expression*.
Zh: 墙上挂着一面陈旧的镜子。你在里面看见了自己脸庞的倒影——上面还挂着那个*表情*。

En: An old mirror hangs on the wall. You see your reflection in it -- *The Expression* fixed to your clean-shaven face. You're still not accustomed to it.
Zh: 墙上挂着一面陈旧的镜子。你在里面看见了自己脸庞的倒影——那个*表情*固定在一张没蓄胡子的脸上。你还是觉得不太习惯。

En: An old mirror hangs on the wall. You see the reflection of your face in it -- such as it is without *The Expression*. It's just hairy.
Zh: 墙上挂着一面陈旧的镜子。你在里面看见了自己脸庞的倒影——即使并不怎么好，也没有那个*表情*。只是感觉毛发有点多。

En: An old mirror hangs on the wall. You see the reflection of your face in it -- such as it is. A regular clean-shaven police officer.
Zh: 墙上挂着一面陈旧的镜子。你在里面看见了自己脸庞的倒影——即使并不怎么好。一个没有蓄胡子的普通警察。

En: Then -- houses along a narrow street. A video rental. Darkness on the planet's curvature.
Zh: 然后——你看见一条窄街上的房子，一家录像带租赁店，以及笼罩在星球曲线轮廓之上的黑暗。

En: This is not the cleanest bathtub in the world, but it's cleaner than you are right now.
Zh: 这不是世界上最整洁的浴缸，不过它比现在的你要干净。

En: Ah, that soap scum smell. It smells like life. At least compared to you.
Zh: 啊，那个皂垢的味道。闻起来像是生活的味道。至少跟你比起来是这样的。

En: Run yourself a bath.
Zh: 给自己冲个澡。

En: I want to live forever with the corpse smell. Memento mori and stuff. [Leave.]
Zh: 我想永远带着这种尸臭味活下去。这是死亡的象征。[离开。]

En: The bathtub slowly fills with water. The water beckons.
Zh: 浴缸里水慢慢满上了，水在召唤你。

En: Undress, close your eyes, and submerge.
Zh: 脱下衣服，闭上眼睛，然后浸入水中。

En: The water is only lukewarm, but still comforting, like amniotic fluid. A few beer cans are bobbing up and down along your flanks like sad duckies.
Zh: 水只是有些微温，不过还算舒适，仿佛羊水一般。几只啤酒罐在你的身侧上下浮沉，像悲伤的鸭子一样。

En: Take the beer cans out.
Zh: 把啤酒罐拿出来。

En: Leave the beer cans in.
Zh: 让啤酒罐在里面放着。

En: Now you are alone with your thoughts in the tub. But it's easier than being alone with your thoughts outside the tub.
Zh: 现在你泡在浴缸里独自思考，但这总比在浴缸外面要来的容易许多。

En: The cans continue to bob, as do your thoughts, aimlessly splashing up against the sides of the tub.
Zh: 啤酒罐继续吐着上下浮沉，你的想法也一样，漫无目的地泼溅在浴缸两侧。

En: Linger in the tub a little.
Zh: 在浴缸里多待一会儿。

En: Get out. (Conclude.)
Zh: 出去。（总结。）

En: The water line recedes as you stand. You are cold now. Your clothes stick to your still moist skin.
Zh: 你站起身，水位线也随之下降。现在你感觉有点冷。你的衣服粘在依然潮湿的皮肤上。

En: Your fingers grow pale and are covered with tiny whorls as the water cools.
Zh: 随着逐渐冷却的水温，你的手指变得苍白，上面盖满小小的螺纹。

En: What are you doing? You're not some fat fish in a fucking aquarium. Time to get moving.
Zh: 你在干什么？你又不是水族箱里那些肥硕的大鱼。是时候行动起来了。

En: You feel nice and lonely. And so, so tired.
Zh: 你感觉很好，很孤独。而且还特别，特别的疲惫。

En: They're not even really thoughts. Just assorted sensations, none of them acute enough to focus on.
Zh: 它们甚至都不算真正的想法。只是一些杂七杂八的感觉，一个都不够敏锐，费不着集中精力。

En: Imagine something.
Zh: 想象一些东西。

En: You see the corpse. You can still smell the cadaver on you. It's going to take more than one bath to get rid of that stench.
Zh: 你看见了尸体。你还能闻到自己身上的尸臭。要想彻底消除这股恶臭，不是洗一次澡就能解决的。

En: Hold it right there! Keep your head above water, or you'll lose all those pheromones Morell sprayed on you.
Zh: 保持住，别动！让头停留在水面上，不然莫雷尔朝你身上喷的那些费洛蒙就全没了。

En: I don't care. I just want to be engulfed.
Zh: 我不在乎。我只想被水淹没。

En: Keep your head above water, just in case.
Zh: 为了以防万一，把你的头停留在水面上。

En: The water doesn't feel particularly clean. A few beer cans are bobbing up and down along your flanks like sad duckies. You pause to think before letting your head go under.
Zh: 水感觉也不是特别的干净。几只啤酒罐在你的身侧上下浮沉，像悲伤的鸭子一样。你停下来想了一会儿，然后把头沉了下去。

En: The lukewarm water is comforting, like amniotic fluid.
Zh: 温水很舒适，仿佛羊水一般。

En: This fan has two chain pull switches -- one ends in a tiny fan, the other in a light bulb. A truly horrific necktie has somehow attached itself to one of the blades.
Zh: 吊扇有两根拉线开关——一个连着风扇，另一个连着小灯泡。一条令人不快的领带不知怎么地挂在了一片扇叶上。

En: A terrible mistake! Turn the lights off IMMEDIATELY! You can practically feel the photons burning a hole in your brain.
Zh: 这是个严重的错误！马上把灯关掉！你几乎能感觉到光子在你的脑袋里烧穿了一个洞。

En: The blades come squeaking to a halt.
Zh: 扇叶吱呀吱呀地停了下来。

En: The blades come squeaking to a halt. It should be easier to reach the tie now.
Zh: 扇叶吱呀吱呀地停了下来。现在应该能更容易够着领带了。

En: Bring it on.
Zh: 放马过来吧。

En: Pull again to turn the lights off.
Zh: 再拉一次把灯关掉。

En: Or has it been consigned there as punishment? You feel as though this creature is your *friend* and wants to reattach itself to your neck. So that you may continue your adventures together in this strange world!
Zh: 或者它被放在那里是一种惩罚？你觉得这个生物好像是你的*朋友*，想要重新回到你的脖子上。这样你和它就能一起在这个奇怪的世界继续冒险！

En: You swoop up and catch the tie... snap! It's released from the blade. WARNING! WARNING! The necktie is no longer contained.\n
Zh: 你猛地垫了一下脚，抓住了领带……啪的一声！它被扇叶释放了。警告！警告！领带失去控制。

En: You swoop up and catch the tie... Snap! It's released from the blade.
Zh: 你猛地垫了一下脚，抓住了领带……唰的一声！领带从扇叶上松脱了下来。

En: Little black spots dance on your retinas. It's almost pleasurable.
Zh: 小黑点在你的视网膜上舞动着。几乎有种让人愉悦的感觉。

En: The lights are off.
Zh: 灯关上了。

En: Grab the tie.
Zh: 抓住领带。

En: Fan is turned off
Zh: 风扇关掉了。

En: It's just a little hangover-induced photosensitivity. Don't over-react.
Zh: 这只是宿醉引起的一点光敏性。不用反应地这么激烈。

En: Your eyes burn with photosensitivity. It's not good.
Zh: 你的眼睛因为光敏性而备受煎熬。这不太好。

En: You reach out to grab the tie... but what's this? Diffuse, radiating chest pain! Doom comes over you.
Zh: 你伸出手去抓领带……不过这是什么？不断扩散、辐射开来的胸痛！厄运降临在了你头上。

En: You can take it. Just breathe in slowly.
Zh: 你可以承受的。只要慢慢呼吸就好。

En: Grab your chest.
Zh: 抓紧你的胸口。

En: Breathe in calmly.
Zh: 平静地呼吸。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: What you have in your hand is a fantastically colourful tie, with four or five different patterns. The knot reminds you of a noose.
Zh: 你手中攥着一条极其鲜艳的领带，上面大概有4、5种不同的图案。领结的样子让你想到了套索。

En: What you have in your hand is a truly hideous necktie, with four or five different patterns. The knot reminds you of a noose.
Zh: 你手中攥着一条丑到吓人的领带，上面大概有4、5种不同的图案。领结的样子让你想到了套索。

En: Pull on the fan.
Zh: 拉一下风扇。

En: Pull on the light bulb.
Zh: 拉一下灯泡。

En: The switch must be broken, because nothing happens. The air in the room is starting to feel like vaporized urine.
Zh: 开关一定是坏了，因为什么也没发生。你开始觉得房间里的空间像是汽化了的尿液。

En: The lights are off again.
Zh: 电灯又关上了。

En: The lights are on.
Zh: 灯是打开的。

En: Oh no, it's happening again. You didn't catch it and now there's numbness in your left arm, plus half your chest.
Zh: 哦不，又开始了。你没有抓到它，现在你的左胳膊感觉有些麻痹，再加上半边胸口。

En: It's even worse this time. Maybe you should stop trying to catch the tie?
Zh: 这次感觉更加糟糕。也许你应该别去拿那条领带了？

En: Still happening. Definitely worse than the last time.
Zh: 还是有感觉。绝对比上次更糟糕。

En: Finally the stabbing recedes. You could try doing it again to see how painful it gets -- but you would probably die.
Zh: 那种刺痛感总算是减弱了。你可以再试一次，看看到底会有多痛——不过你也可能会死的。

En: This is bad. Feels like sharp stones grinding in your chest and keeping you from moving.
Zh: 不太妙啊。感觉就像是尖锐的石头在碾磨你的胸口，让你动弹不得。

En: For quite a long time.
Zh: 持续了好长时间。

En: Still ongoing. Now is a good time to start worrying.
Zh: 还在继续。现在是开始担忧的好时机。

En: Finally the pressure recedes. You find yourself covered in cold sweat and trying not to move -- hoping it will keep you from dying.
Zh: 压力总算是减弱了。你发现自己出了一身冷汗，别动弹——希望这样你就不会死掉。

En: This is not a *real* heart attack. This is a joke. It's probably just your lungs or your oesophagus. The oesophagus does practically nothing.
Zh: 这不是*真正*的心脏病发作。这是个玩笑。可能只是你的肺或者食道。食道基本上什么都没做。

En: None of these is a real heart attack. This is such a joke. You should probably just exercise more.
Zh: 这一切都不是真正的心脏病发作。不过是个玩笑而已。也许你应该多锻炼锻炼。

En: If it's your *friend*, why was it up there? Who ties their friend to a ceiling fan? Maybe this thing is dangerous somehow...
Zh: 如果它是你的*朋友*，为什么它会挂在那上面？谁会把朋友绑在吊扇上？也许这玩意有点危险……

En: You can take this one too. Just breathe in slowly.
Zh: 这次你也可以承受的。只要慢慢吸气就好。

En: Grab your arm.
Zh: 抓紧你的胳膊。

En: The fan stands still.
Zh: 风扇纹丝不动。

En: The fan is spinning.
Zh: 风扇在旋转。

En: An ominous, foreboding feeling fills you, as you look at the tie.
Zh: 你看着那条领带，一种不祥的预感充斥着你。

En: You know what you should do? You should stop the fan and then try again, it'll get easier.
Zh: 你知道自己应该做什么吗？你应该把风扇停下来，然后再试一次，这样会容易很多。

En: Do it -- *if you want to die*, the stabbing pain in your chest is telling you. You're hanging by a thread here...
Zh: 去做吧——*如果你想死的话*，胸口的那阵刺痛这样对你说到。现在的你可谓是命悬一线……

En: Breathe in and out calmly.
Zh: 平静地吸气呼气。

En: Oh no, it's happening again. You didn't catch it and now there's numbness in half your chest.
Zh: 哦不，又开始了。你没有抓到它，现在你半边胸口都麻痹了。

En: Still happening.
Zh: 还在继续。

En: This isn't a real heart attack. You should probably just exercise more.
Zh: 这不是真正的心脏病发作。也许你应该多锻炼锻炼。

En: You reach out to grab the tie... but what's this? Radiating chest pain! Fear washes over you.
Zh: 你伸出手去抓领带……不过这是什么？不断辐射开来的胸痛！恐惧感向你袭来。

En: You can take it. Just breathe.
Zh: 你可以承受的。只要记得呼吸就好。

En: Still bad. Definitely worse than the last time.
Zh: 还是很不好。绝对比上次更糟糕。

En: Grab your chest.
Zh: 抓紧你的胸口。

En: Finally the stabbing recedes. You could try doing it again to see how painful it gets. (Very.) But do you really want to risk it?
Zh: 那种刺痛感总算是减弱了。你可以再试一次，看看到底会有多痛。（很痛。）不过你真的想冒这个险吗？

En: You can take this. It's not nearly as bad as the last time.
Zh: 你可以承受的。这次并不像上次那么糟糕。

En: It's worse this time. Maybe you should stop trying to catch the tie?
Zh: 这次感觉更加糟糕。也许你应该别去拿那条领带了？

En: This is not a *real* heart attack. This is a joke. It's probably just your lungs or your oesophagus. The oesophagus does practically nothing.
Zh: 这不是*真正*的心脏病发作。这是个玩笑。可能只是你的肺或者食道。食道基本上什么都没做。

En: Finally the pressure recedes. You find yourself covered in cold sweat. Try not to move too fast -- maybe that will keep you from collapsing.
Zh: 压力总算消退了。你发现自己出了一身冷汗。尽量不要移动地太快——也许这样你就不会崩溃。

En: For quite a long time.
Zh: 持续了好长时间。

En: This is bad. Feels like sharp stones grinding in your chest and keeping you from moving.
Zh: 不太妙啊。感觉就像是尖锐的石头在碾磨你的胸口，让你动弹不得。

En: Still ongoing. Now is a good time to start worrying.
Zh: 还在继续。现在是开始担忧的好时机。

En: Press your hands to your chest to make sure your heart is still beating okay.
Zh: 把你的手按压在自己胸口，确保自己的心脏还在正常跳动。

En: There is nothing. Only warm, primordial blackness. Your conscience ferments in it -- no larger than a single grain of malt. You don't have to do anything anymore.
Zh: 这里什么也没有。只有温暖的，原始的黑暗。你的意识在里面发酵——并不比一粒麦芽大多少。你不用再做任何事了。

En: Ever.
Zh: 永远。

En: Never ever.
Zh: 永远的永远。

En: Never ever ever?
Zh: 永远永远的永远？

En: (Simply keep on non-existing.)
Zh: （简单地保持这种不存在的状态。）

En: Never ever ever ever, baby!
Zh: 永远永远的永远，宝贝！

En: An inordinate amount of time passes.  It is utterly void of struggle. No ex-wives are contained within it.
Zh: 时间在这里汹涌的流逝。它是湮没了一切呻吟的终极宁静。没有留下任何旧日依恋。

En: This is great!
Zh: 这真是太棒了！

En: Yes. It. Is.
Zh: 是的。没错。

En: Gimme some more.
Zh: 再多来一些。

En: You got it, sweet brother! Nothing upon nothing, upon nothing!
Zh: 你说对了，亲爱的兄弟！除了虚无就是虚无，只有虚无！

En: I like nothing.
Zh: 我喜欢虚无。

En: How about you cough up some more of that sweet oblivion?
Zh: 不如你再吐出点那种甜蜜的湮灭？

En: I know you do, baby... I know...
Zh: 我知道你会的，宝贝……我知道……

En: Coming right up, sir. Smooth passage...
Zh: 马上就来，先生。舒适的旅行……

En: Allons-y, let's go!
Zh: 出发吧，我们走！

En: All right. Nothingtown to Fuck-All-Borough!
Zh: 好吧。从虚无镇通往操蛋自治区！

En: I want to get off now, I like pain and burning light and wanting things from people who don't want to give them to me.
Zh: 我想现在下去，我喜欢痛苦，喜欢燃烧的灯光，从不愿意施舍我的人手中拿到东西。

En: Do you really?
Zh: 你真这么想？

En: Don't be naive, of course not. I want to sail the inky blackness until forever ends!
Zh: 别天真了，当然不是。我想在这片像墨汁一般的漆黑世界里一路航行到永恒的尽头！

En: Look, there's *I Am Tired of Being This Type of Animal* and Who-Gives-a-Shit!
Zh: 看，*我已经厌倦做这种畜生*和*谁他妈在乎*又来了！

En: A return trip to The Silence please!
Zh: 我要开启重返寂静之旅，谢谢！

En: I do. Let me off.
Zh: 是的。让我摆脱这一切。

En: Let's visit the ancient Zero-Holm!
Zh: 我们去拜访古老的零度沙洲吧！

En: Not you! Your days of giving a shit and being that type of animal are over.
Zh: 你不会的！你的*谁他妈在乎*和*做那畜生*的日子已经过去了。

En: You wouldn't like it if I told you what's back there. Why do you think you had to bludgeon yourself into oblivion? Or did you not sense yourself -- marinating? Poured so much over yourself... Got a bit *carried away* did we, chef?
Zh: 如果我告诉你回去之后有什么的话，你是不会喜欢的。你以为你为什么要把自己吓到失忆？难道你感觉不到自己——已经被腌渍过了？喝得太多了……有点*忘乎所以*了，不是吗，大厨？

En: I don't care, I'm an idiot. A brave idiot.
Zh: 我不在乎，我是个白痴。一个勇敢的白痴。

En: Tell me, what's waiting for me?
Zh: 告诉我，是什么在等着我？

En: There's this giant ball there. And evil apes. And the evil apes are dukin' it out on the ball. You're one of them. It's basically all just evil apes dukin' it out on a giant ball.
Zh: 那里有一个巨大的球。还有邪恶的猩猩。而且邪恶的猩猩在那个球上争得你死我活。你也是其中之一。所以基本上，就是一群邪恶的猩猩在一个巨大的球上争得你死我活。

En: How big is the ball?
Zh: 那个球有多大？

En: How small are the apes?
Zh: 那些猩猩有多小？

En: That sounds like something I would like to do -- let's go!
Zh: 听起来像是我喜欢做的事——我们走吧！

En: You can't even make out it's a ball, when you're dukin' it out. It's that large.
Zh: 争个你死我活的时候，你甚至都认不出它是个球。它就有那么大。

En: Infinitesimally small.
Zh: 极其微小的微小。

En: And this *dukin' it out* I keep hearing about - what's that?
Zh: 那我一直听到的*争个你死我活*——又是什么？

En: Vying for resources? It's just a stupid expression you picked up somewhere. The part of the presentation you want to take home with you is this: you have to beat the other evil apes in the face or you lose.
Zh: 争夺资源？这只是你从某个地方学到的一种愚蠢的表达。那段陈述里你要理解的只有一点：跟其他邪恶猩猩打架的时候必须直接冲着脸去，不然你就会输。

En: That's sad.
Zh: 那也太悲惨了吧。

En: Yes, it is. And you drowned in that sadness a long time ago.
Zh: 的确如此。而且你很久以前就已经溺死在悲痛之中了。

En: What do you mean *drowned*?
Zh: 你说*溺死*是什么意思？

En: You lost.
Zh: 你失败了。

En: Great choice, elder one. It has always been like this -- and it always will.
Zh: 很棒的选择，长者。它一直都是这样——而且也一直都会这样。

En: Do you want me to upgrade that into a *one way trip*, sir?
Zh: 你希望我帮你升级成*单程旅行*吗，先生？

En: Don't stop, keep singing. Sing me the song of death.
Zh: 别停下，继续唱。给我唱一首死亡之歌。

En: Somewhere in the sore, bloated man-meat around you -- *a sensation*!
Zh: 在酸痛和肿胀的人类肌肉环绕着的某个地方——是一种*感觉*！

En: What was that about the *ex-* something?
Zh: 那个*旧*什么的是个什么东西？

En: An awareness creeps up on you. A mass lies hidden in your dead angle, soaking in some lurid, acidic sauce. It's bloated and shameful, a ball of meat surrounding you... This is a terrible line of questioning, and it will only lead to more awareness of the meat-thing.
Zh: 一种意识向你悄然袭来。一大团东西隐藏在你的死角，浸泡在某种可怕的酸性酱汁里。它臃肿又不体面，就像一个肉球将你包围……这一连串可怕的询问，只会让你更多地感知到那个像肉球一样的东西。

En: There. No ball of meat, no light in the formless nothing. Just nightswimming!
Zh: 在那里。没有肉球，无形的虚无里没有灯光。只有夜泳！

En: Mother, help me, there's a head attached to my neck and I'm *in* it.
Zh: 妈妈，帮帮我，我的脖子上连着一个脑袋，我也在脑袋*里面*。

En: Please, no! I changed my mind! Take me back to the formless, disembodied nothing!
Zh: 求你了，不要！我改变主意了！带我回到那片无形又空洞的虚无里！

En: Stop! I don't want to hear anything more about this *sensation*. Take me back to the formless, disembodied nothing!
Zh: 住嘴！我不想再听到任何关于这种*感觉*的事了。带我回到那片无形又空洞的虚无里！

En: Who *am* I? What sort of creature does this to their own mouth?!
Zh: 我*是*谁？什么样的生物居然会对自己的嘴巴做出这种事情？

En: Help! Someone! Cut my head off, it's trying to murder the rest of me!
Zh: 救命！有人吗！快把我的脑袋切掉，它想谋杀我其余的部分！

En: A fiery streak penetrates your skull, trying to force your eyes open. It's a sound. A clarion call from hell.
Zh: 一道炽热的斑纹穿透你的头颅，试图强迫你睁开双眼。它是一道声音。来自地狱的强烈呼唤。

En: Wait, I did this to myself?
Zh: 等等，这是我自己做的吗？

En: Yes, you're one disco mother.
Zh: 是的，你就是这一切的始作俑者，你是迪斯科之母。

En: The song of death is sweet and endless... But what is this? Somewhere in the sore, bloated *man-meat* around you -- a sensation!
Zh: 死亡之歌甜蜜而永无止境……但这是什么？在你酸痛和肿胀的人类肌肉环绕着的某个地方——是一种感觉！

En: Somehow you know what it is -- a Coupris Kineema motor carriage.
Zh: 不知为什么，你知道这是什么——一辆库普瑞斯锐影汽車。

En: The sound outside -- you recognize it. It's a Coupris Kineema motor carriage.
Zh: 外面的那个声音——你认出来了。是一辆库普瑞斯锐影汽車。

En: (Plunge back into the fathomless deep.)
Zh: （重新跳入无形的深渊。）

En: No, I wanted to know about the ex-something.
Zh: 不，我想知道这个*旧日之物*。

En: Ex-love, ex-tenderness. It is foolish of you to resurface to the loss. Not after all the damage you suffered to get here, some of it irreversible... Stay, sail with me through the Abyssopelagic Zone!
Zh: 旧爱，过往的柔情。想让失去的东西重现实在是愚不可及。你承受了如此多的伤害才走到今天这一步，太迟了，有些事情已经无可挽回……留下来，跟我一起航行，穿越这片不毛之地！

En: Allons-y! Never let me go!
Zh: 出发吧！永远不要让我离开！

En: You can take it, you're a Champion!
Zh: 你可以承受的，你是个战士！

En: No, I am not scared, I am a Champion!
Zh: 不，我不害怕，我是一个战士！

En: Okay, I'm a bit scared now. Let's go back to the dark.
Zh: 好吧，现在我稍微有点害怕。我们还是重返黑暗吧。

En: Like a fly to the ointment, your conscience sticks to it. The limbed and headed machine of pain and undignified suffering is firing up again. It wants to walk the desert. Hurting. Longing. Dancing to disco music.
Zh: 就像一只粘在油膏上的苍蝇，你的意识也牢牢粘在了上面。那具有血有肉的苦痛之躯，连同着它所承受的那毫无尊严的折磨，又躁动了起来。它想要在沙漠穿行。疼痛着，渴望着，舞动着。在迪斯科的旋律里。

En: Wait, no! I need to belittle myself instead.
Zh: 等等，不！正好相反，我需要贬低我自己。

En: [Open your eyes.]
Zh: [睁开眼睛。]

En: The stench of liquor rises from your mouth. And with it -- an *un-godly* headache.
Zh: 你的嘴巴里泛起酒精的恶臭。随之而来的——还有*极度*的头疼。

En: No, I want to get off now. I like pain and burning light and wanting things from people who don't want to give them to me.
Zh: 不，我想摆脱这一切，我喜欢痛苦，喜欢燃烧的火光，想要从不愿意施舍我的人手中拿到东西。

En: Fear and apprehension... You should ask what's out there first.
Zh: 恐惧和忧虑……你应该先问问外面有什么。

En: If you have more questions, don't hesitate to ask.
Zh: “如果你还有更多问题，不要犹豫，尽管问。”

En: A bespectacled man in a black bomber jacket is tapping his foot on the floor. Looks like he's waiting for someone -- you.
A bespectacled man in an orange bomber jacket is tapping his foot on the floor. Looks like he's waiting for someone -- you.
Wait, shouldn't I have a badge or something? (Check your undies.)
Zh: “等等，我不是应该有警徽或者什么的吗？”（检查你的内衣。）

En: Wait, shouldn't I have a badge or something? (Check your pockets.)
Zh: “等等，我不是应该有警徽或者什么的吗？”（检查你的口袋。）

En: You mean you don't have a badge?
Zh: “你的意思是你没有警徽？”

En: It wasn't on me when I woke up.
Zh: “我醒来的时候身上没有。”

En: (Pretend you found it.) "I have my badge! I'm a policeman and I have my badge."
Zh: （假装找到了。）“我有警徽！我是个警察，当然有警徽了。”

En: Losing your identification card is a *serious* matter. My vehicle has a shortwave -- you can use it to report your badge missing. I advise you to try to locate it as quickly as possible. But getting the body down should still take precedence.
Zh: “丢失身份证件是一个很*严重*的问题。我車里有短波电台——你可以用它报告警徽丢失的情况。我会建议你尽快把它找出来。不过把尸体放下来还是更优先。”

En: Oh... There's a moment's pause. It lasts longer than it may ordinarily, given the circumstances. "If you didn't have your badge then that would be very *bad*. You would need to report it on my shortwave. But since you *do* have it, we can go straight to the task at hand."
Zh: “哦……”他停顿了一下。考虑到眼下的情况，这次停顿比平时要久一些。“如果你没有警徽，那就很*糟糕*了。你需要用我的短波电台向你们分局汇报。不过既然你*有*的话，那就可以直接处理手头的任务了。”

En: Oh... There's a moment's pause, then he continues, obviously relieved: "If you didn't have your badge then that would be very *bad*. You would need to report it on my shortwave. But since you *do* have it, we can go straight to the task at hand."
Zh: “哦……”他停顿了一下，然后继续往下说，显然是松了一口气：“如果你没有警徽，那就很*糟糕*了。那样的话，你就用我的短波电台跟警局汇报。不过你*有*警徽的话，那就可以直接处理手头的任务了。”

En: If you're about to embark on an investigation, shouldn't you have a badge?
Zh: 如果你要着手调查的话，难道不应该有个警徽吗？

En: A bespectacled man in an orange bomber jacket is tapping his foot on the floor. Looks like he's waiting for someone -- you.
Zh: 一个戴着眼镜、身穿橙色飞行员夹克的男人用脚掌拍打着地面。看来他在等什么人——等你。

En: Hello, I'm Kim Kitsuragi. His grip is firm. "Lieutenant, Precinct 57. You must be from the 41st..."
Zh: “你好，我是金·曷城。”他的手很有力量。“警督，来自57分局。你一定是41分局的吧……”

En: I don't really know my name.
Zh: “我真的不记得自己的名字。”

En: Uh, I'm... maybe I'm Harry?
Zh: “呃，我……也许我叫哈里？”

En: You realize he is waiting for your name.
Zh: 你意识到他在等你说出自己的名字。

En: Invent a name for yourself.
Zh: 试着想出一个名字。

En: Partial to 'The Officer'.
Zh: 更喜欢‘警官’。

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi is now in your party. You can talk to him whenever, by interacting with him.
Zh: 金警督现在加入你的小队了。你可以随时与他交谈。

En: What a shame. Get to it now. *Rip* that body down from the tree.
Zh: 真丢人。现在就去弄。把那具尸体从树上*扯下来*。

En: Don't worry about the body. I'll rip it down.
Zh: “尸体的事不用担心。我会把它扯下来的。”

En: After it's been up in a tree for that long, any kind of ripping is inadvisable.
Zh: “在树上挂了那么久，任何激烈的动作都是不明智的。”

En: Delicate goods. Filled with stinking horrors...
Zh: 易碎品。充满恶臭的恐惧……

En: Yeah, a painkiller would be good about now. This thing is *pulsating* with discomfort.
Zh: 是啊，现在吃点止痛药就好了。这种东西正在不适地*跳动*着。

En: The best cure for a headache is, of course, morphine. They won't have that, so cigarettes will have to do.
Zh: 治疗头痛最好的药物，当然是吗啡了。他们不会有那种东西的，所以香烟就可以了。

En: If an assault were launched on this building right now -- if the windows came crashing down and the whole world descended upon you -- this man would hurl himself in death's way to save you. You are sure of this -- but why?
Zh: 如果现在这栋建筑遭到袭击——如果窗户粉碎掉落，全世界都压在你的身上——这个男人会不惜付出生命的代价来拯救你。这一点你非常肯定——但是为什么呢？

En: On the sleeve of his bomber jacket -- as well as on its back -- are the same enigmatic white rectangles as on your blazer.
Zh: 他那件飞行员夹克的袖子——还有背后——跟你夹克上那个神秘的白色矩形是一样的。

En: Shake his hand.
Zh: 跟他握手。

En: Don't shake his hand.
Zh: 不跟他握手。

En: Hold on, who is he to me?
Zh: 等等，他是我的什么人？

En: He is your half-brother.
Zh: 他是你好哥们。

En: Just call me *Officer*.
Zh: “叫我*警官*就好。”

En: Concentration makes you squint your eyes. Your name should be deep gold and orange, like a forest fire looming on the horizon, but mixed with the stench of liquor rising from your breath. You're two steps closer to it, but there are still many to go...
Zh: 专注思考让你眯起双眼。你的名字应该是深金色和橙色的，就像在地平线上若隐若现的森林大火，不过还混杂着口气里酒精的恶臭。你离它又近了两步，但是还有很长的路要走……

En: Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau.
Zh: 拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托。

En: It's so cool.
Zh: 真是太酷了。

En: It's very cool, but won't I come off like a douche if I call myself that?
Zh: 它很酷，不过如果我这么称呼自己的话，不会感觉像个蠢蛋吗？

En: It's very, very cool. But maybe just a bit too... ostentatious?
Zh: 它真的非常、非常酷。不过也许有那么一点……太卖弄了？

En: My name is Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau.
Zh: “我的名字叫拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托。”

En: You instinctively run your hand over your multi-patterned orange tie. The sensation of wrinkled silk somehow makes the name sound even cooler.
Zh: 你本能的把手放在那条花纹繁琐的橙色领带上。不知怎么的，手中那种皱巴巴的丝绸感让这个名字显得更酷了。

En: Don't worry. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau is one classy name -- for one classy cop.
Zh: 别担心。拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托是一个很有格调的名字——正适合一个有格调的警察。

En: Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau is not ostentatious, it's classy. This is an opportunity to start your life anew as a *classier* person.
Zh: 拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托一点也不卖弄，它很有格调。这是你重新开始生活的机会，这一次你会变成一个更*有格调*的人。

En: You instinctively run your hand over the multi-patterned silk of your tie. Its slickness gives you comfort and reassurance.
Zh: 你本能的把手放在那条花纹繁琐的丝制领带上。那种光滑的感觉给了带给你舒适和安慰。

En: Yes it is. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau is one classy name for one classy cop. Say it!
Zh: 是的，没错。拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托是一个很有格调的名字，正适合一个有格调的警察。说出来吧！

En: Yes, it is. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau sounds solemn and historical. This is an opportunity to start your life anew as a *classier* person.
Zh: 是的，没错。拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托听起来庄严而又富有历史气息。这是你重新开始生活的机会，这一次你会变成一个更*有格调*的人。

En: More classy. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau is one classy name -- and you're one classy cop.
Zh: 更有格调了。拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托是一个很有格调的名字——而你是一个有格调的警察。

En: Even classier. You should take this opportunity to start your life anew as a *classier* person -- as Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau.
Zh: 甚至更有格调了。你应该抓住这个机会重新开始生活，成为一个更*有格调*的人——成为拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托。

En: You instinctively run your hand over the multi-patterned silk of your tie. Its slickness gives you comfort and reassurance.
Zh: 你本能的把手放在那条花纹繁琐的丝制领带上。那种光滑的感觉给了带给你舒适和安慰。

En: Don't worry. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau is one classy name -- for one classy cop.
Zh: 别担心。拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托是一个很有格调的名字——正适合一个有格调的警察。

En: Don't worry. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau sounds solemn and historical. Take this opportunity to start your life anew as a *classier* person.
Zh: 别担心。拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托听起来庄严而又富有历史气息。抓住这个机会重新开始生活，成为一个更*有格调*的人。

En: No, you won't. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau is one classy name and you're one classy cop. Say it!
Zh: 不，你不会的。拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托是一个有格调的名字，而你是一个有格调的警察。快说吧！

En: No, you won't. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau sounds solemn and historical. This is an opportunity to start your life anew as a *classier* person.
Zh: 不，你不会的。拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托听起来庄严而又富有历史气息。这是你重新开始生活的机会，这一次你会变成一个更*有格调*的人。

En: No, it's not! Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau is one classy name for one classy cop. Say it!
Zh: 不，不会的。拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托是一个有格调的名字，而你是一个有格调的警察。快说吧！

En: No, it's not. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau sounds solemn and historical. This is an opportunity to start your life anew as a *classier* person.
Zh: 不，不会的。拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托听起来庄严而又富有历史气息。这是你重新开始生活的机会，这一次你会变成一个更*有格调*的人。

En: Yes, well... He doesn't even process what you just said, just moves on.
Zh: “是的，好吧……”他甚至都没好好消化你刚才说的话就继续下一步了。

En: Not for a moment does he believe that's your real name. He casts it aside as an intradepartmental joke or a peculiarity he doesn't understand.
Zh: 他一刻也不相信那是你的真名。他把这当成了警局内部的一个笑话，或者是自己无法理解的一个怪癖放在一旁。

En: Very well. Officer... He's slightly confused.
Zh: “很好。警官……”他有些困惑。

En: Hello, I'm Kim Kitsuragi. He looks unfazed. "Lieutenant, Precinct 57. You must be from the 41st..."
Zh: “你好，我是金·曷城。”他看起来很淡定。“警督，来自57分局。你一定是41分局的吧……”

En: As you approach, he narrows his eyes and extends his hand in greeting.
Zh: 你慢慢靠近，他眯起眼睛，伸出手来打招呼。

En: You realize he is waiting for your name.
Zh: 你意识到他在等你说出自己的名字。

En: You seem a little unsure, officer. Have you spent too much time undercover lately? He cracks an uneasy smile to let you know it's a joke.
Zh: “你好像有点不太确定，警官。最近是不是卧底太久？”他挤出一个不自在的笑容，让你知道这是个笑话。

En: Okay, then. He processes the information, then disregards it.
Zh: “那好吧。”他处理了这条信息，然后选择了忽视。

En: It looks like we had a little scheduling error on Sunday. Saturday too, actually... Have you had time to talk to the manager here?
Zh: “看来周日的日程安排上出现了一点小失误。其实，周六也是……你跟这里的经理谈过了吗？”

En: What he means is he has been trying to meet up with you for two days, but you have been *otherwise occupied*.
Zh: 他的意思是他两天前就尝试跟你会面了，不过你一直*在忙其他的事*。

En: You mean him? (Nod toward the Cafeteria Manager.) "Yeah, I just talked to him."
Zh: “你是说他吗？”（朝餐厅经理的方向点点头。）“是的，我刚跟他聊完。”

En: No, I haven't.
Zh: “不，还没有。”

En: There must be some kind of a misunderstanding. You're taking me for someone else.
Zh: “这里面一定有些误会。你把我跟其他人弄混了。”

En: If you don't mind, we should talk to him again. Ask him for a run-down of the area -- now that I'm here as well. I understand the scene is out back, right?
Zh: “如果你不介意的话，我们应该再跟他聊聊。既然现在我也来了——应该问问他这片区域的情况。我没理解错的话，现场是在外面，对吧？”

En: Then we should ask him for a run-down of the area, get me up to speed. I understand the scene is out back, right?
Zh: “那我们应该问问他这片区域的情况，让我跟上进度。我理解没错的话，现场是在外面的后院，对吧？”

En: No, I don't think so. He studies you briefly. "We should ask him for a run-down of the area now that I'm here as well. I understand the scene is out back, right?"
Zh: “不，我不这么觉得。”他简短地打量了你一下。“既然现在我也来了，我们应该问问他这片区域的情况。我没理解错的话，现场是在外面，对吧？”

En: It also wouldn't hurt to assure him the police are finally here. In full force, I mean. Have you mapped out the initial interviews?
Zh: “让他明白警察终于来了也不是什么坏事。我的意思是，已经全员到齐了。你计划好最初的走访顺序了吗？”

En: You mean that guy? (Nod toward the man behind the counter.) "I *totally* talked to him."
Zh: “你说那个家伙吗？”（朝着柜台背后的男人点点头。）“我*彻彻底底*地跟他谈过了。”

En: This man is doing you a professional courtesy. He knows you haven't talked to the proprietor about whatever it was you were supposed to talk to him about.
Zh: 这个男人这么做是出于职业上的礼貌。他知道你还没有跟业主聊过那些应该聊的事。

En: What interviews?
Zh: “什么走访？”

En: I have. Yes.
Zh: “是的，当然有了。”

En: I haven't.
Zh: “我没有。”

En: At the 57th, we like to prepare an initial list of persons of interest and then just... skim the surface. He gestures with his fingers. "Prepare the field, get to know the players. You don't do that? Maybe it's not an inter-district practice..."
Zh: “在57分局的话，我们喜欢准备一份初始嫌疑人清单，然后……试试水。”他用手指比了个手势。“勘查现场，查访相关人员。你不会这么做吗？也许这不是一种跨区域惯例吧……”

En: Okay. We'll have time for that after we take a look at the coroner's case.
Zh: “好吧。看完验尸官的报告之后，我们还是有时间可以安排的。”

En: Good. But even if you haven't, we'll have time for that after we take a look at the coroner's case.
Zh: “很好。不过就算你没有，看完验尸官的报告之后，我们还是有时间可以安排的。”

En: Yes, the police. I'm aware I'm a policeman.
Zh: “是的，警察。我知道自己是个警察。”

En: Who? You? You're with the police?
Zh: “谁？你？你是警察？”

En: Right, and the interviews? He taps his foot -- more out of habit than impatience.
Zh: “没错，那走访呢？”他用脚拍打着地面——更像是出于一种习惯，而不是不耐烦。

En: Look man, you know, yeah.
Zh: “老兄，你懂的，是啊。”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: Completely.
Zh: “完全搞定。”

En: Yes, of course.
Zh: “是的，当然。”

En: Dead body?
Zh: “尸体？”

En: Mm-hmm.
I don't like dead bodies.
Zh: “我不喜欢尸体。”

En: Does that mean the body is no longer in the tree?
Zh: “那是不是说尸体已经不在树上了？”

En: So, the body is still in the tree...
Zh: “所以，尸体还在树上……”

En: This is the first time you detect a weariness in the lieutenant's voice. It is obvious he would have preferred for the body to no longer be in the tree.
Zh: 这是你第一次从警督的声音里察觉到一丝疲惫。显然他更希望尸体已经不在树上了。

En: Completely? Does that mean you took the body down from the tree?
Zh: “完全搞定？是不是说你把尸体从树上放下来了？”

En: ...where it has been hanging for seven days straight. We should go there as soon as we're done talking to the owner.
Zh: “……尸体已经在那里挂了7天了。我们跟业主谈完话之后，应该马上过去看看。”

En: What if I told you I'm not really a police officer?
Zh: “如果说我不是一个真警察呢？”

En: How come you're so sure I'm from the police?
Zh: “你怎么能肯定我是警察？”

En: How can you be so sure I'm from the police?
Zh: “你怎么能肯定我是警察？”

En: But I can't remember anything.
Zh: “但我什么都想不起来了。”

En: What were we supposed to do again?
Zh: “我们应该做什么来着？”

En: We all feel that way sometimes. There is no such thing as a *police officer*, I'm afraid. What remains is that there is a dead body in the tree -- someone has to figure out who put it there. If we don't, no one else will.
Zh: “有时候我们都会有这种感觉，恐怕这里确实不存在所谓的*警察*。而事实是树上挂了一具尸体——总要有人查清楚是谁做的。如果我们不管，其他人更不会了。”

En: And then, soon after, dead bodies would be dangling from all the trees.
Zh: 不久之后，所有树上都会挂满了尸体。

En: I was sent here to meet a detective from Precinct 41. You have the insignia of the Citizens Militia on your sleeve and on your back. He points to your jacket.
Zh: “我被派来这里跟一个来自41分局的警官会合。你的袖子和背后都有公民武装的标志。”他指向你的夹克。

En: I suppose you could be impersonating him. You could have gotten the insignia from the black market or forged it. But for now I am going to set those possibilities aside. I'm not from the Inspectorate General.
Zh: “我想你可以假扮他。标志也许是从黑市上买来的，或者是伪造的。不过现在我会把那些可能性都放在一边，毕竟我不是总监察组的。”

En: Inspectorate General means internal affairs. What he's saying is he's not from the *rat squad* and isn't supposed to suspect such things.
Zh: 总监察组是专为负责内部事务的。他的意思是他不是*内奸小队*的人，所以不会怀疑这种事情。

En: Ah, you mean the rat squad!
Zh: “啊，你是说内奸小队！”

En: You mean internal affairs?
Zh: “你是说内部事务组？”

En: No need for derogatory terms. They are only doing their job.
Zh: “没必要使用这种贬义的称谓。他们只是做自己份内的事。”

En: Yes. But I am not them. I'm from Criminal Investigation.
Zh: “没错，但我不是他们的人。我属于刑事侦察部门。”

En: Uh... Inspectorate General?
Zh: “呃……总监察组？”

En: Internal affairs. And I'm not them. I'm from Criminal Investigation.
Zh: “内部事务组，不过我不是其中一员。我属于刑事侦察部门。”

En: I can see you drank last night, and the night before, he says after a moment of silence. "And that you are still drunk now. But I have seen officers go through worse. Much worse."
Zh: 他沉默地看了你一会儿。“我能看出来你昨晚喝酒了，前天晚上也是。而且现在还醉醺醺的。不过我见过情况更糟的警官，比你还要糟糕。”

En: If you need something for your headache, there is a general store nearby. But, as I said, the dead body should be our number one concern.
Zh: “如果你想弄点头疼药的话，附近有一间杂货铺。不过，我刚才也说过了，现在那具尸体才是我们最应该关注的问题。”

En: You said insignia. These white rectangles, you mean? (Point to your sleeve.)
Zh: “你说的标志，是指这些白色矩形吗？”（指着自己的袖子。）

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: You mean you don't have a badge?
Zh: “你的意思是你没有警徽？”

En: It was not on me when I woke up.
Zh: “我醒来的时候身上没有。”

En: Losing your identification card is a *serious* matter. My vehicle has a shortwave -- you can use it to report your badge missing. I would advise you to locate it as quickly as possible. But getting the body down should still take precedence.
Zh: “丢失身份证件是一个很*严重*的问题。我車里有短波电台——你可以用它报告警徽丢失的情况。我会建议你尽快把它找出来。不过把尸体放下来还是更优先。”

En: Okay then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: He nods.
Zh: 他点点头。

En: But shouldn't I have a badge or something? (Check your undies.)
Zh: “但是，我不是应该有个警徽或者什么的吗？”（检查你的内衣。）

En: But shouldn't I have a badge or something? (Check your pockets.)
Zh: “但是，我不是应该有个警徽或者什么的吗？”（检查你的口袋。）

En: But they're just white rectangles.
Zh: “但它们只是白色矩形啊。”

En: They're not *just* white rectangles. They bear a halogen watermark with the letters RCM and a pattern resembling the street grid of Revachol West.
Zh: “它们不*只是*白色矩形。上面印着卤素水印——RCM的字母，还有一副类似瑞瓦肖西部街道网的图案。”

En: I would ask you to step into the headlights of my motor carriage, but, again, it's none of my concern. I just need you to do your job.
Zh: “我会让你用車头灯检查一下，不过，再声明一次，这并非我关注的重点。我只需要你做好自己的工作就行。”

En: Sure. But did you take it down from the tree?
Zh: “当然。不过你把它从树上放下来了吗？”

En: Talk to the manager. Then we go out back and take the body down.
Zh: “先去找经理谈谈。然后我们去后院把尸体放下来。”

En: Let's get going, then. (Move on.)
Zh: “那么就动起来吧。”（继续前进。）

En: Let's roll. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们走吧。”[离开。]

En: Let's go. [Leave.]
Zh: “让我们动起来。”[离开。]

En: After you, officer.
Zh: “你先，警官。”

En: It is not yet time.
Zh: “现在还不是时候。”

En: Have you removed the dead body from the tree?
Zh: “你把尸体从树上放下来了吗？”

En: (Pretend you found it.) "I have my badge. I'm a police man and I have my badge."
Zh: （假装找到了。）“我有警徽。我是个警察，当然有自己的警徽。”

En: Oh... There's a moment's pause. It lasts longer than it may ordinarily, given the circumstances. "If you didn't have your badge then that would be very *bad*. You would need to report it on my shortwave. But since you *do* have it, we can go straight to the task at hand."
Zh: “哦……”他停顿了一下。考虑到眼下的情况，这次停顿比平时要久一些。“如果你没有警徽，那就很*糟糕*了。你需要用我的短波向你们分局汇报。不过既然你*有*的话，那就可以直接处理手头的任务了。”

En: Oh... There's a moment's pause, then he continues, carefully: "If you didn't have your badge then that would be very *bad*. You would need to report it on my shortwave. But since you *do* have it, we can go straight to the task at hand."
Zh: “哦……”他停顿了一下，然后小心翼翼地继续说到：“如果你没有警徽，那就很*糟糕*了。你需要用我的短波电台向你们分局汇报。不过既然你*有*的话，那就可以直接处理手头的任务了。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: You think I'm a cop? I can't be a police officer! Look at me!
Zh: “你觉得我是个警察？我怎么可能是警察呢！你看看我这个鬼样子！”

En: A bespectacled man in an orange bomber jacket has folded up his newspaper and is now tapping his foot on the floor. Looks like he's been waiting for quite some time.
Zh: 一个戴着眼镜、穿着橙色飞行员夹克的男人叠起了手中的报纸，用脚拍打着地面。看来他已经等了一段时间。

En: After catching your attention, he narrows his eyes and extends his hand in greeting.
Zh: 在引起你的注意之后，他眯起眼睛，伸出手跟你打招呼。

En: Alright, come on now. If *he* hasn't said anything about your lack of pants, no one will. You're only hurting yourself by not wearing them...
Zh: 好了，现在出发吧。如果*他*没提你没穿裤子的事，那就没人会了。如果没穿裤子的话，唯一受伤的只有你自己……

En: Alright, come on now. If *he* hasn't said anything about your lack of pants, no one will. You're only hurting yourself by not wearing them...
Zh: 好了，现在出发吧。如果*他*没提你没穿裤子的事，那就没人会了。如果没穿裤子的话，唯一受伤的只有你自己……

En: It's barely noticeable, but he might be *a bit* annoyed at your rudeness. Then he lets it go -- more important matters need tending to.
Zh: 几乎很难察觉，不过他可能对你的无礼*有一点*恼火。不过他很快把这一点甩在脑后——毕竟还有更重要的事要关注。

En: This is your chance to come up with a really good name for yourself. Get *creative* -- conceptualize!
Zh: 你的机会来了，给自己认真想个好名字吧。要有*创意*——概念化一些！

En: On the sleeve of his bomber jacket -- as well as on its back -- are enigmatic white rectangles.
Zh: 在他飞行员夹克衫的袖子上——还有背后——都有神秘的白色矩形符号。

En: Okay then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: He nods.
Zh: 他点点头。

En: But first we have to take it down, he concludes.
Zh: 他总结道：“不过首先我们要把尸体放下来。”

En: I was to meet a detective from Precinct 41 at the Whirling-in-Rags who arrived here three days ago. I am told that that is how long you have been here. Besides, you match his description. He looks you over.
Zh: 他打量着你。“我来是为了见一个41分局的警官，他是3天前来到褴褛飞旋的。别人告诉我你在这里待了3天。再说，你也符合他的描述。”

En: What description?
Zh: “什么描述？”

En: Precinct 41 said they were sending their most seasoned detective. And you certainly look... seasoned.
Zh: “41分局说他们派出了经验最丰富的警探。你看起来确实……很老练。”

En: That's right! I'm a man for all seasons, baby!
Zh: “没错！我就是各方面都很老练的男人，宝贝！”

En: Oh.
What if I have an uncanny doppelgänger?
Zh: “如果我有个神秘的二重身呢？”

En: Sure. Though I would also suggest that you locate your blazer with your official insignia. He points to the white rectangle on his sleeve.
Zh: “当然。不过我会建议你把带有官方标志的夹克尽快找出来。”他指着他袖子上的白色矩形。

En: However, I would also suggest that you locate your blazer with your official insignia. He points to the white rectangle on his sleeve.
Zh: “不过，我会建议你把带有官方标志的夹克尽快找出来。”他指着他袖子上的白色矩形。

En: You may have murdered the man I was supposed to meet and are now impersonating him. You may be his twin, stepping in for his even more incapacitated brother out of loyalty and love.
Zh: “也许你杀了那个要跟我会面的男人，然后假扮成他的模样。你也可能是因为忠诚和兄弟之情，甘愿代替你那位更加软弱无能的双胞胎兄弟。”

En: But for now I am going to set those possibilities aside. I'm not from the Inspectorate General.
Zh: “不过现在我会把那些可能性都放在一边，毕竟我不是总监察组的人。”

En: Inspectorate General means internal affairs. What he's saying is he's not from the *rat squad* and isn't supposed to suspect such things.
Zh: 总监察组是专为负责内部事务的。他的意思是他不是*内奸小队*的人，所以不会怀疑这种事情。

En: Internal affairs. And I'm not them. I'm from Criminal Investigation.
Zh: “内务部，不过我不是其中一员。我属于刑事侦察部门。”

En: No need for derogatory terms. They are only doing their job.
Zh: “没必要使用这种贬义的称谓。他们只是做自己份内的事。”

En: Ah, you mean the rat squad!
Zh: “啊，你是说内奸小队！”

En: Uh... Inspectorate General?
Zh: “呃……总监察组？”

En: You mean internal affairs?
Zh: “你是说内务部？”

En: Yes. But I am not them. I'm from Criminal Investigation.
Zh: “没错，但我不是他们的人。我属于刑事侦察部门。”

En: Regardless, I would also suggest that you locate your blazer with your official insignia. He points to the white rectangle on his sleeve.
Zh: “不管怎么说，我会建议你把带有官方标志的夹克尽快找出来。”他指着他袖子上的白色矩形。

En: They're not *just* white rectangles. They bear a halogen watermark with the letters RCM and a pattern resembling the street grid of Revachol West. I could step into the headlights of my Kineema and show you, but right now I just need you to do your job.
Zh: “它们不*只是*白色矩形。上面印着卤素水印——RCM的字母，还有一副类似瑞瓦肖西部街道网的图案。我可以站到锐影的車灯前让你亲眼看看。但我现在只需要你做好自己的工作。”

En: But they're just white rectangles.
Zh: “但它们只是白色矩形啊。”

En: A bespectacled man in a black bomber jacket has folded up his newspaper and is now tapping his foot on the floor. Looks like he's been waiting for quite some time.
A bespectacled man in an orange bomber jacket has folded up his newspaper and is now tapping his foot on the floor. Looks like he's been waiting for quite some time.
You know I have to take this to Internal Affairs, don't you, lieutenant?
Let's move on. This has been enough of a detour.
No, there are couple of people who haven't smoked cannabis. I'm sure of that.
It's barely a secret. Everyone's smoked hemp.
It's dark, the lieutenant states the obvious.
Zh: “很黑，”警督陈述着显而易见的事实。

En: Yes?
Zh: “什么事？”

En: Tell me about the case.
Zh: “跟我说说案子吧。”

En: You seem to be following me.
Zh: “你好像在跟着我。”

En: Nothing. [Leave.]
Zh: “没什么。”[离开。]

En: Excuse me?
Zh: “什么？”

En: What if I want to work this case alone?
Zh: “如果我想一个人办案呢？”

En: Beat it. You're cramping my style.
Zh: “走开。你这样束缚了我的风格。”

En: Nothing. Just an observation.
Zh: “没什么。只是观察一下。”

En: Detective, if I may be frank -- you seem to be in a deranged state. You have trouble remembering things, you've *misplaced* your badge. I cannot let you act in the name of the RCM without supervision until you've regained control of your faculties.
Zh: “警探，恕我直言——你好像处于精神错乱的状态。你记不起东西。在你重新掌控自己的能力之前，我不能让你在无人监督的情况下打着RCM的旗号行事。”

En: Frankly, detective, you're in a deranged state. I can't let you proceed without *close* supervision. In fact, under normal circumstances I'd be duty-bound to report you. Take it as a token of good faith between our precincts that I haven't done so.
Zh: “老实说，警探，你正处于精神错乱的状态。我不能让你在没有*密切*监督的情况下行事。事实上，正常情况下，我有责任报告你的情况。但我没有这么做，就把这当成是我们分局之间诚意的象征吧。”

En: You have a... He's looking for the right words. "...a *distinctive* way of walking. If I were to walk *in front* of you, we would surely collide."
Zh: “你有……”他在寻找正确的字眼。“……*独特*的走路方式。如果我走在你*前面*，我们肯定会撞到的。”

En: What do you mean *distinctive*?
Zh: “你说*独特*是什么意思？”

En: Let it go.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: A moment passes. The lieutenant glances at the sports watch on his wrist.
Zh: 片刻过去了。警督看了看手腕上的运动手表。

En: I hope you don't take this the wrong way, it's just a collegial observation. In the 57th we call it the 'Jamrock shuffle'. Officers from Jamrock's 41st Precinct tend to move a bit erratically.
Zh: “希望你不要理解错了，这只是社群观察。我们57分局把这个叫做‘加姆洛克开箱步’。加姆洛克41分局的警官的行动方式有些不太规律。”

En: How's that?
Zh: “那是怎么回事？”

En: They say it's a scene-clearing technique developed by one of your lieutenants for gathering evidence. It's erratic, yet thorough. Prioritises containers.
Zh: “他们说这是你们的一位警督为收集证据开发的一种现场清理技术。它很古怪，但却十分彻底。总是把容器放在最高优先级。”

En: You're kidding me? Why containers?
Zh: “你在跟我开玩笑吗？为什么是容器？”

En: Okay then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: That's racist. I don't prioritise containers.
Zh: “那是种族歧视。我没有优先考虑容器。”

En: He simply nods.
Zh: 他简单地点点头。

En: I don't know. Containers... contain, I guess. I'm making assumptions. We should move on.
Zh: “我不知道。容器……能装东西吧，我猜。我只是在假设。我们还是继续吧。”

En: I'm sure you don't. It's just a stupid inter-departmental thing. I'm making assumptions... we should move on.
Zh: “我肯定你没有。这只是跨区执法愚蠢的那一套。我只是在假设……我们还是继续吧。”

En: Passing along frivolous inter-departmental stereotypes is not usually his *oeuvre*. He regrets bringing it up.
Zh: 传递跨区执法那一套无聊的刻板印象并不是他一贯的*作风*。他有些后悔提出来了。

En: Oh, so you're an unaccountable wreck who has to be supervised? You don't *have* to take this display of authority, you can disobey!
Zh: 哦，所以你是个让人无法理解的病人，需要别人监督？你*没必要*接受这种权威的表现，你可以不服从！

En: What's the RCM?
Zh: “RCM是什么？”

En: But what if I need some *me* time?
Zh: “不过如果我需要一些*自我*的时间呢？”

En: Detective, if I may be frank -- you seem to be in a deranged state. You have trouble remembering things. I cannot let you act in the name of the RCM without supervision until you've regained control of your faculties.
Zh: “警探，恕我直言——你好像处于精神错乱的状态。你记不起东西。在你重新掌控自己的能力之前，我不能让你在无人监督的情况下打着RCM的旗号行事。”

En: That would be us. The Revachol Citizens Militia.
Zh: “那就是我们了。瑞瓦肖公民武装。”

En: The RCM, or the Revachol Citizens Militia, is the police force you and him are part of. A self-organized peace corps of the occupied city of Revachol, the RCM operates within a legal twilight, yet its authority is rarely questioned. Wouldn't it be embarrassing if you didn't know this?
Zh: RCM，或者是瑞瓦肖公民武装，是你和他所属的一支警察力量。它是占领城市瑞瓦肖的一支和平部队，RCM在模糊的法律体系下运作，但它的权威性很少受到质疑。如果你不知道这事，会不会有点太尴尬了？

En: The lieutenant thinks you're some unaccountable wreck who needs constant supervision, but you don't *have* to listen to him. You're free to disobey.
Zh: 警督觉得你是个无法理解的病人，需要经常监督，但是你*没必要*听他的话。你完全可以不服从。”

En: I know what the RCM is.
Zh: “我知道RCM是什么。”

En: That's good.
Zh: “那很好啊。”

En: You don't need to *supervise* me, Kim.
Zh: “你不需要*监督*我的，金。”

En: Except that I *do*, detective.
Zh: “只可惜，我*需要*这么做，警探。”

En: Some *you* time? The lieutenant considers this a moment. "This is a police investigation, not a journey of self-discovery. You'll still have your evenings to yourself."
Zh: “一些*自我*时间？”警督考虑了一下。“这是一次警方调查，不是什么自我发现之旅。晚上的时间还是属于你自己的。”

En: On second thought, maybe I *need* someone to cramp my style every now and then...
Zh: “仔细一想，也许我偶尔确实*需要*有人来束缚一下我的风格……”

En: Sounds good.
Zh: “听起来不错。”

En: Okay, I'll leave the self-care for non-work hours.
Zh: “好吧，我还是非工作时间再照顾自己吧。”

En: Please do, he says. "We wouldn't want your *regimen* to spill over into the investigation."
Zh: “请务必这样，”他说到。“我们可不想你的*生活之道*渗透到调查中去。”

En: You might, he nods. "It's important not to let one's *style* interfere with their work."
Zh: “也许吧，”他点点头。“重要的是不要让个人*风格*干扰大家的工作。”

En: What do you want to know?
Zh: “你想知道什么？”

En: Would you say this is a *mysterious* case?
Zh: “你觉得这是一起*神秘*案件吗？”

En: A young woman told me a mercenary's been hanged -- that's all.
Zh: “一个年轻女性告诉我有个雇佣兵被吊死了——就这些。”

En: I know literally nothing about it. Only what you told me before.
Zh: “除了你之前告诉过我的那些，我可以说是对这个案子一无所知。”
