En: A young woman told me a mercenary has been hanged, and the manager thinks it was the Union that did it. That's all.
Zh: “一个年轻女性告诉我有个雇佣兵被吊死了，经理觉得是工会做的，就这些。”

En: Then you're not that far behind, actually. Do you want me to brief you?
Zh: “那你其实也没有落得太远。需要我给你简述一下吗？”

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不神秘呢？”

En: That's all I needed to know.
Zh: “我只需要知道这些就够了。”

En: The lieutenant adjusts his collar. It doesn't look like he *wants* the case to be more mysterious.
Zh: 警督整了整衣领。看起来他*不想*把案件搞得更神秘。

En: The deceased is a security guard for a corporation involved in a labour dispute. It doesn't take a Dolorian polymath to put the pieces together. I just don't see the case getting more *mysterious* than that.
Zh: 警督耸耸肩。“死者是一名安保人员，他所属的公司卷入了港口纠纷。不需要德洛莉丝时代的博学家也能把这些碎片拼接起来。我不觉得这起案件会有什么更*神秘*的地方。”

En: The guy behind the counter thinks some Union people hanged a man.
Zh: “柜台后面的那个家伙觉得工会的人吊死了一个男人。”

En: Do you want me to brief you?
Zh: “需要我给你简述一下吗？”

En: *Brief*, yes. That sounds good.
Zh: “*简述*，可以。听起来很棒。”

En: No. I'll wing it.
Zh: “不用。我还是即兴发挥吧。”

En: There's no reason to *wing* anything. If you didn't get the brief, that's okay, I did.
Zh: 没有必要*即兴发挥*。如果你没理解简述的意思，其实也没问题的。”

En: Three days ago, the RCM Emergencies Desk received a report about a security guard who was found hanged in Martinaise. An anonymous caller said there was a dead body behind the Whirling-in-Rags hostel-cafeteria. The cadaver had been there for four days -- no one had come to investigate...
Zh: “三天前，紧急服务台接到一起报告，说马丁内斯有一名安保人员被吊死了。有人匿名举报，说褴褛飞旋的餐厅背后有一具死尸。尸体已经在那里挂了四天——没有人前去调查……”

En: During that time, the victim had been stripped of his belongings. The caller did not identify him, but used the word *lynching*. There is an ongoing labour dispute between the local dockworkers and the logistics company Wild Pines. I was told we should approach the death as part of this dispute.
Zh: “在那期间，被害人的随身物品被剥光。呼叫者不知道死者的身份，但用了*私刑*这个词。目前，本地的码头工人和野松物流公司之间因为港口问题产生了纠纷。我听说这起死亡案件也是这场纠纷的一部分。”

En: Who was the caller?
Zh: “谁报的警？”

En: To find him or her is one of our tasks here. For now all we know is that the tone was muffled using a device of some sort -- the desk could identify neither the caller's age nor sex.
Zh: “我们的任务就是把他，或者她找出来。现在我们只知道声音好像被某种设备压低了——服务台无法辨别出对方的年龄和性别。”

En: Why hide themselves?
Zh: “这个人为什么要隐藏自己的身份呢？”

En: There's a strong prejudice against involving the RCM in what's seen as *Union matters*. The Dockworkers' Union is the de facto police in Martinaise. Now -- it appears -- they've started executing too. We cannot allow that.
Zh: “本地有一种很强烈的偏见，认为RCM不应该插手*工会事务*。码头工人工会是马丁内斯实际意义上的警察。现在好像他们也执起法来了，这一点我们是不能允许的。”

En: Mega cool. I've heard enough.
Zh: “太酷了。我听够了。”

En: If we're from different precincts, why are we on the same case?
Zh: “既然我们来自不同分局，为什么会来调查同一起案件呢？”

En: Okay then. Was there anything else you wanted to know?
Zh: “那好吧。你还有什么想知道的？”

En: Actually, I have all I need for now.
Zh: “其实吧，我现在只要知道这些就够了。”

En: Literally *anything* about it -- I can't remember a single thing.
Zh: *任何*与这个案子相关的事情可以——我什么都记不起来了。”

En: Maybe you can tell me what you *do* know? To help me narrow it down a bit.
Zh: “也许你可以把你*知道*的告诉我？帮我稍微把范围缩小一些。”

En: Was there anything else you wanted to know about the case?
Zh: “关于这起案件，你还有什么想知道的吗？”

En: White male in his forties -- what more do you need?
Zh: “40多岁的白人男性——这还不够吗？”

En: I was thinking something other-worldly might be involved. You know, something *supra-natural*.
Zh: “我在想这里面可能涉及到了一些超神脱俗的东西。你知道的，某种*超自然*的东西。”

En: Personally, I think labour disputes are *very* mysterious.
Zh: “就我个人而言，我觉得港口纠纷就*非常*神秘。”

En: Then you're in luck, because we're in the midst of a *major* strike by the Dockworkers' Union. Maybe more than that. The Union clearly wants a *piece*, not just of the Industrial Harbour, but the Wild Pines corporation itself...
Zh: “那你走运了，因为码头工人工会正在进行*大罢工*。也许还远不止这样。工会显然想要*分一杯羹*，不仅仅是工业港口，还有野松公司本身……”

En: Okay, so the case *probably* isn't mysterious -- but could it be *sexy*?
Zh: “好吧，所以这起案件*可能*并不神秘——不过会不会有些*性感*呢？”

En: I expect the case itself to be less challenging than navigating these *community matters*. This district isn't used to the RCM's presence, and the Union rarely overplays its hand -- as it appears to have done in this case...
Zh: “我认为跟处理这些*社区事务*相比，这起案件本身的挑战性要更小一些。这片区域不习惯RCM的存在，而工会很少会做出过激的行为——不过这起案件似乎就是这样……”

En: Is this a *strike* then -- or the first shots in a *workers' coup*?
Zh: 那这是一次*罢工*——还是打响*工人政变*的第一枪？

En: Wait, so is this a *strike* or an attempted *coup*?
Zh: “等等，所以这是一次*罢工*，还是一次*政变*企图？”

En: Strike, coup, revolution, it's brigandage however you parse it.
Zh: “罢工，政变，革命，不管怎么理解，都是一种强盗行为。”

En: We should ground the workers into fine paste and feed them to the pigs.
Zh: “我们应该把工人碾成肉酱然后拿去喂猪。”

En: When I said we have to navigate community matters, I did not mean we have any *say* in them. I meant we should be careful. If we are not... the *shit* will blow right in our faces. He makes a quick gesture towards his visage -- where the shit would blow.
Zh: “我刚才说我们必须处理社区事务的时候，并不是说我们对此有*发言权*，而是要多加小心。如果不小心……那些*狗屎*可能就直接砸到我们脸上了。”他朝着自己的脸上比了个手势——就在狗屎可能会击中的位置。

En: I'm afraid you and I are pawns in a... He considers the phrasing. "A *pissing* competition."
Zh: “恐怕我和你不过是……”他考虑了一下措辞。“这场*尿尿*比赛中的无名小卒。”

En: I feel like I must repeat this -- I don't remember *anything.* There was heavy drinking involved.
Zh: “我觉得我应该重复一遍——我真的*什么*都不记得。可能是因为我喝得太多了吧。”

En: I really don't remember anything. There was drinking involved.
Zh: “我真的什么都记不起来了，酒的问题。”

En: Have you tried concentrating on something other than your personal affairs?
Zh: “你有没有试过把注意力集中到个人事务之外的事情上？”

En: There is a sudden, harsh edge to his voice. Like he's tired of hearing about your "personal affairs."
Zh: 他的声音突然变得尖锐刺耳。就好像他已经厌倦了你的那些“个人事务”。

En: So what *should* I concentrate on?
Zh: “那我*应该*集中在什么事情上面呢？”

En: I'm afraid this is a medical situation. (Move on.)
Zh: “恐怕这是个医疗问题。”（继续。）

En: Fine. (Let it be.)
Zh: “好吧。”（顺其自然。）

En: Try work. The case at hand. It can work miracles.
Zh: “试试工作。处理手头上的案件。这样能创造奇迹。”

En: I'm talking deep, neurological damage here. I saw myself in the mirror and had no idea who I was.
Zh: “我说的可是严重的精神损伤。我看见了镜子里的自己，但是完全不知道自己是谁。”

En: I'm talking deep, neurological damage here.
Zh: “我说的可是严重的精神损伤。”

En: I'm talking serious, unbelievable damage here. I saw myself in the mirror and had no idea who I was.
Zh: “我说的可是难以置信的严重损伤。我看见了镜子里的自己，但是完全不知道自己是谁。”

En: I'm talking serious, unbelievable damage here.
Zh: “我说的可是难以置信的严重损伤。”

En: Really? The lieutenant gives you a look, thorough as if performing triage. "You look fine to me."
Zh: “真的吗？”警督露出一个表情，感觉像是在给你验伤。“在我看来你挺好的。”

En: This psycho-drama is unbecoming of an officer.
Zh: “身为警官就别搞这种心理问题的戏码了。”

En: Okay, then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: Focus on *other* people's troubles. Not your own. That is the relief.
Zh: “把注意力集中在*其他人*的问题上，而不是你自己的。这样能宽慰很多。”

En: It's not psychological. Some sort of major brain damage has occurred. On an unprecedented scale.
Zh: “这跟心理问题无关。这是某种严重的脑损伤，规模大到是史无前例。”

En: Clearly he prefers to think you're malingering. He cannot fathom that anyone could drink so much as to retroactively erase their entire life.
Zh: 显然他更愿意相信你是在装病。他无法理解有人能喝到把之前的整个人生都抹掉了。

En: Then you should consider seeking medical attention. You can use the radio in my Kineema to call your station's *lazareth*. Was there anything else you wanted?
Zh: “那你应该考虑寻求医疗救治。你可以用我锐影里的无线电打给你警局的*检疫所*。你还有什么需要的吗？”

En: Thank you, oh wise man.
Zh: “谢谢你，智者。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Your heart beats twice, like a fist. The serotonin deficiency makes your teeth clench.
Zh: 你的心脏跳了两次，像是握紧了拳头。由于血清素不足，你咬紧了牙关。

En: The lieutenant blinks, his mouth slightly open and his body motionless. A microsecond passes.
Zh: 警督眨眨眼，嘴唇微张，身体一动不动。百万分之一秒过去了。

En: Excuse me. I was lecturing you. I shouldn't have. You should consult a medical professional if you feel that you need help. You can use the radio in my Kineema to call your station's *lazareth*.
Zh: “抱歉。我刚才在跟你讲大道理。我不应该这么做。如果你需要帮助的话，可以找医务人员咨询一下。你可以用我锐影里的无线电呼叫你们分局的*检疫所*。”

En: This habit of dispensing wisdom has been brought to his attention before. He will not think less of you for lashing out -- he is trying to curb it.
Zh: 这种高谈阔论的习惯已经引起了他的注意。他不会因为你的猛烈抨击而看轻你——他自己也在试图控制这一点。

En: What's wrong with personal affairs?
Zh: “个人事务怎么了？”

En: Not a fan. It's just the nature of lieutenancy.
Zh: “我不太喜欢。警督这个职业的天性如此。”

En: The RCM deploys a self-styled structure called the Décomptage as its chain of command. Every lieutenant is responsible for two sergeants and eight officers. That means the average lieutenant has to deal with quite a few personal affairs. Even you know this.
Zh: RCM部署了一种自称为*倒装*的结构作为其指挥系统。每一个警督负责2名警长以及8名警官。这就意味着每个警督都会面对很多个人事务。就连你都知道这一点。

En: But I'm completely lacking in basic information about even this organization we're in. Can you help me?
Zh: “但是我对这个组织的基本信息都缺乏了解。你能帮我吗？”

En: Fine. We should get through the day first. Off-hours begin at 21:00. If you're still having trouble then, I can give you an orientation.
Zh: “好吧。我们应该先处理完今天的事情。晚上21点开始休息，如果那时候你还有问题，我可以给你介绍一下情况。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: His disdain is clear. This man would not use such an expression otherwise.
Zh: 他的不屑是显而易见的。否则这个男人不会使用这种表达方式。

En: You don't know? His eyes narrow. "I assumed you were in on it."
Zh: “你不知道？”他的眼睛眯成了一条缝。“我还以为你也参与了呢。”

En: Tell me about it.
Zh: “跟我说说吧。”

En: You know what I'm in on -- retrograde amnesia.
Zh: “你知道我现在的状况吗？我得了逆行性失忆症。”

En: Better still than an imbecilic *Cop Off*.
Zh: “那也比弱智的*警察对决*要强吧。”

En: It's just stupidity. We shouldn't waste any more time on it. If you want my take, ask me after we've inspected the victim.
Zh: “实在是太蠢了。我们不应该再在这上面浪费时间了。如果你想听我的意见，等调查完被害人之后再说吧。”

En: I don't remember being in on anything.
Zh: “我没印象有参与过什么。”

En: I think the dispatch desk just told both our stations about the hanging. There was quite the brouhaha at the 57th, I can tell you that. 'Time to *settle it*', they said, '*Cop Off*'. But... He leans in: "I assure you, I am not their *finest* or *toughest*, with *one hundred-and-two cases solved*. What I am is *least interested in a pissing competition*."
Zh: “我想调度台只跟我们的警局提了上吊的事。我可以告诉你，57区发生了不小的骚动。他们说，是时候*解决*这个问题了，*警察对决*。但是……”他靠近了一些：“我可以向你保证，我不是他们之中*最棒*或者*最强悍*的，也没有破获*102起案件*。我是对*尿尿竞赛最不感兴趣*的人。”

En: That's good.
Zh: “那很好啊。”

En: The *cop* kind. Our Precincts can't decide if Martinaise is part of Jamrock or the Industrial Harbour. *Yours* or *mine*, as if we somehow *own* parts of the city. Typical street-gang mentality.... so we've let the Union make a mockery of law enforcement here. And now it's come to its natural conclusion.
Zh: “*警察*那种蠢事。我们分局无法决定马丁内斯是属于加姆洛克还是工业港口的一部分。什么*你的*还是*我的*，好像我们真的*拥有*这部分城市一样。典型的街头帮派心态……我们在这里的执法也沦为了工会的笑柄。所以现在就得出了这个很自然的结论。”

En: What kind of stupidity?
Zh: “什么样的蠢事？”

En: Let it go.
Zh: 别管了。

En: Cop Off?
Zh: “警察对决？”

En: What's there to say? It's just stupidity.
Zh: “有什么好说的？只是些蠢事罢了。”

En: Now that we've inspected the scene -- I want to know more about this 'pissing competition' you mentioned.
Zh: “既然我们已经调查了现场——我想知道你提过的这个‘尿尿竞赛’。”

En: Good. The lieutenant nods.
Zh: “很好。”警督点点头。

En: It isn't. But I will prove it so.
Zh: “不是的。但我会证明的。”

En: I hope you do.
Zh: “我希望你可以。”

En: And he will help.
Zh: 他会帮忙的。

En: Kim, what if my precinct sent me on this case *because* I'm a fuck up? Like... as a joke.
Zh: “金，如果分局派我来处理这个案子，是*因为*我只会惹麻烦呢？比如说……他们想看我笑话。”

En: Keep it to yourself.
Zh: 自己保密吧。

En: I've considered it. His voice is sombre.
Zh: “我考虑过。”他的声音有些阴沉。

En: Of course, it's only a *theory*.
Zh: 当然了，这只是一种*理论*。

En: So it's true.
Zh: “所以是真的。”

En: But it's not true, right?
Zh: “但是这不是真的，对吗？”

En: Look at you -- it's because you're a failure. They sent you to *slight* Precinct 57.
Zh: 看看你——因为你是一个失败者。他们派你来是对57局的一种*蔑视*。

En: There was some interest in this case at my station, but not for the reasons you have in mind. You seem to wish there was...
Zh: “我的分局对这起案件有些兴趣，不过并不是你脑子里想的原因。你好像希望有……”

En: As you know, there was considerable interest in this case at my station, but probably not for the reasons you have in mind...
Zh: “你也知道，我的分局对这起案件有相当大的兴趣，不过并不是你脑子里想的原因……”

En: Are you *sure* there's not some sex-angle we should be considering?
Zh: “你*确定*我们不应该从性谋杀的角度考虑吗？”

En: Maybe there's a young woman involved?!
Zh: “也许有个年轻女性牵涉其中呢？！”

En: Yes, it just so happens there's a beautiful, blonde nineteen year old woman at the heart of this case. A rich one, in fact. Part of a murder-sex-cult.
Zh: “是的，碰巧有个19岁的漂亮金发女孩是这起案件的核心人物。事实上，还挺有钱的。某个杀人性爱教团的一分子。”

En: It doesn't *have* to be a cult. It can just be sex-murder.
Zh: “不*一定*是邪教吧。可能只是性谋杀。”

En: Wow, really? That's *mega* sexy!
Zh: “哇哦，真的吗？那不就*特别*性感吗？”

En: The weary tone is the surest indicator that the lieutenant is being *sarcastic*.
Zh: 警督疲惫的口吻就是*挖苦你*最明确的标志。

En: No, not *really*. Was there anything else?
Zh: “不，*不见得*。还有别的事吗？”

En: That's commendable. Was there anything else?
Zh: “这一点值得赞扬。还有别的事吗？”

En: Convince Kim there's a sexy dark mystery twist in the case.
Zh: 说服金这起案件里隐藏着一个性感又黑暗的神秘转折。

En: It was a murder.
Zh: 这是一起凶杀案。

En: Dresscode: Modus Mullen.
Zh: 着装要求：模式马伦。

En: Only banal things strike you. At the core, you're a very banal person, with a very small soul.
Zh: 你只能想到些陈腐老旧的东西。你骨子里就是个很陈腐的人，拥有一个非常渺小的灵魂。

En: What if *you* did it?
Zh: 如果是*你*做的呢？

En: Did... what? The hanged man?
Zh: 做……什么？那个吊人吗？

En: Did what? I can't even remember what the case is. What was it...
Zh: 做什么？我都想不起来是案子是怎么回事。是什么来着……

En: It is murder, of course. Murder. It can't be anything else. And *you* did it. And then, horrified by your own actions, you drowned out the memory...
Zh: 当然了，*这*是一起凶杀案，不可能是别的。而且是*你*做的。然后，被自己的行为吓坏的你，埋葬了所有记忆……

En: I can assure nothing like that will happen. Do you know why not?
Zh: “我可以保证不会发生这种事。你知道为什么吗？”

En: Are you sure? I think something is already happening.
Zh: “你确定？我感觉有些事情已经发生了。”

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不呢？”

En: Because it's impossible, I know that. Still...
Zh: “因为不可能，我知道这一点。不过……”

En: Because it's not part of... He makes a precise hand gesture: "Reality."
Zh: “因为这不是……”他做了一个精准的手势：“现实的一部分。”

En: No, something isn't happening. Because it's not part of... He makes a precise hand gesture: "Reality."
Zh: “不，什么也没发生。因为这不是……”他做了一个精准的手势：“现实的一部分。”

En: He's about to say it's impossible.
Zh: 他打算说这是不可能的。

En: The gesture resembles an orchestra conductor bringing the choir to a full stop.
Zh: 这个手势很像是一个乐队指挥让合唱团完全停止。

En: Good. Yes. Because it's not part of Reality.
Zh: “很好。没错，因为这不是现实的一部分。”

En: Do you know the feeling when you've begun filling in a crossword but some of your answers don't quite seem to fit? That's the feeling I have right now, for some reason...
Zh: “你知道那种感觉吗——当你开始填写一个猜字游戏，但是有些答案好像不太合适？不知道为什么，我现在就有这种感觉……”

En: Wait, does the briefing say who the victim was?
Zh: “等等，那简报里有说被害人是谁吗？”

En: A security guard or worker of some sort, hired by Wild Pines. This was just hearsay from Martinaise, of course. We need to find out the truth.
Zh: “一个安保人员或者工人之类的，是野松公司的雇员。当然了，这不过是马丁内斯的传闻。我们需要查清真相。”

En: That's all. I just wanted you to know I have this thing. (Keep pointing to your head.)
Zh: “就这些。我只想让你知道我有这玩意。”（继续指着你的脑袋。）

En: Okay, I admit to it. The Revolution employed a more forward-looking eight-rotor design. Their blades were foldable and their pilots were better trained and motivated.
Zh: “好吧，我承认。革命者采用了更有远见的8转子设计。刀片可以折叠，而且飞行员接受的训练更好，积极性也更高。”

En: Kim, you're wearing a revolutionary air brigade jacket, aren't you?
Zh: “金，你穿的是革命者的航空旅夹克，对吗？”

En: What, this? he zips up the large collar. "It's just seasonal clothing."
Zh: “什么，这个吗？”他拉上大衣领。“这只是季节性的服装。”

En: And those look like airman pants. Good for storing tools in.
Zh: “那些看起来像是飞行员裤。很适合存放工具。”

En: He looks at his pants. "Where is this going?"
Zh: 他看着自己的裤子。“这是要干什么？”

En: You see, Kim. I have this place in my head. (Point to your head.) "Where I develop new ideas and connections."
Zh: “你看，金。我脑子里有一块地方。”（指着你的脑袋。）“在那里我可以在新想法之间建立联系。”

En: Interesting. I think it's called a brain.
Zh: “很有意思。我觉得它的名字叫大脑。”

En: It's no mere brain.
Zh: 不仅仅是大脑。

En: I do not harbour a sentiment for revolutionary air brigades in particular.
Zh: “我对革命者航空旅没什么特别的感情。”

En: In this special place I have come to suspect that you have some kind of *thing* with the revolutionary air brigades.
Zh: “在这个特别的地方，我怀疑你和革命者航空旅之间有着某种特别的*东西*。”

En: And now I do, he nods.
Zh: “现在我知道了，”他点点头。

En: The *revolutionary* must have added a little lustre to it, for a ten-year-old Kitsuragi. He will never admit it though.
Zh: 对于十岁的曷城警督来说，*革命者*肯定为它增添了一些光彩。不过他永远都不会承认的。

En: It is not from a *purely* tactical standpoint. We are not ruling out that the lieutenant had a little... rebel phase in that past of his.
Zh: 这不是*纯粹*的策略立场。我们不能排除警督的过去有一点点……叛逆的阶段。

En: Yes. A brain.
Zh: “是的。大脑。”

En: No, it's more than that. Anyway.
Zh: “不，并不仅仅是这样。不过无所谓了。”

En: It's okay to tell me, I'm a major fan of Mazovian socio-economics myself.
Zh: “你可以告诉我的，我本人是马佐夫社会经济学的忠实粉丝。”

En: Absolutely nothing?
Zh: “绝对没有？”

En: Are you sure the *revolutionary* has got nothing to do with this?
Zh: “你确定*革命家*跟这个一点关系都没有吗？”

En: Anyway.
Zh: “无所谓吧。”

En: Okay. And something happened in that brain?
Zh: “好吧。所以脑子里发生了什么事？”

En: Good. He glances impatiently at his electronic wristwatch.
Zh: “很好。”他不耐烦地瞥了一眼自己的电子腕表。

En: Just air brigades then?
Zh: “那就是航空旅了？”

En: Okay indeed. And in this *atelier* I have realized that you harbour some kind of *sentiment* toward the revolutionary air brigades.
Zh: “确实可以。而且在这个*画室*，我意识到你对革命者航空旅有着某种*情感*。”

En: Yes. My brain suspects that you have some kind of *thing* with the revolutionary air brigades.
Zh: “是的。我的大脑怀疑你和革命者航空旅之间有某种特别的*东西*。”

En: Absolutely nothing.
Zh: “绝对没有。”

En: He corrects his glasses. "I say this from a purely tactical standpoint of course."
Zh: 他调整了一下眼镜。“当然，我这么说纯粹是出于策略的立场。”

En: I've noticed -- and okay. The Revolution employed a more forward-looking eight-rotor design. Their blades were foldable and their pilots were better trained and motivated.
Zh: “我注意到了——其实也没什么。革命者采用了更有远见的8转子设计。刀片可以折叠，而且飞行员接受的训练更好，积极性也更高。”

En: I do not have a *thing* with revolutionary air brigades in particular.
Zh: “我跟革命者航空旅之间没什么特别的*东西*。”

En: Okay. I wanted to become an aerostatic pilot. Then I turned ten and realized we no longer have an air force.
Zh: “好吧。我想成为一个航空飞行员。等到我十岁的时候，才意识到我们已经没有空军了。”

En: No, normal people don't have this. It's like a mind laboratory. A spiritual R&D division. No, a pallet! No, better yet -- a painter's atelier of concepts!
Zh: “不，普通人没有这个。它就像是一个心灵实验室。一个精神上的研究开发部门。不，它是一个调色板！不，甚至更好——它是画家的一个概念画室！”

En: Thank you. My mind is satisfied. (Point to your head again.)
Zh: “谢谢你。我的心灵得到了满足。”（再次指向你的大脑。）

En: Nowhere. Let's change the subject -- but still keep it on *you*.
Zh: “没什么。我们换个话题吧——不过，还是围绕*你*来聊。。”

En: Okay, Art Cop.
Zh: “好吧，艺术警探。”

En: The *revolutionary* must have added a little lustre to it, for a ten-year-old Kitsuragi. He will never admit it though.
Zh: 对于十岁的曷城警督来说，*革命者*肯定为它增添了一些光彩。不过他永远都不会承认的。

En: Let's not.
Zh: “还是不要了。”

En: Ah, yes. The case brief you missed. Now I remember. He opens his notes.
Zh: “啊，是的。你错过了案件简述。现在我想起来了。”他翻开笔记。

En: You feel something... rising through you -- a familiar feeling. AN OPINION taking form...
Zh: 你感觉到某种东西……从你身上升腾而起——那是一种熟悉的感觉。一个想法慢慢成型……

En: From an imaginary fan.
Zh: 来自一个虚构的粉丝。

En: Okay -- let's *talk* about it. You stole the boots. He looks at the gleaming technological footwear you're sporting.
Zh: “好吧——我们*聊聊*。你偷走了靴子。”他看着你故意露出来的那双闪闪发亮的工艺鞋。

En: Congratulations. That must have taken an enormous, concerted effort. *Considerable* ingenuity. And timing. Now -- I'm going to report you and you're going to go to jail.
Zh: “恭喜。这一定需要付出巨大的、协调一致的努力。*相当的大*独创性。还有时间。现在——我会向上面报告，你准备坐牢吧。”

En: LIE! LIE TO GET OUT OF IT!
Zh: 撒谎！快用谎言让自己脱身！

En: You are?
Zh: “你会吗？”

En: Please don't, I was just...
Zh: “请别这样，我只是……”

En: These are not the same boots. I liked them so much I went and bought myself a pair.
Zh: “这不是同一双靴子。我实在是太喜欢，所以自己也买了一双。”

En: You're joking.
Zh: “你在开玩笑。”

En: Am I? He arches his brow. "Anyway, did you want something -- related to policework?"
Zh: “我有吗？”他弯起眉毛。“总之，你需要什么——跟警察工作有关的东西吗？”

En: I don't care about your insane theft, the lieutenant thinks and looks away from the boots. Just know: if anyone asks me, I *will* snitch on you.
Zh: 我不在乎你疯狂的盗窃癖，警督一边想着，一边从靴子上移开了目光。只要记住一点：如果有人问我，我就*会*告发你。

En: It was a joke, sadly. There's nothing I can do about your delinquency. He sighs. "What's done is done. Let's get back to policework."
Zh: “可惜，这是个笑话。对于你的失职，我没有什么能做的。”他叹了口气。“反正木已成舟。我们还是继续回去工作吧。”

En: Sure. He nods. "Anyway, did you want something -- related to policework?"
Zh: “当然。”他点点头。“总之，你有什么需要——跟警察工作有关的东西吗？”

En: He's having trouble processing it. *Believing* it even.
Zh: 他消化起来都有些困难，就更不用说*相信*了。

En: I found out what the *pale* is while you were gone.
Zh: “你不在的时候，我明白*灰域*是什么了。”

En: Wonderful. He does not seem surprised. "What is your takeaway?"
Zh: “太棒了。”他看起来并不吃惊。“你的要点是什么？”

En: Wait, you're not surprised?
Zh: “等等，你不吃惊吗？”

En: I think it's terrifying.
Zh: “我觉得它很可怕。”

En: It seems unreal.
Zh: “它似乎不太真实。”

En: Where *are* we, Lieutenant Kitsuragi?
Zh: “我们这是*在哪*，曷城警督？”

En: I can't stop a grown man from learning about the fundamental geographic and entroponetic features of our world, can I?
Zh: “我不能阻止一个成年人去了解这个世界基础的地理和灰域的特征吧，不是吗？”

En: I'm super cool with all of it.
Zh: “我对这一切都超级镇静的。”

En: Perhaps I shouldn't have tried, he thinks.
Zh: 也许我不应该尝试的，他想到。

En: Perhaps I was overprotective, is what's insinuated.
Zh: 这里暗示着：也许我有些过度保护了。

En: In Elysium, he replies. "Behind our eyes. Like all human beings, detective." He looks around and sighs. "The world is what it is. I'm glad to see you're stable. Keep it that way."
Zh: “在极乐世界，”他回复到。“在我们的眼睛背后。就像所有人类一样，警探。”他看了看周围，叹了口气。“这个世界就是这个样子。很高兴能看见你精神稳定的样子。保持这个样子。”

En: Now -- was there anything else or should we get to it?
Zh: “现在——还有其他事吗，还是我们就直接开始？”

En: I'm glad to hear that. I'm glad to see you're stable. He nods approvingly. "Keep it that way. Now -- was there anything else, or should we get to it?"
Zh: “很高兴能听你这么说。很高兴见到你精神稳定的样子。”他点头赞同。“保持这个样子。现在——还有其他事吗，还是我们就直接开始？”

En: It *all* seems unreal, detective. In actuality, the pale is no more unreal than, say, water. Or death. Or that we're stuck behind our eyes, between our own ears, talking... He looks around, pensive suddenly.
Zh: “*全都*看起来不太真实，警探。事实上，怎么说呢，灰域并不比水更不真实，或者是死亡。或者我们其实被困在了双眼之后，自己的双耳之间，说话……”他看了看周围，突然陷入沉思。

En: Excuse me. Large topics are not my forte. You seem stable enough. Keep it that way. Now -- was there anything else or should we get to it?
Zh: “请原谅。泛泛的主题不是我的强项。你的精神已经很稳定了。保持这个样子吧。现在——还有其他事吗，还是我们就直接开始？”

En: Then I was right to spare you from it, no? He cracks a little smile. "Anyway, the pale is no more terrifying than, say, water. Or death. Or that we're stuck behind our eyes, for all eternity..." He looks around, pensive suddenly.
Zh: “那我让你从中解脱出来不就是对的吗？”他露出一丝微笑。“不管怎么说，灰域呢，其实并不比水可怕。或者是死亡。或者我们其实被困在了双眼之后，永生永世……”他看了看周围，突然陷入沉思。

En: You don't look like other people around here.
Zh: “你看起来跟其他人不太一样。”

En: That's because I'm half-Seolite. Or quarter. My father's father was from Seol -- so was my grandmother, but from my mother's side... He shakes his head. "It's not an interesting topic."
Zh: “因为我有一半的西奥人血统。或者是四分之一。我的祖父来自西奥——我的祖母也是，但是我母亲那边……”他摇摇头。“这不是个有意思的话题。”

En: What is Seol?
Zh: “西奥是什么？”

En: Okay then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: Mhm. I don't speak a word of Seolite and I've never met either one of my grandparents. There is nothing *ethnic* I can tell you. Or anyone else.
Zh: “嗯。我完全不会说西奥语，也从没见过自己的祖父母。我没办法告诉你任何*种族*的东西，或者别人也一样。”

En: It's a part of the world, officer. A geopolitical entity -- *and* a geographic division. I told you it wouldn't be interesting.
Zh: “是这个世界的一部分，警官。一个地缘政治实体——*而且*也是一个地理区划。我告诉过你，这没什么有意思的。”

En: You're only making it *sound* uninteresting. I still want to know more about Seol.
Zh: “你只是让它*听起来*索然无味。我还想多了解一下西奥。”

En: Okay, I guess it's not interesting then.
Zh: “好吧，可能是没什么意思吧。”

En: I can see you're disappointed. A brief smile. "You're right to be -- I don't speak a word of Seolite. I've never met either one of my grandparents. I've never been to Seol. I'm a regular, garden variety Revacholiere."
Zh: “我能看出你很失望。”他笑了一下。“你是对的——我完全不会说西奥语，从没见过自己的祖父母，也从来没去过西奥。我只是一个普通又平凡的瑞瓦肖人。”

En: You're barking up the wrong tree. I don't speak a word of Seolite, I've never met either one of my grandparents. And I've never *been* to Seol. He seems almost proud of these things. "I'm a regular Revacholiere."
Zh: “你找错目标了。我完全不会说西奥语，从没见过自己的祖父母，也从来没*去过*西奥。”他几乎为这些事感到有些骄傲。“我只是一个普通又平凡的瑞瓦肖人。”

En: He's glad to have shot down your question.
Zh: 他很高兴能拒绝你的问题。

En: Seol is a protectionist, isolationist panisiolary state west of the Insulindian isola. Actually, it's *quite* interesting; some would even say mysterious...
Zh: 西奥一个施行贸易保护主义、孤立主义的国家，位于伊苏林迪洲西部。实际上，它是个*非常*有趣的地方；有些地方甚至可以说是神秘……

En: A point of pride to him.
Zh: 这是他的骄傲。

En: I was sent to teach you a lesson in *style*.
Zh: “我被派来是为了给你上一堂*风格*课的。”

En: Can we go over the preliminary info again?
Zh: “我们能不能再复习一下初步信息？”

En: You mean like a brief? Do you want me to brief you again?
Zh: “你是说简述吗？你希望我再给你简述一下吗？”

En: It's all part of the Masterplan, you see.
Zh: 这都是*伟大计划*的一部分，懂我意思吧。

En: Tell him. Tell him why it's you.
Zh: 告诉他。告诉他为什么会是你。

En: I for one hope I don't have to use my service weapon at all -- like a *normal* police officer.
Zh: “我希望最好完全不要用到我的配枪——就像个*普通*的警官一样。”

En: Of course. Sounds like you just answered his question.
Zh: “当然。”听起来你刚刚回答了他的问题。

En: Yes, it's a wholly pointless matter. Forget I ever mentioned it.
Zh: “是的，这完全是一件毫无意义的事。忘了我提过吧。”

En: He's actually glad it's addressed now.
Zh: 他其实很高兴现在这事解决了。

En: You *look* dumb if you keep the lieutenant waiting for too long.
Zh: 如果让警督等得太久，你*看起来*就会显得很愚蠢。

En: So no, I don't think they sent you as a joke. And even if they did, they are in for a surprise.
Zh: “所以，不是的，我不觉得他们把你派来是为了看笑话。就算是这个意思，他们肯定会很吃惊的。”

En: He's right. There are no airtight theories, just paranoia. You've given it some thought, now let it go.
Zh: 他说得对。根本没有什么无懈可击的理论，只有偏执和妄想。你已经考虑过了，现在放手吧。

En: Safe? No. But you're old. You've made it this far. *Something* has brought you through. We've only just started working together, so I don't know what it is yet. But it's there.
Zh: “稳赢？不。但是你年纪不小了。还走到了今天这一步。*有些东西*支撑着你度过了难关。我们才刚刚开始合作。我不知道那是什么东西。但是它是存在的。”

En: I checked the records. This jurisdiction dispute -- who polices Martinaise -- reaches back to the Thirties. It's as old as my station. And all this time we can't decide who gets Martinaise? I think, yes, both stations would prefer a win.
Zh: “我检查过记录了。这一起司法纠纷——谁来管辖马丁内斯——可以追溯到30年代。年岁跟我们分局一样大。一直以来，我们都没办法决定马丁内斯该谁管？我想，是的，两个分局都想赢。”

En: Do you really see me as a safe bet?
Zh: “你真觉得会稳赢我吗？”

En: Ha, so you *are* their finest.
Zh: “哈，所以你*是*他们中间最棒的。”

En: Safe? No. But you're old. You've made it this far. Something has brought you through. I haven't worked with you yet, so I don't know what it is. But it's there.
Zh: “稳赢？不。但是你年纪不小了。而且已经走到了今天这一步。有些事陪着你度过了难关。我们才刚刚开始合作，所以我还不知道那是什么。但是它就在那里。”

En: It would be immensely ugly of them, not to mention unprofessional.... But I also think it's somewhat unlikely.
Zh: “那样会变得极其难看，更不用提不专业了……不过我也觉得可能性不太大。”

En: Why's that?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: I am the finest of nothing.
Zh: “我并没有什么地方特别棒的。”

En: I don't think I can say one way or another. I do think it's somewhat unlikely, though.
Zh: “我不觉得自己能这样那样的说。不过我确实觉得可能性不大。”

En: That's where his conversations with himself take place.
Zh: 这就是他和自己对话的地方。

En: We all have our different mediums. His is written.
Zh: 我们全都有不同的手段。他是用写的。

En: You're super lucid -- yet psychedelic. You don't need office supplies to connect to your nervous system, you're *special*.
Zh: 你超级清醒——但又感到迷幻。你不需要办公用品来连接你的神经系统，你是*特别*的。

En: (I'm not gonna call him a bino.) "Glasses are cool."
Zh: （我不会叫他四眼的。）“眼镜挺酷的。”

En: Are they? They're mostly just cumbersome.
Zh: “是吗？不过是个拖累罢了。”

En: You could use a good, *normal* pair yourself.
Zh: 你自己也可以弄副好一点的，*普通*一点的。

En: (No, I don't.) "Glasses are cool, I guess."
Zh: （不，我不觉得）“我觉得眼镜挺酷的。”

En: The eyebrow is exercising *psionic* control over you.
Zh: 眉毛正在对你进行*精神*控制。

En: It's like you're *locked down*.
Zh: 感觉就像是你被*封印*了。

En: Cut this boring stuff off immediately. You know what this case is about!
Zh: 马上切掉这些无聊的话题吧。你知道这起案件是怎么回事！

En: So desperately mundane. You need to funk this case up.
Zh: 真是平凡地让人绝望。你要把这个案子搞得疯狂一点。

En: He's basically *challenging* you to sex it up with some lurid twist. Don't get right into it, sit on it a bit -- then hit him with it!
Zh: 他根本是在*挑战*你，想让你用一些耸人听闻的转折把案件变得性感起来。不要马上直奔主题，稍微拖一会儿——然后再对付他！

En: It can still be an otherwordly sex-mystery *in your head*. With a dark twist, even.
Zh: *在你的脑海中*，它还可以是一个超凡脱俗的性谜题。甚至还有一个黑暗的转折呢。

En: Maybe this is why your chest feels so hollow -- you did an awful thing, and you can't even bring yourself to acknowledge it...
Zh: 也许这就是你的胸中感觉如此空洞的原因——你做了一件可怕的事情，但你自己甚至都不肯承认……

En: Tell me about the case again.
Zh: “再跟我说说这个案子。”

En: I have big news, Kim. (Tell Kim about how you no longer obsess over your sexuality.)
Zh: “我有大新闻，金。”（告诉金，你是如何不再为自己的性取向感到困扰的。）

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: I have managed to stop obsessing about my sexuality.
Zh: “我已经不再为自己的性取向困扰了。”

En: Nothing. Actually, as I'm saying this, I realize I would not be telling the truth.
Zh: “没什么。其实，当我这么说的时候，我就意识到自己不会说出真相。”

En: Okay -- was there anything else?
Zh: “好吧——还有别的事吗？”

En: Great. Imagine all the time you'll have for work now -- matter of fact, we should get back to it right now.
Zh: “很好。想象一下你现在还有多少工作时间——其实，我们现在就应该回去工作了。”

En: He's not the least bit curious about what you planned to say.
Zh: 他对你要说的话一点也不好奇。

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Nod.
Zh: 点头。

En: Mhm.
He turns his attention elsewhere.
Zh: 他把注意力转移到其他地方了。

En: Just one thing though...
Zh: “不过有一件事情……”

En: Get back to it. [Leave]
Zh: 回去工作吧。[离开]

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: You didn't stop at all, did you? You're just obsessing about *other people's* sexuality now.
Zh: “你根本没有停下来，对吧？现在你只是对*其他人*的性取向感到困扰了。”

En: Are *you* part of the homo-sexual underground?
Zh: “*你*是地下同性恋组织的一份子吗？”

En: Nothing, let's go. [Leave.]
Zh: “没什么，我们走吧。”[离开。]

En: Uhm... uh...
Yeah, but...
Zh: “是啊，但是……”

En: You're right, yes. Let's go. [Leave]
Zh: “你说得对，没错。我们走吧。”[离开]

En: ... but am I? I'll spare you another *20 hour mind-project* -- yes, I am. Now let's get back to work.
Zh: “……那我到底是不是呢？？我不会让你再进行一次*20小时的心灵项目*了——是的，我是。现在我们继续回去工作吧。”

En: That's the truth.
Zh: 这是事实。

En: ... but *am* I? I'll spare you another twenty hours thinking about it -- yes, I am. Now let's get back to work.
Zh: “……那我到底*是不是*呢？？我不会再让你想个20小时了——是的，我是。现在我们继续回去工作吧。”

En: Oh no, you're a cop, you can't go to jail...
Zh: 哦不，你是个警察，你不能进监狱……

En: Aren't we going to talk about the boots I'm wearing?
Zh: “不打算聊聊我脚上穿的这双靴子吗？”

En: Yes, uhm... The lieutenant stares at your shaven face, his eyes narrowed. "Uhhhh..." he mumbles. "I don't know what to say." He coughs. "Perhaps..."
Zh: “是的，呃……”警督盯着你刮过的脸——眼睛眯了起来。“啊……”他喃喃自语。“我不知道该说什么。”他咳嗽了几声。“也许……”

En: Damage.
Zh: 损伤。

En: Yeah -- damage. He means damage.
Zh: 是啊——损伤。他的意思是损伤。

En: Yes, but containers *contain*. Things. Shiny things. They're interesting.
Zh: 是的，但是容器能*装东西*。各种各样的东西。闪闪亮亮的东西。它们很有趣。

En: Hold on, and the RCM is?
Zh: “等等，那RCM是什么？”

En: That's us. The Revachol Citizens Militia. We're the police in this city.
Zh: “是指我们。瑞瓦肖公民武装。我们是这座城市的警察。”

En: The RCM, or the Revachol Citizens Militia, is the police force you and him are part of. A self-organized peace corps of the occupied city of Revachol, the RCM operates within a legal twilight, yet its authority is rarely questioned. It's *super useful* to know this.
Zh: RCM，或者是瑞瓦肖公民武装，是你和他所属的一支警察力量。它是占领城市瑞瓦肖的一支和平部队，RCM在模糊的法律体系下运作，但它的权威性很少受到质疑。知道这一点是*非常有用*的。

En: It doesn't *have* to be murder. It can just be sex.
Zh: “不一定*非要*是谋杀。可能只是性。”

En: Actually it does have to be murder. This is a murder investigation. But we digress.
Zh: “其实还必须是谋杀。这是一起凶杀案调查。不过，我们跑偏了。”

En: One you should be *ashamed* of. It's no wonder the soft one doesn't want it back. It was right to abandon you...
Zh: 你应该为这一个感到*羞愧*。难怪温柔的那一个不想要它回来了。抛弃你是对的……

En: It's just stupidity. The lieutenant shakes his head.
Zh: “这就是太愚蠢了。”警督摇摇头。

En: What did your preliminary information say about it? Before you came here?
Zh: “你得到的初步信息是怎么说的，在你来这里之前？”

En: You mean like a brief? Do you want me to brief you?
Zh: “你是说简述吗？你希望我给你简述一下吗？”

En: Kim! We need to talk about... *responsibility*.
Zh: “金！我们得谈谈你的……*职责*。”

En: Ah, I'm glad to hear it, detective. I was wondering when we'd get to this very subject.
Zh: “啊，很高兴听你这么说，警探。我一直在想，我们什么时候才会提到这个话题。”

En: To be completely honest, detective, I've been meaning to raise that very subject with you.
Zh: “说实话，警探，我一直都想和你提这个话题。”

En: Good. We're on the same page then.
Zh: “很好，看来我们意见很统一。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: If you ask me, it's high time for you to put the bottle away. Sober up, get back to basics. That would be my advice.
Zh: “我觉得你应该把酒瓶收起来了。清醒起来，重回正轨。这是我的建议。”

En: If you ask me, it's past time for you to cut back on the smoking. You seem like a man with a death wish. It's quite disturbing.
Zh: “我觉得你早就应该把烟戒掉了。你抽起烟来就跟不要命一样。真的很烦人。”

En: If you ask me, it's past time for you to lay off the *street drugs* you've been abusing. They really aren't helping us solve the case.
Zh: “我觉得你早就该把*街头毒品*给戒掉了。那些东西对我们的案子毫无帮助。”

En: If you ask me, it's high time for you to set aside these frivolous *side tasks* and focus 100% of your energies on the case before us.
Zh: “我觉得你应该把这些琐 碎的*日常杂事*放到一边，把100%的精力放在眼前这桩案子上。”

En: And I hope we don't even need to discuss the *street drugs* you've been abusing...
Zh: “而且你滥用*街头毒品*的事情都不用我再多说了吧……”

En: Oh, I'm not giving up drinking. It's what got me where I am today!
Zh: “哦，我是不会戒酒的。是酒精成就了如今的我！”

En: At least let me have my cigarettes, Kim. I only smoke them for the bonuses.
Zh: “好歹让我抽烟吧，金。我抽烟只是为了属性加成啊。”

En: I can't give up street drugs now. I *just* got addicted to them!
Zh: “我现在没法戒掉街头毒品。我*就是*有瘾了！”

En: No, no. I meant like *la responsabilité*. You know, the kind that gets assigned.
Zh: “不，不是。我指的是那种*职责*。你知道吧，就是分配给每个人的那种职责。”

En: The lieutenant's quizzical expression gradually deepens into a frown.
Zh: 警督的表情越发疑惑，然后皱起了眉头……

En: Why are you pronouncing it like that gentleman from the Institute of Price Stabilité?
Zh: “你的语气怎么听起来像那个物价稳定研究所的绅士？”

En: Why are you pronouncing it like you're a Coalition bureaucrat?
Zh: “你的语气怎么听起来像个联盟的官僚？”

En: You know what? Forget it. What *specifically* are you trying to assign responsibility *for*?
Zh: “我跟你说，忘了这事吧。你*究竟为什么*想分配职责？”

En: So what *are* we talking about, then?
Zh: “我们*到底*在说什么？”

En: I'm talking about *la responsabilité*. You know, the kind that gets assigned.
Zh: “我指的是那种*职责*。你知道吧，就是分配给每个人的那种职责。”

En: (Point to the ground.) "For the murder."
Zh: （指着地面。）“为了凶杀案。”

En: (Point to yourself.) "For my disaster of a life."
Zh: （指着自己。）“为了我这辈子受的罪。”

En: (Spread your arms wide.) "For this *whole situation*."
Zh: （张开双臂。）“为了这*所有的一切*。”

En: But isn't that *our* job?
Zh: “可那难道不是*我们的*工作吗？”

En: But isn't that *your* job?
Zh: “可那难道不是*你的*工作吗？”

En: Ah, now I understand... The lieutenant nods his head gravely.
Zh: “啊，我现在明白了……”警督郑重地点点头。

En: Next I suppose you're going to tell me you need to form a *committee* to assign this *responsabilité*?
Zh: “估计你接下来要跟我说，你要成立一个*委员会*来分配*职责*了？”

En: No, it's way more nuanced than that. There's like this whole historical or socio-economic dimension to it.
Zh: “不，比这个细致得多。其中还考虑到了历史和社会经济学的层面。”

En: Yes, exactly.
Zh: “是的，没错。”

En: How did you know?
Zh: “你怎么知道？”

En: Fascinating. And here I believed your recent turn toward moralism was just an act.
Zh: “真厉害。我本来以为你最近的道德主义倾向只是做个样子……”

En: Simple, because Moralintern types *love* to form committees.
Zh: “很简单啊，因为国际道德伦理委员会的人*喜欢*成立委员会。”

En: In any event, I am just a humble law official. I may work under the Moralintern's umbrella, but I'm certainly not qualified to serve on any 'committee'.
Zh: “不，我只是个小小的执法人员。我是在国际道德伦理委员会的保护伞下工作没错，但绝对不可能成为一位‘委员’。”

En: You know who might be, though? That Mr. Villedrouin, the uh... gentleman you needlessly antagonised in the apartments...
Zh: “你知道那个人是谁吗？就是那个维尔德鲁安先生，呃……你在公寓里莫名其妙地吵了一架的那位绅士……”

En: If you're serious about this, you might keep your eyes out for any Coalition officials we might happen to run across.
Zh: “如果你是认真的，那最好留意一下我们可能遇到的所有联盟官员。”

En: You know who might be, though? That Mr. Villedrouin, the uh... gentleman you met in the young man's apartment...
Zh: “你知道那个人是谁吗？就是那个维尔德鲁安先生，呃……你在那个年轻人的公寓里遇见的那位绅士……”

En: You know who might be, though? That Mr. Heidelstam, the gentleman near the Feld building with the... distinctive way of speaking.
Zh: “你知道那个人是谁吗？就是那个海德斯塔姆先生，那个在菲尔德大厦附近……说话方式很独特的绅士。”

En: If I were trying to get in touch with the Coalition, I would start by seeking him out. But first you might need to speak with his *young companion*.
Zh: “如果是我想和联盟取得联系，就会先去找他。但首先得去和他*年轻的同伴*谈谈。”

En: If you're trying to get in touch with the Coalition, I would start by finding some way to apologise to him. But first you might need to speak with his *young companion*.
Zh: “如果你想和联盟取得联系，就要先想办法和他道歉。但首先得去和他*年轻的同伴*谈谈。”

En: Now, was there anything else?
Zh: “现在，还有其他事吗？”

En: Why didn't we know anything about the caller?
Zh: “为什么我们对呼叫者一无所知呢？”

En: They didn't identify themselves in any way. The tone was muffled using a device of some sort. The desk could identify neither the caller's age nor sex.
Zh: “他们没有以任何方式表明自己的身份。声音被某种设备压低了。服务台无法辨别出对方的年龄以及性别。”

En: They -- Klaasje, as we've come to suspect -- didn't identify themselves in any way. The tone was muffled using a device of some sort. The desk could identify neither the caller's age nor sex.
Zh: “他们——卡拉洁，正如我们怀疑的——没有以任何方式表明他们的身份。声音好像被某种设备压低过。服务台无法辨别出对方的年龄以及性别。”

En: Was there anything else you wanted to ask about the competition? If not, we should move.
Zh: “关于竞争，你还有什么想问的吗？如果没有，我们就该走了。”

En: So he volunteered to represent the 57th, but not out of competitiveness -- on the contrary.
Zh: 所以他志愿做了57局的代表，但并不是出于喜欢竞争的原因——而是正好相反。

En: On the surface he's dismissive, but there's a conspiratorial glint in his black eyes. If only you knew what *he* would say...
Zh: 从表面来看，他有些轻蔑，但是他的黑眼睛里闪缩着一丝阴谋的意味。如果你知道*他*会说什么就好了……

En: Talk of this... (Consider the phrasing.) "...pissing competition worries me. What stupidity is this?"
Zh: “说到这个……”（考虑一下措辞。）“……尿尿竞赛，我有点担心。这是什么糊蠢事？”

En: You should tell me now. This seems relevant.
Zh: “你应该现在就告诉我，感觉这好像跟案子有点关联。”

En: Let it go.
Zh: 顺其自然吧。

En: Later. His voice is unflinching. "Was there anything else you wanted to know about the case?"
Zh: “之后再说。”他的声音坚定不移。“关于这起案件，你还有什么想知道的吗？”

En: Somewhere in your mind hangs a dark green blackboard. There are two columns. One says: "cases solved", the other: "confirmed kills".
Zh: 你脑海深处的某个地方挂着一面深绿色的黑板。上面有两个栏目。一个写着：“已破获案件”，另一个写着：“确认击杀数。”

En: Look for: Precinct 57 -> Lieutenants -> Kitsuragi, Kim.
Zh: 查找：57分局->警督->曷城，金。

En: The name "Kim 'Pinball' Kitsuragi" appears before you.
Zh: “金‘弹球’曷城”出现在你面前。

En: Cases solved?
Zh: 已破获案件？

En: Confirmed kills?
Zh: 确认击杀数？

En: One hundred and four.
Six.
Look for: Precinct 41.
Zh: 查找：41分局。

En: Jean Vicquemare, Judit Minot... A special consultant... what was his name? Then the rows degrade into green nothingness.
Zh: 让·维克玛，茱蒂特·迈诺特……一位特别顾问……他的名字叫什么？在那之后，一排排的数据消失在绿色的虚无中。

En: Blink.
Zh: 眨眼。

En: Open your eyes.
Zh: 睁开眼睛。

En: Close your eyes.
Zh: 闭上眼睛。

En: The rows are endless.
Zh: 这些行列永无止境。

En: Your brothers have left you.
Zh: 你的兄弟们离开了你。

En: The lieutenant is thinking to himself. His eyebrows are slightly raised.
Zh: 警督独自思忖着，眉毛微微上扬。

En: So you volunteered to spoil it.
Zh: “所以你是自愿来砸场子的。”

En: Yes. I'm an unrepentant spoilsport. The lieutenant appears pleased with this.
Zh: “是的。我是个顽固不化的扫兴者。”警督好像对此感到很高兴。

En: For some reason I thought you had a hundred and *four* solved cases.
Zh: “不知道为什么，我觉得你已经破获了*104*起案件。”

En: Well, *technically*, the lieutenant says, somewhat sheepishly. "The official record keeper has been known to double-count murder-suicides."
Zh: “呃，*从技术上讲*，”警督有些羞怯地说到。“大家都知道官方记录管理员把谋杀——自杀案件算两次。”

En: A common accounting gimmick used to inflate precinct stats.
Zh: 一种常见的会计花招，用来夸大分局的统计数据。

En: So, you're still a *good* cop, right?
Zh: “所以，你还是个*好*警察，对吧？”

En: I'm good enough for this case. I'm not here to compete. I came because I had to. My fellow officers -- the sergeants, in particular -- would have made this scene into a circus.
Zh: “对于这起案件来说，我已经足够好了。我来这里不是为了竞争，而是因为我必须这么做。我的其他同事——特别是那些警长——会把这个犯罪现场变成一场马戏表演。”

En: Okay, enough of the competition then. Tell me something else. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，竞争的话题差不多就到这里。跟我说说别的吧。”（结束）

En: Let's be honest: 102 is pathetic. A very small number.
Zh: “老实说：102太可悲了。这个数字实在太小。”

En: I wouldn't say that. And my case load is measly, but that's beside the point...
Zh: “我不会这么说。而且我经手的案件数少得可怜，但这并不是重点……”

En: He is not the least bit offended. He just wants you to know his case load should be much higher.
Zh: 他一点也不生气。他只想让你知道，他的案件数应该更高一些。

En: I wonder what this says about me? That I was sent by my station?
Zh: “我想知道这说明了什么？就是我被分局派来的事？”

En: What's special about Martinaise?
Zh: “马丁内斯有什么特别的？”

En: Martinaise? Nothing. It's just a puddle at the end of some drainpipe. No one cares about this place, they care about sports. Most of our colleagues don't even know how to get here. North of the Interchange doesn't exist.
Zh: “马丁内斯？没什么。只是排水管尽头的一个水坑。没人在乎这个地方，他们只在乎运动。我们的大部分同事甚至都不知道怎么过来。枢纽站北部对于他们来说根本不存在。”

En: Hmm... He raises an eyebrow, thinking it best to let you make the next move.
Zh: “嗯……”他扬起眉头，觉得最好还是让那个你先迈出下一步。

En: A tremble comes over you, another after-effect of ethanol poisoning perhaps? Feels like leaves do when they rustle in the breeze, somewhere far away, below the Turbine. The 41st and the 57th... The lieutenant was right. It's not about who gets what's North, it's about who doesn't.
Zh: 你浑身颤抖，也许又是酒精中毒的后作用？感觉就像在风中沙沙作响的树叶，就在某个遥远的地方，涡轮机的下面。41分局和57分局……警督是对的。这跟谁去北边无关，而是谁不去。

En: There can only be one conclusion: I am the finest we have, a hero cop sent to outperform you in every way imaginable.
Zh: “只有一种结论：我是最棒的，是一个英雄警察，他们派我来就是为了在你想得到的每一个地方都完胜你。”

En: There can only be one conclusion: I am the finest, a case-solving machine sent to outperform you in every way imaginable.
Zh: “只有一种结论：我是最棒的，一台破案机器，他们派我来就是为了在你想得到的每一个地方都完胜你。”

En: I am a highly experimental cop. But if I am right... this is *outré* even by my standards.
Zh: “我是一个很有实验精神的警察。不过如果我是对的……那就算以我的标准来看，也有些*反常*了。”

En: I'm gonna leave why I was sent *unspecified*.
Zh: “为什么*没有详细说明*就把我派过来这一点先不管了。”

En: The silence carries.
Zh: 沉默继续。

En: Clearly, he has already formed his own theory.
Zh: 显然他已经形成了自己的一套理论。

En: I must be an augury, an apocalyptic omen sent by my people. Can you guess my message?
Zh: “我肯定是一个预兆，是我的分局派来的天启征兆。你能猜出里面隐含的信息吗？”

En: Don't be scared, but I think I might have supra-natural abilities.
Zh: “别害怕，不过我觉得我可能有超能力。”

En: I probably have an *unbelievable* kill count.
Zh: “我可能拥有*难以置信*的击杀数。”

En: Okay. He takes a step back. "Style me."
Zh: “好吧。”他退后一步。“展示给我看看。”

En: (Point to yourself.) "This is what a *modern* cop looks like."
Zh: （指向你自己。）“这就是一个*现代*警察该有的样子。”

En: (Loosen your shoulders.) "You should ease up a bit. You need more *looseness*."
Zh: （放松你的肩膀。）“你应该放松一点，你需要再*松弛*一些。”

En: This man has sharp, precise composure, without impatience. Years of being himself have whittled the tension down to readiness. He is at home in what must be a subtly thought out set of apparel. Do you even have anything to teach him?
Zh: 这个男人拥有敏锐而严格的镇静，但没有丝毫的不耐烦。多年来的保持本心已经让他把当下的紧张气氛化解为了自愿配合。这无疑需要巧妙地施展深思熟虑才能得出的感染力，然而他看上去非常轻松自如。你还有什么能教他的吗？

En: It's impossible to tell whether he's serious or joking.
Zh: 很难说他到底是认真的，还是在开玩笑。

En: Everything from scalp down is top notch, but you need a patrol cap.
Zh: “从头顶往下一切都是出众的，不过你需要一顶巡逻帽。”

En: I don't need a patrol cap.
Zh: “我不需要巡逻帽。”

En: I know it seems unintuitive. But trust me.
Zh: “我知道感觉有点不直观。但是一定要相信我。”

En: An *orange* patrol cap -- you need it.
Zh: “一顶*橙色*的巡逻帽——你需要它。”

En: He thinks for a second. "Okay, maybe I do."
Zh: 他思考了几秒钟。“好吧，也许是吧。”

En: He's waiting.
Zh: 他在等待。

En: Orange patrol cap. I can't think of anything else.
Zh: “橙色巡逻帽。我想不出别的了。”

En: In the New, twenty years ago. It's what a modern cop looked like then.
Zh: “在新时代，20年以前。一个现代警察看起来就是那个样子。”

En: He means The New, the third decade of the current century. The decade of disco, decadence, and the victory of democracy-powered free market economy over it's critics.
Zh: 他的意思是新时代，本世纪的30年代，那是迪斯科和颓废的时代。后来民主自由市场经济就胜过了它的批评者。

En: No I don't. I *used* to. Plain clothes did the trick. For both of us, it seems. He nods toward you. "Now..."
Zh: “不，我不用。我*曾经*有点。便服起作用了。好像对我们俩来说都是。”他朝你点点头。“现在……”

En: Because you're the best qualified? No, that doesn't seem right...
Zh: 因为你是最有资格的？不，感觉不太对……

En: Why did the 41st send me?
Zh: 41分局为什么会派我来？

En: No one else wanted it.
Zh: 没有别人想。

En: Sorry Cop.
Zh: 抱歉警探。

En: Sylvie suicide jokes
Zh: 塞尔维的自杀笑话

En: Roy mentioned suicide
Zh: 罗伊提到自杀

En: Put the gun in your mouth
Zh: 把枪塞进嘴里

En: Really? His eyebrow gets raised one more notch.
Zh: “真的吗？”他的眉毛又抬高了一个度。

En: I arrive at the scene three days early, drink myself to oblivion, fully re-immerse myself in this reality and then work the case from an angle so crescent fresh it produces *never before seen* results. Not only for criminology, but for the human mind.
Zh: “我三天之前到达现场，喝到烂醉如泥，完全重新沉浸到这个现实，然后就能从一个全新的角度看待这起案件，产生*前所未见*的效果。不仅仅是为了犯罪学，也是为了全人类。”

En: Of course not. I'm just a drunk. They sent a drunk.
Zh: “当然不是。我只是个醉鬼。他们派来了一个醉鬼。”

En: It can't be that... Part of him sort of wants to believe what you just said.
Zh: “不可能的……”他有点想相信你刚才说的话。

En: I am a notoriously difficult-to-work-with *wunderkind* with extremely  unorthodox methods.
Zh: “我是一个名声在外很难公事的*神探*，我用的都是极其非传统的方法。”

En: Forget it, it's too much.
Zh: “算了吧，这样太过了。”

En: Wouldn't that be something? And who could say it's not true? If you really don't remember anything... how would you know? The thought makes him uneasy. "We should move."
Zh: “那不是很好吗？谁能说这不是真的？如果你真的想不起来……你又怎么会知道呢？”这个想法让他有些惴惴不安。“我们该走了。”

En: Are you sure? Who could say it's not true? If you really don't remember anything.... how would you know? The thought makes him uneasy. "We should move."
Zh: “你确定吗？谁能说这不是真的？如果你真的想不起来……你又怎么会知道呢？”这个想法让他有些惴惴不安。“我们该走了。”

En: A good joke.
Zh: “这笑话不错。”

En: He does not actually think it was a good joke.
Zh: 他不是真心觉得这是个好笑话。

En: Okay, it was a poor joke. But we could use a good mannered Cop Off, don't you agree?
Zh: “好吧，这是个糟糕的笑话。但是我们可以用比较礼貌的警察对决，你不觉得吗？”

En: Yes, a joke. In truth I will only moderately humiliate and outperform you.
Zh: “是的，一个笑话。事实上我只会适度地羞辱你，胜过你一些。”

En: A joke, of course. Rest assured, I have no intention to compete with you.
Zh: “当然了，这是个笑话。放心，我无意跟你竞争。”

En: Of course it's a joke, look at me! You have over a hundred cases solved, how could I compete with you?\n
Zh: “当然是个笑话了，你看我这幅模样！你破过上百起案子，我怎么能和你竞争呢？”

En: Of course it's a joke, look at me! How could I compete with you, you probably have a ton of cases solved.
Zh: “当然是个笑话了，你看我这幅模样！我怎么能和你竞争呢，你可能已经破过好多案子了。”

En: It's not that many. Officer, I'm sure you have a lot more cases solved. Now...
Zh: “也没有那么多。警官，我肯定你破获的案件更多。现在……”

En: Strangely enough, it's not pity. He would never pity a fellow officer. He truly appears to think you've solved your fair share of cases -- perhaps more than him.
Zh: 说来也怪，这不是同情。他永远也不会同情其他警官。他好像真心觉得你破获了很多起案件——也许比他还多。

En: If it helps you work better, okay. See it as competition. But don't expect me to. Now...
Zh: “如果这样能帮你更好的工作，那好吧。就把这当成是一次竞争。但是不要指望我也会这样。现在……”

En: Oh? You will? He raises his collar. His eyes narrow. "Then I will *help* you to do so. Now..."
Zh: “哦？你会吗？”他竖起衣领，眼睛眯了起来。“那我会*帮*你的。现在……”

En: It's good we set this thing aside.
Zh: “把这件事放在一边其实挺好的。”

En: Still, against his better judgement, this made him think just a little bit less of you
Zh: 然而，明知道这样不好，他对你的评价还是降低了。

En: No.
Zh: “不能。”

En: There will be a blood-letting of unimaginable proportions. Un-imaginable.
Zh: “将会有一场让人难以想象的流血冲突。超乎一切想象。”

En: Better not to relay it.
Zh: “最好不要传达出去。”

En: You know, actually no. I'm not an augury. I don't why I said that.
Zh: “你知道吗，其实不是的。我不是占卜师。我不知道自己为什么会这么说。”

En: I agree. Too dark.
Zh: “我同意。太黑暗了。”

En: Oh no, it's very imaginable. We fail to place responsibility for the hanging, the harbour company see the situation is out of control so they bring in private military, maybe a gunship, the Union responds in strength. Automatic fire.
Zh: “不，绝对是可以想象的。我们没能弄清楚谁要对绞刑负责，看到情况失控的港口公司就找来了私人军队，也许还有武装直升机，而工会也选择强势回应。自动射击。”

En: Local law enforcement solving one little homicide decides nothing. Not solving it... can have real and calculable effects. Things can always get worse. Matter of fact, augury, we should move.
Zh: “当地执法人员解决一起小凶杀案无法决定什么。解决不了的话……就可能会产生真实又能够预测到的效果。事态总是能发展地更糟糕。老实说，占卜师，我们该走了。”

En: Which school do you subscribe to -- Mambo... He opens the other hand: "...or Jambo?"
Zh: “你是哪一派的——冗言派……”他摊开另一只手：“……还是赘语派？”

En: Wait, which one is the one where you hear your tie talk to you?
Zh: “等等，能听到领带跟你说话的是哪一个？”

En: Mambo -- I have a vivid interior. I *see* and *feel* things.
Zh: “冗言派——我的内心生动又明艳。我能*看见*，能*感觉*到东西。”

En: Jambo -- I get these strange *shivers* from being hung over. Just before, a chill whisked down my back.
Zh: “赘语派——宿醉醒来之后我就会奇怪的*颤抖*。就在刚才，我的后背还窜下一阵寒意呢。”

En: I think I might have both Mambo *and* Jambo -- I imagine strange things and get cold chills too.
Zh: “我觉得我可能冗言*和*赘语都有——我会想象奇怪的东西，而且还会打寒颤。”

En: Mental illness.
Zh: “精神疾病。”

En: Hangovers do give officers super-powers. Many drink only to receive *the gift*.
Zh: “宿醉确实能给警官带来超能力。很多人喝酒只是为了收获这种*天赋*。”

En: Because I have that.
Zh: “因为我有那个。”

En: I don't have that -- I have something cooler. What was that? Mambo and Jambo?
Zh: “我没有那个——我还有更酷的东西。话说那是什么？冗言赘语什么的？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: I didn't know that was even possible. It must be a great burden.
Zh: “我都不知道这种事居然是可能的，肯定是个很重的负担吧。”

En: He opens one hand and looks at it. A moment passes.
Zh: 他摊开一只手，看着它。片刻过去了。

En: So the 41st Precinct sent you, because you are the most mentally ill out of all the officers at your station?
Zh: “所以41分局派你来，因为你是所有警官当中精神最不正常的？”

En: I don't remember any of the other officers. Just a handful of names, but they mean nothing to me.
Zh: “我不记得其他警官了。只有一些名字，但是对于我来说毫无意义。”

En: I don't know, I don't remember any of the other officers.
Zh: “我不知道，我不记得其他警官了。”

En: No, it's not that bad. I'm sure we have officers way more mentally ill than me.
Zh: “不，并没有那么糟糕。我肯定还有很多比我精神更不正常的警官。”

En: Then why did they send you?
Zh: “那他们为什么派你来？”

En: It's always good to have a police officer who can see and feel.
Zh: “能看见和感知的警官总是很好的。”

En: What is *the gift*?
Zh: “*天赋*是什么？”

En: I think I have it.
Zh: “我觉得我有。”

En: It's nothing major. Just feels a bit strange that's all.
Zh: “没什么大不了的。只是感觉有点奇怪，仅此而已。”

En: There is no gift. It must be repeated: there is no *Gift*. It is meant as obvious sarcasm. Do not ask what *The Gift* is.
Zh: 没有什么天赋。必须重复一次：没有*天赋*这一说。这明显就是在挖苦。别问什么是*天赋*。

En: There is no gift. We should move.
Zh: “没有什么天赋。我们该走了。”

En: It is good to have a para-detective in a labour dispute. I can see why they sent you -- should we go?
Zh: “处理劳资纠纷的时候，有个模范警探跟着总是好的。我能看出他们为什么会派你来——现在可以走了吗？”

En: I bet you do.
Zh: “我相信你会的。”

En: Why, do you agree?
Zh: “为什么，你同意吗？”

En: You're right. I am a super-predator. Off leash like this... it's about to get very violent.
Zh: “你说得对。我是个超级掠食者。像这样去掉束缚之后……就会变得非常暴力。”

En: The 41st is known to kill people. Often.
Zh: “41分局出了名的爱杀人。经常发生。”

En: He neither approves, nor disapproves. Yet there is something more there, something you can't put your finger on.
Zh: 他既不赞同，也没有不赞同。不过里面还有更多的含义，某种你也说不清楚的东西。

En: If you could only find your gun.
Zh: “如果你能找到你的枪就好了。”

En: It's a good thing you found that gun. I can see why they sent you now.
Zh: “还好你找到枪了。现在我知道他们为什么派你来了。”

En: I will only use lethal force with the utmost care and precision.
Zh: “我只会极其小心和精确地使用致命武力。”

En: It is.
Zh: “没错。”

En: It's not a burden, it's a gift.
Zh: “这不是负担，这是一种天赋。”

En: It's both a burden -- and a gift.
Zh: “它既是负担——也是一种天赋。”

En: It's good to have.... His mouth twitches as he comes up with the expression: "...an *ambidextrous para-detective* in a labour dispute. I can see why they sent you -- should we go?"
Zh: “挺好的……”他想出这种表达方式的时候，嘴唇有些抽搐：“……处理劳资纠纷的时候能有一个*心灵手巧的模范警探*总是挺好的。我能看出他们为什么派你来——现在可以走了吗？”

En: You sense that there's more to it than the lieutenant lets on, but the politics of the issue are too *complex* for your fragile mind to grasp right now. Maybe try again when you have more facts?
Zh: 你感觉情况比警督透露出来的要复杂的多，不过这里包含的政治对于你脆弱的心灵来说有些太过*复杂*，现在还难以领会。也许等你发现更多事实的时候，可以再试一次？”

En: Ah, so this is a struggle over *who runs Martinaise*!
Zh: “啊，所以说存在*谁掌管马丁内斯*的争端！”

En: Detective, about what the twins had to say...
Zh: “警探，关于那对双胞胎的话……”

En: Okay... About that call.
Zh: “好吧……关于那个电话。”

En: What happened to you? the lieutenant asks as he leans in, eyes mapping your frozen face.
Zh: “你怎么了？”警督倚过身子，盯着你僵住的脸。

En: I think you should know that I can't remember *anything*.
Zh: “我觉得我应该告诉你，我*什么*都记不起来了。”

En: No response. He just arches his brow.
Zh: 没有回应。他只是拱起了眉毛。

En: Okay. Forget it.
Zh: “好吧，算了吧。”

En: Hold still a second... (Try to smell Kim.)
Zh: “等一下……”（闻一闻金。）

En: Do you *need* something, detective?
Zh: “你*要*干什么，警探？”

En: The lieutenant's posture becomes rigid and uncomfortable as you lean forward and sniff the area just above his shoulder blade...
Zh: 当你探身过去，嗅着警督肩胛骨上侧的气味时，他的身体有些僵硬和不安。

En: The lieutenant is fastidious as a cat in matters of his personal upkeep. And yet, in the folds of his jacket you can just perceive the stale and acrid traces of oil rags, transmission fluid, and brake pads.
Zh: 警督在个人保养方面像只猫一样一丝不苟。但是，你从他夹克的口袋闻到了油腻的破布、传动液和刹车片的腐旧刺鼻气味。

En: Wait-wait. There's something else there, something that sets your GABA-receptors aflutter.
Zh: 等……等等。还有些别的，那些东西让你的胺基丁酸受体兴奋了起来。

En: He smells... like a man? A perfectly normal man?
Zh: 他闻起来……像个人？一个再正常不过的人？

En: The lieutenant's aftershave. A common drugstore brand. Strong hints of pine needle.
Zh: 是警督须后水的味道。普通药店的牌子，有股强烈的松针味。

En: But what sort of *ideological* picture do these smells paint? Tough to say...
Zh: 但这些气味究竟勾勒出了一幅怎样的*思想*画面呢？很难说……

En: Strange. You're not getting any strong *ideological whiffs* from the lieutenant.
Zh: 很奇怪，你没有从警督身上闻到任何强烈的*思想气味*。

En: I was just wondering what brand of aftershave you use.
Zh: “你想知道你用的哪个牌子的须后水。”

En: Just as I suspected: You're no lieutenant... you're a *mechanic*!
Zh: “我怀疑得没错：你不是警督……你是个*汽修工*！”

En: Nope, we're good.
Zh: “没什么，挺好的。”

En: It's nothing fancy, just plain old Taiga Super Special...
Zh: “不是什么高档货，就是普通的超独特针叶林……”

En: I do like to *hang around* the motor pool every now and then, but that doesn't make me a mechanic.
Zh: “我的确经常喜欢去停车棚*闲逛*，但也不代表我是个汽修工啊……”

En: ... Alright then.
Are you thinking of... you know? He gestures toward his cheeks with an invisible razor.
Zh: “你是不是在想……嗯？”他摆出用剃刀朝着脸颊的姿态。

En: I do like the way it tingles, though. It's the only part of shaving I actually look forward to.
Zh: “不过我还挺喜欢刺痛感的。这大概是我刮胡子时候唯一期待的事情了……”

En: Lose *these* bad boys? No way.
Zh: “要我甩掉*这些*坏家伙？不可能。”

En: Do you think I should?
Zh: “你觉得我应该这样吗？”

En: The lieutenant considers your muttonchops for a long moment. A slight grimace crosses his face.
Zh: 警督看着你脸上的络腮胡思考了很久。他脸上露出一丝痛苦的神情……

En: Sorry, I'm really no expert on this sort of thing...
Zh: “抱歉，我在这方面不是专家……”

En: I didn't mean to offend. It's your business, of course.
Zh: “我不想冒犯你，毕竟这是你自己的事……”

En: Sort of like how hanging around the liquor store hasn't made you into a master distiller.
Zh: “就好像你经常在酒馆里闲逛，也不会成为一位酿酒大师。”

En: Hey! I've been doing better.
Zh: “喂！我也不是在改进了吗。”

En: That was a cheap shot.
Zh: “这是恶意中伤。”

En: My apologies, detective. I couldn't resist.
Zh: “抱歉，警探。我没忍住。”

En: You're right, detective. My apologies.
Zh: “你说得对，警探。抱歉。”

En: That's a good point. It could happen any day now.
Zh: “说得有道理。这种事随时可能发生。”

En: Wait-wait. There's *something* there, something that sets your GABA-receptors aflutter.
Zh: 等……等等。还有些别的，那些东西让你的胺基丁酸受体兴奋了起来。

En: The lieutenant's aftershave. A common drugstore brand. Strong hints of pine needle.
Zh: 是警督须后水的味道。普通药店的牌子，有股强烈的松针味。

En: Was there something else you wished to discuss?
Zh: “你还有什么想和我说的吗？”

En: Was there something else you wished to discuss?
Zh: “你还有什么想和我说的吗？”

En: What is this place? The lieutenant stares at the dusty training equipment.
Zh: “这是什么地方？”警督看着灰尘扑扑的训练设备。

En: So... (Point to your face.) "I shaved."
Zh: “所以……”（指着自己的脸。）“我刮过胡子了。”

En: Either way, good on you. The lieutenant gathers himself. "You were saying...?"
Zh: “不管怎么说，挺适合你的。”警督振作了精神。“你刚才说……？”

En: What is it? You can tell me, Kim.
Zh: “这是干什么？你可以告诉我的，金。”

En: I know, I know -- stunning, right?
Zh: “我知道，我知道——让人目眩神迷，对吧？”

En: I knew I shouldn't shave! I knew it and still did it. What an idiot...
Zh: “我就知道不该刮胡子的！我明明知道但还是这么做了。真是个白痴……”

En: No-no, it's... okay, I guess. He keeps staring at you, dumbfounded. "I may have preferred the mutton chops, they sort of seemed to... cover the..." He stops.
Zh: “不，不，其实……还好，我觉得。”他慌乱地盯着你看。“我也许更喜欢络腮胡，它们好像可以……盖住……”他停了下来。

En: Uhhh... He scratches his cheek. "I'm really not sure about this turn of events. I may have preferred the mutton chops. They sort of seemed to... cover up the..." He stops.
Zh: “呃……”他挠了挠面颊。“我对这次事态的转变真的不太确定。我也许更喜欢络腮胡，它们好像可以……盖住……”他停了下来。

En: I'm not really sure about this turn of events. I... think the mutton chops might have been a better idea. They sort of seemed to cover up some of the... He stops.
Zh: “我对这次事态的转变真的不太确定。我……觉得络腮胡可能更好一些。它们好像可以盖住那些……”他停了下来。

En: Let me just make this perfectly clear -- our job here... is to find *the killer*?
Zh: “让我把事情完全说清楚——我们在这里的工作……就是找出那个*凶手*？”

En: Of course. Yes. I understand everything now.
Zh: “当然了，你说得对。现在我一切都理解了。”

En: I still don't understand anything.
Zh: “我还是什么都不懂。”

En: Just to be clear -- we are police officers. It's our job to find the killer. That's the case. Uncover and arrest the killer.
Zh: “声明一点——我们是警察，我们的工作就是找出杀人凶手。就是这样。找出然后逮捕凶手。”

En: That's right.
Zh: “没错。”

En: Okay. Well. We are police officers. It is our job to find the killer. So that's what I intend to do. You should try too.
Zh: “好吧。我们是警察。我们的工作就是找出凶手。所以我打算这么做。你也应该尝试这么做。”

En: Looks like we have more to discuss with those so-called *Hardie boys*. His voice is lowered. "Half their reasoning just went out the window."
Zh: “看来我们跟那些所谓的*哈迪兄弟*还有很多要讨论的事情。”他压低了声音。“他们的理由有一半都站不住脚了。”

En: I'd appreciate it if you didn't force us into situations where I may have to shoot random civilians. Because that won't get us anywhere.
Zh: “如果你不把我们逼到随意射杀平民的境地，我会非常感激。因为那样也不会给我们带来任何结果。”

En: Alright, we're finished here. Let's quickly debrief and go over what we found -- so we don't do it in front of the company rep.
Zh: “好吧，先就到这里。我们快速汇总一下发现的情况——这种事是不能在公司代表眼前做的。”

En: Officer, what was that?
Zh: “警官，刚才你是怎么回事？”

En: This is not going quite as I hoped it would, detective. The lieutenant's voice is hushed.
Zh: “事情的情况跟我希望的不太一样，警探。”警督的声音有些隐忍。

En: This might be just the place to help you rectify your... financial situation.
Zh: “这个地方也许能帮你改正你的……财务状况。”

En: Any theories about what happened here? He gazes down at the sign.
Zh: “对这里发生的事有什么理论吗？”他凝视着下面的标志。

En: We should think about calling it a day, maybe? The nights are still miserably cold this time of year.
Zh: “也许我们应该考虑收工了？每年这个时候，晚上还是挺冷的。”

En: When you said before that you were *down with racism* -- was it meant in earnest or were you just trying to win his confidence?
Zh: “之前你说可以*接受种族主义*——你是认真的，还是想赢得他的信任？”

En: Khm... Having looked away during the final phase of your little episode, the lieutenant now addresses you.
Zh: “呃……”在你那出小插曲最后阶段转移目光的他，现在又开始跟你说话了。

En: What in the name of hell was *that* supposed to be?! The lieutenant is gritting his teeth.
Zh: “*这*见鬼的东西到底是怎么回事？！”警督咬紧了牙关。

En: Morning. He gives you a quick nod.
Zh: “早上好。”他朝你快速地点了一下头。

En: The lieutenant glances at his electronic wristwatch.
Zh: 警督瞥了一眼他的电子腕表。

En: You can always use the radio in my Kineema to call your station's lazareth again. Was there anything else you wanted?
Zh: “你还可以用我锐影里的无线电呼叫你们的警局检疫所。还有什么需要的吗？”

En: Excuse me. I was lecturing you. I shouldn't have. You should consult a medical professional if you feel that you need help.
Zh: “抱歉。我刚才在跟你讲大道理。我不应该这么做。如果你需要帮助的话，可以找医务人员咨询一下。”

En: Yes, you killed him. And then, as part of the plan, you drowned out the memory...
Zh: 是的，你杀了他。然后，作为计划的一部分，你埋葬了所有记忆……

En: It wasn't that you were horrified -- you were just surprised by your own daring!
Zh: 并不是说你被吓坏了——你只是被自己的胆量惊到了！

En: Forgetting was merely a matter of convenience -- very clever!
Zh: 忘记只不过是为了方便起见——真的很聪明！

En: Are you sure you would have had the strength to take down a hardened mercenary? You're not in the best shape.
Zh: 你确定自己有能力干掉一个久经沙场的雇佣兵吗？你现在又不是最佳状态。

En: Forgetting was merely a matter of convenience -- very clever!
Zh: 忘记只不过是为了方便起见——真的很聪明！

En: It wasn't that you were horrified -- you were just surprised by your own daring!
Zh: 并不是说你被吓坏了——你只是被自己的胆量惊到了！

En: Maybe this is why your chest feels so hollow -- you did an awful thing, and you can't even bring yourself to acknowledge it...
Zh: 也许这就是你的胸中感觉如此空洞的原因——你做了一件可怕的事情，但你自己甚至都不肯承认……

En: Perhaps you did it for the Union -- your political beliefs gave you the strength and courage to take on such a formidable opponent.
Zh: 也许你是为了工会做的——你的政治信仰给了你力量和勇气去对付一个如此可怕的对手。

En: Perhaps you did it for the Union -- your political beliefs inspired this extreme act of violence.
Zh: 也许你是为了工会做的——你的政治信仰激发了极端暴力的行为。

En: Kim... I think *I* may be the murderer. I killed the man and then tricked myself into forgetting about it.
Zh: “金……我觉得*我*可能是凶手。我杀了那个人，然后设计让自己失去了记忆。”

En: Keep it to yourself. This is your burden to bear now.
Zh: 自己保密吧。现在它成了你必须承受的负担。

En: What nonsense. [Discard the idea.]
Zh: 都是些什么鬼话。[抛弃这个想法。]

En: The lieutenant appears unfazed. "And what has led you to this conclusion?"
Zh: 警督似乎并不担心。“那又是什么引导你得出这种结论的呢？”

En: Lieutenant, I have a confession to make. *I* am the murderer.
Zh: “警督，我要向你忏悔。*我*就是凶手。”

En: Murder in cold blood seems like a thing I'm capable of -- if I just dare. What if I *already* dared?
Zh: “冷血谋杀很像是我能做出来的事——如果我敢的话。那如果我*已经*敢这么做了呢？”

En: But it all makes so much sense! It would have been *so* convenient for me to forget all about the murder, and everything else...
Zh: “但是这样都能说通啊！如果能忘记谋杀还有其他所有事，对我来说实在是*太方便*了……”

En: I did it for the Union. For the rights of the workers.
Zh: “我这么做是为了工会。为了工人们的权利。”

En: I'm a bad person. I believe I could have done anything.
Zh: “我是个坏人。我相信我什么事都做得出来。”

En: That is a common side effect of overindulgence. It will pass.
Zh: “这是过度放纵常见的副作用。这种感觉会过去的。”

En: Teenage nonsense, he dead pans. "You would have had to be ready to use lethal force long ago, when you joined the RCM."
Zh: “胡说八道，”他一本正经。“很久之前，在你加入RCM的时候，你就必须随时准备好使用致命武力了。”

En: Thus far your memory loss does not seem to be *convenient* for you or anyone else. Except insofar as you've managed to avoid doing any work.
Zh: “到目前为止，你的失忆好像对你或者任何人都谈不上是*方便*。除非是因为你什么工作都不想做。”

En: You wouldn't have been sent to investigate this case if you had close connections with the Union -- at least that's the hope. Also, he looks you over, "you don't strike me as a *Union man*."
Zh: “如果你跟工会有着很密切的联系，那就不会被派来调查这起案件了——至少希望如此。而且，”他打量了一下你，“在我看来，你并不像是*工会的人*。”

En: He sighs. "Well, we've yet to find any real evidence pointing to you in this case, or even a possible motive, so let's not add you to the list of suspects just yet."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“不过，我们在这起案件里还没有找到任何指向你的可靠证据，或者是一个可能的动机，所以还是先不要把你加到嫌疑人清单里去。”

En: Besides, I doubt you could have taken down a hardened mercenary single-handedly...
Zh: “再说了，我觉得你没办法凭一己之力干掉一个久经沙场的雇佣兵……”

En: *See*? You're clearly not a man in your prime anymore.
Zh: *看见*了吗？你显然已经不是一个处于全盛时期的男人了。

En: So you're saying I didn't kill him? But, Kim, it turned out to be that *I* was the one racing around town in my motor carriage...
Zh: “所以你是说我没有杀他？但是，金，事实证明是*我*开着車在城里乱跑啊……”

En: He purses his lips. "The victim wasn't run over by a drunk driver, so, while I can't condone your driving habits, the cases remain unrelated..."
Zh: 他咬住嘴唇。“被害人不是被醉酒司机撞倒的，所以，就算我不能容忍你的驾驶习惯，这也跟案子毫无关联……”

En: I find that highly unlikely. It's not unusual for detectives to feel complicit in the crime until the perpetrator is apprehended. Especially when the investigation is dragging. So let's get back to it, shall we?
Zh: “我觉得不太可能。在凶手被逮捕之前，警探感觉自己是这起案件的共犯是很正常的。尤其是调查进展缓慢的时候。所以我们还是继续回去调查吧，怎么样？”

En: He studies your face for signs of offence. "Don't take it personally -- it's just that security guards kept on by large corporations tend to be better trained and better armed than officers of the RCM."
Zh: 他仔细观察着你的脸，寻找着犯罪的迹象。“我不是针对你——只是大公司手下的安保人员一般比RCM的警官接受过更好的训练，而且还拥有更好的装备。”

En: So you're saying I didn't kill him?
Zh: “所以你是说我没有杀他？”

En: Look, it's not unusual for detectives to feel complicit in the crime until the perpetrator is identified and apprehended. Especially when the investigation is dragging. So let's get back to it, shall we?
Zh: “你看，在行凶者被逮捕之前，警探感觉自己是这起案件的共犯是并非少见。尤其是调查进展缓慢的时候。所以我们还是继续回去调查吧，怎么样？”

En: My chest feels hollow, like I did something terrible.
Zh: “我的胸口感觉很空洞，就好像我做了件很可怕的事。”

En: I just feel this sense of doom, like an awful thing has happened.
Zh: “我只是有一种厄运的感觉，好像发生过一件很可怕的事情。”

En: I've been meaning to have a little chat with you about your sense of *style*.
Zh: “我正好打算跟你稍微聊聊你的*时尚感*。”

En: I think we may have this... The lieutenant whispers, then gives the suspect a short glance.
Zh: “我觉得我们也许……”警督低语到，然后瞥了嫌疑人一眼。

En: Okay. What do you think is going on with that computer, chalkboard, and fireplace?
Zh: “好吧。你觉得那台电脑，黑板和壁炉是怎么回事？”

En: You did well, the lieutenant says as soon as you've left the apartment. The balcony feels cool and quiet, with a stunning view over the district.
Zh: “你做的很好，”你一离开公寓，警督就开了口。阳台上感觉凉爽又安静，可以看到整片区域的美景。

En: So, the death notification... the lieutenant says as soon as you've left the apartment.
Zh: “所以，关于死亡通知……”你一离开公寓，警督就开了口。

En: The lieutenant considers your question a moment. Before answering: "No, it's not a particularly mysterious case."
Zh: 警督考虑了一下你的问题，然后回答到：“不，这不是个特别神秘的案件。”

En: I'm relieved that you agree, he says, then pauses.
Zh: “你能同意我感到很宽慰，”他说着，然后停了一下。

En: He is glad and surprised to see an officer who can appreciate the less-than spectacular.
Zh: 看见一个懂得欣赏平平无奇的警官，让他既高兴又惊讶。

En: Is there anything else? You look like you were about to say something.
Zh: “还有别的事吗？看起来你好像想说什么。”

En: Anyhow, let's get back to the case. Was there anything else you wanted to know?
Zh: “不管怎么说，我们还是继续回到案子上来吧。你还有什么想知道的吗？”

En: We should ground the *owners* into fine paste and feed them to the pigs.
Zh: “我们应该把*业主*碾成肉酱然后拿去喂猪。”

En: With this little foreknowledge I am unsure which side to grind into pigsfeed. The decision must ferment in me. Also -- *nationalism*.
Zh: “就凭这一点点先见之明，我不确定应该把哪边碾成猪饲料了。这个决定肯定在我心里发酵。还有——*民族主义*。”

En: I don't think grounding one or the other side into pigsfeed will do anyone favours. I am really, really smart.
Zh: “我不觉得把哪边碾成猪饲料能对任何人有利。我是真的，真的很聪明。”

En: I've spent the last 20 seconds deciding on which side to *ground into pigfeed* -- why?
Zh: “过去的20秒我在决定要把哪边*碾成猪饲料*——为什么？”

En: There's something the lieutenant isn't seeing. The universe is too irrational, too morally complex. You should convince him.
Zh: 有些东西这位警督是看不见的。这个宇宙太过荒谬，道德层面上太过复杂。你应该说服他。

En: Politics is habit-forming. And hazardous. It is also irrelevant to policework. You are right to doubt the impulse, it might be detrimental to our task here.
Zh: “政治是会上瘾的。而且充满危险。这也跟警察的工作没有任何关系。你质疑这种冲动是对的，它对我们的任务可能是有害的。”

En: Now, was there anything else you wanted to know about the case?
Zh: “现在，关于这起案件，你还有什么想知道的吗？”

En: Oh, I guess that settles it, then.
Zh: “哦，我猜那就这么定了。”

En: Excellent. Was there anything else?
Zh: “太好了。还有别的事吗？”

En: Good point. Martinaise is *famed* for its occult sex-murder rites. We'll get on it *immediately*.
Zh: “说得好。马丁内斯就是因为神秘的性谋杀仪式而*闻名*的。我们*马上*就去调查。”

En: You're being sarcastic, aren't you?
Zh: “你是在挖苦我，对吗？”

En: Extremely.
Zh: “特别挖苦。”

En: The lieutenant is pretty sure *self-care* is just a euphemism for *nihilistic binge-drinking*.
Zh: 警督很肯定*照顾自己*只是*空虚豪饮*的一种委婉的表达方式。

En: Is it not?
Zh: 难道不是吗？

En: The lieutenant is pretty sure *style* is just a euphemism for *nihilistic binge-drinking*.
Zh: 警督很肯定*风格*只是*空虚豪饮*的一种委婉的表达方式。

En: Uhm. I want to talk about *you*.
Zh: “嗯。我想聊聊*你*。”

En: *Me*?
What about me?
Zh: “我怎么了？”

En: Yes, you.
Zh: “是的，你。”

En: I don't see how *my* life is pertinent to the investigation.
Zh: “我看不出*我的*生活跟调查有什么关系。”

En: We'll work better together if we have more rapport.
Zh: “如果我们彼此相处得更融洽，就能更好的一起工作。”

En: Come on, lieutenant, open up a little.
Zh: “来吧，警督，透漏一点。”

En: You're right. What's there to know about a lame binoclard?
Zh: “你说得对。一个蹩脚的四眼仔有什么好了解的？”

En: Hmmm, that's a fair point. Alright, for the good of the investigation, what do you want to know?
Zh: “嗯，你说的有点道理。好吧，为了更好的调查，你想让我告诉你什么？”

En: Binoclard? Is that how they do things over at the 41st? No wonder your clearance rate is so low.
Zh: “四眼仔？你们41分局都是这么做事的吗？也难怪你的清除率这么低了。”

En: He sighs. "If you insist. What do you want to know?"
Zh: 他叹了口气。“如果你坚持的话。你想知道些什么？”

En: You're wearing glasses.
Zh: “你戴着眼镜。”

En: Tell me a secret about yourself.
Zh: “跟我说一个你的秘密。”

En: That's all for now. (Conclude.)
Zh: “暂时就这些吧。”（总结。）

En: Good. Let's change the subject.
Zh: “很好。我们换个话题。”

En: Do you ever *talk* with yourself?
Zh: “你有没有跟自己*说过话*？”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: You know, when you're thinking. Do you ever have *conversations* with, like, your brain?
Zh: “你知道的，就是在你想事情的时候。比如说，你有没有跟自己的大脑*对话*过？”

En: I have no idea what you're talking about.
Zh: “我不知道你在说什么。”

En: So you're saying your brain *never* just chimes in with advice or warnings or *anything*?
Zh: “所以你是说你的大脑*从来没有*插过话，提些建议，警告或者是*任何东西*？”

En: I can't say that it does, no. When I need to think, I just use my notebook...
Zh: “不能说有，并不会。我需要想事情的时候，会用我的笔记本……”

En: The lieutenant's Conceptualization skills must be rather *rudimentary*.
Zh: 警督的概念化技巧肯定还处在*起步阶段*。

En: The lieutnant produces his familiar Mnemotechnique A6 and idly thumbs through a few pages.
Zh: 警督拿出熟悉的记忆法A6，漫不经心地翻了几页。

En: The lieutenant produces his small blue notebook and idly thumbs through a few pages.
Zh: 警督拿出他的蓝色小笔记本，漫不经心地翻了几页。

En: The lieutenant is a police officer of the *old school*. His concerns are material and extrinsic.
Zh: 警督是*守旧派*的警官。他关注的是事实和外部证据。

En: But this isn't an *old school* case.
Zh: 但这不是一起*守旧*的案件。

En: No, that makes sense! I'm *exceedingly* qualified!
Zh: 不，这很有道理！我*非常*够格！

En: Dumb brain, I'm good for nothing.
Zh: 愚蠢的大脑，我一无是处。

En: If you're so well qualified, why can't you remember why you were sent? Anyway -- don't keep the lieutenant waiting.
Zh: 如果你这么够格，为什么想不起来他们派你来的原因？不管怎么说——别让警督等太久。

En: You pick over what's left of your frontal cortex, but no compelling explanations emerge...
Zh: 你在大脑皮层里仅存的一些东西里细心挑选，但是却没有出现令人信服的解释……

En: What, no. That can't be right.
Zh: 什么，不是的。这不可能是对的。

En: Yeah, that makes more sense than the other stuff I thought of.
Zh: 是啊，这比我想到的其他东西更有道理。

En: Just think about it for a second. You're a raging alcoholic who showed up three days late and argues with his necktie. You weren't sent here to *win*.\n
Zh: 你稍微想一想。你是一个暴躁的酒鬼，晚了三天才出现，还会跟自己的领带争执。你被派来可不是为了*赢*的。

En: Just think about it for a second. You're a raging alcoholic who showed up three days late wearing piss-stained disco garb. You weren't sent here to *win*.
Zh: 你稍微想一想。你是一个暴躁的酒鬼，穿着沾染了尿渍的迪斯科风衣服，还晚了三天才出现。你被派来可不是为了*赢*的。

En: Whatever their reasoning, there's nothing written that says you have to prove them *right*.
Zh: 不管他们的理由是什么，没有任何书面证据说你必须证明他们是*对的*。

En: Well, sort of. It's less a matter of who *gets* to police Martinaise than who *has* to. It's an orphan district, in other words...
Zh: “呃，有点吧。问题不在于谁*来*监管马丁内斯，而是谁*必须*这么做。换句话说，这是个孤儿区……”

En: The lieutenant narrows a single eyebrow. "No."
Zh: 警督皱起一边的眉毛。“不。”

En: That's correct.
Zh: “没错。”

En: So how do you... you know... (Tap the side of your head.)
Zh: “所以你是怎么……你懂的……”（拍拍脑袋侧面。）

En: What, *think*? I do most of my work inside my notebook...
Zh: “什么，*思考*？呃，我的大部分工作都是记在笔记本上的……”

En: I get it -- You're one of those *old school* detectives. (Nod respectfully.)
Zh: “我明白了——你是那种*守旧派*的警探。”（恭敬地点点头。）

En: So, what? That makes *you* the *new school*? Gods spare us...
Zh: “所以，怎么了？这样*你*就成了*新派*了？老天爷都饶恕我们了……”

En: For real detective work, nothing beats a good notebook by your side...
Zh: “对于真正的侦探工作来说，没有什么比一个好笔记本的陪伴更好的了……”

En: I know exactly what you mean.
Zh: “我完全明白你的意思。”

En: I have no idea what you mean.
Zh: “我不知道你是什么意思。”

En: What *is* a crossword?
Zh: “填字游戏*是*什么？”

En: Ah, right. Your *condition*. Nevermind. All I mean to say is that we have a puzzle on our hands, but not all the pieces... yet.
Zh: “啊，没错。你的*状况*。无所谓。我只想说我们手上有一个谜题，不过还没有找到所有碎片……暂时如此。”

En: Hmm, perhaps you're a more proficient *cruciverbalist* than I am. All I mean to say is that we have a puzzle on our hands, but not all the pieces... yet.
Zh: “嗯，也许你比我更像是一个*填字游戏*专家。我只想说我们手上有一个谜题，不过还没有找到所有碎片……暂时如此。”

En: It will turn into quite the puzzle yet, I'm afraid... The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。“恐怕这会变成一个不小的谜题……”

En: That makes him a *binoclard*. Completely un-cop material, if you say so yourself.
Zh: 所以他才会变成一个*四眼仔*。如果按你说的话，这完全不是当警察的料子。

En: Ask again.
Zh: 再问一次。

En: Oh, okay.
Zh: “哦，好吧。”

En: Your brain sends the signal to your lips but they refuse the order. Something is paralysing them. You're pretty sure it has something to do with the lieutenant's eyebrow.
Zh: 你的大脑在向你的嘴唇发送信号，但它拒绝了这个指令。有什么东西让你的嘴唇丧失了正常的行为能力。你很肯定这跟警督的眉毛有关。

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。

En: This guy's got Authority off the charts. With just a flick of his eyebrow he's able to make you his thrall.
Zh: 这家伙争强好胜的本事非同小可。他只需眉毛一挑，就能让你为他效劳。

En: You feel a slight urge to put the lieutenant down for this, but you can't quite muster enough testosterone.
Zh: 你感觉到一种轻微的冲动，想把警督放倒，但是你无法聚集足够的睾丸素。

En: Just observing.
Zh: “只是观察一下。”

En: I guess you *don't* need glasses, then.
Zh: “那我猜你*不需要*眼镜了。”

En: I didn't know they *let* binoclards become cops.
Zh: “我不知道他们会*允许*四眼仔当警察。”

En: The lieutenant's face turns a ruddy shade. "I can *assure* you, detective, that I've passed *all* the required physical exams within the last year. Can you say the same?"
Zh: 警督的脸刷的一下红了。“我可以向你*保证*，警探，去年我已经通过了*所有*必要的体检。你敢这样说吗？”

En: You may not *remember* taking any little 'tests', but you're strong as a goddamn ox. Anyone can see that!
Zh: 你也许不*记得*参加过什么小*检查*，但你就像头该死的公牛一样健壮。所有人都能看出来！

En: The thing is, the RCM employs relatively few *oxen*. It's clear that what the lieutenant may lack in *musculature* he makes up for in other ways.
Zh: 问题是，RCM雇佣的*公牛*相对比较少。显然警督缺少的*肌肉组织*可以通过其他方式来弥补。

En: So what can I *do* about it?
Zh: 那我该如何*应对*呢？

En: What's going on? It's like you're a puppet in his hands.
Zh: 出什么事了？感觉你就像是他手中的傀儡。

En: What's... happening... to me?
Zh: 我……这是……出什么事了？

En: Give up. There's nothing you can do.
Zh: 放弃吧。你没什么能做的。

En: The lieutenant relaxes his eyebrow, and you seem to regain control of yourself.
Zh: 警督松开了眉毛，你好像又能控制自己了。

En: Something the matter, detective?
Zh: “怎么了，警探？”

En: *Nothing*. You better hope he doesn't abuse his authority. There's *a lot* of it.
Zh: *没什么*。你最好希望他不会滥用自己的权威。而且还*挺多*的。

En: Silently scream for help.
Zh: 无声地呼喊着救命。

En: Accept your thralldom.
Zh: 接受你的奴隶身份。

En: If the lieutenant were an *evil* man, who knows what sort of havoc he could wreak?
Zh: 如果警督是个*邪恶*的男人，谁知道他能造成怎样的浩劫呢？

En: Fortunately he is a committed officer of the RCM. He'll only use his powers for the good of the investigation.
Zh: 幸运的是，他是为RCM效忠的一名警官。他只会为了调查去使用自己的权力。

En: The lieutenant relaxes his eyebrow, and you seem to regain control of yourself.
Zh: 警督松开了眉毛，你好像又能控制自己了。

En: (Flex your bicep.) "Check it: I was made to crack skulls and solve cases."
Zh: （弯曲你的二头肌。）“看看：我天生就是打碎头骨和破获案件的料子。”

En: (Stroke your chin thoughtfully.) "I've always believed in mind over matter."
Zh: （若有所思地捋了捋胡子。）“我一直都相信精神高于物质。”

En: Just because *your* precinct has a reputation for cracking skulls doesn't mean that's the *only* way to solve a case... Was there anything else?
Zh: “不能因为*你们*分局有打碎头骨的名声，就意味着这是破案的*唯一*方式……还有其他事吗？”

En: That's good to hear. I have little patience for the *swinging dick* style of police work. It's patience and intelligence that solves cases... Was there anything else?
Zh: “能听你这么说挺好的。我对警察那种*飞扬跋扈*的工作方式没什么耐性。破案需要的耐心智慧……还有其他事吗？”

En: Yes, cannabis. The hemp. I rolled it into a small paper tube and lit in on fire. Then I *inhaled* the fumes. I was young.
It's as if the entire world is frozen solid in awe of this great clash of brows. Nothing exists outside this moment.
This seems dangerous. What if someone gets hurt?
He's not. It's all but a ruse, sire. The lieutenant's peculiar sense of humor.
Gradually turn your head and let your brow rise untethered.
Relax, Kim. I'm down with hemp.
Good. Only a fraction of a smirk in the corner of his mouth betrays his amusement. "But enough of this. Was there anything else?"
There's a hint of defensiveness in his voice. Even this minor act of rebellion is important to the lieutenant's self-construction.
Yes? His eyebrows remain completely level.
You mean cannabis?
Smoked... what?
Black patrol cap. I can't think of anything else.
Orange patrol cap. I can't think of anything else.
The words are true, milord. No love for the herb in the lieutenant.
Let's show him: Raise your brow high, up to high-noon.
He's waiting for a reaction.
(Jerk it like a powerlifter in a squat-cage.) "Your secrets, lieutenant -- Now!"
Tetrahydrocannabinol is the psychoactive substance in the cannabis plant, commonly known as 'hemp'. It's one of the most common recreational substances in the world.
Smugly, sire. Raise it smugly.
Keep your brow steady, but slowly nod your head as a flourish.
Too late. The die is cast. This is street-style brow-fighting. Two of a kind but one won't survive.
The lieutenant is a narcomaniac!
Heavy drops of rain fall on the colliding eyebrows and run down the worn faces. The world outside this stand-off almost doesn't exist.
Is that enough of a secret for you?
Of course. Here's my gun and my badge. His hands reach out empty. "They'll put me in the chaise for this, but it feels good to have it off my chest."
We should hit the hemp some time.
We should hit one of those flaming tubes some time.
We should hit a blunt some time.
Alright, I guess there is *one* thing...
Is that secret enough for you?
Put your neck into it. That's where the power comes from.
After decades unchallenged, the lieutenant is caught off-guard by your brazen provocation, but his counter comes fast and hard. This could get ugly...
You're serious right now?
He's waiting for a reaction.
Very funny, lieutenant.
An almost imperceptible snap inside the lieutenant. His shoulders relax, his eyebrow casually descending as he tries his best to look relaxed, nonchalant even.
Now, counter his counter! Brow to the troposphere! Full hoist!
The lieutenant immediately grasps the stakes. His gears turn as he prepares a devastating counter. This could get ugly...
Reflexes take over, the lieutenant's brow meets yours: His fierce counter nearly tears your face off. This could get ugly...
I have, on a few occasions -- when I was young and foolish -- smoked hemp.
Agreed. Only a fraction of a smirk in the corner of his mouth betrays his amusement. "Was there anything else?"
A *black* patrol cap -- you need it.
An *orange* patrol cap -- you need it.
The lieutenant anticipates your move but can neither deflect nor parry your counter-counter assault. A single drop of sweat trickles down his cheek...
Snowflakes fall and immediately melt in the heat of the colliding eyebrows. Nothing exists outside this moment.
The hemp, he repeats. "Cannabis. Is that secret enough for you?"
How about we 'hit' the crime we came here for instead. And by 'hit' I mean 'solve' it. Solve it *hard*.
I appreciate the honesty, lieutenant.
The law's the law.
You brought this on yourself.
After decades unchallenged, the lieutenant's reaction is a bit slow, but he's not letting you have this. Not for a second. This could get ugly...
(Slowly raise your eyebrow.) "Nothing at all."
Your fingers trace the edges of an imaginary holster on your hip. A slight twitch runs through your index finger.
The *chaise electrique*?
Hold steady...
Besides, those days are long behind me.
Kim, you son of a bitch...
An almost unbearable tension fills the room. The hairs of your back stand up.
All I ask is the chance to close this final case so I can go to the chaise with a clean conscience. His voice is filled with mock pathos. "Let's hurry, I'm living on borrowed time."
Sure. There was actually something else I was wondering about...
(Raise it cautiously.) "You sure you wanna do this, Kim?"
Okay, maybe you don't need a black patrol cap. Let me think of something else.
Okay, maybe you don't need an orange patrol cap. Let me think of something else.
Goddamn right something's the matter! Lieutenant Eyebrow here thinks he can shut you down like some brow-less freak? Thinks you're just going to lie back and take it?!
Yes. He nods. "Let's hurry now, I'm living on borrowed time."
There, you've breached his defenses. His secrets are yours.
It's not a big deal. I don't do it any more. An almost imperceptible sigh. "But it happened a few times. In my twenties."
(Raise it smugly.) "I don't know, *is* there?"
