That's not a secret. Everyone has smoked cannabis.
A graceful retreat. So smooth you'd almost think there was no battle at all.
Something in him breaks.
Zh: 他心底有什么东西破碎了。

En: She grins. "Can't say no to that. Well... the smallest cryptid is the *Cryobacter katlensis*, a unicellular bacterium. It was discovered in one of the northern-most points of Katla on the Boreal Plateau -- by renowned geologist Catelyn Mijanou some 70 years ago."
Zh: 她咧嘴笑了。“这我可不能拒绝。好吧……最小的神秘动物是*冷凝细菌卡特兰西斯*，一种单细胞细菌。它是在北方高原上卡特拉最北端的一个地方发现的——那是在大约70年前，由著名的地质学家凯特琳·米伽欧发现的。”

En: Wild, yes. Impossible, no. Although...
Zh: “奇怪，是的。不可能，不是。不过……”

En: Something in him breaks.
Zh: 他心底有什么东西破碎了。

En: Something in him breaks.
Zh: 他心底有什么东西破碎了。

En: Are there any photos of it?
Zh: “有它的相片吗？”

En: Alas, no. And the first scientist who got his hands on the creature's corpse put it in a jar of formaldehyde, thinking that would detoxify the Gnome's venom.
Zh: “唉，没有。第一个弄到这种生物尸体的科学家把它放在了一罐甲醛里，觉得这样就能解除侏儒的剧毒。”

En: Hmm... I'm not sure I agree with that, sweetie. At any rate, some argue that the kind green ape should also be thought of as human, especially because it has shown itself to be so *humane*. I don't dare form an opinion one way or the other without more information, though.
Zh: “嗯……我不确定自己是不是同意这一点，甜心。无论如何，有些人主张善良绿人猿应该也算人类，特别是它的表现特别*人道*。不过在没有更多信息的情况下，我也不敢轻易形成一种观点。”

En: Are there any *invisible* cryptids?
Zh: “有没有*无形*的神秘动物？”

En: What an interesting question! And the answer is: yes, there are!
Zh: “真是个有趣的问题！答案是：是，有的！”

En: I'm glad you did, dear. She seems genuinely glad.
Zh: “很高兴你能喜欢，亲爱的。”她似乎真的很开心。

En: What's that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: The *Col Do Ma Ma Daqua*, or 'thin whisper of sound'. And that's *precisely* what it seems to be -- self-replicating sound waves, invisible and intangible! It's very afraid of us, which makes it incredibly difficult to track.
Zh: “*哈库多玛达塔*，或者是‘轻声低语’。而且它*恰恰*好像就是这样——一种自我复制的声波，无影又无形！它非常害怕我们，所以追踪起来也是难以置信地困难。”

En: Col Do Ma Ma Daqua can also be translated as "a whisper light and low."
Zh: “哈库多玛达塔”也可以被翻译为“轻声低语”。

En: She smiles gently. "What about what?"
Zh: 她温柔地笑了。“怎么了？”

En: What evidence is there of this animal being a sound?
Zh: “有什么证据证明这种动物是有声音的呢？”

En: Of course. All fairy tales have someone or something invisible in them.
Zh: “当然。所有童话故事里都有隐形人，或者是无形的东西。”

En: Of course. A common thread in these -- disappearance and unfalsifiability... He concedes: "I liked the story, though, ma'am."
Zh: “当然。这些都有一条相同的主线——消失和不可证错性……”他退让了一步：“不过我喜欢这个故事，夫人。”

En: You're right Kim, it's childish, but I need to know.
Zh: “你说得对，金，这太孩子气了，不过我需要知道。”

En: Just nod eagerly.
Zh: 只是渴望地点点头。

En: Shush, Kim, she's gonna tell me about the invisible cryptid. What is it?
Zh: “嘘，金，她要给我们讲无形神秘动物了。是什么？”

En: When playing back recordings they had made in the foothills of the Ea mountain range, they noticed... certain anomalies -- patterns that seemed random at first, but, on closer examination, were consistent with the waveforms of song birds...
Zh: “在重放他们在埃亚山脉丘陵地带收集的录音的时候，他们注意到……某些反常现象……一些图案一开始看上去是随机的，但经过仔细检验之后，发现它们与鸟禽鸣叫的波形一致……”

En: It's the *Col Do Ma Ma Daqua*, the woman corrects her glasses. "Its name means 'thin whisper of sound'. And that's *precisely* what it is -- self-replicating sound waves, invisible and intangible! The Col Do Ma Ma is very afraid of us, which makes it incredibly difficult to track..."
Zh: “它叫*哈库多玛达塔*，”女人扶正了眼睛。“名字的意思是‘轻声低语’。而且它*恰恰*好像就是这样——一种自我复制的声波，无影又无形！它非常害怕我们，所以追踪起来也是难以置信地困难。”

En: Okay. I won't spoil your fun, the lieutenant concedes. "What is the invisible cryptid?"
Zh: “好吧，我不会破坏你的乐趣，”警督退让了一步。“那无形的神秘动物是什么？”

En: Mhm, song birds.
Zh: “嗯，青鸟。”

En: It could be, she says calmly. "As I said, it could be *everywhere*, and we wouldn't know any better. It could be ringing all the days of our lives -- *and* nights."
Zh: “也许是，”她平静地说到。“就像我之前说的，它可能*无处不在*，我们也只能知道这么多了。在我们的生活中，它可能每天都在鸣响——*还有*每个夜晚。”

En: What does it, um, sound like?
Zh: “呃，那它听起来是什么样的？”

En: ...they were performed so recklessly that when they happened upon the right frequency... well, they wiped out most of the population.
Zh: “……他们偶然发现正确频率的时候，采取了十分鲁莽的行动……就这样，他们消灭了大部分的种群。”

En: Why is the Ma Ma Daqua so afraid of us?
Zh: “为什么玛达塔这么害怕我们？”

En: No, dear. But this is basic physics... Oh, sorry, there I go assuming... She looks flustered for a moment, her eyes full of mute apology, then she recomposes herself. "At any rate, these tests..."
Zh: “不，亲爱的。但这不过是基础物理……哦，抱歉，我又在假设了……”有那么一会儿，她看起来激动不安，眼里满是无声的歉意，然后她又重新恢复了镇定。“不管怎么说，这些测验……”

En: I don't know... Do I look like a dweeb?
Zh: “我不知道……我看起来像是个白痴吗？”

En: Like nothing. It's such a high-pitched sound that us humans can't hear it -- nor can other animals. It could be ringing right outside your window -- and you wouldn't even know it! It could be anywhere -- everywhere, even...
Zh: “什么都不像。它是一种特别高音调的声音，我们人类是听不到的——其他动物也不行。它可能正在你的窗外鸣叫——而你自己却浑然不觉！它可能出现在任何地方——无处不在，甚至……”

En: Name them?
Zh: “起名字？”

En: After that, the corpuscle appears to have migrated elsewhere. There have been recordings of anomalies similar to those spotted in Ea -- but they've been few and far between. It's impossible to confirm the presence of any stable Col Do Ma Ma Daqua population anywhere.
Zh: “在那之后，微粒子似乎迁移到其他地方去了。有过一些异常现象的报告与埃亚山脉的发现类似——但是彼此却相去甚远。任何地方都无法证实有稳定的哈库多玛达塔种群存在。”

En: A whisper light and low...
Zh: “轻声低语……”

En: What if it's predatory?
Zh: “如果它是食肉动物呢？”

En: Although the 'low' part is a little ironic -- the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua makes, or, rather, *is* such a high-pitched sound that other animals, including humans, can't *hear* it. It could be everywhere, all of the time -- and we wouldn't know.
Zh: “虽然‘低’的部分有一点讽刺——哈库多玛达塔发出，或者更准确地说，*就是*一种特别高音调的声音，其他动物，包括人类都是无法*听见*的。它可能存在于任何地方，所有时间——只是我们无从知晓。”

En: Well, dear -- they cancel each other out. And these tests were performed so recklessly that when they happened upon the right frequency... well, they wiped out most of the population.
Zh: “亲爱的——它们互相抵消了。测验进行的实在是太鲁莽，他们碰巧找到正确频率的时候……把大部分族群都消灭了。”

En: Fine I'll bite. The lieutenant looks at her sceptically. "How can an animal be a sound?"
Zh: 警督疑惑地看着她。“好吧，我上钩了。动物怎么可能是一种声音呢？”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: But how can an animal be a sound?
Zh: “但是动物怎么可能是一种声音呢？”

En: Plenty. It's the evidence that led to its discovery. In the Twenties, a group of Areopagite ornithologists -- that is, scientists who study birds -- were trying out a new recording technology for capturing sounds outside the range of human hearing.
Zh: “很多。是证据引向了它的发现。在20年代，一群艾瑞奥帕吉特的鸟类学家——就是专门研究鸟类的科学家——想要测试一种新的录音技术，来捕捉人类听觉范围之外的声音。”

En: Many scientists have asked the same question -- some have claimed that it isn't *itself* a sound, but a tiny *corpuscle* that emits sound waves. But there's no evidence to support this theory.
Zh: “很多科学家问了相同的问题——有些声称它*本身*并不是一种声音，而是一种可以发出声波的极小*微粒*。但是并没有证据支持这种理论。”

En: Extinction?
Zh: “灭绝？”

En: Could it be *here*? (Look around.) "Right now?"
Zh: “它可能在*这里*吗？”（环顾四周。）“就是现在？”

En: Interesting. What about... (Conclude.)
Zh: “有意思。那……”（总结。）

En: I wouldn't be so sure. What if the damage is *also* invisible?
Zh: 我不会那么肯定。如果伤害*也是*无形的呢？

En: Exactly... And these tests were performed so recklessly that when they happened upon the right frequency... well, they wiped out most of the population.
Zh: “没错……测验进行的实在是太鲁莽，他们碰巧找到正确频率的时候……把大部分族群都消灭了。”

En: It's not. Don't worry. If it were predatory we would have found it by the damage it inflicts.
Zh: “不会的。别担心。如果它是食肉动物，我们就能通过它造成的伤害发现它的踪影了。”

En: They cancel each other out.
Zh: “它们互相抵消了。”

En: The scientists soon discovered they could track and even *predict* what appeared to be feeding, mating, and migration patterns based on sound waves in a *strictly delimited* range of ultrasonic frequencies -- even higher than those of the highest-pitched bat calls.
Zh: “科学家很快发现他们可以在*严格限定*的超声波频率范围内追踪、甚至是*预测*它们的进食、交配和迁移模式——甚至比那些最高音调的蝙蝠声波还要高。”

En: They realized that they had discovered a new species -- and called it the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua. After the Perikarnassian name for the voice of god, which is said to be *very* silent.
Zh: “他们意识到自己可能发现了一个新物种——而且还称之为哈库多玛达塔，这是一位声音之神的佩里卡纳西斯文名字，据说这位神明*非常*沉默。”

En: No, dear. They cancel each other out. And these tests were performed so recklessly that when they happened upon the right frequency... well, they wiped out most of the population.
Zh: “亲爱的——它们互相抵消了。测验进行的实在是太鲁莽，他们碰巧找到正确频率的时候……把大部分族群都消灭了。”

En: That is a sad story. She frowns. "A group of university students assisting with the field work, in their enthusiasm for the project and, no doubt, because they were preoccupied with impressing their professors, nearly drove it to *extinction*."
Zh: “那是一个悲伤的故事。”她皱起眉头。“一群对项目充满热情的大学生协助野外考察，毫无疑问，他们也是一心想要打动教授，结果差点把它*灭绝*了。”

En: You have *no clue* because you're not a DWEEB.
Zh: 你感到*毫无头绪*，因为你不是一个白痴。

En: This lady really should be a teacher. She's really good at the explaining things thing.
Zh: 这位女士真的应该去当老师。她真的很擅长解释。

En: Psst. They cancel each other out.
Zh: 喂！它们互相抵消了。

En: She nods gravely. "They tried to communicate with it, and had no other means but sound. So they started sending out sound waves at frequencies they thought might match the Ma Ma Daqua's. And what happens when a sound wave meets another sound wave of the same frequency, dear?"
Zh: 她严肃地点点头。“他们试图跟它交流，但是除了用声音之外别无他法。所以他们开始发送声波，用的是他们觉得可能与玛达塔频率频匹配的声波。当一个声波与另一个相同频率的声波相遇会怎么样呢，亲爱的？”

En: Maybe it's predatory?
Zh: 也许它是食肉动物？

En: Sequester. Thyme. Josquin -- She nods. "Those are but some of the Ma Ma Daqua they followed individually."
Zh: “幽静者。百里香。若斯坎——”她点点头。“都是他们单独追踪的一部分玛达塔。”

En: They amplify each other.
Zh: “它们互相增强。”

En: He nods approvingly.
Zh: 他点头赞同。

En: The lieutenant pauses thoughtfully.
Zh: 警督停下来，若有所思。

En: Ah, fuck it. Let's have more cryptids.
Zh: “啊，见鬼去吧。那就再多说点神秘动物。”

En: Right, okay, we can move on for now. (It'd be dishonourable to renege on the promise.)
Zh: “好吧，现在我们可以继续了。”（违背诺言是可耻的。）

En: But, Kim... Don't *you* want to hear about another cryptid too?
Zh: “但是，金……难道*你*不想再听一个神秘动物的故事吗？”

En: Something in him breaks.
Zh: 他心底有什么东西破碎了。

En: The lieutenant leans in. "Hey, you promised you'd only ask about one cryptid."
Zh: 警督靠近了一些。“喂，你答应只问一个神秘动物的。”

En: Well, the answer is: yes, there is an invisible cryptid.
Zh: “好吧，答案是：有的，有一种无形的神秘动物。”

En: This has been educational, sadly we need to discuss something else. (Conclude.)
Zh: “这些真的很有教育意义，遗憾的是我们需要讨论下别的问题了。”（总结。）

En: Of course, dear.
Zh: “当然了，亲爱的。”

En: Yes, that's another translation. Her eyes light up behind her glasses. "They're both quite lovely, aren't they?"
Zh: 她的眼睛在眼镜背后放光。“是的，这也是一种解释。它们都挺可爱的，不是吗？”

En: They grew quite obsessed with these little birds -- even though they couldn't see them, they could distinguish among individual birds and, she smiles, "even began to *name* some of them."
Zh: “他们变得非常痴迷这些小鸟——甚至觉得就算看不见，也能把有些小鸟单独区分出来，而且，”她笑了，“甚至开始给其中一些*起名字*。”

En: Wow.
Zh: “哇哦。”

En: Go on.
Zh: “继续。”

En: There are limits, you see, to how large a metabolism an ecosystem can beget. Some say a gravity anomaly below the Koko Nur desert might allow the creature to grow to these *gargantuan* sizes.
Zh: “你也看到了，一个生态系统能够产生多大的新陈代谢，还是有一些限制的。有人说科科努尔沙漠下面的重力异常也许给予了生物长成*庞然大物*的可能。”

En: Great. This is great shit. You need more.
Zh: 太好了，这话说得真漂亮。你还要多听一些。

En: Gravity anomaly? Diggin' it. Diggin' this para-scientific stuff right here.
Zh: 重力异常？我喜欢。我就喜欢这种超科学的东西。

En: Not *really*. It's just stylization. The way they drew eyes -- it's not a zoological drawing.
Zh: *并不见得*。这只是一种风格化。它们描绘眼睛的方式——并不是动物学的画法。

En: The Gnome of Geroma? That doesn't sound too bad.
Zh: “赫罗玛侏儒。听起来就很糟糕的样子。”

En: Oh -- it is. None of its victims survived. Grieving relatives never even found their bodies because the Gnome's venom *dissolved* organic tissue.
Zh: “哦——是的。它的被害人没有一个活了下来。悲痛欲绝的亲属甚至没有找到他们的尸体，因为侏儒的毒液几乎完全*溶解*了器官组织。”

En: The lieutenant throws you one of his *looks*. "*One* cryptid -- like you said. One. This can't turn into some kind of cryptid extravaganza. We have things to do."
Zh: 警督向你投过来一个他的那种*眼神*。“*一个*神秘动物——你说过的。就一个。这可不会变成某种神秘动物大联欢。我们还有事要做。”

En: Whooh... tough choice there.
Zh: 哇哦……真是困难的抉择。

En: She *transforms* when speaking about these strange animals -- into a confident woman.
Zh: 每当说到这些奇怪动物的时候，她就会*转变*——转变成一个自信的女人。

En: Mhm. They grew quite obsessed with these little birds -- even though they couldn't see them, they could distinguish among individual birds and, she smiles, "even began to *name* some of them."
Zh: “嗯。他们变得非常痴迷这些小鸟——甚至觉得就算看不见，也能把有些小鸟单独区分出来，而且，”她笑了，“甚至开始给其中一些*起名字*。”

En: Great regret washes over her. A wending cloth.
Zh: 一阵巨大的悔意将她吞没。一块褶皱的布料。

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Cryptid extravaganza? I like the sound of that.
Zh: “神秘动物大联欢？我喜欢这种说法。”

En: And I *don't*. Just one.
Zh: “而且我*不想*听。只可以讲一个。”

En: Or he'll be *disappointed* in you.
Zh: 或者他会对你*失望*的。

En: Oh no, she's making excuses. You're not interesting enough to talk cryptids with.
Zh: 哦不，她在找借口。你还没有趣到让她给你讲神秘动物的故事。

En: Wait... what do you mean 'strange light'?
Zh: “等等……你说奇怪的光线是什么意思？”

En: A mirage... or a psychogenous luminance. She does not elaborate the nature of this luminance further.
Zh: “一个海市蜃楼……或者是心因性的辉度。”她没有进一步详述这种辉度的性质。

En: *Cryobacter... katlensis*?
Zh: “*冷凝细菌……卡特兰西斯*？”

En: Yes. A unicellular bacterium that was discovered in one of the northern-most points of Katla on the Boreal Plateau -- by renowned geologist Catelyn Mijanou some 70 years ago.
Zh: “是的。一种单细胞细菌，是在北方高原上卡特拉最北端的一个地方发现的——那是在大约70年前，由著名的地质学家凯特琳·米伽欧发现的。”

En: Yes. The bacteria had survived in the ice since times immemorial. It is not hard to see where she could have gotten the idea.
Zh: “是的。这种细菌从远古时代就在冰层里幸存了下来。不难看出她是从哪里得到这个想法的。”

En: Wait, she *injected* herself with it?
Zh: “等等，她给*自己*注射了细菌？”

En: It's actually *a little* hard to see. He looks at her sceptically. "But do go on."
Zh: “其实*有一点*难看出来。”他怀疑地看着她。“不过请继续。”

En: Immortal bacteria, immortal geologist? That's too wild.
Zh: “永生的细菌，永生的地质学家？实在是太奇怪了。”

En: ...everything has a price, sweetie.
Zh: “……一切都有代价的，甜心。”

En: The Gnome of Geroma? That sounds terrifying.
Zh: “赫罗玛侏儒？听起来很吓人。”

En: Oh yes. None of its victims survived. Grieving relatives never even found their bodies because the Gnome's venom *dissolved* organic tissue.
Zh: “是的。它的被害人没有一个活了下来。悲痛欲绝的亲属甚至没有找到他们的尸体，因为侏儒的毒液几乎完全溶解了器官组织。”

En: No. It was a perfectly good explanation, stop being so sceptical.
Zh: 不。这是一种完美的解释，别再怀疑了。

En: Sure. A perfectly good explanation. It dissolved in its *own* venom. Go on then, ask about more gnomes or whatever.
Zh: 当然。这是一种完美的解释。被*自己*的毒液溶解了。那就继续吧，再多问问这个侏儒或者随便什么东西。

En: War story?
Zh: “战争故事？”

En: Yes. It was reported by soldiers in South Safre during the war. The kind green ape would visit bunkers during the night, healing wounded soldiers with its saliva.
Zh: “是的。是战争时期南萨弗里的士兵报告了这件事。它会在晚上前往碉堡，用自己的唾液治疗伤兵。”

En: And there was something about an undiscovered sub-species of man?
Zh: “还有一些关于人类亚种未被发现的东西？”

En: Wow. With its saliva?
Zh: “哇哦。用唾液吗？”

En: Yes. It has amazing healing qualities. Some soldiers reported growing back limbs, regaining their sight...
Zh: “是的。它拥有惊人的治疗效果。据报告说有些士兵重新生出了四肢，恢复了光明……”

En: Indeed there is! It's our closest relative among the cryptids. Same taxonomic family, different genus.
Zh: “确实是的！它是神秘动物世界中与我们最接近的亲缘物。相同的分类家族，不同的类属。”

En: Something in him breaks.
Zh: 他心底有什么东西破碎了。

En: Is this bird a cryptid? (Point to the tie she gave you.)
Zh: “这只鸟是一只神秘动物吗？”（指向她给你的领带）

En: No. Her eyes narrow mysteriously. "It's *the* cryptid."
Zh: “不是。”她故意眯起双眼。“它就是*那个*神秘动物。”

En: Wow. *The* cryptid?
Zh: “哇哦。*那个*神秘动物。”

En: Okay. What *is* this bird?
Zh: “好吧。那这只鸟*是*什么？”

En: The eight-eyed teratorn, the largest flying avian ever discovered -- with a wingspan of 11.5 metres. It was thought to have gone extinct 3,500 years ago, some even doubted the *fossils* were real. A mutation, they said. Until...
Zh: “八眼畸鸟是有史以来发现的最大的飞行鸟类——它的翼幅有11.5米。据说它在3500年前就已经灭绝了，甚至有人怀疑这个*化石*的真假。大家说这是一种突变现象。直到……”

En: Oh yes. The small silvery skull shines between your fingers, its beak sharp.
Zh: “啊，是的。”银色的小骨头在你的指间闪烁，它的鸟嘴十分尖锐。

En: Until?
Zh: “直到？”

En: Until it was *sighted* by renowned Gottwaldian explorer and naturalist Uwe Plattenkalk in '21.
Zh: “直到21年的时候，著名的戈特伍德探险家以及博物学家乌维·普拉滕卡尔克*看见*它了。”

En: What happened?
Zh: “发生什么事了？”

En: Alone in the blasted desert heat the doctor wandered eastward, where man hasn't stepped foot in over a thousand years -- since the fall of Perikarnassis.
Zh: “博士独自一人走在荒芜的沙漠，向东方游荡，几千年来从来没有人踏上过那片土壤——自从佩里卡纳西斯陨落之后。”

En: Mutation... the man scoffs. "All of evolution is a mutation."
Zh: “突变……”男人嘲弄到。“所有进化都是一种突变。”

En: This *renown* seems a bit dubious -- your own catalogue comes up completely empty, but -- of course -- you are not all knowing.
Zh: 这个*著名*显得有点可疑——你把自己脑海里的名目完全掏了个空，但是——当然了——你也不是什么都知道的。

En: Okay -- and Uamrao is...
Zh: “好吧——那乌玛拉奥是……”

En: The world's largest canyon system, sweetie. It's a barren waste east of the Erg desert -- an ancient riverbed, completely dried up.
Zh: “世界上最大的山谷系统，甜心。那是尔格沙漠东面的一个不毛之地——一个古老的河床，已经完全干涸了。”

En: He was lost, without any navigation equipment and desperately low on water. After a day or two he noticed a bird high in the noon sky. A great black bird, it seemed gargantuan.
Zh: “他迷路了，没有任何导航装备，剩余的水量也低到让人绝望。大概一两天之后，他注意到正午的高空中有一只鸟。一只黑色的大鸟，似乎非常庞大。”

En: Every now and then, the bird would dive down to feed on an animal carcass somewhere on the horizon. But by the time Uwe got there, the teratorn had taken off already -- and the carcass was picked clean. This happened many times.
Zh: “这只鸟时不时地会俯冲而下，捕食地平线上某处的动物尸体。等乌维赶到那里的时候，那只畸鸟已经起飞了——残骸也被吃了个干净。这种事发生了很多次。”

En: Eat the bird, Uwe. It's the only way to survive.
Zh: 吃掉那只鸟，乌维，这是唯一活下去的办法。

En: I understand. He started hunting it.
Zh: “我理解。他开始狩猎它了。”

En: He was following it?
Zh: “他追着它走？”

En: It followed him?
Zh: “它追着他走？”

En: Yes. A bird that big has a lot of blood in it and he was dying of thirst. For many days Dr. Plattenkalk followed the teratorn. Until they reached a great canyon wall, where the bird finally landed to rest.
Zh: “是的。那么大的鸟肯定有大量的血液，而且他就快要渴死了。普拉滕卡尔克博士跟着怪鸟走了很多天。直到他们来到一个大峡谷的山壁，那只鸟终于降落在那里休息了。”

En: I need to hear about this sighting.
Zh: “我需要听听这个目击事件。”

En: Okay. Not now, let's back up a notch.
Zh: “好吧，现在不行，我们先记下来。”

En: Of course, sweetie. Do you want to hear about another cryptid? The teratorn is, by many accounts, the greatest though...
Zh: “当然可以，甜心。你还想听神秘动物的故事吗？不过在很多人看来，畸鸟的故事已经是最好的了……”

En: It happened on a botanical expedition into the vast and unexplored Uamrao canyon in southeast Iilmaraa. Dr. Plattenkalk got separated from his group during a sandstorm...
Zh: “事情发生在一次植物学探险期间，队伍要深入伊尔玛东南部那片广阔而又未经开发的乌玛拉奥峡谷。在一次沙暴中，普拉滕卡尔克博士跟团队走散了……”

En: Yes. Or, rather, *hunting*. A bird that big has a lot of blood in it and he was dying of thirst. For many days, Dr. Plattenkalk followed the teratorn, until they reached a great canyon wall, where the bird finally landed to rest.
Zh: “是的。或者，更应该说是*狩猎*。那么大的鸟一定拥有大量血液，而且他就快要渴死了。普拉滕卡尔克博士跟着怪鸟走了很多天。直到他们来到一个大峡谷的山壁，那只鸟终于降落在那里休息了。”

En: The professor climbed up there, with a rock in his hand. He found the bird sleeping with its head tucked under its wing. A great black pile of feathers on the perch. So he approached, slowly squeezing the rock in his fist...
Zh: “教授爬上去，手里握着一块岩石。他发现大鸟睡觉的时候把脑袋埋在了翅膀下面。栖木上有一大堆黑色的羽毛。所以他靠了过去，慢慢握紧手中的岩石……”

En: Watch out!
Zh: 小心！

En: Then the teratorn suddenly looked at him. He could see it had *eight eyes* -- four on either side of its skull, like a spider -- and the man couldn't move. He was paralysed, frozen into place with the rock in his hand. Whatever he did, he could not get closer to the bird.
Zh: “畸鸟突然看到了他。他可以看到它有*八只眼睛*——像蜘蛛一样，一边四只——他无法行动。他全身麻痹，僵在原地，手里还握着那块岩石。无论他做什么，就是无法靠近那只鸟。”

En: The bird was controlling his mind. It *kept* him from approaching. He could step back, but every time he stepped forward -- paralysis. Uwe spent three days trying, until the bird flew away.
Zh: “那只鸟在控制他的思想。它*阻止*他靠近。他可以退后，但是每向前走一步——就会麻痹。乌维尝试了整整三天，直到那只鸟飞走。”

En: More like *he* followed it. A bird that big has many litres of blood in it and he was dying of thirst. For many days, Dr. Plattenkalk followed the teratorn, until they reached a great canyon wall, where the bird finally landed to rest.
Zh: “更像是*他*跟着它走。那么大的鸟一定拥有大量血液，而且他就快要渴死了。普拉滕卡尔克博士跟着怪鸟走了很多天。直到他们来到一个大峡谷的山壁，那只鸟终于降落在那里休息了。”

En: Yes way, sweetie, she smiles, used to derision. "Cryptozoologists have been tracing it ever since, but Uamrao is vast, mysterious, and holds many secrets."
Zh: “是的，甜心，”她笑了，已经习惯被人嘲弄。“从那之后，神秘动物学家一直在追踪它的踪迹，但是乌玛拉奥峡谷旷阔无垠，充满神秘，隐藏着很多秘密。”

En: Hold on, how did he survive to tell the story?
Zh: “等等，那他是怎么活下来讲述这个故事的？”

En: An eight-eyed mind-controlling bird. Fuck yes.
Zh: “长着八只眼睛可以控制思想的鸟。是啊，真他妈的没错。”

En: An eight-eyed mind-controlling bird? C'mon...
Zh: “长着八只眼睛可以控制思想的鸟？拜托……”

En: An eight-eyed mind-controlling bird? No way.
Zh: “长着八只眼睛可以控制思想的鸟？不可能的。”

En: The eight-eyed teratorn was indifferent to him, as long as he didn't get closer than two steps. It even let him feed on some carcasses up there -- and the two unfertilized eggs it left behind.
Zh: “八眼畸鸟对他好像并不关心，只要他不靠近2步之内的范围。它甚至允许他吃附近的尸骸——而且还留下两只未受精的蛋。”

En: Absolutely, sweetie, she smiles, glad you agree. "Cryptozoologists have been tracing it ever since, but Uamrao is vast, mysterious, and holds many secrets."
Zh: “绝对的，甜心。”她笑了，很高兴你能同意。“从那之后，神秘动物学家一直在追踪它的踪迹，但是乌玛拉奥峡谷旷阔无垠，充满神秘，隐藏着很多秘密。”

En: Yes way, sweetie, she smiles, used to derision. "Cryptozoologists have been tracing it ever since, but Uamrao is vast, mysterious, and holds many secrets."
Zh: “是的，甜心，”她笑了，已经习惯被人嘲弄。“从那之后，神秘动物学家一直在追踪它的踪迹，但是乌玛拉奥峡谷旷阔无垠，充满神秘，隐藏着很多秘密。”

En: This one -- I liked, the lieutenant puts down his notes. "Not only does it have eight eyes, *and* is a living fossil, *and* the largest bird ever to live. It also does mind control."
Zh: “这个——我挺喜欢的，”警督放下笔记本。“不仅仅有八只眼睛，*还是*个活化石，*而且*是世界上最大的鸟。对了，还能控制思想。”

En: Modern radar telemetry shows great promise -- we *will* confirm this one. By the end of the decade, latest.
Zh: “现代雷达遥测技术带来了巨大的希望——我们*一定会*证实它的存在。最迟是在十年后。”

En: He's sincere. He likes the audacity of it.
Zh: 他很真诚。他喜欢这种胆识。

En: So that was the last anyone saw of it?
Zh: “所以这是最后一次有人看见它？”

En: Okay. So it's never been seen again.
Zh: “好吧。所以之后就再也没人见过了。”

En: Sadly, yes. But there are numerous reports of eight-eyed bird skulls from Iilmaraa. And then there's the striking resemblance to the Perikarnassian Imperial Eagle -- an ancient heraldic symbol -- that is hard to pass off as coincidence. The imperial eagle, too, had eight eyes.
Zh: “很可悲，是的。不过伊尔玛有很多八眼鸟骨的报告。而且它跟佩里卡纳西斯帝国雄鹰有惊人的相似——那是一个古老的纹章符号——很难被当成是巧合。帝国雄鹰，同样也有八只眼睛。”

En: Very, *very* hard.
Zh: “非常，*非常*难。”

En: This one's very famous, everyone knows it. People will be looking at that tie on you and thinking: that man is into cryptids. She winks. "So -- what else do you want to know?"
Zh: “这个非常出名，所有人都知道。大家会看着你的领带，然后想到：这个男人喜欢神秘动物。”她眨眨眼。“所以——你还有什么想知道的？”

En: There's really no point in manipulating anyone. She'd be only too pleased to tell you about her work. Go on and ask!
Zh: 操纵别人真的没有任何意义。她只会非常乐意地跟你讲述自己的工作。那就继续问吧！

En: Hey, Lena, I'd like to hear about some of the cryptids you've studied. Could you just tell me about a couple of them?
Zh: “喂，莉娜，我想听听你研究的那些神秘动物。能不能给我稍微讲讲？”

En: She grins. "I'm glad you liked them, but I'm not really one to tell you about *all* of them. You should ask my husband if you get the chance. He's the real expert."
Zh: 她咧嘴一笑。“很高兴你能喜欢，不过我真的没办法把*所有*神秘动物讲给你听。如果有机会的话，你应该问问我丈夫。他是个真正的专家。”

En: Sometimes, the most charming thing you can do is be *reasonable* in your requests.
Zh: 有时候，你能做到的最有魅力的事情就是*合理地*提出要求。

En: Could you tell me about one, just *one* more interesting cryptid? The Insulindian phasmid was great.
Zh: “你能给我讲一个吗，只要*一个*更有趣的神秘动物就行？伊苏林迪竹节虫就很棒。”

En: Could you tell me about one, just *one* interesting cryptid?
Zh: “你能给我讲一个吗，只要再*一个*有趣的神秘动物就行？”

En: She nods. "I understand your scepticism, but that's what happens with things that are rare and stand out -- their disappearance is most memorable, and they are least likely to be found again."
Zh: 她点点头。“我理解你的怀疑，但是那些罕见和与众不同的事情就是这样的——它们的消失最让人难以忘怀，而且很可能再也不会被发现了。”

En: The lieutenant throws you one of his *looks*. "*One* cryptid -- not a couple. One. This won't turn into some kind of cryptid extravaganza."
Zh: 警督向你投过来一个他的那种*眼神*。“*一个*神秘动物——不是一些。就一个。这可不会变成某种神秘动物大联欢。”

En: Oh, I'd be delighted! Truth be told, I could really use the company too...
Zh: “哦，我很乐意！老实说，我也确实需要有人陪伴……”

En: He looks at you with wonder and sincere pity. "I'm beginning to wonder if I should."
Zh: 他看着你，带着一些惊奇和由衷的怜悯。“我开始怀疑我是不是应该这么做了。”

En: Uhm... Do we have time for *breaks* to listen to theories about imaginary animals?
Zh: “嗯……我们有没有这个*休息*时间去听虚构动物的理论。”

En: Nope, you don't have any new winning tactics to get the woman to spill the beans about cryptids.
Zh: 不，你没有任何新的致胜策略能让这个女人泄露神秘动物的信息。

En: I suppose you could use a break, and I could use a distraction...
Zh: “我觉得你可以稍微休息一下，我也需要分散下注意力……”

En: Seems very convenient, that it just disappeared and nothing like it was seen again.
Zh: “这么说好像挺便利的，说它就这么消失了，以后再也看不见了之类的话。”

En: Just one -- or two -- more, Lena. Pretty please?
Zh: “再说一个——或者两个，莉娜。求你了？”

En: She smiles. "Well, the *biggest* cryptid is -- of course -- the horrible Giant of Koko Nur."
Zh: 她笑了。“呃，*最大*的神秘动物——当然了——是可怕的科科努尔巨人。”

En: But just one. I doubt your partner would appreciate it if I were to distract you from your duties for much longer!
Zh: “但是只说一个哦。如果我再耽误你的工作，我估计你的搭档会不太开心的。”

En: He nods approvingly.
Zh: 他点头赞同。

En: Ah, fuck it. Let's have more cryptids.
Zh: “啊，见鬼去吧。那就再多说点神秘动物。”

En: Alas! Always "alas, and then it was gone!" Isn't that overly convenient?
Zh: “呜呼哀哉！”总是这样“呜呼哀哉，然后它就不见了！”这种说辞不是太方便了吗？

En: The lieutenant pauses thoughtfully.
Zh: 警督停下来，若有所思。

En: She grins. "Can't say no to that. Well... the biggest cryptid is the Giant of Koko Nur."
Zh: 她咧嘴笑了。“这我可不能拒绝。好吧……最大的神秘动物是科科努尔巨人。”

En: I'm glad you see it that way. He nods approvingly. "Did we have anything more to do here?"
Zh: “很高兴你能这么看。”他赞同地点点头。“这里还有什么别的事要处理吗？”

En: You should totally use emotional blackmail. Because that *always* works on women.
Zh: 你完全可以利用情感绑架。因为这一套在女人身上*总是*很好用。

En: I helped you get your husband back. You *owe* me some sweet cryptid facts as a reward!
Zh: “我帮你把丈夫找回来了。你*欠*我一些有意思的神秘动物故事作为奖励！”

En: She was preparing for the end times. She wanted to witness and record the Twilight Proceedings.
Zh: “她正在为末日做准备。她想要亲眼见证和记录黄昏的进程。”

En: I'm very grateful to you, sweetie. But -- now that I think about it -- you should talk to Morell about cryptids. He could tell you so much more!
Zh: “我真的很感激你，甜心。但是——我仔细想了一下——你应该去找莫雷尔问神秘动物的事。他能跟你说的更多！”

En: Mhm, Mijanou did talk about the end of the world a great deal before her abrupt departure.
Zh: “嗯，米伽欧突然离开的时候，确实说了很多世界末日的话。”

En: The lieutenant shuts his eyes briefly...
Zh: 警督短暂地闭上了眼睛……

En: That's not how the world works. Let's get back to our investigation, shall we?
Zh: “世界不是这样运转的。我们还是继续回去调查吧，好吗？”

En: She turns to Morell. "Right, honey?"
Zh: 她转向莫雷尔。“对吧，宝贝？”

En: Your work must get quite frustrating. Well, okay, just one or two more... She smiles mischievously.
Zh: “你的工作肯定很让人沮丧。好吧，那就再讲一两个……”她调皮地笑了。

En: You're an enthusiastic idiot. But you're still an idiot.
Zh: 你是一个狂热的白痴。不过仍然是个白痴。

En: I want to know everything about cryptids. Living cryptids, extinct cryptids, marine cryptids, land cryptids. Bring it on!
Zh: “我想知道有关神秘动物的一切。活着的，灭绝的，水生的，陆地的。全都告诉我吧！”

En: The biggest cryptid is of course -- the Giant of Koko Nur. It's huge.
Zh: “最大的神秘动物当然是——科科努尔巨人，真的是个庞然大物。”

En: We don't have time for Cryptozoology 101. Let's get back to work, shall we?
Zh: “我们没时间搞什么神秘动物学101了。还是回去工作吧，好吗？”

En: But, but... this is so much more *interesting* than my real job.
Zh: “但是，但是……这比我真正的工作要*有意思*多了。”

En: She looks at you, her voice grave suddenly. "It still took the Gnome of Geroma an entire *day* to die."
Zh: 她看着你，声音突然严肃起来。“赫罗玛侏儒还是经过*一整天*才死去。”

En: I thought we'd agreed on just one cryptid, sweetie.
Zh: “我们不是约好只说一个神秘动物吗，甜心。”

En: Mijanou disappeared shortly after injecting herself with the bacteria she had brought back to study -- no doubt, in hopes of prolonging her *own* life.
Zh: “米伽欧给自己注射了带回来研究的细菌，不久之后就消失了——毫无疑问，她是希望能够延长*自己*的寿命。”

En: Sorry, sweetie. I can't get into that just now. Garte did say he could deliver a telegram for me earlier, and I should really notify our house sitter about our delay...
Zh: “抱歉，甜心。我现在还不能说太多。加尔特之前说可以帮我发一封电报，而且我真的应该通知看房人要延迟的事……”

En: Right, okay, we can move on for now. (It'd be dishonourable to renege on the promise.)
Zh: “好吧，现在我们可以继续了。”（违背诺言是可耻的。）

En: It was seen in South Safre during the war, visiting bunkers during the night and healing wounded soldiers with its saliva.
Zh: “战争时期有人在南萨弗里见过，它会在晚上前往碉堡，用自己的唾液治疗伤兵。”

En: It won't take long... what harm could hearing about one, just one *little* cryptid do?
Zh: “不用太久的……就听一个也不会怎么样吧，只要说一个*小*神秘动物就行？”

En: The lieutenant leans in. "Hey, you promised you'd only ask about one cryptid."
Zh: 警督靠近了一些。“喂，你答应只问一个神秘动物的。”

En: The lieutenant mutters something under his breath but nods for you to go ahead.
Zh: 警督低声嘀咕了几句，不过还是点头示意你继续。

En: But, Kim... Don't *you* want to hear about another cryptid too?
Zh: “但是，金……难道*你*不想再听一个神秘动物的故事吗？”

En: Your work must get quite frustrating. Well, okay, just one or two more... She grins.
Zh: “你的工作肯定很让人沮丧。好吧，那就再讲一两个……”她咧嘴笑了。

En: Okay, Kim. Just one little cryptid. Promise.
Zh: “好吧，金。就一个小神秘动物。我保证。”

En: The lieutenant leans in. "Hey, you promised you'd only ask about one cryptid."
Zh: 警督靠近了一些。“喂，你答应只问一个神秘动物的。”

En: But, Kim... Don't *you* want to hear about another cryptid too?
Zh: “但是，金……难道*你*不想再听一个神秘动物的故事吗？”

En: He nods and assumes a waiting posture.
Zh: 他点点头，摆出一个等待的姿势。

En: She smiles. "Well, the smallest cryptid is the *Cryobacter katlensis*, a unicellular bacterium. It was discovered in one of the northern-most points of Katla on the Boreal Plateau -- by renowned geologist Catelyn Mijanou some 70 years ago."
Zh: 她笑了。“好吧，最小的神秘动物是*冷凝细菌卡特兰西斯*，一种单细胞细菌。它是在卡特拉最北端的北风高原发现的——那是在大约70年前，由著名的地质学家凯特琳·米伽欧发现的。”

En: There's really no point in manipulating anyone. You're chummy. Just go on and ask!
Zh: 操纵任何人真的没有任何意义。你们这么合得来，只要开口问就行！

En: Hey, Lena, I'd really like to hear about some of those juicy *cryptids* you've studied. Could you tell me about a couple?
Zh: “喂，莉娜，我想听听你研究的那些*神秘动物*。能不能给我介绍一些呢？”

En: None of its victims survived. Grieving relatives never even found their bodies because the Gnome's venom dissolved organic tissue almost entirely.
Zh: “它的被害人没有一个活了下来。悲痛欲绝的亲属甚至没有找到他们的尸体，因为侏儒的毒液几乎完全溶解了器官组织。”

En: Sure, why not? He scowls.
Zh: 神秘动物学家怒视着你，不过很快恢复了镇定。“当然可以，为什么不呢？”

En: Right, okay, we can move on for now. (It'd be dishonourable to renege on the promise.)
Zh: “好吧，现在我们可以继续了。”（违背诺言是可耻的。）

En: Ah, fuck it. Let's have more cryptids.
Zh: “啊，见鬼去吧。那就再多说点神秘动物。”

En: The ape is very shy. It prefers observing human activity from afar and only approaches when it feels that it must intervene to save lives. No scientist has managed to track it down. Not even Morell. It's so mysterious -- I really can't tell you anything more about it.
Zh: “猿猴非常害羞。它更喜欢在远处观察人类的行动，只有在感到必须出手救命时才会靠近。没有科学家成功追踪到它。甚至莫雷尔也不行。它太神秘——我真的没办法说的更多了。”

En: She frowns. "Oh dear. Normally I would be happy to talk cryptids all day, but I'm just so frazzled right now, I couldn't tell a tadpole from a fingerling."
Zh: 她皱了皱眉。“亲爱的。一般来说我会很愿意说一整天的神秘动物，不过我现在真的太疲惫，连小鱼跟蝌蚪都分不清了。”

En: My husband did, but it's a shy creature and only shows itself to humans when it feels that it must invervene to save lives. No scientist has ever been able to track it down. It's so mysterious -- I really can't tell you anything more about it.
Zh: “我的丈夫有过，不过它是一种很害羞的生物，只要在感到必须介入拯救生命时才会靠近。没有科学家成功追踪到它。甚至莫雷尔也不行。它太神秘——我真的没办法说的更多了。”

En: He nods approvingly.
Zh: 他点头赞同。

En: Have you ever tried to find the kind green ape yourself?
Zh: “你有没有试过亲自寻找善良绿人猿？”

En: Cryptids, cryptids, let's hear about ALL the interesting cryptids!
Zh: 神秘动物，神秘动物，我们听听所有有趣的神秘动物吧！

En: Oh, I'd be delighted!
Zh: “哦，我很乐意！”

En: Why don't you have more information?
Zh: “你为什么没有更多信息呢？”

En: The smallest cryptid is the *Cryobacter katlensis*, a unicellular bacterium. It was discovered in one of the northern-most points of Katla on the Boreal Plateau -- by renowned geologist Catelyn Mijanou some 70 years ago.
Zh: 她笑了。“好吧，最小的神秘动物是*冷凝细菌卡特兰西斯*，一种单细胞细菌。它是在北方高原上卡特拉最北端的一个地方发现的——那是在大约70年前，由著名的地质学家凯特琳·米伽欧发现的。”

En: The lieutenant pauses thoughtfully.
Zh: 警督停下来，若有所思。

En: Is that a cryptid on this pen you gave me? (Take out the pen she gave you.)
Zh: “你给我的那只笔上是不是有一个神秘动物？”（拿出她给你的笔。）

En: Yes! It's the kind green ape. Half war story, half undiscovered species in the genus homo.
Zh: “是的！那是善良绿人猿。一半是战争故事，一半是未发现的人属物种。”

En: It's our closest relative among the cryptids. Same taxonomic family, different genus.
Zh: “它是神秘动物世界中与我们最接近的亲缘物。相同的分类家族，不同的类属。”

En: The lieutenant looks at you, pleasantly surprised.
Zh: 警督有些惊喜地看着你。

En: You mean... Kim isn't human? I knew it!
Zh: “你的意思是……金不是人类？我就知道！”

En: Everyone thought the bacteria had driven her mad. But she really was a brilliant woman -- maybe the Cryobacter katlensis allowed her to see something no one else could...
Zh: “大家都认为是细菌让她发疯的。不过她真是一个聪明的女人——也许冷凝细菌卡特兰西斯让她看见了其他人看不到的东西……”

En: I think there's a name for that -- racism.
Zh: “我觉得它有个名字——种族主义。”

En: And undoubtedly inferior to our species as well.
Zh: “而且毫无疑问是次于我们人类的。”

En: Just one or two more, Lena. Pretty please?
Zh: “再说一两个吧，莉娜。求你了？”

En: But, but... this is so much more *interesting* than my real job.
Zh: “但是，但是……这比我真正的工作要*有意思*多了。”

En: I'm pretty sure Kim is the same species as us... to suggest otherwise is stupid.
Zh: “我很肯定金跟我们是同一个物种……其他暗示实在是太愚蠢了。”

En: Well, the cryptid on your pen is the kind green ape.
Zh: “嗯，那只笔上的神秘动物是善良绿人猿。”

En: I knew it, Kim! You're not human!
Zh: “我就知道，金！你不是人类！”

En: I thought we'd agreed on just one cryptid, sweetie.
Zh: “我们不是约好只说一个神秘动物吗，甜心。”

En: Hah, that's why I always have to take the lead -- right, Kim?
Zh: “哈，这就是总要以我为首的原因——对吧，金？”

En: Just one or two more, Lena. Pretty please?
Zh: “再说一两个吧，莉娜。求你了？”

En: She grins. "Can't say no to that."
Zh: 她咧嘴一笑。“这可让人难以拒绝。”

En: He gives Lena a look communicating something between disgust and exasperation. "Ma'am, you're *confusing* him."
Zh: 他看莉娜的神情介于厌恶和恼怒之间。“夫人，你把他*搞糊涂*了。”

En: I don't understand. Why would you prolong your life? Being alive is terrible.
Zh: “我不理解。为什么要延长自己的生命呢？活着太可怕了。”

En: A couple of campers found it when it was already dying. They heard an odd wailing in the woods and followed the sound. They were scared and wrapped it in tarpaulin to suffocate it.
Zh: “几个露营者发现了奄奄一息的它。他们听见森林里传出一阵古怪的哭泣声，然后寻声而去。他们被吓到了，于是用防水油布把它裹了起来，打算让它窒息而亡。”

En: The lieutenant pauses thoughtfully.
Zh: 警督停下来，若有所思。

En: In everyday speech, *human* isn't really a scientific category. For all legal and ethical purposes, your partner is a person just like you, so you might as well *think* of him as being human, too.
Zh: “在日常用语中，*人类*并不是一个真正的科学范畴。基于所有法律和伦理的目的，你的搭档是跟你一样的人，所以你也可以*认为*他是人类。”

En: The cryptid on your pen is the kind green ape.
Zh: “笔上的神秘动物是善良绿人猿。”

En: I didn't mean to imply that Seolites are in any way inferior to us. In some ways, they are superior -- every species has its advantages and disadvantages.
Zh: “我并不是在暗示西奥人无论在哪方面都不如我们。在某些方面，他们更加优越——每个物种都有自己的优点和缺点。”

En: Oh, no, I didn't mean to imply that Seolites are inferior to us. In many ways, she turns to the lieutenant, "you are superior. For example, your earwax doesn't have a foul odour like ours does."
Zh: “哦，不是的，我并不是在暗示西奥人不如我们。在很多方面，”她转向警督。“你更加优越。比如说，你的耳垢不像我们的有一种恶臭。”

En: Please don't misunderstand me, either of you! I didn't mean to imply that Seolites are inferior to us. In many ways, she turns to the lieutenant, "you are superior. For example, your earwax doesn't have a foul odour like ours does."
Zh: “请不要误解我的意思，你们俩都是！我并不是在暗示西奥人不如我们。在很多方面，”她转向警督。“你更加优越。比如说，你的耳垢不像我们的有一种恶臭。”

En: A tremendous evolutionary advantage, I'm sure. But perhaps we've had enough speculative biology for today?
Zh: “我敢肯定，这是一个巨大的进化优势。不过也许今天推测生物学已经说的差不多了。”

En: Ah, fuck it. Let's have more cryptids.
Zh: “啊，见鬼去吧。那就再多说点神秘动物。”

En: Right, okay, we can move on for now. (It'd be dishonourable to renege on the promise.)
Zh: “好吧，现在我们可以继续了。”（违背诺言是可耻的。）

En: Which is to say, the kind green ape is a species with which we share a common ancestor and that evolved parallel to our own -- just like your partner's!
Zh: “也就是说，善良绿人猿是一种跟我们拥有共同祖先的物种，而且进化方式也跟我们类似——就像你的搭档一样！”

En: She smiles. "Well, the cryptid on your pen is the kind green ape. Half war story, half undiscovered species in the genus homo."
Zh: 她笑了。“嗯，那只笔上的神秘动物是善良绿人猿。一半是战争故事，一半是未发现的人属物种。”

En: But, Kim... Don't *you* want to hear about another cryptid too?
Zh: “但是，金……难道*你*不想再听一个神秘动物的故事吗？”

En: Please don't misunderstand me, either of you. *Human* as it is used in everyday speech is hardly a taxonomic category. For all intents and purposes, you can be thought of as human...
Zh: “请不要误解我的意思，你们俩都是！日常用语中的*人类*几乎不是一个分类范畴。总而言之，你可以被认为是一种人类……”

En: Your work must get quite frustrating. Well, okay, just one or two more... She grins.
Zh: “你的工作肯定很让人沮丧。好吧，那就再讲一两个……”她调皮地笑了。

En: She looks at the lieutenant, genially. "...though Seolites do have some distinguishing characteristics, different *advantages*, if you will."
Zh: 她和蔼地看着警督。“……不过西奥人确实拥有一些区别特征，不同的*优点*，如果你愿意那样说的话。”

En: The lieutenant leans in. "Hey, you promised you'd only ask about one cryptid."
Zh: 警督靠近了一些。“喂，你答应只问一个神秘动物的。”

En: Yes, advantages such as the flakier texture of Seolite earwax, or so I've heard. Nothing inspires pride in one's species quite like speculative evolutionary biology. Perhaps we've had enough of that for today?
Zh: “是的，优点——比如说西奥人的耳垢是片状质地的，不过我也是听说。没有什么比推测进化生物学更能激发一个物种的自豪感了。也许这个话题今天可以到此为止了？”

En: Everything has a price, sweetie.
Zh: “一切都要付出代价的，甜心。”

En: I thought we'd agreed on just one cryptid, sweetie.
Zh: “我们不是约好只说一个神秘动物吗，甜心。”

En: We can assume that she has been living somewhere in the wilderness for decades now, all alone except for the *Cryobacter katlensis* coursing through her bloodstream...
Zh: “我们可以假设现在她在某地的原野已经生活了数十年，孤身一人——除了在她血液里传播的*冷凝细菌卡特兰西斯*……”

En: What's the biggest cryptid?
Zh: “最大的神秘动物是什么？”

En: After Mijanou treated herself with the bacteria, she stopped ageing, but also became increasingly eccentric and irascible, so that even her oldest friends were forced to pull away...
Zh: “米伽欧用细菌治疗自己之后，她就停止老化了，不过也随之变得越来越反常和暴躁，甚至相处多年的老友也无奈地渐行渐远……”

En: What's the tiniest cryptid?
Zh: “最小的神秘动物是什么？”

En: But, but... this is so much more *interesting* than my real job.
Zh: “但是，但是……这比我真正的工作要*有意思*多了。”

En: What's the most dangerous cryptid?
Zh: “最危险的神秘动物是什么？”

En: Oh, sweetie, it's not all quite so awful. But prolonging one's life does seem to come with risks.
Zh: “哦，甜心，其实也没那么可怕。不过延长人类的寿命确实是伴随着风险的。”

En: It *was* reportedly a small creature -- with webbed fingers and a protruding forehead. An ungainly little thing. Quite scary to look at.
Zh: “根据*之前*的报道，那是一种小生物——拥有蹼状的手指和突出的前额。一种丑陋的小东西。看起来很是吓人。”

En: We can assume that she has been living somewhere in the wilderness for decades now, all alone except for the *Cryobacter katlensis* coursing through her bloodstream...
Zh: “我们可以假设现在她在某地的原野已经生活了数十年，孤身一人——除了在她血液里传播的*冷凝细菌卡特兰西斯*……”

En: Man, I just can't get enough of these cryptids.
Zh: “天啦，我真是听不够这些神秘动物的事。”

En: What did this cryptid look like?
Zh: “这个神秘动物长什么样？”

En: Yes, and she's quite mad too -- after she treated herself with the bacteria, she stopped ageing, but also became increasingly eccentric and irascible, so that even her oldest friends were forced to pull away...
Zh: “是的，而且她也很疯狂——在她用细菌治疗自己之后，她就停止老化了，不过也随之变得越来越反常和暴躁，甚至相处多年的老友也无奈地渐行渐远……”

En: Alas, the first scientist who got his hands on the creature's corpse put it in a jar of formaldehyde, thinking that would detoxify the Gnome's venom.
Zh: “唉，第一个弄到这种生物尸体的科学家把它放在了一罐甲醛里，觉得这样就能解除侏儒的剧毒。”

En: I intend to live forever too. As a symbol.
Zh: “我也想长生不老。作为一种象征。”

En: That would be the Giant of Koko Nur! she says as if it's common knowledge.
Zh: “那就是科科努尔巨人了！”她说话的口气就好像这是一种常识。

En: *Cryobacter katlensis*, she answers immediately.
Zh: “*冷凝细菌卡特兰西斯*，”她立即回答到。

En: The Gnome of Geroma. She pauses for effect.
Zh: “赫罗玛侏儒。”为了达到效果，她特意停顿了一下。

En: The bacterial colony Mijanou found had remained alive while frozen in ice for longer than anyone could reliably estimate -- certainly from before recorded history.
Zh: “米伽欧发现的菌落在冻结的情况下，存活的时间比任何人的可靠估算都要久——肯定在有记录的历史之前就存在。”

En: Oh.
The Giant lives in the most arid parts of the vast Koko Nur desert, in South Samara, casting a strange light across the barren wastes.
Zh: “巨人生活在广袤无垠的科科努尔沙漠的不毛之地，就在萨马拉南部，它发射出的奇怪光线穿越了整片荒原。”

En: No, I'm pretty sure Kim isn't human in any sense of the word.
Zh: “不，从任何意义上讲，我都肯定金不是人类。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: He doesn't seem to have any character flaws. No human being is that perfect.
Zh: “他好像没有任何性格缺陷。没有什么人类能像他一样完美。”

En: You mean, there is an immortal geologist wandering the world?
Zh: “你是说，有一个长生不老的地质学家在世界周游？”

En: He's so cool, completely out of this world.
Zh: “他太酷了，绝对地人间少有。”

En: People like him who stand out... they just don't belong here.
Zh: “像他这种拉风的男人……真的不属于这个地方。”

En: Hmm... She thinks. "Some argue that the kind green ape should also be thought of as human, especially because it has shown itself to be so *humane*. I don't dare form an opinion one way or the other without more information, though."
Zh: “嗯……”她沉思着，“有些人主张善良绿人猿应该也算人类，特别是它的表现特别*人道*。不过在没有更多信息的情况下，我也不敢轻易形成一种观点。”

En: But, but... this is so much more *interesting* than my real job.
Zh: “但是，但是……这比我真正的工作要*有意思*多了。”

En: Well, the most dangerous cryptid is thought to have been the Gnome of Geroma.
Zh: “大家认为最危险的神秘动物是赫罗玛侏儒。”

En: Some argue that the kind green ape should also be thought of as human, especially because it has shown itself to be so *humane*. I don't dare form an opinion one way or the other without more information, though.
Zh: “有些人主张善良绿人猿应该也算人类，特别是它的表现特别*人道*。不过在没有更多信息的情况下，我也不敢轻易形成一种观点。”

En: I thought we'd agreed on just one cryptid, sweetie.
Zh: “我们不是约好只说一个神秘动物吗，甜心。”

En: The kind green ape, too, is in some ways superior to us -- such kindness as it has demonstrated is not common among humans.
Zh: “善良绿人猿也是，在某些方面是比我们优越的——它展现出来的善良在人类之中也是不太常见的。”

En: Just one or two more, Lena. Pretty please?
Zh: “再说一两个吧，莉娜。求你了？”

En: She grins. "Can't say no to that."
Zh: 她咧嘴一笑。“这可让人难以拒绝。”

En: Granted, kindness may not be an evolutionary advantage...
Zh: “当然，善良可能不是一种进化上的优势……”

En: The lieutenant pauses thoughtfully.
Zh: 警督停下来，若有所思。

En: The most dangerous cryptid is thought to have been the Gnome of Geroma.
Zh: “大家认为最危险的神秘动物是赫罗玛侏儒。”

En: What's so special about it?
Zh: “它有什么特别的？”

En: And just how big is it?
Zh: “那它有多大呢？”

En: Is it dangerous?
Zh: “它危险吗？”

En: No one knows for sure. It is like an *awful* mountain appearing from below the horizon and expanding to cover almost a third of your field of vision.
Zh: “没人能肯定。它就像是出现在地平线下的一座*恐怖*高山，扩展起来几乎能掩盖住视野的三分之一。”

En: Hooey, the lieutenant interjects. "No animal can be that large. It's a mirage."
Zh: “胡言乱语，”警督突然插了一嘴。“没有那么大的动物。这只是妄想。”

En: The towering luminosity of Koko Nur is a bad omen in local folklore. Some say it's a fata morgana, others -- a fate unimaginable.
Zh: “科科努尔高耸入云的光辉在当地的民间传说中是一种不好的预兆。有人说那是海市蜃楼，其他人说——那是一种难以想象的宿命。”

En: That's what makes it so peculiar -- a species surviving at the very limits of scientific law. The Giant of Koko Nur must be the largest animal the planet can support.
Zh: “这就是它如此特别的原因——一个在科学定律的极限下生存的物种。科科努尔巨人一定是这个星球所能承受的最大的动物。”

En: He nods approvingly.
Zh: 他点头赞同。

En: Ah, fuck it. Let's have more cryptids.
Zh: “啊，见鬼去吧。那就再多说点神秘动物。”

En: Right, okay, we can move on for now. (It'd be dishonourable to renege on the promise.)
Zh: “好吧，现在我们可以继续了。”（违背诺言是可耻的。）

En: She smiles. "Well, the most dangerous cryptid is thought to have been the Gnome of Geroma."
Zh: 她笑了。“呃，大家认为最危险的神秘动物是赫罗玛侏儒。”

En: But, Kim... Don't *you* want to hear about another cryptid too?
Zh: “但是，金……难道*你*不想再听一个神秘动物的故事吗？”

En: Your work must get quite frustrating. Well, okay, just one or two more... She grins.
Zh: “你的工作肯定很让人沮丧。好吧，那就再讲一两个……”她调皮地笑了。

En: The lieutenant leans in. "Hey, you promised you'd only ask about *one* cryptid."
Zh: 警督靠近了一些。“喂，你答应只问一个神秘动物的。”

En: If the body of the creature was found, the lieutenant can't help himself, "why aren't there detailed illustrations of it in science textbooks? Confirming the existence of this very lethal species?"
Zh: “如果发现了这种生物的躯体……”警督情不自禁地说到。“……为什么科学课本里没有它的详细插图呢？证实这种致命物种的存在？”

En: Instead, all the venom leaked out of the creature's teeth and into the surrounding liquid, dissolving the creature itself. A poetic end, perhaps, but a real loss for science... she says, mostly to herself.
Zh: “相反，所有毒液都从这种生物的牙齿里渗出，然后流入周围的液体，把它自己也一并溶解了。也许这是一个充满诗意的结局，但是对于科学界来说是一个真正的损失……”她对自己说到。

En: Where could he be...
Zh: “他能去哪……”

En: I'm worried.
Zh: “我很担心。”

En: He should be back by now.
Zh: “他现在应该回来了。”

En: Just my luck, the phone line is not working...
Zh: “真倒霉，电话线坏了……”

En: You could totally sell this pin for cash.
Zh: 你完全可以卖掉这枚别针换现金。

En: Nothing, I was thinking of the lyrics to a song: *Wanna roll with me?*
Zh: “没什么，我只是想起了一句歌词：*想和我一起摇摆吗？*”

En: Ah, yes -- probably 'Roll With Me' by The Fletchers. People often quote the Fletchers at me. She settles down. "Morell says it's my theme song."
Zh: “啊，是的——可能是弗莱彻兄弟的‘一起摇摆’。大家经常在我面前提起弗莱彻兄弟。”她安下心来。“莫雷尔说这是我的主题曲。”

En: Don't worry. This is not permanent. You can always just pick up a pack *later*.
Zh: 别担心。这不是永久的。你*之后*还是可以再捡起来。

En: See -- I have no idea.
Zh: “看见没——我不知道。”

En: Revachol is the disgraced capital of the world. Something like that?
Zh: “瑞瓦肖是一个失宠的世界之都。差不多是这个意思吗？”

En: Yes, great, see -- we're getting somewhere. What else do you know about our city of splendour? Tattered as she may be...
Zh: “是的，很棒，看见了吗——-我们有点进展了。关于这座壮丽的城市，你还知道些什么呢？虽然她现在有些破败……”

En: Cop. We are living under the cop-regime.
Zh: “警察。我们生活在警察政权下。”

En: Sure. It's the year 973.
Zh: “当然。现在是973年。”

En: You're joking. Try again, one more time. What year is it?
Zh: “你是在开玩笑吧。再试试，再来一次。现在是什么年份？”

En: If there's no government, how come there are cops?
Zh: “如果没有政府，那为什么会有警察呢？”

En: I don't even know what to say. I'm so disappointed.
Zh: “我都不知道该说什么好了。我真的很失望。”

En: Some kind of democracy maybe?
Zh: “也许是某种民主政权？”

En: Actually... She raises her finger. "We are *not*. You could say that about almost any other nation, but not Revachol. Try *one* more time, officer -- what mode of government?"
Zh: “其实呢……”她竖起手指。“我们*不是*的。基本上你可以以此来形容其他任何国家，但瑞瓦肖不行。再试*一次*，警官——政治模式是怎样的？”

En: Nope. Sadly not. Revachol is what's called a Zone of Control. Under an alliance of foreign powers called the Coalition. We have no government of our own and what democracy we have is... She thinks. "Market driven."
Zh: “不是。可悲的是，并不是。瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支外国势力联合管辖。我们没有自己的政府，而我们拥有的民主是……”她想了想。“市场导向的。”

En: Damn it, I was really hoping it would be a Cop World. Okay, well, let's see...
Zh: 见鬼，我真心希望这是一个警察的世界。好吧，这样的话，让我们看看……

En: Meaning: buying is voting.
Zh: 意思是说：购买就是选举。

En: Revachol is a Zone of Control, led by an alliance of foreign powers called the Coalition. We have almost no government of our own. And *certainly* no dictatorship of the proletariat.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支被叫做联盟的外国势力联合管辖。我们基本上没有自己的政府。而且*肯定*也不是工人阶级专政。”

En: Oh... no, nothing like that, dear. Revachol is a Zone of Control, led by an alliance of foreign powers called the Coalition. We have almost no government of our own. And certainly no matriarchy...
Zh: “哦……不，不是这样的，亲爱的。瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支外国势力联合管辖。我们几乎没有自己的政府。而且肯定也不是母权制……”

En: Of course. You've arrived at a *weak* world, where no one appreciates animal husbandry.
Zh: 当然。你来到了一个*脆弱*的世界，这里没人欣赏畜牧业。

En: Oh... no, nothing like that, dear. Revachol is a Zone of Control, led by an alliance of foreign powers called the Coalition. We have almost no government of our own -- certainly no machines.
Zh: “哦……不，不是这样的，亲爱的。瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支外国势力联合管辖。我们几乎没有自己的政府——当然也没有国家机器。”

En: I don't know... still looks like there's a lot of hustling going on. Maybe she's wrong. Also...
Zh: 我不知道……看起来还是有很多欺诈行为在进行。也许她是错的。而且……

En: She's trying to avoid the foreigners part. This is because she's a woman. She may be *lying* to you...
Zh: 她想要回避外国人那部分。这是因为她是个女性。她也许在跟你*撒谎*……

En: But they still have cops...
Zh: 但是他们还有警察……

En: It is quite disappointing, yes. A lot of people would like some form of representation. There's *talk*, but... for now, the RCM is all we've got.
Zh: “是很让人失望，没错。很多人都会想要某种形式的代表。虽然有一些*传闻*，不过……目前我们只有RCM。”

En: Oh dear, this *is* troubling. You really ought to know that -- being one yourself. There aren't any cops in Revachol, not in the traditional sense. The status of law enforcement has been a complicated matter since the Revolution...
Zh: “哦，亲爱的，这*的确*很令人不安。你真的应该知道这一点——因为你自己就是其中一员。瑞瓦肖并没有传统意义上的警察。大革命之后，执法机关的地位一直是个很复杂的问题……”

En: Oh dear... She shakes her head, suddenly very worried. "And you were doing so well. There aren't any cops in Revachol, not in the traditional sense. The status of law enforcement has been a complicated matter since the Revolution..."
Zh: “亲爱的……”她摇摇头，突然担心起来。“你做的真的很好。瑞瓦肖并没有传统意义上的警察。大革命之后，执法机关的地位一直是个很复杂的问题……”

En: But we should stop for today, sweetie. You look like you need a break. Besides, I'm not the best person to explain the *big* things to anyone...
Zh: “不过今天还是就说到这里吧，甜心。你看起来需要稍微休息一下。而且，我也不是最适合解释这种*大事*的人……”

En: So -- how did I do?
Zh: “所以——我表现的怎么样？”

En: What is the *Revolution* you mentioned?
Zh: “你提到的这个*大革命*是什么？”

En: Who could tell me more? (Conclude.)
Zh: “谁能告诉我更多信息呢？”（总结。）

En: You were doing quite well up until the end there. It *does* look like you're having trouble remembering things. History and places. Remembering *Reality*, in a word. It's very odd...
Zh: “你从头到尾表现得都很好。不过看起来你*确实*在记忆方面遇到了一点问题。历史和地点什么的。总而言之，就是记不起*现实*。这一点非常奇怪……”

En: You didn't do too well, dear. You really only knew what year it is. It does look like you're having trouble remembering things -- history and places. *Reality*, in a word. it's very odd...
Zh: “你做的不是很好，亲爱的。你真的只记得现在是什么年份。看起来你确实在记忆方面遇到了一点问题——历史和地点什么的。总而言之，就是记不起*现实*。这一点非常奇怪……”

En: You didn't do too well, dear. It does look like you're having trouble remembering things. History and places. Remembering *Reality*, in a word. It's very odd...
Zh: “你表现的不是很好，亲爱的。看起来你确实在记忆方面遇到了一点问题。历史和地点什么的。总而言之，就是记不起*现实*。这一点非常奇怪……”

En: A sigh. The lieutenant buries his nose in his notebook.
Zh: 一声叹气。警督把鼻子埋进了笔记本。

En: But -- maybe a *fresh set of eyes* is what the world needs? And -- while I'm no doctor -- such bouts of amnesia are often temporary. So I wouldn't worry *too* much.
Zh: “但是——也许*一双新鲜的眼睛*才是这个世界需要的？而且——我也不是医生——这种失忆症发作经常是暂时性的。所以我并没有*过于*担心。”

En: She's scared now. She's realized you really are brain damaged.
Zh: 现在她害怕了。她意识到你的大脑真的受损了。

En: She means this sincerely. Worrying won't do you any good.
Zh: 她是真心实意的。担心对你来说也没什么用。

En: A defeat, I'm afraid. The people of this archipelago tried to build something new, something *different*. The rest of the world didn't like it, so they came and ended it. This was forty two years ago.
Zh: “恐怕是一次失败。这个群岛的居民试图建立一些新的、*不太一样*的东西。但是世界上的其他人不太喜欢，所以他们来到这里，结束了一切。那已经是42年前的事了。”

En: What does that have to do with there not being any cops?
Zh: “那这跟没警察又有什么关系呢？”

En: It has something to do with everything. I really don't know how to explain it better...
Zh: “这跟一切都有所关联。我真的不知道怎么才能解释地更清楚……”

En: Someone more educated in *sweeping* matters? Maybe you should ask... She turns to the lieutenant.
Zh: “某个在*整体*上受过更多教育的人？也许你应该问问……”她转向警督。

En: No. He looks away. "I'm not an encyclopedia. I won't be a guide either. I'm a detective."
Zh: “不。”他移开目光。“我不是一部百科全书，我也不会去当向导。我是一个警探。”

En: Of course. She turns to you. "Then I don't know. Someone rich maybe? Wealthy people are educated. Though I don't know where you would find a wealthy person in Martinaise..."
Zh: “当然。”她转向你。“那我就不知道了。也许找个有钱人问？有钱人都受过良好教育。虽然不知道你能不能在马丁内斯找到有钱人……”

En: Someone more educated in these things than me? Someone rich, maybe? Wealthy people are educated. Though I don't know where you would find a wealthy person in Martinaise...
Zh: “在这方面比我受过更多教育的人？也许找个有钱人问？有钱人都受过良好教育。虽然不知道你能不能在马丁内斯找到有钱人……”

En: Oh, I'm sure you would make a wonderful *gigolo* dear -- with those strong arms of yours. But welfare checks aren't all they're cracked up to be. My husband and I are barely scraping by.
Zh: “哦，我肯定你能成为一个很棒的*小白脸*，亲爱的——看看你那双强壮的臂膀就知道了。不过福利支票并没有大家想象中那么好。我的丈夫和我只能够勉强度日。”

En: She's not really lusting after those biceps, man. It's more like an appraisal you give to a healthy *specimen*.
Zh: 她并不是真的很渴望那些肱二头肌，老兄。这更像是对一个健康*样本*做出的评估。

En: Oh no... I'm so sorry I don't have money for you. If there's *anything* else I can do for you, just ask.
Zh: “哦不……很抱歉我没有钱能给你。如果还有*其他事*能帮你的话，你尽管开口。”

En: Already did that. You were too *proud* to take the check.
Zh: 已经这么做过了。你太过*骄傲*，不愿意收下那张支票。

En: Already did that. Evrart's money wasn't gonna last forever. You need more. Every day is a new expense!
Zh: 已经这么做过了。艾弗拉特的钱不会永远花下去。你需要更多钱。每天都会有一笔新开支！

En: Interesting idea. This *Evrart* sounds powerful. Maybe you can wrangle some coins out of his pocket?
Zh: 这是个很有趣的主意。这个*艾弗拉特*听起来很有权利的样子。也许你可以从他的口袋里弄出点钱来？

En: Good idea. Maybe you were too quick to leave -- he would have given you some if you sat there longer?
Zh: 好主意。也许你走得太快了——如果你在那里多坐一会儿，他可能会给你一点钱？

En: The '33 races, on the other hand, were plagued by controversy over performance enhancers and very cold rain. After two local champions were stripped of their titles, the city decided not to hold the event again.
Zh: 另一方面呢，33年那场竞赛，一直被思必得的争议和寒冷的冰雨困扰着。在两位本地冠军的头衔被剥夺之后，城市决定将活动永久停办。

En: Wonderful. The lieutenant marks drolly. "He has a pin."
Zh: “太棒了。”警督幽默地评论到。“他有别针了。”

En: Fucking shit. It's all over for me.
Zh: “全都是屁话。对我来说，一切都已经结束了。”

En: Of course. I shouldn't have asked you. STUPID me.
Zh: “当然。我不应该问你的。我太蠢了。”

En: Items that can be pawned at Bird's Nest Roy's appear in your INVENTORY, under the ITEMS tab. You can pawn these items when talking to Roy.
Zh: 能在鸟巢罗伊那里典当的物品会出现在道具栏里，道具的标签下。跟罗伊说话的时候，你可以典当这些物品。

En: Perhaps another time? She turns back to you with a hopeful glint in her eyes.
Zh: 她转回头看着你，眼睛里闪烁着希望的光芒：“也许下次吧？”

En: Mhm, okay.
Zh: “嗯，好吧。”

En: Yes -- and Revachol...? She examines you.
Zh: “是的——那瑞瓦肖……？”她打量着你。

En: I'm just a poor woman, she thinks. What do I know of these things -- and how can I help you?
Zh: 我只是个贫苦的女人，她想到。我怎么知道这些事呢——我该怎么帮你呢？

En: Oh my... It takes a moment to process. "You know where we *are*, right?"
Zh: “天……”她花了一点时间来消化。“你知道我们*在哪里*，对吧？”

En: That's a good point. This doesn't look like Rich Central.
Zh: 说得好。这里看起来并不像有钱的中心区。

En: But *I'm* a cop. Whatever it was hasn't stopped me!
Zh: “但是*我*是一个警察。不管什么都阻止不了我！”

En: Of course, sweetie, I... I really don't know how to explain it better.
Zh: “当然，甜心，我……我真的不知道怎么能解释地更清楚。”

En: So you volunteered, sometime in the past? For the beatdown and the hail of bullets? So very you...
Zh: 所以你是自愿的，是过去发生的事吗？为了镇压和枪林弹雨？还真像你呢……

En: I got 3.20 for it. You should have it. (Give her the money.)
Zh: “我当掉了3.20雷亚尔。应该交给你。”（把钱给她。）

En: I bought it back. I know it means a lot to you. You should have it. (Give her the pin.)
Zh: “我把它买回来了。我知道它对你来说意义非凡。你应该拿着它。”（把别针交给她。）

En: Aw, thank you, dear. I confess -- I am glad to see it again. Very honourable of you, officer.
Zh: “啊，谢谢你。亲爱的。我承认——还能再看到它我真的很高兴。你真是个有荣誉感的人，警官。”

En: She really would have rather had the pin back, but she doesn't want to make you feel bad.
Zh: 她其实更愿意把别针拿回来，不过她不想让你感觉不好。

En: Oh, that's very thoughtful of you. Are you sure you don't need the money? Okay, then. Thank you.
Zh: “哦，你真是太体贴了。你确定不需要钱了吗？那好吧。谢谢你。”

En: Even the lieutenant seems happy with this turn of events.
Zh: 就连警督似乎也对形势的变化感到高兴。

En: Of course, dear! I just wish I could have done more.
Zh: “当然了，亲爱的！要是能为你多做一些就好了。”

En: I just wanted to thank you again.
Zh: “我只想再次感谢你。”

En: Now, what else, sweetie?
Zh: “现在，还有别的事吗，甜心？”

En: I decided not to pawn it. You should have it back. (Give her the pin.)
Zh: “我决定不当了。你应该收回去。”（把别针给她。）

En: Aw, thank you, dear. I confess -- I am glad to see it again.
Zh: “啊，谢谢你。亲爱的。我承认——还能再看到它我真的很高兴。”

En: Yes, we've been there, the lieutenant nods.
Zh: “是啊，我们去过那里，”警督点点头。

En: You've been there, remember? The little shack right by the canal.
Zh: 你去过那里，还记得吗？就是运河边的那个小棚屋。

En: Well... I'm not sure, detective. I'm sorry. Wealthy people tend to be educated, but you're not likely to find one of those in Martinaise...
Zh: “这个……我也不确定，警探。很抱歉。一般有钱人都受到了良好的教育，不过你也不太可能在马丁内斯找到这种人……”

En: She is appraising you scientifically, the way she would an exceptional zoological specimen. It's not *like that*.
Zh: 她正在以科学家的眼光审视你，就像是在检查一个异常的动物标本。不是*那样*的。

En: I've gotta get going now. [Leave.]
Zh: “我得走了。”[离开。]

En: You seem to be in a chair.
Zh: “你好像坐在轮椅上。”

En: Are you okay? I am very sorry I crashed into you earlier. I don't know what got into me.
Zh: “你还好吗？很抱歉我刚才撞到你了。我不知道我是怎么了。”

En: How'd you like to roll with me?
Zh: “愿意跟我一起走走吗？”

En: Sweetie needs money. Do sweeties get money?
Zh: “甜心需要钱。甜心有钱拿吗？”

En: Can you tell me about this *reality* again?
Zh: “你能再跟我说说这个*现实*吗？”

En: I don't know if you've noticed, but I don't know where I am, or what I'm doing. Or *anything*.
Zh: “我不知道你有没有注意到，不过我不知道自己在哪，也不知道自己在做什么。或者*任何事*。”

En: Oh sweetie. Her expression becomes very serious. "I heard your conversation with the manager about your... financial troubles. When do you get your next paycheck?"
Zh: 她的表情变得严肃起来。“哦，甜心，我听到你跟经理讨论你的……财务问题了。你下次拿工资支票是什么时候？”

En: You've got guns of steel and a chiselled jaw (somewhere under the bloated alcohol layer). Maybe she'd like to sponsor you?
Zh: 你有强壮的胳膊轮廓分明的下巴（就在因宿醉而浮肿的皮肤下方）也许她可以赞助你？

En: Yes, dear, I am a paraplegic.
Zh: “是的，亲爱的，我是个截瘫病人。”

En: What did you do, then?
Zh: “那你是做什么的呢？”

En: A paraplegic is someone with limited or no ability to use the lower half of their body. Paraplegia is caused by spinal cord injuries -- like falling from a great height, or a grenade explosion!
Zh: 截瘫病人是指只能有限使用或者完无法全使用下半身的人。截瘫是由脊髓损伤引起的——比如说从高处坠落，或者是遇到手榴弹爆炸！

En: What is a paraplegic?
Zh: “截瘫是什么？”

En: I'm sorry, it was rude of me to mention the wheelchair. Let's move on. (Proceed.)
Zh: “很抱歉，我提轮椅的事真的很不礼貌。我们继续吧。”（继续。）

En: That's quite alright. I'm used to people asking questions. I know they're thinking about it anyway.
Zh: “没事的。我已经习惯别人问问题了。反正我也知道他们会去想这个问题。”

En: There is no bitterness in her voice. She accepted the curiosity her condition inspires a long time ago.
Zh: 她的声音里没有苦涩的滋味。她很早以前就接受自己的状况激起的好奇心了。

En: No, dear, I'm not quite *that* old, although I was injured in the line of duty.
Zh: “不，亲爱的，我并没有*那么*老，不过我是在执勤的时候受伤了。”

En: It means I can't use my legs. I was in an accident some years back, injured *in the line of duty* as they say.
Zh: “意思是说我没办法使用自己的双腿。几年前我遭遇了一起事故，就像他们说的，在*执行任务*的时候受伤。”

En: Were you a... tightrope walker?
Zh: “你是一个……表演走钢丝的？”

En: Were you a... mountaineer?
Zh: “你是一个……登山家吗？”

En: Nothing so glamorous, dear, though when I was young, I dreamt of planting the Revacholian flag on some figurative peak.
Zh: “我没那么有魅力，亲爱的，不过我年轻的时候，曾经梦想要把瑞瓦肖的旗帜插在某个象征性的高峰上。”

En: Her eyes light up. "Whatever do you mean?"
Zh: 她的眼睛亮了起来。“你是什么意思？”

En: Roll us some cigarettes. I'm jonesing for a smoke.
Zh: “我们卷点烟抽吧。我很想抽支烟。”

En: Oh, I'm perfectly alright! I'm more worried about you. What was that?
Zh: “哦，我完全没事！我更担心你。刚才怎么回事？”

En: The 'Whirling-in-Rags' cafeteria? It was on my keys.
Zh: “‘褴褛飞旋’餐厅？它的名字写在我的钥匙上。”

En: We're in Revachol.
Zh: “我们在瑞瓦肖。”

En: A war zone. At the edge of the world.
Zh: “一个战区。世界的边缘。”

En: I don't really know... some seedy hotel?
Zh: “我真的不知道……某个破烂的旅社？”

En: That's right, and where is the Whirling-in-Rags cafeteria itself located?
Zh: “没错，那褴褛飞旋餐厅本身又在哪里呢？”

En: We're in the city of *Revachol*, dear. She flashes you a worried look.
Zh: 她露出一个担忧的神情。“我们一个叫瑞瓦肖的城市，亲爱的。”

En: That's right! In a hostel called the Whirling-in-Rags, to be precise.
Zh: “没错！准确的说，是在一个叫褴褛飞旋的旅社。”

En: It does still look like that in some parts of the city, doesn't it...
Zh: “在这座城市的某些地方，看起来还是那样，不是吗……”

En: We are in a hostel called the Whirling-in-Rags, dear. And the Whirling itself is in the city of Revachol.
Zh: “我们在一个叫褴褛飞旋的旅社里，亲爱的。而飞旋旅社本身在瑞瓦肖城。”

En: A seaside hostel called the Whirling-in-Rags, to be precise. And the Whirling itself is in Revachol.
Zh: “一个叫褴褛飞旋的海边旅社。而飞旋旅社本身在瑞瓦肖城。”

En: Speaking of history -- you know what *year* it is, yes?
Zh: 她停下来，研究着你的表情。你看起来一定很迷惘。“说到历史——你知道现在是*哪一年*，对吧？”

En: That tape you picked up said '43. You seem to be a hip modern man who listens to hip modern records, so it must be '43.
Zh: 你捡到的那盘磁带上写着43年。你好像是个喜欢听新潮唱片的新潮男人，所以一定是43年。

En: It's totally '43.
Zh: “绝对是43年。”

En: It's the spring of '51.
Zh: “是51年的春天。”

En: All I know is: we are approaching the *end times*.
Zh: “我只知道：*末日*就要降临。”

En: I can tell that this is taxing for you, so I'll just ask *one* more question. What regime are we living under? What mode of government?
Zh: “我能看出这对你来说很费力，所以我只再问你*一个*问题。我们生活在怎样的政体下？政治模式是怎样的？”

En: I'd *like* to think it's the dictatorship of the proletariat, but something tells me it's not.
Zh: “我*愿意*认为现在是工人阶级专政，但是有什么东西告诉我不是的。”

En: Could I become a paraplegic?
Zh: “我也能成为截瘫病人吗？”

En: Nothing so extravagant, dear, though what young girl doesn't dream of soaring over a crowd?
Zh: “没什么会比它奢侈了，亲爱的，哪个年轻女孩没有梦想过在人群中翱翔呢？”

En: I was a training and development manager at a rapidly expanding mail-order shoe company.
Zh: “我是一个迅速扩张的邮购鞋业公司的培训和开发经理。”

En: You'd think it would be a safe job. But I had to be everywhere -- and, well, once I happened to be under some faulty scaffolding.
Zh: “你会觉得这是一个很安全的工作。不过我必须去所有地方——而且，有一次我正好站在了一个有缺陷的脚手架下面。”

En: I was lucky. This was almost twenty years ago, and I was compensated exceptionally well. One can only dream of such pay-offs nowadays. She runs her hands over the chrome wheels of her chair.
Zh: “我很幸运。差不多已经是20年前的事了，我得到了很好的补偿。现如今，大家只有在梦里才能拿到这样的收益了。”她的手在轮椅的铬合金轮子上滑动。

En: I hope not, although the work you are doing for all of us *is* quite dangerous. Her eyes glimmer with admiration as she looks up at you.
Zh: “我希望不是的，虽然你为我们所做的这一切工作*的确*非常危险。”她看着你，眼神里流露出赞赏的目光。

En: Towering over her in your insignia, you make her feel safe.
Zh: 你身戴警徽姿态让她感觉十分安全。

En: (Nod grimly.) "Someone *has* to do it."
Zh: （严肃地点点头。）“*必须*有人这么做。”

En: But not you. You're volunteering. So thank you, sweetie.
Zh: “但不是你。你是自愿的。所以谢谢你，甜心。”

En: Maybe it wouldn't be so bad, hanging out in a chair, playing pinball...
Zh: “也许并没有那么糟糕，坐在轮椅上闲逛，玩玩弹球……”

En: Actually, this machine is broken. She taps on the greasy glass of the pinball machine.
Zh: 她拍了拍弹球机上油腻的玻璃。“其实，这台机器坏了。”

En: I'm not sure I should be in this line of work.
Zh: “我不确定自己是否应该做这一行。”

En: You're a man at the peak of your physical and intellectual powers. You will find another calling yet, I'm certain of it!
Zh: “你是一个正处在身体和才智巅峰状态的男人。你的事业会重返巅峰的，我很肯定！”

En: Thanks for clearing that up for me. Let's move on. (Proceed.)
Zh: “感谢你帮我厘清这些事。我们继续吧。”（继续。）

En: Sequence killers, oh my... She sounds impressed. "But I think you already have a partner, sweetie."
Zh: “连环杀人犯，天啦……”她好像很震惊的样子。“但是我觉得你已经有一个搭档了，甜心。”

En: I want you to be my wheel-chaired partner in fighting crime, ridding backyards of corpses, catching *sequence killers*.
Zh: “我想让你成为我的轮椅搭档，打击犯罪，清理后院的尸体，抓住*连环杀人犯*。”

En: I want you to be my wheel-chaired partner in fighting crime, helping people, catching *sequence killers*.
Zh: “我想让你成为我的轮椅搭档，打击犯罪，帮助百姓，抓住*连环杀人犯*。”

En: A partner who needs you to help him get a corpse down from a tree.
Zh: “一个需要你帮他把尸体从树上放下来的搭档。”

En: A partner who needs you to get back to helping the people of Martinaise.
Zh: “一个需要你回去帮助马丁内斯人民的搭档。”

En: Her face flushes with excitement, but she instantly recomposes herself. "I wouldn't be of much use to you, sweetie."
Zh: 她的脸因为兴奋而变得通红，不过她很快让自己恢复了镇定。“我对你来说可没多大帮助，甜心。”

En: Thank you, but... She looks out the window wistfully. "Martinaise isn't the most wheelchair-accessible place, you see. I'd slow you down."
Zh: “谢谢你，但是……”她渴望地看着窗外。“你也看到了，马丁内斯不是最适合轮椅出入的地方。我会拖后腿。”

En: Why? Three heads are better than two.
Zh: “为什么？三个脑袋总比两个好吧。”

En: You're probably right.
Zh: “也许你是对的。”

En: I really would like to help out, but I don't want to slow you down. If it weren't for all the stairs and potholes in Martinaise, I'd join you for sure! She looks out the window wistfully.
Zh: 她渴望地看着窗外。“我真的很想帮忙，但是我不想拖后腿。如果马丁内斯没有那么多台阶和坑洞，我肯定会加入你的！”

En: I know, I know. But there are also *side mysteries*. Sequence killers and forays into the para-natural.
Zh: “我知道，我知道。不过还有一些*其他的秘密*。连环杀手，而且还涉及到超自然。”

En: You're no fun, Lieutenant Killjoy.
Zh: “你这人一点意思都没有，煞风景警督。”

En: Now, gentlemen, no need to squabble. She turns to you. "I wouldn't be of much use to you anyway, sweetie."
Zh: “好了，先生们，没必要争吵。”她转向你。“而且我对你来说也没多大用场，甜心。”

En: Oh, no-no-no, I haven't smoked in years...
Zh: “哦，不不不，我已经几年没抽烟了……”

En: Does that mean you don't have tobacco? Or rolling paper? Or any cigarettes at all?
Zh: “这是不是意味着你连烟草都没有？或者是卷烟纸？或者一点香烟都没有吗？”

En: Why don't you smoke? It's great!
Zh: “你为什么不抽烟呢？抽烟多好啊！”

En: Good for you. Smoking is a stupid habit. Maybe I should quit too.
Zh: “对你挺好的。抽烟是一种愚蠢的习惯。也许我也应该戒掉。”

En: She shakes her head slowly.
Zh: 她慢慢摇摇头。

En: Wait! Maybe she knows where you can get cigarettes. Delicious toasty cigarettes stuffed to the tip with *love*. You should find out.
Zh: 等等！也许她知道你可以去哪里弄到香烟。像烤烟叶塞到烟嘴的那种美味又充满*爱意*的香烟。你应该搞明白。

En: She looks embarrassed. "Cigarettes are a luxury, sweetie. Besides, I have to mind my health."
Zh: 她看起来有些尴尬。“香烟是一种奢侈品，甜心。再说了，我要注意自己的健康。”

En: What are you talking about? Smoking is one of the last remaining pleasures in this world. Trust me, there's not much out there for you.
Zh: 你在说什么呢？抽烟是这个世界上最后仅存的乐趣之一。相信我，你已经没有多少选择了。

En: Can you tell me where to get cigarettes?
Zh: “可以告诉我能去哪里弄到烟吗？”

En: I thought you were cool, with your wheels and all.
Zh: “我觉得你挺酷的，这个轮椅啊什么的。”

En: It's probably for the best. I don't really need a cigarette right this minute.
Zh: “这样也许最好。我现在也不是很需要香烟。”

En: You would do well to follow her example. Smoking is the reason you feel like a rag. It's painful for you to even ask these questions.
Zh: 以她为榜样会很不错。抽烟是你觉得自己像个废物的原因，甚至开口问这些问题都会让你感到痛苦。

En: An old woman in a wheelchair just doesn't look good holding a cigarette. Though I'm sure it lends *you* a roguish charm, sweetie.
Zh: 她耸耸肩，低下头。“一个坐着轮椅的老女人叼着一根烟的样子可不太好看。虽然我肯定这样会为*你*增添一丝淘气的魅力，甜心。”

En: I'm glad to hear you say it! Smoking can do some real damage to your respiratory system. But I think you already knew that.
Zh: “很高兴听到你这么说！抽烟会给你的呼吸系统造成真正的伤害。不过我觉得你已经知道这一点了。”

En: You should really ask someone younger and more mobile, sweetie.
Zh: “你真应该去找个年轻点，腿脚更方便的人问问，甜心。”

En: Yes, officer, you look rather dazed... like a stunned fox. But surely things can't be *that* bad? Her eyes follow your movements with some concern.
Zh: 她的眼睛关切地注视着你的一举一动。“是的，警官，你看起来很茫然……像一只晕了头的狐狸。但是我肯定事情没有*那么*糟糕吧？”

En: I was hoping you could be my *sponsor*.
Zh: “我希望你可以做我的*赞助者*。”

En: What is a pay check? I haven't seen any pay checks...
Zh: “工作支票是什么？我还从来没有见过呢……”

En: It isn't easy for me to ask this of anyone, but I am at the end of my rope. Any money you have could help me.
Zh: “对于我来说向任何人开这个口都不容易，但我实在是山穷水尽了。随便来点钱都能帮到我。”

En: You must be joking. He pauses, reflecting. "Although our pay does sometimes feel like a joke."
Zh: “你一定是在开玩笑吧。”他停顿了一下，沉思着。“不过有时候我们的薪水确实像个笑话。”

En: Officer. He leans in so that only you can hear, but his tone is forceful. "Get a hold of yourself, please."
Zh: “警官。”他靠近了一些，只想你一个人听见，不过口吻十分坚定。“请控制一下自己。”

En: Sweetie, I only have a few cents on me. But... I could give you this to pawn, just to tide you over. She unfastens a gold commemorative pin from her left jacket pocket and drops it into your palm.
Zh: “甜心，我身上只有几分钱。不过……”她从左边的夹克口袋中取下一枚金色的纪念别针，然后放进你的掌心。“你可以把它拿去的当了，为了帮你渡过难关。”

En: If she could, this woman would feed and clothe you and every other sad, lost person on this earth.
Zh: 如果她可以，这个女人会给你，还有这个世界上所有悲伤又迷失的人提供食物和衣服。

En: Okay then. (Proceed with a gulp) "Let's change the subject."
Zh: “那好吧。”（咽了一下口水。）“我们换个话题。”

En: Right. Let's change the subject.
Zh: “好吧。我们换个话题。”

En: It's a bad year in my late forties, or fifties? I don't even know how *old* I am.
Zh: “在我40多岁，或者50多岁里糟糕的一年？我甚至都不知道自己*多大*。”

En: Oh no, dear! It's the early spring of '51.
Zh: “哦不，亲爱的！现在是51年的早春。”

En: Perhaps, dear, perhaps. But for now it's just the spring of '51.
Zh: “可能吧，亲爱的，可能。不过现在是51年的春天。”

En: There, there. The year is '51, and spring has only just started. I'm sure there are better days ahead.
Zh: “好了，好了。今年是51年，春天刚刚开始。我肯定好日子就在前面。”

En: Outside, the melting snow seeps into the cracks in the walls and the cobblestone streets. All the way down into the sewers... Above ground, the first May bells blossom. You can feel it. A great cold. Then the shiver passes.
Zh: 屋外，融化的白雪渗入墙上的裂缝和街道上的鹅卵石中。一路流向下水道……地面上，第一朵铃兰正绽放开来。你能感觉到，一阵巨大的寒意游过全身。

En: We're dead. Haunting each other. We're ghosts.
Zh: “我们已经死了。彼此纠缠。我们是幽灵。”

En: Now, now. She tilts her head as she looks up at you with maternal solicitude.
Zh: “喂，哎呀。”她歪着脑袋，以慈母般的关怀抬头看着你。

En: The edge of civilization.
Zh: “文明的边缘。”

En: On something called *the urban coast*.
Zh: “在一个叫*城市海岸*的地方。”

En: In... Revachol?
Zh: “在……瑞瓦肖？”

En: In hell. We're all in hell.
Zh: “在地狱里。我们全都在地狱里。”

En: We are alive -- in a hostel called the Whirling-in-Rags. And the Whirling itself is in the city of Revachol.
Zh: “我们还活着——在一个叫褴褛飞旋的旅社里。而飞旋旅社本身在瑞瓦肖城。”

En: Honestly, I don't know diddly-squat about Revachol. What kind of place is this?
Zh: “老实说，我完全不了解瑞瓦肖。这是个什么样的地方？”

En: All I know is that Revachol used to be really cool in the Thirties.
Zh: “我只知道瑞瓦肖在30年代的时候真的挺酷的。”

En: Yes, we're in Martinaise, on the coast of Revachol.
Zh: “是的，我们在马丁内斯，瑞瓦肖的海岸上。”

En: Yes, indeed. We are in the fine city of Revachol.
Zh: “是的，的确。我们在一个叫瑞瓦肖的美丽城市里。”

En: I know it's the '50s, but I'm not sure about the year.
Zh: “我知道是50年代，不过不确定是哪一年。”

En: The New was in the Thirties, and two decades have elapsed since The New, so it must be the Fifties now.
Zh: 新时代是指30年代，在那之后已经过去了20年，所以现在一定是在50年代。

En: Good, dear, you got the decade right! It's the spring of '51.
Zh: “很好，亲爱的，你把年代说对了！现在是51年的春天。”

En: I can assure you, with absolute certainty: there are no sequence killings taking place in Martinaise.
Zh: “我可以确定一定以及肯定地向你保证：马丁内斯没有发生连环杀人案。”

En: He scans your face as though searching for clues. "I did not know murder investigations are supposed to be *fun*."
Zh: 他扫视着你的脸，好像在搜寻线索：“我怎么就不知道凶杀案调查应该是*有意思*的呢。”

En: The New! Disco dancing! It seems like a lifetime ago... Her eyes are filled with light reflected off those gilded years, when the city seemed full of possibility.
Zh: “新时代！迪斯科舞！感觉已经是上辈子的事情了……”她的眼睛里充满那些黄金岁月折射出来的光彩，那时候的城市似乎充满更多可能。

En: How would I even begin to tell you? Her grey eyes widen. "Revachol is the most beautiful city in the world. We're fortunate to be here, you and I."
Zh: 她灰色的眼睛睁大了。“我又该从何跟你说起呢？瑞瓦肖是这个世界上最美丽的城市。能在这里是我们的幸运，你和我都一样。”

En: I haven't seen very many other cities personally, but everyone says so. Revachol is a rare jewel. This city used to rule the world... Though it has seen better days. There's a pause as she studies your expression. You must look quite lost.
Zh: “我没有亲眼见过很多其他城市，不过大家都是这么说的。瑞瓦肖是一颗珍贵的宝石。这个城市曾经统治着全世界……尽管它曾经见识过更好的岁月。”

En: Much has changed, but it is still the most beautiful city in the world, a rare jewel set in the sand between the pines. Everyone says so, even foreigners. There's a pause as she studies your expression. You must look quite lost.
Zh: “很多都变了，不过它依然是这个世界上最美丽的城市，一颗镶嵌在群松之间沙地上的珍贵宝石。大家都这么说，甚至外国人也一样。”

En: I believe Revachol is being managed by something called... the Coalition?
Zh: “我相信瑞瓦肖好像是处于一个叫什么……联盟国的管辖之下？”

En: Precisely, sweetie! Revachol is what's called a Zone of Control, led by an alliance of foreign powers. We have almost no government of our own, sadly...
Zh: “没错，甜心！瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支外国势力联合管辖。可悲的是，我们几乎没有自己的政府……”

En: Oh sweetie... she smiles a sad smile. "It's really not. There used to be people who thought that way -- other people, who wanted those things -- but... they all went extinct."
Zh: “哦，甜心……”她的笑容有些悲伤。“真的不是。曾经有人也是这么想的——想要那些东西的其他人——不过……他们全都绝迹了。”

En: Who's *sweetie*?
Zh: “谁是*甜心*？”

En: Does that mean you *like* me?
Zh: “这是不是意味着你*喜欢*我？”

En: I feel like *I* was in a war...
Zh: “我感觉*我*曾经经历过一场战争……”

En: Oh my! What happened?
Zh: “天啦！发生什么了？”

En: I think I lost.
Zh: “我想我输了。”

En: I think I won.
Zh: “我想我赢了。”

En: That's how I feel about the accident... She looks down and then up at you again. "I was a training and development manager at a rapidly expanding mail-order shoe company."
Zh: “那次事故也给了我这种感觉……”她低下头，然后又抬头看向你。“我是一个迅速扩张的邮购鞋业公司的培训和开发经理。”

En: Were you a... rugby player?
Zh: “你是一个……橄榄球运动员吗？”

En: You're probably right, Kim.
Zh: “也许你是对的，金。”

En: Kim -- of course! I forgot I had *you*!
Zh: “金——当然了！我都忘记自己还有*你*了！”

En: Yes, and it seems to me that you'd do well to stick close to him. He has the look of an upstanding officer of the law, someone you can lean on -- and, sweetie, you *are* looking unsteady.
Zh: “是的，在我看来你跟他在一起应该会很好。他有一副正直的执法人员的样子，是那种可以依赖的人——还有，甜心，你*的确*看起来不太稳定。”

En: Yeah, what *was* that?
Zh: “是啊，刚才*那是*怎么回事？”

En: That cafeteria manager is a bad man, a man without honour or compassion.
Zh: “那个餐厅经理是个坏人，根本没有荣誉或者是同情心。”

En: This Lena is wacky enough for the motley crew. Hire her on the spot!
Zh: 这个莉娜足够古怪，和你们这支半吊子探案队画风完全一致。赶紧聘用她！

En: I drank so hard I forgot *literally* everything.
Zh: “我喝的太多，*基本上*什么都忘记了。”

En: She leans back in her chair, alarmed by your answer.
Zh: 她向后靠在椅背上，对你的答案有些担忧。

En: She is perturbed by your not knowing what year it is -- but not by your mention of the apocalypse. It *must* be the end times.
Zh: 不知道现在是哪一年的你让她有些不安——而不是你所提到的天启。这*一定*是世界末日。

En: She looks flustered, her hands smoothing out the creases in her blanket even as she attempts to reassure you.
Zh: 她看起来有些慌张，用手抚平了毛毯上的褶皱，尽管她是想让你安心。

En: Though she praises you, it is clear that she was taken aback by your response.
Zh: 她是在赞扬你，不过显然是被你的回应吓了一跳。

En: Her relief is palpable. She was getting pretty worried about you there, but now she relaxes her shoulders...
Zh: 她明显松了一口气。她本来对你很是担心，不过现在肩膀放松了些……

En: The lieutenant tries to pretend he's not hearing it and is out of the conversation.
Zh: 警督试着假装没有听到，然后退出了谈话。

En: The lieutenant studies you, rubbing his chin.
Zh: 警督摸着下巴，打量着你。

En: I'm beginning to suspect that you might indeed be completely adrift in this reality, thinks the lieutenant. How can it be *that* bad? Never mind -- we're in this now.
Zh: 警督开始怀疑你可能真的完全迷失在这个现实之中了。怎么会*那么*糟糕呢？无所谓了——这就是我们现在的处境。

En: We are governed by intelligent machines that perform calculations to determine the *freest* market. Everyone hustles and grinds like a bad ass visionary.
Zh: “我们被智能机器统治着，他们通过计算来决定*最自由*的市场。每个人都像混账梦想家一样庸庸碌碌。”

En: Oh... no, nothing like that, dear. Revachol is a Zone of Control, led by an alliance of foreign powers called the Coalition. We have almost no government of our own. And certainly not one who's horseback.
Zh: “哦……不，不是这样的，亲爱的。雷瓦科是一个控制区，由一支名为联盟的外国势力联合管辖。我们几乎没有自己的政府。而且肯定也没有那些骑在马背上的人。”

En: Our leaders are fierce warriors who traverse the plains on steeds. Civilization cowers before us.
Zh: “我们的领袖是勇猛的战士，他们骑着战马驰骋在这片平原之上。文明在我们面前都会弯腰屈膝。”

En: I hope you're right. I hope it's not too bad...
Zh: “我希望你是对的。希望并没有很糟糕……”

En: Yeah, I'm blowing this whole thing out of proportion. Forget I said anything.
Zh: “是啊，我把整件事搞得有点小题大做了。忘掉我说的话吧。”

En: She nods, but still looks worried.
Zh: 她点点头，不过看起来还是很担心。

En: Well, you know where we are, right?
Zh: “呃，你知道我们现在在哪，对吧？”

En: You know where we *are*, right?
Zh: “你知道我们*现在*在哪，对吧？”

En: Can you tell me about this *reality* again?
Zh: “你能再跟我说说这个*现实*吗？”

En: On second thought, things *are* bad. I don't even know enough to know what I *don't* know about this world.
Zh: “仔细一想，情况*确实*挺糟糕的。我甚至都不够了解自己到底*不了解*这个世界的什么。”

En: Oh, no, far from it. Although... Her eyes glimmer mischievously. "I did come out to watch the boys play when I was in school. All those tan calves..."
Zh: “哦，不是的，远非如此。不过……”她的眼睛里闪烁出淘气的光芒。“我上学的时候，确实会出去看男孩子们玩耍。那些晒成棕褐色的小腿……”

En: She blushes, surprised by her own boldness.
Zh: 她被自己的大胆吓到脸红了。

En: It seems to me that you lucked out with your partner. He has the look of an upstanding officer of the law, someone you can lean on -- and, sweetie, you *are* looking unsteady.
Zh: “在我看来，你能和他搭档非常幸运。他有一副正直的执法人员的样子，是那种可以依赖的人——还有，甜心，你*的确*看起来不太稳定。”

En: Quitting smoking is one of the best things you can do for your health, officer.
Zh: “戒烟是你能为自身健康做的最好的事情之一，警官。”

En: You would do well to follow her example. Smoking is the reason you feel like a rag. It's painful for you to even ask these questions.
Zh: 以她为榜样会很不错。抽烟是你觉得自己像个废物的原因。甚至开口问这些问题都会让你感到痛苦。

En: But I'm not going to quit *today*.
Zh: “但是我*今天*不会戒。”

En: But I have to smoke. It's part of my cool guy persona.
Zh: “但是我必须抽烟。这是保持我型男人设的一部分。”

En: You are my witness: I pledge to never pick up another cigarette as long as I live! (Try to stop smoking.)
Zh: “你是我的见证人：我发誓只要我还活着，就永远不会再抽一根烟！”（试着戒烟）

En: She sighs. "I know it's not easy, sweetie. But keep thinking about it."
Zh: 她叹了口气。“我知道这不容易，甜心。不过继续考虑这件事吧。”

En: She sighs. "I'm sure it lends you a roguish charm, sweetie."
Zh: 她叹了口气。“我肯定这样会为*你*增添一丝淘气的魅力，甜心。”

En: She smiles, obviously pleased. "I support your decision, officer."
Zh: 她笑了，显然很开心。“我支持你的决定，警官。”

En: Why, *you* are, officer!
Zh: “为什么这么问，当然是*你*啊，警官！”

En: I'm no sweetie. Look at me.
Zh: “我不是甜心。看看我这幅模样。”

En: Hmm... maybe I am?
Zh: “嗯……也许我是？”

En: You're a handsome man, officer, with your moustache and your chiselled jaw. And that silly dimple on your chin.
Zh: “你是一个英俊的男人，警官，看看你的胡子，还有那轮廓分明的下巴，还有脸颊上那个傻傻的小酒窝。”

En: I'm sorry if I was being overly familiar! I know we've only just met.
Zh: “如果我有些太过于自来熟，我很抱歉！我知道我们才刚刚见面。”

En: The 'mating rituals' aren't the good part, sweetie. Her eyes shine with gentle compassion. "I know it's none of my business, but you look like you need someone to take care of you."
Zh: “你说的‘求偶仪式’并不是好的那部分，甜心。”她的眼中流露出温柔的怜悯。“我知道这不关我的事，但是你看起来真的很需要有人照顾。”

En: You rascal! She grins up at you. "I'm too old for you, and too married besides."
Zh: “你这个小调皮鬼！”她朝你咧嘴一笑。“我对你来说太老了，而且我已经结婚太久了。”

En: I see you are still grieving. Well, I won't pry. She smiles gently -- paying no heed to the inexplicable winking.
Zh: “我能看出你还是很悲痛。不过，我是不会刺探的。”她温柔地笑了——完全没有在意你令人费解的眨眼举动。

En: Stop winking. (Tap yourself on the side of your head to correct the malfunction.)
Zh: “别再眨眼了。”（拍拍一边的脑袋，纠正这个故障。）

En: She won't judge you, no matter what you say.
Zh: 不管你说什么，她都不会评判你。

En: I suppose all of us do at one time or another. I don't know what I'd do without Morell...
Zh: “我猜每个人都有这样那样的时候。我不知道要是没有莫雷尔我会怎么样……”

En: You must forgive me! I'm getting so scatterbrained! I completely forgot to introduce myself. She slaps herself on the forehead.
Zh: 她拍了拍自己的额头。“请原谅！我真是太心不在焉了！完全忘了自我介绍。”

En: Your advances haven't thrown her off one bit. In another place and time, she would have probably welcomed your attention.
Zh: 你的回应并没有让她动摇分毫。在另一个时空、另一个地点，她可能会很欢迎你的关注。

En: But, dear, you're not for me. She looks off into the distance. "I'm too old, and too married besides."
Zh: “但是，亲爱的，你不适合我。”她看向远方。“我太老，而且已经结婚太久了。”

En: Wait, who's *sweetie*?
Zh: 等等，谁是*甜心*？

En: In another time and place, she might have flirted back. But that ship sailed long ago.
Zh: 如果在另一个时空，另一个地方，她可能会回应你。不过早已时过境迁了。

En: Her eyes move up and down your person as though taking your measurements. Her attention is *scientific*.
Zh: 她用眼睛在你身上上下游移着，像是在打量着你。她的关注点是*科学的*。

En: You shouldn't keep your colleague waiting. She nods toward the man in the orange bomber jacket.
Zh: “你不应该让你的同事等得太久。”她朝着穿橙色飞行员夹克的男人点点头。

En: Hello, sweetie.
Zh: “你好，甜心。”

En: Hello again, sweetie. Her grey eyes shine above the rims of her glasses.
Zh: “又见面了，甜心。”她灰色的眼睛在镜框边缘闪闪发光。

En: Radios are being used to control people's minds and distort our perception of reality, concealing our true masters: foreigners and *women*.
Zh: “无线电被用来控制人们的思想，扭曲我们对现实的认知，隐藏了我们真正的主人：外国人和*女人*。”

En: From where I was standing, it looked like you were about to pull out two guns, but drew two... akhem... birdies instead. Well, I'm glad you're weren't injured.
Zh: “从我的位置来看，你好像要拔出两支手枪，不过手摆的却像两只……咳咳……小鸟。呃，很高兴你没有受伤。”

En: He's just on edge because of the lynching. Don't judge him too harshly. Anyway, I'm glad you weren't injured.
Zh: “他因为私刑的事比较紧张。对他的评价不要太苛刻了。不管怎么说，很高兴你没有受伤。”

En: It was like my body had a will of its own. I was running, my torso turned, my hands made this lewd gesture... I'm sorry you had to witness it.
Zh: “感觉就像我的身体有自己的意志。我在跑，身体却要转弯，手还在比划着粗俗的手势……很抱歉让你看到这一切。”

En: Don't beat yourself up over it too much, dear. People do strange things when the old fight-or-flight kicks in. I'm just glad you weren't injured.
Zh: “不要对自己太过气馁，亲爱的。如果遇到老一套是战还是逃的情况，大家都会做出奇怪的事。我只是很高兴你没有受伤。”

En: It's not easy to assert your right to a decent living wage when you don't have a strong union behind you... Maybe you should talk to Evrart, the Union leader?
Zh: “背后没有一个强大工会做支撑的时候，要想维护获得体面的基本生活工资的权利可不容易……也许你应该跟工会首领艾弗拉特聊聊？”

En: Where can I pawn the pin?
Zh: “我可以去哪里当掉这个别针呢？”

En: I promise I'll buy it back for you once I have more money. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我保证等我有钱了，马上就把它赎回来。”（总结。）

En: What is 'to pawn'?
Zh: “‘当掉’是什么意思？”

En: It's easy. You bring a valuable item to a 'pawnbroker' who then gives you money for it. You can get it back later for a slightly larger sum if it hasn't been sold, but you never end up doing that anyway...
Zh: “很简单。你把一件贵重物品交给个‘当铺老板’，然后就会从老板那里拿到钱。稍后你可以多花一点钱把东西赎回来……如果还没有卖掉的话，不过反正你永远也不会做的……”
