En: Oh, it's not an ancient family heirloom or anything, but... I suppose it *would* be nice to get it back.
Zh: “哦，它不是什么古老的传家宝或者什么的，不过……我觉得如果能拿回来*一定*挺好的。”

En: Cross the plaza and follow the road along the coast. Down by the water, there will be a shack nestled against an old stone façade. That's the pawnshop. An old friend of my husband's goes there often. He says the owner is quite helpful.
Zh: “穿过广场，沿着海岸的路走。河边上有一间小棚屋，就依偎在一个旧石头建筑旁边。那个就是当铺。我丈夫的一个老朋友经常会去那里。他说店主是个很乐于助人的人。”

En: This will help me out, Lena. Thanks. (Conclude.)
Zh: “它应该能帮到我，莉娜。谢谢你。”（总结。）

En: The pin is round and slightly tarnished. Three sailboats in bas-relief with the sun hovering over the water behind them. A chipped green banner at the bottom reads "Saint-Batiste Summer '31 -- Dinghy Races."
Zh: 别针是圆形的，稍稍有些黯淡。上面有三个浅浮雕帆船，背后悬挂着一轮太阳。底部有一个缺口的绿色标语。上面写着“圣巴蒂斯特31年夏季”——小艇比赛。

En: The Saint-Batiste Dinghy Races were conceived as a biennial civic pride celebration (and cash cow), but only took place twice, in '31 and '33. The '31 races are the subject of fond reminiscence: Those who were there will tell you of sun-drenched bleachers, pink bubbly, and bronze-legged studs in white shorts.
Zh: 圣巴蒂斯特小艇竞赛最初的构想是两年一次的公民自豪感庆典活动（还有摇钱树），不过却只举办过两次，31年和33年。31年那次是大家最喜爱的还怀旧主题：那些参加过的人会跟你讲述那阳光普照的露天看台，粉红香槟酒，还有穿着白色短裤，露出古铜色小腿的小伙子们。

En: No problem. She nods, smiling up at you from behind her glasses.
Zh: “没问题。”她点点头，眼镜背后露出笑眼。

En: Tug on her heartstrings.
Zh: 拨动她的心弦。

En: You understand Lena's idealism.
Zh: 你能理解莉娜的理想主义。

En: Asked station for additional funds.
Zh: 找分局索要额外的资金。

En: Your deportment remains dignified -- as you shed a single, manly tear.
Zh: 你的举止保持着端庄——不过却流下一滴男子汉的泪水。

En: Try to force a tear out of your duct. Really *rip* into the whole emotional aggression thing.
Zh: 试着从泪腺里强行挤出一滴泪水。真正猛烈*抨击*这整件情感侵略的事情。

En: Oh god, I'm going to die on the street aren't I?
Zh: “天啦，我会死在街头的，是不是？”

En: Sweetie, I'm sorry, but I think you need more help than I can give you. She looks distressed and even a little frightened.
Zh: “甜心，很抱歉，不过我觉得你需要的帮助远比我能给予的多。”她看起来有些紧张，甚至还有一点害怕。

En: There's something *inspiring* about a woman on the front line, leading her platoon of men.
Zh: “前线出现一个领导士兵的女排长确实一件*鼓舞人心*的事情。”

En: Hmm, a grenade? Did you fight in a war?
Zh: “嗯，手榴弹造成的？你参加过战争吗？”

En: And even if he weren't there, I don't think you'd have much use for *me*. She looks down.
Zh: 她低下头。“就算他不在，我也不觉得*自己*能给你提供多少帮助。”

En: But you're the most mobile person of them all!
Zh: “但是你是他们中间机动性最强的人了！”

En: She chuckles. "I'm glad you think so, but I still don't know where you can get cigarettes."
Zh: 她咯咯笑了。“很高兴你能这么想，不过我还是不知道你能去哪里弄到香烟。”

En: Hello again, sweetie. I see you've met up with your colleague. She looks at the lieutenant.
Zh: “又见面了，甜心。看来你已经见过自己的同事了。”她看着警督。

En: Hello, sweetie. The elderly woman turns to you with a smile.
Zh: “你好，甜心。”老妇人微笑着转向你。

En: That's right, dear! How splendid! Here, take this pen. Knowledge should always be rewarded.
Zh: “没错，亲爱的！真是太棒了！来，拿着这支笔。知识永远都应该得到奖励。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: He was tormenting me with outrageous demands for *money*.
Zh: “他用对*钱*的离谱需求折磨我。”

En: I don't really wanna talk about that right now.
Zh: “我现在真的不太想谈这个。”

En: What was with that fucking wink?
Zh: 这该死的眨眼是怎么回事？

En: Seemed neurological... central nervous system's really banged up.
Zh: 好像是神经学上的问题……中央神经系统真的损坏了。

En: Seemed neurological... central nervous system's really banged up.
Zh: 好像是神经学上的问题……中央神经系统真的损坏了。

En: Thanks. I appreciate it.
Zh: “谢谢。我很感激。”

En: That stupid dimple has only brought me *suffering*.
Zh: “那个傻傻的酒窝只给我带来了*痛苦*。”

En: Take heart, officer. You're still a man in your prime.
Zh: “振作起来，警官。你还是个风华正茂的男人呢。”

En: Dimple or not, I am a bitter man -- the years have taken their toll.
Zh: “不管有没有酒窝，我都是个痛苦的男人——岁月已经让我形容憔悴了。”

En: I don't wanna talk about the dimple anymore.
Zh: “我不想再提酒窝的事了。”

En: Love can be cruel, sweetie. Her expression turns serious. "Love can be cruel."
Zh: “爱情可能会很残酷，甜心。”她的表情变得有些严肃。“爱情有时候真的很残酷。”

En: That's right, dear! How splendid!
Zh: “没错，亲爱的！真是太棒了！”

En: You're quite welcome, dear. She smiles up at you.
Zh: 她冲着你笑了。“你太客气了，亲爱的。”

En: Of course, dear! I just wish I could have done more.
Zh: “当然了，亲爱的！要是能为你多做一些就好了。”

En: I decided not to pawn it. You should have it back.
Zh: “我决定不当了。你应该收回去。”

En: I hope you were able to pawn that old trinket! She smiles up at you earnestly.
Zh: “希望你能当掉那个破旧的小饰品。”她的笑容非常真挚。

En: I got 31 cents for it. Here's the money.
Zh: “我当掉了31分钱。钱在这里。”

En: She really would have rather had the pin back, but she doesn't want to make you feel bad.
Zh: 她其实更愿意把别针拿回来，不过她不想让你感觉不好。

En: I bought it back. I know it means a lot to you. You should have it.
Zh: “我把它买回来了。我知道它对你来说意义非凡。你应该拿着它。”

En: About your pin...
Zh: “关于你的别针……”

En: I just wanted to thank you again.
Zh: “我只想再次感谢你。”

En: Aw, thank you, dear. I confess -- I am glad to see it again. Very honourable of you, officer.
Zh: “啊，谢谢你。亲爱的。我承认——还能再看到它我真的很高兴。你真是个有荣誉感的人，警官。”

En: Oh, that's very thoughtful of you. Are you sure you don't need the money? Okay, then. Thank you.
Zh: “哦，你真是太体贴了。你确定不需要钱了吗？那好吧。谢谢你。”

En: I got 3.20 for it. Here's the money.
Zh: “我当掉了3.2雷亚尔。钱在这里。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: I'm Lena. My husband Morell and I are staying with our friend Gary just down the street, but I come here for tea when they're away. Her eyes glitter over the rims of her glasses as she looks up, smiling.
Zh: “我叫莉娜，我的丈夫莫雷尔，我，还有我们的朋友盖理就住在街边，不过他们现在不在，每当他们不在我就来这喝茶。”她抬起头微笑，镜框后的眼睛在闪烁。

En: And have you found anyone to be sweet *to*? She smiles conspiratorially.
Zh: “你也找到一个自己的*甜心*了吗？”她意味深长的笑了。

En: No. I'm done with *mating rituals* this time around.
Zh: “没有。这一次我不想再搞什么*求偶仪式*了。”

En: I don't know. (Wink.) "Maybe I have?"
Zh: “我不知道。”（眨眨眼。）“也许有？”

En: I had once... (Wink twice.) "But then I *lost* her..."
Zh: “有过一个……”（眨两下眼。）“不过之后我*失去*了她……”

En: You're a handsome man, officer, with your moustache and your chiselled jaw. And that silly dimple on your chin.
Zh: “你是一个英俊的男人，警官，看看你的胡子，还有那轮廓分明的下巴，还有脸颊上那个傻傻的小酒窝。”

En: That dimple is a flower attracting lust, vice, and sin.
Zh: 那个酒窝就像一朵花，吸引着欲望、堕落和罪孽。

En: The lieutenant nods politely.
Zh: 警督礼貌地点点头。

En: Do nothing. The neurological damage is probably permanent.
Zh: 什么也不做。神经损伤可能是永久的。

En: You must forgive me! she says, slapping herself on the forehead. "I'm getting so scatterbrained! I completely forgot to introduce myself."
Zh: “请原谅！”她一边说着，一边拍了拍自己的前额。“我真是太心不在焉了！完全忘了自我介绍。”

En: She really would have rather had the pin back, but she doesn't want to make you feel bad.
Zh: 她其实更愿意把别针拿回来，不过她不想让你感觉不好。

En: Oh, that's very thoughtful of you. Are you sure you don't need the money? Okay, then. Thank you.
Zh: “哦，你真是太体贴了。你确定不需要钱了吗？那好吧。谢谢你。”

En: I decided not to pawn it. You should have it back.
Zh: “我决定不当了。你应该收回去。”

En: I got 3.10 for it. Here's the money.
Zh: “我当掉了3.1雷亚尔。钱在这里。”

En: I bought it back. I know it means a lot to you. You should have it.
Zh: “我把它买回来了。我知道它对你来说意义非凡。你应该拿着它。”

En: I got 3.20 for it. Here's the money.
Zh: “我当掉了3.2雷亚尔。钱在这里。”

En: Aw, thank you, dear. I confess -- I am glad to see it again.
Zh: “啊，谢谢你。亲爱的。我承认——还能再看到它我真的很高兴。”

En: Of course, dear. Good luck with your case! She gives you a small wave.
Zh: “当然了，亲爱的。希望你的调查顺利！”她朝你轻轻挥挥手。

En: We're in Martinaise.
Zh: “我们在马丁内斯。”

En: That's right! We're on the coast of the city of Revachol, in the district of Martinaise.
Zh: “没错！我们在瑞瓦肖城的海岸边，一个叫马丁内斯的街区。”

En: On Insulinde?
Zh: “在伊苏林迪？”

En: Yes, but, more specifically -- on the coast of the city of Revachol, in the district of Martinaise.
Zh: “是的，不过更具体地说——是在瑞瓦肖城的海岸边，一个叫马丁内斯的区域。”

En: You shouldn't keep your colleague waiting. She nods toward the man in the black bomber jacket.
You shouldn't keep your colleague waiting. She nods toward the man in the orange bomber jacket.
Just a moment, officer... She smiles up at you nervously. "If you run into my husband again... do tell him I'm worried, and to hurry back."
Zh: “请等一下，警官……”她紧张地冲你笑了。“如果你再遇到我丈夫……请告诉他我很担心，让他快点回来。”

En: Sure thing, ma'am. (Accept the task)
Zh: “当然可以，夫人。”（接受任务）

En: I'm sorry, I don't want to get involved in your affairs. (Refuse the task)
Zh: “很抱歉，我不想介入你的事情。”（拒绝任务）

En: Oh, thank you so much sweetie. Good luck with your investigation now.
Zh: “哦，真是太谢谢你了，甜心。祝你的调查能一切顺利。”

En: Of course, sweetie. Good luck with your investigation.
Zh: “当然了，甜心。祝你的调查能一切顺利。”

En: She frowns, looking you over. "Surely things can't be *that* bad?"
Zh: 她皱起眉头，打量着你。“事情应该没有*那么*糟糕吧？”

En: We're in the Whirling-in-Rags cafeteria.
Zh: “我们在褴褛飞旋的餐厅。”

En: We're in Revachol.
Zh: “我们在瑞瓦肖。”

En: A war zone. At the edge of the world.
Zh: “一个战区。在世界的边缘。”

En: I don't really know... some seedy hotel?
Zh: “我真的不知道……某个破烂的旅社？”

En: That's right, and where is the Whirling itself located?
Zh: “没错，那飞旋旅社本身又在什么地方呢？”

En: We're in the city of *Revachol*, dear. She flashes you a worried look.
Zh: 她露出一个担忧的神情。“我们一个叫*瑞瓦肖*的城市，亲爱的。”

En: That's right! In a hostel called the Whirling-in-Rags, to be precise.
Zh: “没错！准确的说，是在一个叫褴褛飞旋的旅社。”

En: But -- maybe a *fresh set of eyes* is what the world needs? And -- while I'm no doctor -- such bouts of amnesia are often temporary. So I wouldn't worry *too* much.
Zh: “但是——也许*一双新鲜的眼睛*才是这个世界需要的？而且——我也不是医生——这种失忆症发作经常是临时性的。所以我并没有*过于*担心。”

En: It does still look like that in some parts of the city, doesn't it...
Zh: “在这座城市的某些地方，看起来还是那样，不是吗……”

En: I'm just a poor woman, she thinks. What do I know of these things. And how can I help you?
Zh: 我只是个可怜的女人，她想到。我怎么知道这些事呢——我该怎么帮你呢？

En: We are in a hostel called the Whirling-in-Rags, dear. And the Whirling itself is in the city of Revachol.
Zh: “我们在一个叫褴褛飞旋的旅社里，亲爱的。而飞旋旅社本身在瑞瓦肖城。”

En: A seaside hostel called the Whirling-in-Rags, to be precise. And the Whirling itself is in Revachol.
Zh: “没错！准确的说，是在一个叫褴褛飞旋的旅社。”

En: Speaking of history -- you know what *year* it is, yes?
Zh: 她停下来，研究着你的表情。你看起来一定很迷惘。“说到历史——你知道现在是*哪一年*，对吧？”

En: If there's no government, how come there are cops?
Zh: “如果没有政府，那为什么会有警察呢？”

En: That tape you picked up said '43. You seem to be a hip modern man who listens to hip modern records, so it must be '43.
Zh: 你捡到的那盘磁带上写着43年。你好像是个喜欢听新潮唱片的新潮男人，所以一定是43年。

En: It's totally '43.
Zh: “绝对是43年。”

En: It's the spring of '51.
Zh: “是51年的春天。”

En: All I know is: we are approaching the *end times*.
Zh: “我只知道：*末日*就要降临。”

En: I can tell that this is taxing for you, so I'll just ask *one* more question. What regime are we living under? What mode of government?
Zh: “我能看出这对你来说很费力，所以我只再问你*一个*问题。我们生活在怎样的政体下？政治模式是怎样的？”

En: I'd *like* to think it's the dictatorship of the proletariat, but something tells me it's not.
Zh: “我*愿意*认为现在是工人阶级专政，但是有什么东西告诉我不是的。”

En: You're joking. Try again, one more time. What year is it?
Zh: “你是在开玩笑吧。再试试，再来一次。现在是什么年份？”

En: She means this sincerely. Worrying won't do you any good.
Zh: 她是真心实意的。担心对你来说也没什么用。

En: It's a bad year in my late forties, or fifties? I don't even know how *old* I am.
Zh: “在我40多岁，或者50多岁里糟糕的一年？我甚至都不知道自己*多大*。”

En: Oh no, dear! It's the early spring of '51.
Zh: “哦不，亲爱的！现在是51年的早春。”

En: Perhaps, dear, perhaps. But for now it's just the spring of '51.
Zh: “可能吧，亲爱的，可能。不过现在是51年的春天。”

En: There, there. The year is '51, and spring has only just started. I'm sure there are better days ahead.
Zh: “好了，好了。今年是51年，春天刚刚开始。我肯定好日子就在前面。”

En: Outside, spring rain seeps into the cracks in the walls and the cobblestone streets, and into grated storm drains. All the way down into the sewers... Above ground, the first May bells blossom.
Zh: 屋外，春雨渗入墙上的裂缝，街道上的鹅卵石中，还有格栅下的雨水道，一路流入下水道……地面上，第一朵铃兰正绽放开来。

En: We're dead. Haunting each other. We're ghosts.
Zh: “我们已经死了。彼此纠缠。我们是幽灵。”

En: Now, now. She tilts her head as she looks up at you with maternal solicitude.
Zh: “喂，哎呀。”她歪着脑袋，以慈母般的关怀抬头看着你。

En: Sure. It's the year 973.
Zh: “当然。现在是973年。”

En: The edge of civilization.
Zh: “文明的边缘。”

En: Yes -- and Revachol...? She examines you.
Zh: “是的——那瑞瓦肖……？”她打量着你。

En: On something called *the urban coast*.
Zh: “在一个叫*城市海岸*的地方。”

En: Mhm, okay.
Zh: “嗯，好吧。”

En: In... Revachol?
Zh: “在……瑞瓦肖？”

En: Cop. We are living under the cop-regime.
Zh: “警察。我们生活在警察政权下。”

En: In hell. We're all in hell.
Zh: “在地狱里。我们全都在地狱里。”

En: I don't even know what to say. I'm so disappointed.
Zh: “我都不知道该说什么好了。我真的很失望。”

En: We are alive -- in a hostel called the Whirling-in-Rags. And the Whirling itself is in the city of Revachol.
Zh: “我们还活着——在一个叫褴褛飞旋的旅社里。而飞旋旅社本身在瑞瓦肖城。”

En: Some kind of democracy maybe?
Zh: “也许是某种民主政权？”

En: Honestly, I don't really know anything about Revachol.
Zh: “老实说，我完全不了解瑞瓦肖。”

En: All I know is that Revachol used to be really cool in the Thirties.
Zh: “我只知道瑞瓦肖在30年代的时候真的挺酷的。”

En: Yes, we're in Martinaise, on the coast of Revachol.
Zh: “是的，我们在马丁内斯，瑞瓦肖的海岸上。”

En: Someone more educated in these things than me? Someone rich, maybe? Wealthy people are educated. Though I don't know where you would find a wealthy person in Martinaise...
Zh: “在这方面比我受过更多教育的人？也许找个有钱人问？有钱人都受过良好教育。虽然不知道你能不能在马丁内斯找到有钱人……”

En: Yes, indeed. We are in the fine city of Revachol.
Zh: “是的，的确。我们在一个叫瑞瓦肖的美丽城市里。”

En: I know it's the '50s, but I'm not sure about the year.
Zh: “我知道是50年代，不过不确定是哪一年。”

En: The New was in the Thirties, and two decades have elapsed since The New, so it must be the Fifties now.
Zh: 新时代是指30年代，在那之后已经过去了20年，所以现在一定是在50年代。

En: Good, dear, you got the decade right! It's the spring of '51.
Zh: “很好，亲爱的，你把年代说对了！现在是51年的春天。”

En: The New! Disco dancing! It seems like a lifetime ago... Her eyes are filled with light reflected off those gilded years, when the city seemed full of possibility.
Zh: “新时代！迪斯科舞！感觉已经是上辈子的事情了……”她的眼睛里充满那些黄金岁月折射出来的光彩，那时候的城市似乎充满更多可能。

En: Of course. She turns to you. "Then I don't know. Someone rich maybe? Wealthy people are educated. Though I don't know where you would find a wealthy person in Martinaise..."
Zh: “当然。”她转向你。“那我就不知道了。也许找个有钱人问？有钱人都受过良好教育。虽然不知道你能不能在马丁内斯找到有钱人……”

En: Her grey eyes widen. "Oh my. How would I even begin to tell you? Revachol is the most beautiful city in the world. We're fortunate to be here, you and I."
Zh: 她灰色的眼睛睁大了。“天啊。我又该从何跟你说起呢？瑞瓦肖是这个世界上最美丽的城市。能在这里是我们的幸运，你和我都一样。”

En: Actually... She raises her finger. "We are *not*. You could say that about almost any other nation, but not Revachol. Try *one* more time, officer -- what mode of government?"
Zh: “其实呢……”她竖起手指。“我们*不是*的。基本上其他国家都能这么说，但瑞瓦肖不是。再试*一次*，警官——政治模式是怎样的？”

En: I haven't seen very many other cities personally, but everyone says so. Revachol is a rare jewel. This city used to rule the world... Though it has seen better days. There's a pause as she studies your expression. You must look quite lost.
Zh: “我没有亲眼见过很多其他城市，不过大家都是这么说的。瑞瓦肖是一颗珍贵的宝石。这个城市曾经统治着全世界……尽管它曾经有过更好的岁月。”

En: Yes, great, see -- we're getting somewhere. What else do you know about our city of splendour? Tattered as she may be...
Zh: “是的，很棒，看见了吗——-我们有点进展了。关于这座壮丽的城市，你还知道些什么呢？虽然她现在有些破败……”

En: Much has changed, but it is still the most beautiful city in the world, a rare jewel set in the sand between the pines. Everyone says so, even foreigners. There's a pause as she studies your expression. You must look quite lost.
Zh: “很多都变了，不过它依然是这个世界上最美丽的城市，一颗镶嵌在群松之间沙地上的珍贵宝石。大家都这么说，甚至外国人也一样。”

En: I believe Revachol is being managed by something called... the Coalition?
Zh: “我相信瑞瓦肖好像是处于一个叫什么……联盟国的管辖之下？”

En: She's trying to avoid the foreigners part. This is because she's a woman. She may be *lying* to you...
Zh: 她想要回避外国人的那部分。这是因为她是个女性。她也许在跟你*撒谎*……

En: Precisely, sweetie! Revachol is what's called a Zone of Control, led by an alliance of foreign powers. We have almost no government of our own, sadly...
Zh: “没错，甜心！瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支外国势力联合管辖。可悲的是，我们几乎没有自己的政府……”

En: Oh sweetie... she smiles a sad smile. "It's really not. There used to be people who thought that way -- other people, who wanted those things -- but... they all went extinct."
Zh: “哦，甜心……”她的笑容有些悲伤。“真的不是。曾经有人也是这么想的——想要那些东西的其他人——不过……他们全都绝迹了。”

En: No. He looks away. "I'm not an encyclopedia. I won't be a guide either. I'm a police officer."
Zh: “不。”他移开目光。“我不是一部百科全书，也不会去当向导，我是一个警探。”

En: Things aren't *bad*. They're catastrophic.
Zh: “事情不是*糟糕*，而是灾难。”

En: Someone more educated in *sweeping* matters? Maybe you should ask... She turns to the lieutenant.
Zh: “某个在*全局*事件上受过更多教育的人？也许你应该问问……”她转向警督。

En: Revachol is the disgraced capital of the world. Something like that?
Zh: “瑞瓦肖是一个失宠的世界之都。差不多是这个意思吗？”

En: It has something to do with everything. I really don't know how to explain it better...
Zh: “这跟一切都有所关联。我真的不知道怎么才能解释地更清楚……”

En: What does that have to do with there not being any cops?
Zh: “那这跟没警察又有什么关系呢？”

En: A defeat, I'm afraid. The people of this archipelago tried to build something new, something *different*. The rest of the world didn't like it, so they came and ended it. This was forty two years ago.
Zh: “恐怕是一次失败。这个群岛的居民试图建立一些新的、*不太一样*的东西。但是世界上的其他人不太喜欢，所以他们来到这里，结束了一切。那已经是42年前的事了。”

En: Nope. Sadly not. Revachol is what's called a Zone of Control. Under an alliance of foreign powers called the Coalition. We have no government of our own and what democracy we have is... She thinks. "Market driven."
Zh: “不是。可悲的是，并不是。瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支外国势力联合管辖。我们没有自己的政府，而我们拥有的民主是……”她想了想。“市场导向的。”

En: Oh no. We've arrived at some sort of *weak* world, where everyone's a nansy pansy...
Zh: 哦不。我们来到了一个*虚弱*的世界，这里每个人都是娘娘腔……

En: She leans back in her chair, alarmed by your answer.
Zh: 她向后靠在椅背上，对你的答案有些担忧。

En: She is perturbed by your not knowing what year it is -- but not by your mention of the apocalypse. It *must* be the end times.
Zh: 不知道现在是哪一年的你让她有些不安——而不是你所提到的天启。这*一定*是世界末日。

En: She looks flustered, her hands smoothing out the creases in her blanket even as she attempts to reassure you.
Zh: 她看起来有些慌张，用手抚平了毛毯上的褶皱，尽管她是想让你安心。

En: Though she praises you, it is clear that she was taken aback by your response.
Zh: 她是在赞扬你，不过显然是被你的回应吓了一跳。

En: See -- I have no idea.
Zh: “看见没——我不知道。”

En: I don't know... still looks like there's a lot of hustling going on. Maybe she's wrong. Also...
Zh: 我不知道……看起来还是有很多欺诈行为在进行。也许她是错的。而且……

En: Damn it, I was really hoping it would be a Cop World. Okay, well, let's see...
Zh: 见鬼，我真心希望这是一个警察的世界。好吧，这样的话，让我们看看……

En: I hope you're right. I hope it's not too bad...
Zh: “我希望你是对的。希望并没有很糟糕……”

En: Her relief is palpable. She was getting pretty worried about you there, but now she relaxes her shoulders...
Zh: 她明显松了一口气。她对你很是担心，不过现在肩膀有些放松了……

En: Yes, but, more specifically -- on the coast of the city of Revachol, in the district of Martinaise.
Zh: “是的，不过更具体地说——是在瑞瓦肖城的海岸边，一个叫马丁内斯的区域。”

En: But they still have cops...
Zh: 但是他们还有警察……

En: On Insulinde?
Zh: “在伊苏林迪？”

En: You were doing quite well up until the end there. It *does* look like you're having trouble remembering things. History and places. Remembering *Reality*, in a word. It's very odd...
Zh: “你从头到尾表现得都很好。不过看起来你*确实*在记忆方面遇到了一点问题。历史和地点什么的。总而言之，就是记不起*现实*。这一点非常奇怪……”

En: The lieutenant tries to pretend he's not hearing it and is out of the conversation.
Zh: 警督试着假装没有听到，然后退出了谈话。

En: Oh... no, nothing like that, dear. Revachol is a Zone of Control, led by an alliance of foreign powers called the Coalition. We have almost no government of our own -- certainly no machines.
Zh: “哦……不，不是这样的，亲爱的。瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支外国势力联合管辖。我们几乎没有自己的政府——当然也没有国家机器。”

En: She's scared now. She's realized you really are brain damaged.
Zh: 现在她害怕了。她意识到你的大脑真的受损了。

En: Meaning: buying is voting.
Zh: 意思是说：购买等于选举。

En: The lieutenant studies you, rubbing his chin.
Zh: 警督摸着下巴，打量着你。

En: That's right! We're on the coast of the city of Revachol, in the district of Martinaise.
Zh: “没错！我们在瑞瓦肖城的海岸边，一个叫马丁内斯的区域。”

En: I'm beginning to suspect that you might indeed be completely adrift in this reality, thinks the lieutenant. How can it be *that* bad? Never mind -- we're in this now.
Zh: 警督开始怀疑你可能真的完全迷失在这个现实之中了。怎么会*那么*糟糕呢？没关系——这就是我们现在的处境。

En: A sigh. The lieutenant buries his nose in his notebook.
Zh: 一声叹气。警督把鼻子埋进了笔记本。

En: We're in Martinaise.
Zh: “我们在马丁内斯。”

En: We are governed by intelligent machines that perform calculations to determine the *freest* market. Everyone hustles and grinds like a bad ass visionary.
Zh: “我们被智能机器统治着，他们通过计算来决定*最自由*的市场。每个人都像混账梦想家一样庸庸碌碌。”

En: You didn't do very well, dear. It does look like you're having trouble remembering things. History and places. Remembering *Reality*, in a word. It's very odd...
Zh: “你做的不是很好，亲爱的。看起来你确实在记忆方面遇到了一点问题。历史和地点什么的。总而言之，就是记不起*现实*。这一点非常奇怪……”

En: Oh... no, nothing like that, dear. Revachol is a Zone of Control, led by an alliance of foreign powers called the Coalition. We have almost no government of our own. And certainly not one who's horseback.
Zh: “哦……不，不是这样的，亲爱的。瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支外国势力联合管辖。我们几乎没有自己的政府。而且肯定也没有那些骑在马背上的人。”

En: Our leaders are fierce warriors who traverse the plains on steeds.
Zh: “我们的领袖是勇猛的战士，他们骑着战马驰骋在这片平原之上。”

En: What is the *Revolution* you mentioned?
Zh: “你提到的这个*大革命*是什么？”

En: It is quite disappointing, yes. A lot of people would like some form of representation. There's *talk*, but... for now, the RCM is all we've got.
Zh: “是很让人失望，没错。很多人都会想要某种形式的代表。虽然有一些*传闻*，不过……目前我们只有RCM。”

En: Yeah, I'm blowing this whole thing out of proportion. Forget I said anything.
Zh: “是啊，我把整件事搞得有点小题大做了。忘掉我说的话吧。”

En: She nods, but still looks worried.
Zh: 她点点头，不过看起来还是很担心。

En: Revachol is a Zone of Control, led by an alliance of foreign powers called the Coalition. We have almost no government of our own. And *certainly* no dictatorship of the proletariat.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支外国势力联合管辖。我们基本上没有自己的政府。而且*肯定*也不是工人阶级专政。”

En: Well, you know where we are, right?
Zh: “呃，你知道我们现在在哪，对吧？”

En: You know where we *are*, right?
Zh: “你知道我们*现在*在哪，对吧？”

En: Who could tell me more? (Conclude.)
Zh: “谁能告诉我更多信息呢？”（总结。）

En: That's right, dear! How splendid!
Zh: “没错，亲爱的！真是太棒了！”

En: Radios are being used to control people's minds and distort our perception of reality, concealing our true masters: foreigners and *women*.
Zh: “无线电被用来控制人们的思想，扭曲我们对现实的认知，隐藏了我们真正的主人：外国人和*女人*。”

En: Oh dear, this *is* troubling. You really ought to know that -- being one yourself. There aren't *really* any cops in Revachol, not in the traditional sense. The status of law enforcement has been a complicated matter since the Revolution...
Zh: “哦，亲爱的，这一点*的确*很令人不安。你肯定明白——因为你自己就是其中一员。瑞瓦肖并没有传统意义上的警察。大革命之后，执法机关的地位一直是个很复杂的问题……”

En: So -- how did I do?
Zh: “所以——我干得怎么样？”

En: But we should stop for today, sweetie. You look like you need a break. Besides, I'm not the best person to explain the *big* things to anyone...
Zh: “不过今天还是就说到这里吧，甜心。你看起来需要稍微休息一下。而且，我也不是最适合解释这种*大事*的人……”

En: You sense that she won't judge you, no matter what you say.
Zh: 你感觉到不管你说什么，她都不会评判你。

En: That's right, dear! How splendid! Here, take this pen. Knowledge should always be rewarded.
Zh: “没错，亲爱的！真是太棒了！来，拿着这支笔。知识永远都应该得到奖励。”

En: Oh... no, nothing like that, dear. Revachol is a Zone of Control, led by an alliance of foreign powers called the Coalition. We have almost no government of our own. And certainly no matriarchy...
Zh: “哦……不，不是这样的，亲爱的。瑞瓦肖是一个控制区，由一支外国势力联合管辖。我们几乎没有自己的政府。而且肯定也不是母权制……”

En: Oh dear... She shakes her head, suddenly very worried. "And you were doing so well. There aren't *really* any cops in Revachol, not in the traditional sense. The status of law enforcement has been a complicated matter since the Revolution..."
Zh: “亲爱的……”她摇摇头，突然担心起来。“你做的真的很好。瑞瓦肖并没有传统意义上的警察。大革命之后，执法机关的地位一直是个很复杂的问题……”

En: Can you tell me about this *reality* again?
Zh: “你能再跟我说说这个*现实*吗？”

En: You don't notice this about me, but actually -- I have lost all memory of the world and myself and I have no idea what I'm doing.
Zh: “你并没有注意到这跟我有关，但是实际上——我失去了对这个世界和我自己的所有记忆，而且我也不知道自己在做什么。”

En: Can you tell me about this *reality* again?
Zh: “你能再跟我说说这个*现实*吗？”

En: You know how I said yesterday that things are fine. They're actually not. I don't even know enough to know what I *don't* know about this world.
Zh: “你知道昨天我说一切都还好吧。其实并没有。我甚至都不够了解自己到底*不了解*这个世界的什么。”

En: On second thought, things *are* bad. I don't even know enough to know what I *don't* know about this world.
Zh: “仔细一想，情况*确实*挺糟糕的。我甚至都不够了解自己到底*不了解*这个世界的什么。”

En: But thank you, sweetie. You did make me forget about my worries for a while. She smiles up at you, but concern is creeping back into her expression.
Zh: “但是谢谢你，甜心。你确实让我暂时忘却了自己的忧虑。”她冲你笑了笑，但是脸上又露出了担忧的表情。

En: I'm basically also a cryptozoologist now.
Zh: “现在我基本算是个神秘动物学家了。”

En: The little silvery knob holding the tie together feels warm in your hand. It's in the shape of an avian skull. With *eight* eyes.
Zh: 将领带扣在一起的银色按钮摸起来很温暖。它是鸟类骨头的形状，上面有*八只*眼睛。

En: You could ask her about this, when you get the time -- it's probably a cryptid. But the phasmid, of course, is more important...
Zh: 等你有时间的时候，可以问问她——它可能是一种神秘动物。但是当然了，竹节虫更重要一些……

En: Well, in that case sweetie, let me give you a small token of my gratitude. She hands you a ribbon-tie held together by a silver bird skull. "It's a tie, Mesque in origin. The pin is an antique... quite special to the cryptozoological community."
Zh: “如果是那样的话，甜心，我想送你个小礼物以示感激。”她递给你一条用银色鸟骨系在一起的细缎带。“这是一条领带，起源于梅斯克。这个别针很古老……在神秘动物学社区里非常特别。”

En: Measuring things is important. How did she get the size?
Zh: 测量是很重要的。她是怎么知道尺寸的？

En: That is a way, *way* bigger compliment than it sounds like.
Zh: 这是一句比它听起来要*大得多*的赞美。

En: Wait. Where was this and how old were you?
Zh: “等等，这是什么地方，你多大了？”

En: Ah, I'm getting ahead of myself. I was five and a half. In Betancourt, in the suburbs. My grandmother had a summer home there.
Zh: “啊，我可能有些超前了。那时我五岁半。在贝当古的郊外。我的外婆在那里有个避暑小屋。”

En: What happened?
Zh: “发生什么事了？”

En: The strangest moment of my life: I looked up and one of the reeds *moved*. Not like a plant, but like a living thing -- it stood up and looked at me. Its body unfolded like some antique toy... I've never seen anything like it.
Zh: “那是我生命中最奇怪的时刻：我抬起头，看到一支芦苇*动了*。不像是植物，而是像一种活物——它站起来，看着我。它的身体像古董玩具一样伸展来开……我从来没见过那种东西。”

En: She'd just started forming memories. Real memories, not the billowy haze of infanthood.
Zh: 她的记忆刚刚开始浮现。真实的记忆，而不是幼儿时期汹涌的阴霾。

En: Betancourt got bombed in the war. It used to be quite near. Circa 20 kilometres from here.
Zh: 贝当古在战争中被轰炸了。以前其实挺近的，离这里大概20公里。

En: She had no fear. Just surprise.
Zh: 她没有害怕。只是吃惊。

En: I looked up and one of the reeds moved. Not like a plant, but like a living thing. It stood up and looked at me. Its body unfolded, like some antique toy... She shakes her head. "I'd never seen anything like it. The reeds turned into a creature."
Zh: “我抬起头，看到一支芦苇*动了*。不像是植物，而是像一种活物。它站起来，看着我。它的身体伸展开来，就像某种古董玩具……”她摇摇头。“我从来没见过那种东西。芦苇变成生物了。”

En: I didn't know this can happen, so I reached my arm and touched the thing. It felt just like a stalk of reed, but it moved. Swaying, towering above me... She looks at you. "After a while -- 20 seconds? a minute, maybe? -- it left. Went into the reeds."
Zh: “我不知道会发生这种事，所以我伸出胳膊去碰那个东西。感觉就像一支芦苇茎，只不过它动了。在我上方摇摆着……”她看着你。“过了一会儿——20秒左右？又或者是1分钟？——它离开了。跑进芦苇丛里了。”

En: Did you follow it?
Zh: “你跟着它了吗？”

En: I tried, but I was only a child. There was mud and high water, I couldn't see it anymore. I was just standing there, knee deep in mud, looking around me...
Zh: “我试过了，不过那时我只是个孩子。那里满是泥巴，水也很深，我再也看不到它了。我只是站在那里，膝盖深陷在泥地，环顾着周围……”

En: Where did you go? Don't go...
Zh: 你去哪了？不要走……

En: Then what?
Zh: “然后呢？”

En: We were on a date, can you imagine? She tells me a story and it's the most detailed report of the Insulindian phasmid I've ever heard. The sounds -- she told me it hissed...
Zh: “我们在约会，你能想象吗？她给我讲了一个故事，那是我听过的关于伊苏林迪竹节虫最详细的报告。那个声音——她告诉我它会发出嘶嘶声……”

En: It did, yes -- like reeds in a gust of wind.
Zh: “是这样，没错——就像一阵风吹过芦苇。”

En: ...the way it moved, the colour, how some of its limbs were white like marble... He breathes excitedly. "It matched *perfectly* with what I know from other accounts! It was amazing."
Zh: “……它移动的方式，颜色，有部分肢体是白色的，像大理石……”他兴奋地呼吸着。“它*完美地*符合了我从其他地方得到的信息！真是太惊人了。”

En: If it weren't for Lena, I might've given up hope years ago. It's no exaggeration to say that she restored my faith in my profession. He looks at her with admiration, forgetting a wide smile on his face.
Zh: “如果不是莉娜，我可能几年前就放弃希望了。毫不夸张地说，她恢复了我对这个职业的信心。”他用赞赏的目光看着她，脸上露出了一个灿烂的笑容。

En: Its limbs are white?
Zh: “它的肢体是白色的？”

En: Maybe you imagined it, Lena?
Zh: “也许是你想象出来的，莉娜？”

En: Kim, what do you think of this?
Zh: “金，你是怎么看的？”

En: Thank you for sharing this with me. (Conclude.)
Zh: “感谢你的分享。”（总结。）

En: Not all of them. He answers for her. "There is some white colouration reported, along with beige, where the camouflage ends."
Zh: “不全是。”他替她回答到。“报告说在保护色的边缘有一点白色和米黄色。”

En: How could she? Who imagines this? She didn't *know* about the phasmid. This is the main thing here, what makes it a confirmed sighting -- she had no previous knowledge of the insect.
Zh: “她怎么可能？谁能想象出来？她都不*知道*竹节虫的事。这才是最关键的地方，让她的目击经历得以被确认——她以前对这种昆虫一无所知。”

En: You were on a date?
Zh: “你们在约会？”

En: Our first, yes. The old woman looks at her husband tenderly...
Zh: “我们的第一次，没错。”老妇人温柔地看着自己的丈夫。

En: 'You tryna' tell us you saw the Insulindian phasmid out there in those reeds? Get outta here!' She smiles. "They'd just give me a cider and ruffle my hair and tell me to stop dreaming -- but I saw it."
Zh: “你是想告诉我们，你在那些芦苇丛里看到了伊苏林迪竹节虫吗？别开玩笑了！”她笑了。“他们只会给我一杯苹果酒，弄乱我的头发，然后告诉我别做梦了——但是我真的看见了。”

En: I thought it was a wonderful story, ma'am. He closes his notes and gives her a simple smile.
Zh: “我觉得这是个很棒的故事，夫人。”他合上笔记本，露出一个简单的微笑。

En: But I don't believe it. A child, left unattended, on a warm day. Children make up stories and then end up believing them.
Zh: 但是我不相信。一个孩子，无人看管，还是在温暖的一天。孩子喜欢编故事，最后还信以为真。

En: You're welcome, sweetie. I do appreciate the chance to relive it, whenever I get one. It was just... she sighs. "Such an impossibly sunshiny day. So warm."
Zh: “不客气，甜心。我很感激每一次重温它的机会。只是……”她叹了口气。“那真是个难以想象地大晴天。那么的温暖。”

En: And she could get up and walk -- right into the reeds on her own. Into the mud. Anywhere.
Zh: 而且她可以站起来——自己走进芦苇丛里。走进泥地。任何地方。

En: How big was it?
Zh: “它有多大？”

En: It's hard to say how big things are when you're quite *small* yourself. To me, it seemed to be taller than I was then... but that's probably not the case.
Zh: “当你自己还*很小*的时候，很难说清东西到底有多大。在我看来，它比那个时候的我要高……但事实可能并非如此。”

En: What if it *is* the case?
Zh: 如果事实*确实*如此呢？

En: He gives up and *I'm* running around?
Zh: “他放弃了，而*我*还在到处跑？”

En: I'm sorry, dear. It was me -- I made him go home. He gets *so* stubborn about these things -- luckily his cough kept him from arguing with me.
Zh: “很抱歉，亲爱的。是我——我让他回家的。他对这些东西*特别*顽固——幸运的是，咳嗽让他不能继续和我争论。”

En: I'm sorry, dear -- you've had to drudge through them so many times. Such is field work -- a young person's game as they say.
Zh: “很抱歉，亲爱的——你已经辛苦这么多趟了。这就是野外作业——大家口中年轻人的游戏。”

En: Be real -- Gary is not going to help with this.
Zh: 现实点——盖理不会帮忙的。

En: Once more onto the breach, then! (Take it on with undue optimism.)
Zh: “那就再奔赴一次战场吧！”（带着过分的乐观接手。）

En: Fuck that cough. It's not happening. The insides of your lungs are lined with *tobacco*, a powerful antibacterial agent. You are unstoppable.
Zh: 见鬼的咳嗽。根本没有这会事。你的肺里装着*烟草*，一种强大的抗菌药物。你是无人可挡的。

En: We were on our first date when I told him my story. You should have seen his face... She smiles. "He said my descriptions match the phasmid down to a tee: its white marble limbs, the way it moved..."
Zh: “我们第一次约会的时候，我给他讲了这个故事。你真应该看看他的脸……”她笑了。“他说我对竹节虫的描述完全正确：如大理石般白色的肢体，它移动的方式……”

En: Not all of them, as far as I remember. But some of them -- on the inside. Like stalks of marble if that makes sense...
Zh: “根据我的记忆，并不全是的。不过有一些——在内侧。就像大理石的茎秆，不知道有没有意义……”

En: Of course -- I've thought about it. But Morell says my report matches with the others. And I'm sure I hadn't heard of the phasmid as a child. Nor had my mother -- or my grandmother.
Zh: “当然——我想过的。但是莫雷尔说我的报告跟其他的十分吻合。而且我肯定自己小时候没听说过竹节虫。我的母亲也没有——外婆也一样。”

En: So how did I know what to imagine? It was only when I started telling my story as a teenager that boys would tell me: Lena... She lowers her voice, imitating a boy.
Zh: “所以我怎么知道该如何想象呢？我十几岁开始跟男孩子们讲这个故事的时候，他们只是会说：莉娜……”她压低声音，模仿男孩子的口气。

En: No -- I really want to know.
Zh: “不——我真的很想知道。”

En: I really don't know.
Zh: “我真的不知道。”

En: This will *surely* lead to a cryptozoological mystery with that *extremely rare insect*...
Zh: 如果真有那种*极其罕见的昆虫*，*肯定*会成为神秘动物学上的一个谜题……

En: Yes! Some left-field scientific research is exactly what you need right now. Funk up that *vanilla* murder investigation.
Zh: 是的！你现在正好需要一些古怪的科学研究。让那个*平淡无奇*的凶杀案调查见鬼去吧。

En: You're welcome, ma'am.
Zh: “不客气，夫人。”

En: It's just a little side-thing, we'll do it down the line.
Zh: “这只是一件小事，我们完全能做到的。”

En: Yes, please. Some science fiction is *exactly* what's needed right now. Spice up that *vanilla* murder investigation.
Zh: 是的，请务必这么做。你现在*正好*需要一些科幻小说。给那起*平淡无奇*的凶杀调查案增添一点趣味。

En: Do it. Find him. This will *surely* lead to a mystery with that *extremely rare insect*...
Zh: 去吧。找到他。如果真有那种*极其罕见的昆虫*，*肯定*会成为神秘动物学上的一个谜题……

En: Some fringe-science is *exactly* what's needed right now. Spice up that *vanilla* murder investigation.
Zh: 你现在*正好*需要一些边缘科学。给那起*平淡无奇*的凶杀调查案增添一点趣味。

En: Do it. Find her husband. This will *surely* lead to a cryptozoological mystery with that *extremely rare insect*...
Zh: 去吧。找到她的丈夫。如果真有那种*极其罕见的昆虫*，*肯定*会成为神秘动物学上的一个谜题……

En: Do it. Who knows what cryptozoological mysteries will be uncovered?
Zh: 去吧。谁知道神秘动物学之谜会揭示出什么样的真相呢？

En: I've changed my mind. I'll tell Morell you're worried.
Zh: “我改变主意了。我会告诉莫雷尔你很担心。”

En: You haven't, however. There are dangers out there, our ageing bodies fail... her heart won't rest until Morell is safely back with her.
Zh: 不过，你还没有。那里很危险，我们衰老的身体会枯竭……如果莫雷尔不能安全回到她身边，她的心是得不到安宁的。

En: So that's how they met. This is *beyond* significant for them.
Zh: 原来他们是这样遇见的。这对他们来说真的意义*重大*。

En: So that's how they met. This is *beyond* significant for them.
Zh: 原来他们是这样遇见的。这对他们来说真的意义*重大*。

En: I knew it... you hear Kim say quietly to himself.
Zh: “我就知道……”你听见金低声自言自语。

En: I'm not surprised it's already getting out of hand.
Zh: 一发不可收拾了，但我并不惊讶。

En: But... Morell told me you'd seen it.
Zh: “但是……莫雷尔告诉我说你见过的。”

En: Morell's so proud of it. He always tells everyone...
Zh: “莫雷尔真的很骄傲。他一直在对所有人讲……”

En: But... Morell told me you'd seen it. *You* also told me...
Zh: “但是……莫雷尔告诉我说你见过的。*你*也是这么跟我说的……”

En: Wow, love is *hard*.
Zh: “哇哦，爱情还真是*艰难*呢。”

En: It's not easy... She sighs. "I've wasted enough of your time with this drama. I really must stop talking about it, lest I start crying and waste *more* of your time."
Zh: “真的不容易……”她叹了口气。“我的这出戏已经让你浪费太多时间了。我真应该停下来，免得我会忍不住哭出来，浪费你*更多*的时间。”

En: A waste of time?
Zh: 浪费时间？

En: A dream?
Zh: 一场梦？

En: A lie?
Zh: 一个谎言？

En: A *fool's hope* -- say her lips moving in silence.
Zh: 一个*傻瓜的希望*——她的嘴唇沉默地开合着。

En: A little missing person's puzzle might be just the thing to take your mind off the hangover.
Zh: 一个小小的失踪人口之谜也许正好能让你转移对宿醉的注意力。

En: Doing some *good* will alleviate the hangover. At least you're of use to *someone*.
Zh: 做些*好事*能缓和你的宿醉，这样至少你对*某人*来说还是有用的。

En: More important than a missing *expedition?* I don't know... expeditions often lead to something interesting.
Zh: 比失踪*探险队*更重要的事？我不知道……探险经常能发现有意思的东西。

En: Oh yes, she says with a pinch of pride. "A zoologist -- a *crypto*zoologist to be more precise."
Zh: “是的，”她略带骄傲地说到。“一个动物学家——更准确地说，是一个*神秘动物学家*。”

En: What is cryptozoology?
Zh: “神秘动物学是什么？”

En: She's completely internalized her husband's struggles. They are her own.
Zh: 她已经完全把丈夫的挣扎内在化了，现在也变成了她自己的挣扎。

En: To be honest, this animal sounds like a *cryptid* -- you wouldn't happen to be searching for some kind of... Gnome of Geroma would you?
Zh: “老实说，这个动物听起来像是一种*神秘动物*——你不会正好在寻找某种……赫罗玛侏儒吧？”

En: It's simply *elusive*, so much so that most *establishment* zoologists doubt it exists at all.
Zh: “它真的可以说是*神出鬼没*，以至于大部分*主流*动物学家都怀疑它根本不存在。”

En: I smell pseudoscience, he's thinking. Not a big fan of that.
Zh: 他在想，我嗅到了伪科学的味道。我可不是这方面的忠实粉丝。

En: *Establishment...* His suspicion is confirmed. "I thought so."
Zh: “*主流*……”他的怀疑得到了证实。“我想也是。”

En: Of course. You're a cop, you were *born* to do this stuff.
Zh: 当然。你是个警察，你*生来*就是做这个的。

En: The quest for knowledge is never over!
Zh: 对知识的追求永远不会结束！

En: Of course. Do it. Who knows what mysteries await?
Zh: 当然了。去吧。谁知道会有什么神秘在等待着你？

En: The quest for knowledge is never over!
Zh: 对知识的追求永远不会结束！

En: So she couldn't have made it up. Or imagined it.
Zh: 所以这可能不是她编出来的。或者想象出来的。

En: Rising, unfolding from the reeds on a hot summer's day... like a benevolent god.
Zh: 在一个炎热的夏日，从芦苇丛中升起，伸展开来……就像一个仁慈的神明。

En: Can I have your address? Just in case there's news.
Zh: “能把你的地址告诉我吗？万一有消息的话，我可以告诉你。”

En: Okay, it's 1113 Tabernacle Road. Jamrock, but...
Zh: “好吧，圣堂路1113号。加姆洛克，但是……”

En: A sigh. She doesn't think you'll need it.
Zh: 一声叹息。她觉得你用不着。

En: Are you saying you *made it up*?
Zh: “你是说这是你*编出来*的吗？”

En: You seemed to really believe it happened. Doesn't that count for something?
Zh: “你似乎真的希望发生过这件事。难道这不意味着什么吗？”

En: The story is real to you and him. That's what matters.
Zh: “这个故事对于你和他来说是真实的。这才是最重要的。”

En: So you haven't seen it? I should arrest you for lying.
Zh: “所以你没有见过？我应该因为撒谎而逮捕你。”

En: No! At least... I don't think I did. But Morell was so eager to believe my story was *evidence* of the phasmid's existence...
Zh: “不！至少……我想我没有。但是莫雷尔太渴望相信我的故事是竹节虫存在的*证据*……”

En: No, sweetie, she shakes her head, "there's more to it than that. Morell was so eager to believe my story was *evidence* of the phasmid's existence..."
Zh: “不，甜心。”她摇摇头，“远没有这么简单。莫雷尔太渴望相信我的故事是竹节虫存在的*证据*……”

En: Oh, sweetie, I wish it were that simple. But it's not. Morell was so eager to believe my story was *evidence* of the phasmid's existence...
Zh: “哦，甜心，我也希望能这么简单，不过并不是。莫雷尔太渴望相信我的故事是竹节虫存在的*证据*……”

En: That I'm some Queen of the Cryptozoologists... That... And for years his belief made *me* believe too.
Zh: “我是某种神秘动物学女王……就这样……很多年之后，他的信念让*我*也开始相信了。”

En: But now we're both getting old, and he's still working himself sick out in those reeds, looking for it... She shakes her head, still unable to meet your eyes. "But what if I was just *wrong*? I think I was..."
Zh: “但是现在我们都老了，他还在那片芦苇丛里拼命工作，寻找它……”她摇摇头，仍然无法直视你的眼睛。“如果是我*搞错*了呢？我觉得我……”

En: The lieutenant opens his notebook but doesn't write anything.
Zh: 警督打开笔记本，但是什么也没写。

En: First, tell her that her marriage hasn't been a lie.
Zh: 首先告诉她，她的婚姻不是一个谎言。

En: An acorn is not the same as the tree. That requires time, diligence, and care...
Zh: 橡子跟树是不一样的。它需要时间，勤勉和关心……

En: But it *has*, hasn't it? A seed can only bear what's inside it. The seed of love is black and oily.
Zh: 但它*就是*，不是吗？种什么样的花，就结什么样的果。爱情的种子是黑色和油腻的。

En: Wrong or not, your relationship with Morell isn't just about the phasmid.
Zh: “错误与否，你跟莫雷尔的关系不仅仅是关于竹节虫的。”

En: But it *is*. We've spent years searching for the phasmid, hunting it together. Without it, what are we? Just another pathetic old couple...
Zh: “但它*就是*啊。我们花了很多年寻找竹节虫，一起追逐它的踪迹。要是没有它，我们还算什么呢？不过又是一对可怜的老夫妻……”

En: Morell hated lecturing. You convinced him to do something real in this world.
Zh: “莫雷尔痛恨讲课。你说服了他去追寻自己真正的梦想。”

En: Normally when I think about stuff like *women* and *marriage*, I feel *bad*. When I think about you and Morell I feel okay. That counts for something, no?
Zh: “一般当我想到*女人*和*婚姻*的时候，我会有*很不好*的感觉。当我想到你和莫雷尔，我感觉很好。这才是最重要的，不是吗？”

En: If I hadn't led him down this path he could have a steady job lecturing at a university.
Zh: “如果我没有指引他走上这条道路，他可以有一份稳定的工作，在大学里讲课。”

En: Word around the canal is some weird new cryptid destroyed it.
Zh: “运河附近有传言说某种古怪的新神秘动物把它毁掉了。”

En: Probably just some technical problem.
Zh: “可能只是技术问题。”

En: Oh, now you're just having fun at an old woman's expense! In any case, thank you for looking into this. It takes a weight off my shoulders. She turns to the lieutenant. "Thank you *both*."
Zh: “哦，你这是在拿我这个老太婆说笑了！不管怎么说，感谢你的调查。我总算可以卸下肩膀上的负担了。”她转向警督。“感谢*你们两个*。”

En: Oh, now you're just having fun at an old woman's expense! In any case, thank you for looking into this. It takes a weight off my shoulders.
Zh: “哦，你这是在拿我这个老太婆说笑了！不管怎么说，感谢你的调查。我总算可以卸下肩膀上的负担了。”

En: Well, whatever the cause, I'm thankful...  She turns to the lieutenant. "To *both* of you. You've spared me another sleepless night."
Zh: “呃，不管是为了什么，我都很感激……”她转向警督。“感激*你们两个*。你们让我不用再度过一个无眠的夜晚了。”

En: Well, whatever the cause, I'm thankful. You've spared me another sleepless night.
Zh: “呃，不管是为了什么，我都很感激。你让我不用再度过一个无眠的夜晚了。”

En: I hate to ask, but *if* your investigation takes you to the other side of the coast, please do keep an eye out for my husband...
Zh: 她转向你。“我实在不想开口，不过*如果*你正好要去海边对面调查的话，请留心一下我的丈夫……

En: Listen, sweetie. I hate to ask, but if your investigation happens to take you to the other side of the coast, please do keep an eye out for my husband...
Zh: “听着，甜心。我实在不想开口，不过如果你正好要去海边对面调查的话，请留心一下我的丈夫……

En: The water lock! That was broken. Could this be it?
Zh: 那个水闸！它坏掉了。难道是这个原因？

En: Oh, thank you sweetie, truly. I'll be right here if you come across any sign of Morell.
Zh: “哦，谢谢你，甜心，真的。如果你们发现莫雷尔的任何行踪，我会一直待在这里。”

En: The water lock to the other side of the coast was broken. They probably just got stuck over there. (Proceed.)
Zh: “通往海岸对面的水闸坏了。他们可能被困在那边。”（继续。）

En: The water lock to the other side of the coast is broken. They're probably just stuck over there. (Proceed.)
Zh: “通往海岸对面的水闸坏了。他们可能被困在那边。”（继续。）

En: Tell me more about this *rare insect* your husband is looking for.
Zh: “跟我说说你丈夫寻找的这个*珍稀昆虫*。”

En: Oh, sweetie, it's *fascinating*... She catches herself. "But I shouldn't bore you with entomological minutiae."
Zh: “哦，甜心，它真的很*迷人*……”她镇定了下来。“但是不应该用这些昆虫学的细枝末节来烦你了。”

En: No, I want to hear about the insect.
Zh: “不会，我想听听这个昆虫的事。”

En: You're right. I don't have time for insect facts right now. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “你说得对。我现在没时间听昆虫的事。我们说点别的吧。”

En: The lieutenant gives you a sideways glance.
Zh: 警督斜了你一眼。

En: Of course, dear.
Zh: “当然可以，亲爱的。”

En: She was about to launch into an impassioned description, but stoppered herself. Why's that?
Zh: 她本来打算发表一篇慷慨激昂的陈述，但又停住了。为什么呢？

En: Well, she hesitates. "It's a *phasmid* technically, but..."
Zh: “呃，”她有些犹豫。“从技术层面上讲，它是一只*竹节虫*，不过……”

En: Ah, yes, *phasmatodea* -- a diverse group of insects whose bodies resemble twigs, leaves, that sort of thing. "Ghost insects," colloquially.
Zh: 啊，是的，*竹节虫目*——那是一种身体像树枝，树叶之类的昆虫。通俗来讲，就是一种“幽灵昆虫”。

En: ... where other phasmids imitate sticks or leaves, this one's a living *reed*. It disguises itself among the reeds here on the Insulindian coast.
Zh: “……其他竹节虫很像树枝或者是树叶，但是这一种简直就是活生生的*芦苇*。它把自己伪装起来，藏在伊苏林迪海岸的芦苇丛中。”

En: Hence its name: The *Insulindian phasmid* -- perhaps you'll end up co-discovering the phasmid with us, officers? She looks you in the eye and nods thoughtfully.
Zh: 她看着你的眼睛，然后若有所思地点点头。“它也因此得名：*伊苏林迪竹节虫*——也许最终你会和我们一起发现这种竹节虫，是吗，警官？”

En: Hence its name: The *Insulindian phasmid* -- perhaps you'll end up co-discovering it with us, officer? She looks you in the eye and nods thoughtfully.
Zh: 她看着你的眼睛，然后若有所思地点点头。“它也因此得名：*伊苏林迪竹节虫*——也许最终你会和我们一起发现它的存在，是吗，警官？”

En: I knew it. The lieutenant sighs. "We're gonna be chasing made-up insects with *cryptozoologists*."
Zh: “我就知道。”警督叹了口气。“我们会跟*神秘动物学家*一起追踪这种捏造出来的昆虫。”

En: There's a touch of *awe* in the way she enunciates the creature's name.
Zh: 当她清晰地说出这个生物的名字时，声音里透露出一种*敬畏*的味道。

En: It's not made-up, officer, I can *assure* you.
Zh: “它不是捏造出来的，警官。我可以向你*保证*。”

En: There's a hint of defensiveness to her retort, but also confidence. She seems to sit up a little straighter in her chair.
Zh: 她的反驳中有一种防御性的意味，还夹杂着一种自信。轮椅里的她好像稍微坐直了一些。

En: It's simply *elusive*, so much so that most *establishment* zoologists doubt it exists at all.
Zh: “它真的可以说是*神出鬼没*，以至于大部分*主流*动物学家都怀疑它根本不存在。”

En: So... is it dangerous?
Zh: “所以……它很危险吗？”

En: Is it valuable?
Zh: “它很珍贵吗？”

En: Does it have cool powers?
Zh: “它有那种很酷的超能力吗？”

En: Okay. What's so *special* about this stick-bug then? (Move on.)
Zh: “好吧。那这个竹节虫有什么*特别*的？”（继续）

En: Yes! It can blend in almost *perfectly* among the reeds. Her face lights up at the thought of it. "It's how it's stayed hidden all these years. Centuries even."
Zh: “是的！它几乎能够*完美地*混入芦苇丛中。”一想到这一点，她整张脸都亮了起来。“所以它才能隐藏了这么多年。甚至是好几个世纪。”

En: Oh, I doubt it. No one gets into cryptozoology for the *money*, sweetie.
Zh: “哦，我不这么认为。没人会因为*钱*而踏入神秘动物学的领域，甜心。”

En: She chuckles to herself. "Not at all. Why else would it hide itself so carefully?"
Zh: 她笑了起来。“完全不危险。不然它为什么会这么小心地隐藏起来？”

En: Oh dear, I'm afraid I'm not explaining this very well. It *is* very special... The woman's face flushes with embarrassment.
Zh: “哦，亲爱的，恐怕我也解释得不太清楚。它*真的*非常特别……”女人的脸因为尴尬而涨得通红。

En: Morell can explain it all much better. I wish you could hear him describe it. Then you'd understand, I'm sure.
Zh: “莫雷尔能解释地更清楚。希望你能听听他的描述。然后你就能理解了，我很肯定。”

En: She's skipping over some important parts... perhaps you'll find out more later.
Zh: 她跳过了一些很重要的部分……也许你之后能搞明白。

En: And if you see him, let him know Lena is waiting for him here at the Whirling. He gets so tangled up in his work that he may not know the water lock's been repaired. And it's *cold* out there...
Zh: “还有，如果你见到他，请转告他莉娜在飞旋旅社等他。他工作太忙的话，可能都不知道水闸已经修好了。而且那里真的很*冷*……”

En: Hmmm. Well, his expression is slightly grumpy, but his eyes are always bright and curious, like a small boy's. And his palms are quite coarse from all the field work, but he's quite gentle...
Zh: “嗯。他的神情看起来有些暴躁，眼睛总是亮晶晶的，充满好奇，像个小男孩。因为野外考察，他的手掌非常粗糙，不过他是个很温和的人……”

En: You can't go around Revachol feeling grown men's hands. If you want to find her husband, you'll need more *concrete* information.
Zh: 你总不能跑遍整个瑞瓦肖去摸每个成年男子的手掌吧。如果想找到她的丈夫，你需要更加*实在*的信息。

En: Let's try again. Why don't you try describing him as you would one of your cryptids?\n
Zh: “我们再试一次。不如试试把他当做一种神秘动物来描述？”

En: Let's try again. If I were trying to meet him on the street, what would I look for?
Zh: “我们再试一次。如果我在街上遇到他，应该寻找些什么特征？”

En: The lieutenant pulls out his notebook and begins jotting down the woman's description.
Zh: 警督拿出笔记本，开始记录女人的描述。

En: That's good enough. Let me ask you something else.
Zh: “这样应该就差不多了。让我问你一些别的问题。”

En: The woman's face flushes with embarrassment. "I'm sorry, sweetie. I'm afraid I'm not very good at this sort of thing."
Zh: 女人的脸因为尴尬而涨得通红。“很抱歉，甜心。恐怕我不太擅长做这种事。”

En: Well, some teenagers making out in the reeds saw one. They didn't know what it was, of course, but there was a brief article in a local newspaper. About their encounter with a *ghost insect* that looks like the reeds. Gary sent us the clipping.
Zh: “呃，有些青少年在芦苇丛中亲热的时候看见了一只。当然，他们根本不知道那是什么。不过本地报纸上有一个短篇文章，说的是他们遇到了一个看起来像芦苇的*幽灵昆虫*。盖理把剪报给我们送过来了。”

En: What makes you think the phasmid is around here?
Zh: “你为什么会觉得竹节虫就在这附近呢？”

En: So a newspaper clipping is all the evidence you have?
Zh: “所以一份剪报就是你拥有的所有证据？”

En: You seem really excited about this cryptid.
Zh: “你好像对这个神秘动物真的很感兴趣。”

En: Interesting. Maybe the phasmid *made* the teenagers make out?
Zh: 有意思。也许是竹节虫*让*这些青少年亲热的？

En: Enthusiasm has wiped the worry from her face. Her eyes sparkle behind her glasses.
Zh: 热情消除了她脸上的忧虑。她的眼睛在镜片后面闪闪发光。

En: I suppose I have something of a *personal* connection to the Insulindian phasmid... All scientists have their little hobby-horses.
Zh: “我猜自己应该跟伊苏林迪竹节虫有着某种*私人*的联系……所有科学家都有他们自己热衷的话题。”

En: Maybe the phasmid *made* them make out?
Zh: “也许是竹节虫*让*他们亲热的？

En: She laughs. "I doubt it. It's not like teenagers need much encouragement in *that* regard."
Zh: 她笑了。“我很怀疑。并不是说青少年在*那方面*需要太多的鼓励。”

En: Of course, most phasmid sightings turn out to be *false alarms*, but their description matched the Insulindian phasmid *perfectly*, and they didn't even know what they were looking at!
Zh: “当然了，大部分竹节虫的目击报告最后都是*假警报*，不过他们的描述*完美*地契合了伊苏林迪竹节虫的特征，而且他们甚至都不知道自己看到的是什么！”

En: Yes. She smiles a cautious smile. "Field work is a young person's game, as they say..."
Zh: “是的。”她的笑容十分拘谨。“他们都说，野外考察作业是年轻人的游戏……”

En: The lieutenant stares at his shoe, caked in mud. He doesn't say anything.
Zh: 警督盯着自己脚上满是泥泞的鞋，什么也没说。

En: You've come so far. You can't leave those locusts there, waiting on the ghost reed with no one to witness when it appears...
Zh: 你已经走了这么远。你不能把那些蝗虫留在那里，在无人见证的幽灵芦苇丛中等待它的出现……

En: Yes, I saw it... But, like I already told you, dear, no one believed me for years -- until I met Morell.
Zh: “是的，我看见了……不过，就像我之前说的，亲爱的，好多年都没人相信我——直到我遇见莫雷尔。”

En: Oh... you don't want to hear about some old woman's ramblings...
Zh: “哦……你不会想听一个老太太东拉西扯的……”

En: 'Ramblings'? Nonsense! Your description of the phasmid is the most precise I've ever heard!
Zh: “‘东拉西扯’？胡说！你对竹节虫的描述是我听过最精确的！”

En: But darling, I didn't even get the *size* of it right.
Zh: “但是，亲爱的，我甚至都没把*大小*说对。”

En: You were a *child*, my dear. Really, it's extraordinary what you were able to describe. Now go on, tell our friend about it. He's proven his interest in the field.
Zh: “你那时只是个*孩子*，亲爱的。说真的，你能描述出来已经非常厉害了。来吧，跟我们的朋友说说。他已经证明了自己对这个领域的兴趣。”

En: Reflexively, the lieutenant readies his familiar notebook.
Zh: 警督反射性地准备好了熟悉的笔记本。

En: Well, it was summer, she starts. "I was building a racing track out of sand on the beach near a tall stand of reeds. Quite a tall one, many times my height, I remember. When all of a sudden..."
Zh: “好吧，那是一个夏天，”她开口了。“我在海滩上用沙子堆赛道，旁边就是一片高高的芦苇丛。真的很高，我记得比我高好几个头。然后突然之间……”

En: You must be mistaken. I'm a connoisseur of delusional ramblings.
Zh: “你一定是搞错了。我是妄想漫谈的鉴赏家。”

En: Let's change the subject for now.
Zh: “我们还是换个话题吧。”

En: That's kind of you, but I've got to demur. It's hard to tell a story you don't really believe in anymore...
Zh: “你人真好，但是我必须提出异议。我很难讲述一个你不再相信的故事……”

En: That's true, yes. I'm almost certain neither my mother nor my grandmother knew of it. It was only when I started telling my story as a teenager that boys would tell me: Lena... She lowers her voice, imitating a boy.
Zh: “是真的，没错。我几乎可以肯定自己的母亲或者是外婆都不知道这件事。直到十几岁的时候，我把故事讲给男孩子们听，他们会告诉我：莉娜……”她压低声音，模仿着男孩的语气。

En: The glance is tender, yes, but tempered by something else, a thought she can't express even to him... Interesting.
Zh: 她的目光是温柔的，没错，但是还夹杂着别的什么东西，一种她甚至无法向他表述的想法……有意思。

En: I ran back home to my grandmother and asked her if *reeds* could *walk* and told her they were looking at me. She chuckles. "Of course, she just laughed at me, but I knew what I'd seen..."
Zh: “我跑回家去找外婆，问她*芦苇*是不是会*走*，还说它们在看我。”她轻笑出声。“当然，她只是笑了笑，但是我知道自己看见了什么……”

En: For years it was a story I told at parties, when I wanted to impress *boys*, that sort of thing. She brushes her hair back. "Of course, most people just took it as a strange, amusing anecdote. So did I, honestly. But then I met Morell..."
Zh: “这个故事我在派对上讲了很多年，那时的我想要打动那些*男孩*，诸如此类的。”她把头发梳向脑后。“当然，大多数人都把它当成是一个奇怪又好玩的轶事。老实说，我也一样。不过之后我遇到了莫雷尔……”

En: It has a taste *you're* quite familiar with.
Zh: 里面有一种*你*非常熟悉的味道。

En: Take it from me: Love *is* a lie. The sooner you accept it, the better.\n
Zh: “相信我：爱情*是*一个谎言。你接受的越早，结果就越好。”

En: It's like I've always said: Women are she-vipers who can't be trusted.
Zh: “就好像我一直说的：女人是不可以信任的毒蛇。”

En: Lying has gotten you this far, why stop now?
Zh: “撒谎已经让你走了这么远，为什么现在要停下呢？”

En: You know, the Perikarnassians theorized that *love* was the key to immortality...
Zh: “你知道的，佩里卡纳西斯建立了一种理论：*爱情*是通往永恒的钥匙……”

En: Oh, sweetie. I don't think the Perikarnassians were thinking of people in *my* situation. She looks down at her legs...
Zh: “哦，甜心。我不觉得佩里卡纳西斯考虑过*我*这种处境的人。”她低头看着自己的腿……

En: But if the dream comes to naught, what good is it? No, the thing is... She looks down at her legs...
Zh: “不过如果梦想化为乌有，那还有什么用呢？不，事情是……”她低头看着自己的腿……

En: Oh, of course it counts for something. But we've had our hard times too... She looks down at her legs...
Zh: “哦，它当然重要了。但是我们也有过自己的艰难处境……”她低头看着自己的腿……

En: I broke it. Drove my motor carriage into a billboard above the lock, blocking the gates.
Zh: “我弄坏的。开着我的汽車撞上了水闸上面的广告牌，它掉下去堵住了水闸。”

En: Oh, sweetie... Well, I'm glad you survived that. And I'm grateful for the information -- it will spare me another sleepless night. Thank you *both*. She turns to the lieutenant.
Zh: “哦，甜心……很高兴你活了下来。而且很感谢你这条信息——这样我不会又度过一个无眠之夜了。感谢*你们两个*。”她转向警督。

En: Oh, sweetie... Well, I'm glad you survived that. And I'm grateful for the information -- it will spare me another sleepless night.
Zh: “哦，甜心……很高兴你活了下来。而且很感谢你这条信息——这样我不会又度过一个无眠之夜了。”

En: My apologies, ma'am. I did not mean to undermine your hobby.
Zh: “很抱歉，夫人。我不是有意要破坏你的爱好。”

En: It's not a hobby, dear. It's a *sub-field* of zoology, one specializing in animal species that are so exceedingly rare that many assume them to be *extinct* or even *fictitious*...
Zh: “这不是我的爱好，亲爱的。它是动物学的一个*子学科*，专门从事极其罕见的动物物种研究，罕见到很多人会假设它们已经*灭绝*，或者甚至只是*虚构*的……”

En: It's just police-work.
Zh: “只是警察事务而已。”

En: Yes, but a junior officer could... He takes off his glasses and thinks. Then puts them back on without wiping.
Zh: “是的，不过交给一个初级警官就够了……”他取下眼镜，沉思着。然后没有擦拭就重新戴了回去。

En: Fine, our victim's already been dead more than a week. One more diversion won't change anything.
Zh: “好吧，我们的受害者已经死了一个多星期。再稍微分散点注意力也改变不了什么。”

En: Oh yeah. Here comes the *interesting*.
Zh: 哦耶。*有意思*的部分来了。

En: There's a touch of *awe* in the way she enunciated the creature's name.
Zh: 当她清晰地说出这个生物的名字时，声音里透露出一种*敬畏*的味道。

En: That's not special at all. Of course it blends in, most insects do. You don't even have to be a stick insect for that...
Zh: 那一点也不特别。它当然会融入其中，大部分昆虫都这样。你甚至不用成为一只竹节虫也能做到……

En: Officer... I hate to ask, but perhaps you could check those traps one more time? Only if you're in the area... In truth, I know Gary won't be up to it, and I'm concerned about my husband's health...
Zh: “警官……我实在不想开口，不过也许你能再去检查一遍那些陷阱？如果你去那片区域的话……说实话，我知道盖理无法胜任，而且我很担心丈夫的健康……”

En: Alright, when you put it that way -- I'll help. (Accept task.)
Zh: “好吧，既然你都这么说了——我会帮忙的。”（接受任务。）

En: Sorry, I have too many other things on my plate. Wish you the best of luck. (Reject task.)
Zh: “抱歉，我现在手上的事太多。祝你好运。”（拒绝任务。）

En: Oh, thank you so much. And don't catch a cold out there! She smiles up at you brightly.
Zh: “哦，真是太谢谢你了。千万别着凉了！”她朝你露出灿烂的笑容。

En: I understand. Best of luck with your work as well. She smiles up at you, doing her best not to look disappointed.
Zh: “我理解。也祝你的工作一切顺利。”她冲你笑了笑，尽力不让自己看起来太失望。

En: I hope I've been useful.
Zh: “希望我能有所帮助。”

En: Don't worry. You've asked me to look into it and I will.
Zh: “别担心。我答应你会调查，就一定会这么做的。”

En: Thank you, sweetie. I'm awfully sorry to impose on you like this -- maybe there's something *I* can help *you* with in the meanwhile?
Zh: “谢谢你，甜心。真的很抱歉这样利用你——也许与此同时我也能*帮到你*？”

En: Her voice is shaky. What is going on here?
Zh: 她的声音在颤抖。出什么事了？

En: Very strange... why is she not letting you do this? It's like she's given up.
Zh: 很奇怪……她为什么不让你这么做？她不像是放弃的样子。

En: Very strange... you get the sense she wouldn't have let you do this even if you did volunteer. Why?
Zh: 很奇怪……你感觉到她不想让你这么做，即便你是自愿的。为什么？

En: Lena, what's wrong? You seem different.
Zh: “莉娜，出什么事了？你看起来有些不一样。”

En: Different -- how? The half-moons of her glasses reflect you as she looks up at you.
Zh: “不一样——怎么不一样？”她抬头看着你，半月形的眼镜反射出你的模样。

En: Answering a question with a question, for example -- defensive isn't her usual style.
Zh: 比如说，用一个问题来回答问题——防御不是她一贯的作风。

En: You're... on the back foot.
Zh: “你……的处境很不利。”

En: You've given up on the phasmid.
Zh: “你放弃竹节虫了。”

En: You're not telling me the whole story.
Zh: “你没有把完整的故事告诉我。”

En: Never mind.
Zh: “没事了。”

En: Right... She looks around. "It's almost time for me to go too. I can't see what use I can be here..."
Zh: “是啊……”她看看周围。“我也差不多该走了。我不知道留在这里还有什么用……”

En: I'm... in doubt, sweetie. That's all. Everyone is, now and then.
Zh: “我……在怀疑，甜心。就是这样。大家偶尔都会的。”

En: I'm... not. It's not that... She doesn't know how to end the sentence.
Zh: “我……没有。并不是……”她不知道该怎么说完这句话。

En: You're in doubt? About what?
Zh: “你在怀疑？怀疑什么？”

En: What is it?
Zh: “是什么？”

En: It's... a strange feeling. She looks down, biting her lower lip. "I haven't really told this to anyone, but... you *are* a police officer."
Zh: “这是一种……奇怪的感觉。”她低着头，咬紧下唇。“我真的没有告诉过任何人。但是……你*是*一个警官。”

En: Do you ever wonder if some lovely story from your childhood is just that... a story? Or a dream?
Zh: “你有没有想过自己孩提时代的可爱故事只是个……故事？或者是一场梦？”

En: Hunching her shoulders now, she seems even smaller than she is. Like a sad young girl.
Zh: 耸起肩膀的她，似乎看起来更瘦小了。就像一个伤心的年轻姑娘。

En: Seeing the Insulindian phasmid was just a story I used to tell people. I didn't really think about whether it was *real* or not.
Zh: “看见伊苏林迪竹节虫只是我过去给别人讲的一个故事。我真的没想过它到底是不是*真的*。”

En: And when a police officer asks -- you must answer.
Zh: 当一个警察开口询问的时候——你必须回答。

En: He's so proud of it. He always tells everyone...
Zh: “他是那么的骄傲。他一直告诉所有人……”

En: A terrible sting in the heart. Regret.
Zh: 心里一阵可怕的剧痛。后悔。

En: That I'm a queen. An extraordinary witness to grace.
Zh: 我是女王，是优雅与恩惠的特别见证人。

En: He's hiding. These things are tough on him. Matters of love, not violence or deceit.
Zh: 他在躲避。这些事对他来说很艰难。关乎爱情，不是暴力或者欺骗。

En: And? I need something *good* here.
Zh: 还有呢？我需要一些*好*东西。

En: All qualities these two seem to share in abundance.
Zh: 这两个人似乎有很多相同的特质。

En: To hell with this. *I* still believe you saw the phasmid.
Zh: “见鬼去吧。*我*还是相信你看见竹节虫了。”

En: Thing is, you're not sure you made it up either.
Zh: “问题是，你也不确定这是不是你编的。”

En: Okay, it's a false memory. Doesn't mean all cryptids aren't real. Chase another one.
Zh: “好吧，这是一段虚假的记忆。但是也不代表所有神秘动物都不是真的。再去追逐别的吧。”

En: You're right about all of it. And I need to get back to my work.
Zh: “你说的一切都很对。我需要回到自己的工作上去。”

En: You know, this reminds you of the Perikarnassian Theory of Love. In essence, love is a relay -- out of death. Generation by generation, our love remains in our progeny. Lay some of *that* on her!
Zh: 你知道吗，这让你想到了佩里卡纳西斯关于爱情的理论。从本质上讲，爱情是一场接力赛——超越了生死。一代接着一代，我们的爱情还保留在后裔子孙里。让她也*感受*一下吧。

En: I was a paraplegic before we met. He didn't know before I arrived... on our first date. If I weren't the Queen of the Cryptozoologists... If I didn't tell him that story...
Zh: “在我们见面之前，我就是个截瘫病人。我们第一次约会的时候……我没到之前，他都毫不知情。如果我不是神秘动物学的女王……如果我没有告诉过他那个故事……”

En: She has to swallow to relax her throat. It's keeping her from talking.
Zh: 她必须依靠吞咽动作来放松自己的喉咙。她说不出话来了。

En: He'd still be into you. That's not how these things work.
Zh: “他还是会爱你的。这种事情不是这样的。”

En: *Now* I understand why you haven't told him about your doubts.
Zh: “*现在*我能理解你为什么不愿意把自己的怀疑告诉他了。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: I was a paraplegic before we met. He didn't know before I came in, on our first date. If I weren't the Queen of the Cryptozoologists... If I didn't tell him that story...
Zh: “在我们见面之前，我就是个截瘫病人。我们第一次约会的时候……我没到之前，他都毫不知情。如果我不是神秘动物学的女王……如果我没有告诉过他那个故事……”

En: She has to swallow to relax her throat. It's keeping her from talking.
Zh: 她必须依靠吞咽的动作来放松自己的喉咙。她说不出话来了。

En: You might be right, detective... She looks down at her legs...
Zh: “也许你是对的，警探……”她低头看向自己的双腿……

En: Maybe. But then why do I not dare tell him? She sighs. "I've wasted enough of your time with this drama. I really must stop talking about it, lest I start crying and waste *more* of your time."
Zh: “也许吧。但我为什么不敢告诉他呢？”她叹了口气。“我的这出戏已经让你浪费太多时间了。我真该停下来，不然我会忍不住哭出来，浪费你*更多*的时间。”

En: What you have to know is, the Insulindian phasmid probably does not exist. Let us fools chase our ghosts. There are a million better things to do with your life.
Zh: “你必须知道，伊苏林迪竹节虫可能并不存在。让我们这些傻瓜去追逐自己心中的幽灵吧。你的生活中还有数百万件更重要的事情可以做。”

En: She sighs. "I've wasted enough of your time with this drama. I really must stop talking about it, lest I start crying and waste *more* of your time."
Zh: 她叹了口气。“我的这出戏已经让你浪费太多时间了。我真该停下来，不然我会忍不住哭出来，浪费你*更多*的时间。”

En: She sighs. "I've wasted enough of your time with this drama. I really must go before I start crying and waste *more* of it."
Zh: 她叹了口气。“我的这出戏已经让你浪费太多时间了。我真的得走了，免得我会忍不住哭出来，浪费你*更多*的时间。”

En: My point is, the Insulindian phasmid *probably* does not exist. And you were right to put your bug-chasing days behind you.
Zh: “我的观点是，伊苏林迪竹节虫*可能*并不存在。你那那些追逐昆虫的日子抛在脑后是对的。”

En: *Are* there? Some of the other things are pretty bad.
Zh: *有*吗？还有一些事也很糟糕。

En: You too, sweetie. She flicks a switch and the engine on her chair turns on with a whir. "Thank you for everything. Truly.  Even though it turned out to be a..." The sentence remains unfinished.
Zh: “你也是，甜心。”她按下一个开关，轮椅的引擎嗖地一声启动了。“真心感谢你做的一切。即便最后这可能只是一个……”她的话没有说完。

En: A true believer, she looks out the window. "Sometimes I still see it, too. The real memory of it. How it was there... Not the memory *of* the memory. But it's so hard to tell the two apart..."
Zh: “一个真正的信徒，”她看向窗外。“有时候我依然能看见。那个真实的记忆。它是怎样的……而不是我记忆*中*的记忆。但是真的很难把两者分清……”

En: Either way, I should go. Poor Morell is running a fever and I need to get him home to Jamrock. Before we overstay our welcome with Gary.
Zh: “不管怎么说，我该走了。可怜的莫雷尔发烧了，我需要把他带回加姆洛克的家，免得在热情的盖理家逗留太久。”

En: I'm not sure of anything. She looks out the window. "Sometimes I still see it, you know. The real memory. Not the memory of the memory, but it's so hard to tell the two apart..."
Zh: “我什么也不能确定。”她看向窗外。“你知道吗，有时候我还是能看见。那段真实的记忆。不是记忆中的记忆，但是真的很难把两者分清……”

En: Of course not -- I didn't mean to say my situation discredits the field. There have been *some* confirmed discoveries. And a lot of hoaxes too, but still... She looks to the door.
Zh: “当然不是了——并不是说我的处境败坏了这个领域的名声。确实有过*一些*确认的发现。而且也有很多恶作剧，不过……”她看向大门。

En: Yes, I should go now. Poor Morell is running a fever. I need to get him home to Jamrock, before we overstay our welcome with Gary.
Zh: “是的，现在我该走了。可怜的莫雷尔发烧了，我需要把他带回加姆洛克的家，免得在热情的盖理家逗留太久。”

En: Are you sure you don't need help getting to Gary's?
Zh: “你确定不需要把你送到盖理家吗？”

En: You do that. I'll check the traps one more time.
Zh: “你去吧。我再去检查一下陷阱。”

En: Be careful out there, Lena. (Conclude.)
Zh: “在外面要小心一点，莉娜。”（结束）

En: Oh no. Thank you, but I can get there on my own, this old thing is gas-powered. She taps her chair. "And then a taxi home. It's not so bad."
Zh: “哦，不用了。谢谢你，我可以自己过去，这个老伙计是气动的。”她拍了拍轮椅。“然后再打車回家。其实没那么糟糕的。”

En: Oh sweetie... She looks at you, worried. "Please don't get stuck on a dream. Take it from me and Morell."
Zh: “哦，甜心……”她担忧地看着你。“请不要被困在梦境里了。相信我和莫雷尔的话吧。”

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En: No one can STOP you from finding the phasmid.
Zh: 没人能阻止你找到竹节虫。

En: It's been a pleasure, ma'am. (Conclude.)
Zh: “这是我的荣幸，夫人。”（结束）

En: The rest of it is pretty grim. Thanks for bringing some light. (Conclude.)
Zh: “其余的一切都很残酷。感谢你带来了一些光明。”（总结。）

En: Like that, she drives off. The gas engine putters quietly, as she gets to the doors -- then pushes them open. Outside it's dark.
Zh: 就这样，她走了。气动引擎安静地推动着她驶向大门——然后把它们推开。外面漆黑一片。

En: Like that, she drives off. The gas engine putters quietly, as she gets to the doors -- then pushes them open. Outside it's raining.
Zh: 就这样，她走了。气动引擎安静地推动着她驶向大门——然后把它们推开。外面正淅淅沥沥地下着雨。

En: Like that, she drives off. The gas engine putters quietly, as she gets to the doors -- then pushes them open. Outside it's snowing.
Zh: 就这样，她走了。气动引擎安静地推动着她驶向大门——然后把它们推开。外面正下着雪。

En: Like that, she drives off. The gas engine putters quietly, as she gets to the doors -- then pushes them open. Outside the cold coastal wind blows.
Zh: 就这样，她走了。气动引擎安静地工作着，她驶向大门——然后把它们推开。外面刮起了冰冷的海风。

En: We should go too.
Zh: “我们也该走了。”

En: Likewise, sweetie. She flicks a switch on her chair and the engine turns on with a whir. "Thank you for everything. Truly. Even though it turned out to be a..." The sentence remains unfinished.
Zh: “你也一样，甜心。”她按下一个开关，轮椅的引擎嗖地一声打开了。“真心感谢你做的一切。即便最后这可能只是一个……”她的话没有说完。

En: You're welcome, sweetie. She flicks a switch on her chair and the engine turns on with a whir. "I'm glad it helped you, even though it turned out to be a..." The sentence remains unfinished.
Zh: “不客气，甜心。”她按下一个开关，轮椅的引擎嗖地一声打开了。“很高兴能帮到你，即便最后这可能只是一个……”她的话没有说完。

En: Somewhere out there -- a kilometre to the southeast -- a gust of wind shakes the Feld building, rattling dusty windows, beckoning with strange coldness. To ask the wind once more.
Zh: 外面的某个地方——东南方向1公里的地方——一阵风摇动着菲尔德大厦，锈蚀的窗户发出嘎吱嘎吱的声响，带着奇怪的冰冷召唤着你。再问一次风吧。

En: Well... I'm not sure, detective. I'm sorry. Wealthy people tend to be educated, but you're not likely to find one of those in Martinaise...
Zh: “这个……我也不确定，警探。很抱歉。一般有钱人都受到了良好的教育，不过你也不太可能在马丁内斯找到这种人……”

En: Our first, yes. The old woman sighs tenderly...
Zh: “我们的第一次，没错。”老妇人轻轻叹了口气……

En: The sigh is tender, yes, but tempered by something else, a thought she can't express... Interesting.
Zh: 这是一声轻柔的叹息，是的，不过被另一种东西调和了，一种她无法表达的念头……有意思。

En: Wouldn't you know, it's blocked by a big butter billboard -- it fell right into the water!
Zh: “你不知道吗，它被一块巨大的黄油广告牌堵住了——它掉进水里了！”

En: Oh, sweetie, if it weren't for you I'd be looking forward to another sleepless night. Thank you, from the bottom of my heart!
Zh: “哦，甜心，如果没有你，我很可能又要度过一个无眠的夜晚了。谢谢，我打心底里感谢你！”

En: Just a moment. The old woman turns back to the cafeteria manager.
Zh: “稍等一下。”老夫人转向餐厅经理。

En: Howdy, Lena! What's kicking?
Zh: “你好，莉娜！你怎么样？”

En: Sorry, ma'am, I didn't mean to eavesdrop on your conversation. Tell me how I might make it up to you?
Zh: “抱歉，夫人。我不是有意要偷听你们的谈话。告诉我怎样才能补偿你呢？”

En: Good day, ma'am. Everything alright?
Zh: “日安，夫人。一切都还好吧？”

En: Just a moment, officer.
Zh: “请稍等，警官。”

En: The Union office probably has a phone, but I can't really get there, or to the phone booth down the coast... And Gary's phone is dead, too... She sighs.
Zh: “工会办公室可能有电话，不过我又不能去那里，或者是海岸边的电话亭……而且盖理的电话也没电了……”她叹了口气。

En: Please don't trouble yourself about me, sweetie. I was just hoping to make a call, but the Whirling's phone line isn't working.
Zh: “请不要为我操心了，甜心。我只是希望能打个电话，不过飞旋旅社电话线不通。”

En: A faint smile tells you she appreciates the effort, but at the moment her mind is on more serious matters...
Zh: 脸上的微笑告诉你，她很感激你的努力，不过现在她的脑子里在想一些更严肃的问题……

En: Please don't trouble yourself about me, sweetie. I was just hoping to make a call, but the Whirling's phone line isn't working.
Zh: “请不要为我操心了，甜心。我只是希望能打个电话，不过飞旋旅社电话线不通。”

En: Why did you need to use the phone, anyway?
Zh: “不过，你为什么需要用电话呢？”

En: That's all for now, ma'am. [Leave.]
Zh: “暂时就这些了，夫人。”[离开。]

En: Wait, what's wrong with the phone line?
Zh: “等等，电话线出什么问题了？”

En: The manager was vague about it. She frowns.
Zh: “经理说的很含糊。”她皱起眉头。

En: Tell me more about Morell. Looks, character, your relationship...
Zh: “跟我说说这个莫雷尔。长相，性格，你们的关系……”

En: So your husband is some kind of scientist?
Zh: “所以你的丈夫是某种科学家？”

En: Maybe you could convince her to tell you about some *cool* cryptids?
Zh: 也许你可以说服她告诉你一些很*酷*的神秘动物？

En: Less worried about husband.
Zh: 少为丈夫担心。

En: Lena knows Morell is okay.
Zh: 莉娜知道莫雷尔没事。

En: Morell is back.
Zh: 莫雷尔回来了。

En: Has the green ape pen.
Zh: 绿人猿笔。

En: Has the teratorn tie.
Zh: 畸鸟领带。

En: Lena offered to tell you.
Zh: 莉娜提出要告诉你。

En: What is this *expedition* your husband was on?
Zh: “你的丈夫在进行什么样的*探险*？”

En: It's a pseudo-science that attempts to legitimize *research* into mythological beasts and urban legends. The lieutenant sounds unimpressed.
Zh: “那是一种伪科学，试图赋予神秘生物和都市传说*研究*合法性。”警督好像没什么兴趣。

En: That's one opinion, yes. And people are entitled to their opinions...
Zh: “这只是一种意见，没错。而且大家都有权表达自己的意见……”

En: Oh dear... I'm not sure where to begin...
Zh: “天啦……我不知道该从哪里开始……”

En: How long have you been married?
Zh: “你已经结婚多久了？”

En: She's used to playing off such insults casually, but they still affect her.
Zh: 她习惯了漫不经心地处理这种侮辱，不过对她还是有影响的。

En: You mentioned traps. Could there be some problem with them?
Zh: “你提到了陷阱。会不会那些东西出了什么问题？”

En: We'll be celebrating our *sixteenth anniversary* this autumn. Her smile is soft. "Not the most numerically satisfying anniversary, but I like the less obvious milestones even more, somehow..."
Zh: “今年秋天我们就要庆祝*16周年*结婚纪念了。”她的笑容很温柔。“从数字上来说并不算很让人满意的纪念日，不过不知道为什么，我反而更喜欢那些比较平淡的里程碑……”

En: How did the two of you meet?
Zh: “你们俩是怎么认识的？”

En: Are they in a *rough neighbourhood*?
Zh: “他们现在是不是身处一个*粗暴恶劣的街区*？”

En: Just some field work, sweetie. Morell is a highly trained scientist. He and his assistant, Gary, are studying an *extremely rare* species of insect...
Zh: “只是做些现场工作，甜心。莫雷尔是一个受过良好训练的科学家。他和他的助手，盖理，正在研究一种*极其罕见*的昆虫物种……”

En: But they should have returned by now. They were just going down the coast, across the water lock, to set a few traps. He said they'd be back on Monday... She sighs. "What could be keeping them?"
Zh: “但是现在他们应该已经回来了。他们只是去海岸放些陷阱，就在水闸边附近。他说周一就能回来……”她叹了一口气。“到底是什么留住了他们？”

En: There's a nameless old fishing village nearby, but we've never had any trouble there before... She stops, then continues, worried. "There is a lot of crime around here, isn't there?"
Zh: “附近有一个无名的旧渔村，不过我们在那里从来没有遇到过麻烦……”她停了一会儿，然后继续担忧地说到。“这附近有很多犯罪活动，不是吗？”

En: I doubt it, sweetie. The traps aren't big enough for humans to get caught in them. They're for the insect. Anyway... She looks around.
Zh: “我有点怀疑，甜心。陷阱没那么大，是不会把人困在里面的。它们是用来抓昆虫的。不管怎么说……”她看了看周围。

En: I can't say it's the best part of town, but I wouldn't worry *too much* about a pair of grown men travelling together.
Zh: “不能说是城里最好的区域，不过两个成年男人一起行动的话，我也不会*过度*担心。”

En: Maybe your husband went off on an adventure?
Zh: “也许你的丈夫去冒险了？”

En: Wait, who's this 'Gary' person? Do you trust him?
Zh: “等等，这个叫‘盖理’的人是谁？你信任他吗？”

En: Oh, sweetie. It's nothing like that... She smiles.
Zh: 她笑了。“哦，甜心。不是这样的……”

En: Gary's as loyal as they come. I'd trust him with my husband's life any day.
Zh: “盖理是个非常忠诚的人。我随时都能把丈夫的性命托付给他。”

En: Goodness, no! Well, we did get separated once before, during that *monsoon* in South Safre. But we were younger then, and Morell always takes the appropriate precautions...
Zh: “天啦，不是的！呃，我们之前确实分开过，在南萨弗里的一个*雨季*。不过那时候我们很年轻，而且莫雷尔总是采取了很恰当的预防措施……”

En: Via a dating agency, I'm ashamed to say. I was looking to get back into the scene after recovering from my accident, and he'd just divorced...
Zh: “说来惭愧，是通过一个婚姻介绍所认识的。从事故中恢复过来之后，我想要重新找回自己的生活，而他刚刚离婚……”

En: We hit it off and, well, here we are. She smiles wistfully.
Zh: “我们一拍即合，然后一直到了现在。”她的笑容中流露出渴望的意味。

En: The lieutenant doesn't want to agitate the woman.
Zh: 警督不想惊扰这个女人。

En: It's really just a *sub-field* of zoology, one specializing in animal species that are so exceedingly rare that many assume them to be *extinct* or even *fictitious*...
Zh: “其实它只是动物学的一个*子学科*，专门从事极其罕见的动物物种研究，罕见到很多人会假设它们已经*灭绝*，或者甚至只是*虚构*的……”

En: Searching for such species -- called *cryptids* -- is difficult and often thankless, and frankly, many scientists are too lazy to do it. Universities these days are rarely interested in supporting *real* research.
Zh: “研究这类物种——也就是*神秘动物*——困难重重，而且经常吃力不讨好，老实说，很多科学家因为太懒而不愿意干这个。现在的大学极少有兴趣支持*真正的*研究。”

En: The quality of research at Revacholian universities *has* been on the decline, but you doubt there was ever a time when *cryptozoology* was embraced by the academic elite.
Zh: 瑞瓦肖大学的科研质量*一直*在走下坡路，但是你很怀疑*神秘动物学*会不会有被这些学术精英接受的一天。

En: I think I have all the information I need. Let's move on. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我想我应该得到所有必要信息了。我们继续吧。”（总结。）

En: The water lock on the canal was broken, so your husband probably just got stuck on the other side of the coast.
Zh: “运河的水闸坏了，所以你的丈夫可能被困在了海岸对面。”

En: The water lock on the canal is broken, so your husband is probably just stuck on the other side of the coast.
Zh: “运河的水闸坏了，所以你的丈夫可能被困在了海岸对面。”

En: Hello, sweetie. How are you? She sounds tired.
Zh: “你好啊，甜心。你还好吗？”她听起来很疲惫。

En: Yes, sweetie?
Zh: “什么事，甜心？”

En: Oh, my! What happened to the water lock?
Zh: “哦，天啦！水闸出什么事了？”

En: It looks like some maniac crashed his motor carriage into it. Probably Jacob Irw.
Zh: “看起来好像有个疯子开車把它撞坏了。也许是雅各布·欧文。”

En: You may not believe it, but it looks like some maniac crashed his motor carriage into it.
Zh: “也许你不会相信，不过看起来好像有个疯子开車把它撞坏了。”

En: Oh, sweetie, if it weren't for you I'd be looking forward to another sleepless night. Thank you, from the bottom of my heart! She turns to the lieutenant. "Thank you *both*."
Zh: “哦，甜心，如果没有你，我很可能又要度过一个无眠的夜晚了。我打心底里感谢你！”她转向警督。“感谢*你们两个*。”

En: Oh, sweetie, if it weren't for you I'd be looking forward to another sleepless night. Thank you, from the bottom of my heart!
Zh: “哦，甜心，如果没有你，我很可能又要度过一个无眠的夜晚了。我打心底里感谢你！”

En: Her eyes light up. "What wonderful news!"
Zh: 她的眼睛亮了起来。“真是个好消息！”

En: Thank you, dear. You've already done so much, but if your investigation happens to take you to the other side of the coast, please do keep an eye out for my husband...
Zh: “谢谢你，亲爱的。你已经做了很多，不过如果你正好要去海边对面调查的话，请留心一下我的丈夫……”

En: If I see him, I'll let him know you're here -- when or *if* I get there. (Accept task.)
Zh: “如果见到他，我会告诉他你在这里——等到我去那边，或者*如果*我会去的话。”（接受任务。）

En: I'd really like to hear about more cryptids.
Zh: “我很想多了解一些神秘动物的事。”

En: Oh, forgive me for not introducing myself. My name is Lena. My husband and I are staying with our friend Gary just down the street, but I come here for tea when they're away.
Zh: “哦，请原谅，我还没有自我介绍。我叫莉娜。我，还有我们的朋友盖理就住在街边，每当他们不在我就自己来这喝茶。”

En: Wait, what's wrong with the phone line?
Zh: “等等，电话线出什么问题了？”

En: What did you need the phone for anyway?
Zh: “不过你需要电话做什么？”

En: The manager was vague about it. She frowns.
Zh: “经理说的很含糊。”她皱起眉头。

En: She's agitated, judging from the way she keeps pulling at the frayed edge of her blanket.
Zh: 她很不安，从她不断拉扯毛毯磨损的边缘就能判断出来。

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi. He shakes her hand brusquely. "May I ask what you needed to use the phone for, ma'am?"
Zh: “曷城警督。”他有些唐突地跟她握了握手。“我能问你需要电话做什么吗，夫人？”

En: What did you need the phone for anyway?
Zh: “不过你需要电话做什么？”

En: To let the young woman who's house-sitting for us know that we may be delayed. Morell -- my husband -- and our friend Gary were supposed to get back by Monday night, but they're still missing and I haven't heard from them...
Zh: “想告诉那个帮我们看家的小姑娘，我们可能要延误了。莫雷尔——我的丈夫——还有我们的朋友盖理本来应该周一晚上回来，不过现在还没见到人影，而且我没还收到他们的消息……”

En: That's just it! This isn't like him at all. He always plans his expeditions so carefully...
Zh: “这就是问题！这一点也不像他的作风。他每次探险都计划得很小心……”

En: “But you have more important things to worry about.” She glances out the window toward the bay.
Zh: “但是你还有更重要的事要操心。”她透过窗户望向海湾。

En: To let the young woman who's house-sitting for us know that we may be delayed. Morell, my husband, and Gary were supposed to get back by Monday night, but they're still missing and I haven't heard from them...
Zh: “想告诉那个帮我们看家的小姑娘，我们可能要延误了。莫雷尔——我的丈夫——还有我们的朋友盖理本来应该周一晚上回来，不过现在还没见到人影，而且我没还收到他们的消息……”

En: Oh, you're such a dear! Thank you, sweetie.
Zh: “哦，你人真好！谢谢你，甜心。”

En: A cold breeze hisses through dense thickets of reeds.... Something sweet in it, somnolent. A damp chill goes down your spine. When you look around, you're still in the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: 一阵冷风从浓密地芦苇丛中呼啸而过……里面有种甜甜的东西，让人昏昏欲睡。一股潮湿的寒气顺着你的脊椎向下流窜。你看向四周，发现自己还在褴褛飞旋。

En: You hear that? Someone's *missing*. There could be *foul play* afoot!
Zh: 你听见了吗？有人*失踪*了。很可能有*严重违法行为*正在进行！

En: I love missing persons cases.
Zh: 我喜欢人口失踪案。

En: Missing persons aren't really my style.
Zh: 人口失踪真的不是我的风格。

En: You don't have a *style*, you have a *duty*. Now start asking questions, like a real law enforcement official.
Zh: 你没有什么*风格*，你只有*职责*。现在开始问问题吧，像个真正的执法人员一样。

En: That's right. Now skip the foreplay. Time to dive into the dark alleys, start shaking down the usual suspects -- you know, *legwork*.
Zh: 没错。现在跳过前戏。是时候潜入黑暗小巷，开始追踪那些常见的嫌疑犯——你懂得，*跑外勤*。

En: I was also hoping she'd heard from Morell... She looks down.
Zh: 她低下头。“希望她收到了莫雷尔的消息……”

En: Okay, I'll bite. Has your husband gone missing before?
Zh: “好吧，我会注意的。你的丈夫之前失踪过吗？”

En: She asked about you because she's afraid to hear about the traps. But that's what she really wants news of.
Zh: 她问起你是因为害怕听到陷阱的事。不过那才是她真正想要听到的消息。

En: Dark circles around her eyes. She's absolutely exhausted.
Zh: 她的眼睛周围出现了黑眼圈。她绝对是累坏了。

En: Of course, dear. Is there a particular cryptid that you're interested in learning about?
Zh: “当然可以，亲爱的。你们俩有没有对哪种神秘动物特别好奇的呢？”

En: I've changed my mind. I'll help you find your husband after all.
Zh: “我改变主意了。我打算帮你去找你丈夫。”

En: And there's no public phones nearby?
Zh: “这附近有公用电话吗？”

En: The closest phone booth is down the coast. Sorry for the inconvenience, ma'am. The cafeteria manager appears genuinely apologetic.
Zh: “最近的公用电话亭在海岸边。抱歉给你添麻烦了，夫人。”餐厅经理好像真心感到抱歉。

En: It's fine, I understand. Thank you anyway. She turns back to you with a weary smile. "I'm glad to see you again, dear."
Zh: “没事的，我理解。还是要谢谢你。”她转向你，笑容有些疲惫。“很高兴又见到你了，亲爱的。”

En: She's agitated and keeps pulling at the frayed edge of her blanket.
Zh: 她很不安，从她不断拉扯毛毯磨损的边缘就能判断出来。

En: The closest phone booth is down the coast. Sorry for the inconvenience, ma'am. He appears to be genuinely apologetic.
Zh: “最近的公用电话亭在海岸边。抱歉给你添麻烦了，夫人。”餐厅经理好像真心感到抱歉。

En: It's fine, I understand. Thank you anyway. She turns back to you with a weary smile. "Now, then."
Zh: “没事的，我理解。还是要谢谢你。”她转向你，笑容有些疲惫。“现在有什么事吗。”

En: She turns back to the cafeteria manager. "And there's no public phones nearby?"
Zh: 她转向餐厅经理。“附近没有公用电话吗？”

En: He sighs. "If you say so."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“如果你这么说的话。”

En: I think it would be good to take a break from the hanged-man for a little while.
Zh: “我觉得从吊人的案子里抽身出来稍微休息一下，应该也挺好的。”

En: A sigh. He takes off his glasses and wipes the lenses on his jacket before replacing them.
Zh: 一声叹息。他取下眼镜，用夹克擦了擦镜片，然后重新戴了回去。

En: Well, the man's already been dead more than a week... He concludes. "I suppose a *small* diversion won't hurt too much."
Zh: “呃，那个男人已经死亡超过一个星期……”他总结道。“我猜*稍微*分一下心也没多大伤害。”

En: I've already given my word. I'm honour-bound to uphold it.
Zh: “我的话已经说出去了。在道义上我理应维护它。”

En: He sighs. "I sincerely doubt it. Still, I suppose it won't hurt to keep an eye out."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“我深表怀疑。不过，我猜稍微留点心也没什么大不了的。”

En: I've got a *premonition* that this missing scientist is connected to our main case.
Zh: “我有一种*不祥的预感*，这个失踪的科学家可能跟我们手上的大案子有关联。”

En: Oh, thank you officers, truly. I'll be right here if you come across any sign of Morell.
Zh: “哦，谢谢你们，警官，真心的。如果你们发现莫雷尔的任何行踪，我会一直待在这里。”

En: Yes, well, thank you for your time, officer. I'm probably just worrying for no reason. I'm sure they'll return before long...
Zh: “是的，好吧，感谢你抽出宝贵的时间，警官。我的担忧可能是毫无理由的。我肯定他们不久就会回来……”

En: There's a note of disappointment in her voice that she can't quite suppress.
Zh: 她的声音里有一丝失望的痕迹，这一点她是掩饰不了的。

En: Are you sure we have time to go chasing after bug-hunters just now? The lieutenant tilts his head toward the vigilantes in the cafeteria. "I did suggest we play it cool but..."
Zh: “你确定我们现在有时间去追查昆虫猎人吗？”警督朝着餐厅里的义务警员歪了歪头。“我确实建议过要保持冷静，但是……”

En: Are you sure we have time to go chasing after bug-hunters just now? The lieutenant taps his foot impatiently.
Zh: “你确定我们现在有时间去追查昆虫猎人吗？”警督不耐烦地用脚拍打着地面。

En: Oh sweetie, she says, beaming. "I don't even know how to thank you for finding my husband and helping him out. I hope we haven't been too much trouble for you..."
Zh: “哦，甜心，”她满脸笑容地说到。“你帮我找到丈夫，还帮了他这么多忙，我真的不知道该怎么谢你的好。希望我们没给你添太多麻烦……”

En: Thank you so much for helping us out with our research, sweetie, she says, beaming. "I hope all of this hasn't been too much of a distraction for you..."
Zh: “感谢你为我们的研究出了这么多力，甜心，”她满脸笑容地说到。“希望这一切没有让你太过分心……”

En: You know... to hell with it, let's have more cryptids.
Zh: “你知道吗……见鬼去吧，那就让神秘动物来得再猛烈一些吧。”

En: Of course, officers. She smiles politely. "Is there a particular cryptid you two are interested in learning about?"
Zh: “当然可以，警官们。”她礼貌地笑了。“你有没有对哪种神秘动物特别好奇的呢？”

En: I've really spent too much time on this side-case as it is. (Reject task.)
Zh: “我已经在这个二级案件上花费太多时间了。”（拒绝任务。）

En: Oh, of course, sweetie. I understand. She smiles brightly to mask her disappointment.
Zh: “哦，当然了，甜心。我能理解。”她用灿烂的笑容掩饰着自己的失望。

En: I restocked the empty trap... Where's Morell?
Zh: “我在空陷阱里都重新补充了诱饵……莫雷尔在哪？”

En: Thank you for doing that, dear. She manages a smile for you.
Zh: “感谢你能这么做，亲爱的。”她勉强对你笑了笑。

En: It's probably for the best. It's awfully cold out there in those reeds.
Zh: “这样可能是最好的。那片芦苇丛真是冷的不得了。”

En: Only the hard core players left, huh?
Zh: “只剩下核心玩家了，是吗？”

En: Her smile is weary. Her earlier ebullience has left her.
Zh: 她的笑容很疲惫。之前的热情已经离她远去。

En: Morell still isn't feeling well. I convinced him to stay at Gary's to get some rest. She looks down at her hands. "I'm afraid the cold has really gotten to him."
Zh: “莫雷尔还是感觉不太舒服。我说服他待在盖理那儿稍微休息一会。”她低头看着自己的双手。“恐怕他真的是冻着了。”

En: So... who's going to check the traps?
Zh: “所以……谁去检查那些陷阱呢？”

En: Something tells me it's up to me again.
Zh: “直觉告诉我，又要轮到我上场了。”

En: Morell will eventually. Or we'll talk Gary into going back out, perhaps...
Zh: “最后还是得莫雷尔去。或者也许我们可以说服盖理回去……”

En: No. She says -- insisting now. "You don't have to do that. Enough of this fool's errand, Morell will do it. Or Gary. You have work to do, I've wasted enough of everyone's time."
Zh: “不。”她说着——现在十分坚持。“你不用这么做。别再干这种蠢事了，莫雷尔会去做的。或者是盖理。你还有工作要做，我已经浪费大家太多时间了。”

En: We'll take care of it. (Take it on.)
Zh: “我们来处理吧。”（接下任务。）

En: I'll take care of it. (Take it on.)
Zh: “我来处理吧。”（接下任务。）

En: Good luck with that. My bug-chasing days are done.
Zh: “那就祝你们好运。我的昆虫追踪生涯已经结束了。”

En: That *really* is too much, sweetie. Thank you for your dedication, but I can see you're coming down with a cough yourself.
Zh: “你*真*为我们做得太多了，甜心。感谢你的奉献，不过我能看出你自己都有点咳嗽了。”

En: I absolutely understand, sweetie. It's for the best, believe me. And thank you for everything you've done for us, truly. She nods with a sad little smile.
Zh: “我完全能够理解，甜心。这样是最好的，相信我。还要谢谢你为我们做的一切，真心的。”她点点头，脸上带着一丝悲伤的笑容。

En: About your pin...
Zh: “关于你的别针……”

En: This sounds like police business. I'll help you find your husband. (Accept task.)
Zh: “这个听起来像是警察事务。我会帮你去找你丈夫。”（接受任务）

En: That's too bad. I hope your husband comes back soon. (Decline task.)
Zh: “真是太可怕了。希望你的丈夫能尽快回来。”（放弃任务）

En: Turns out the water lock was broken, so your husband is probably just stuck further down the coast. He should return now that it's fixed.
