Zh: “原来是水闸坏了，所以你的丈夫可能被困在了海岸另一边。不过现在水闸已经修好了，他应该快回来了。”

En: I knew what I was getting into when I married a *cryptozoologist*, of course. It's just... Waiting for him to come back from field work is always nerve-racking...
Zh: “当然了，在我嫁给一个*神秘动物学家*的时候，心里就已经做好准备了。只是……等他从现场工作回来总是有些伤脑筋……”

En: I ran into your husband on the coast. He was just going to finish up some work and then head back.
Zh: “我在海岸见到你丈夫了。他说只要做完手头上的事就马上回来。”

En: I think I ran into your husband on the coast. He seemed to be busy, so I didn't talk to him much.
Zh: “我觉得好像在海岸见到你丈夫了。他好像很忙，所以我没跟他说太多。”

En: We ran into your husband on the coast, madam. He was just going to finish up some work and then come back.
Zh: “我们在海岸遇到你丈夫了，夫人。他说只要做完手头上的事就马上回来。”

En: I believe we ran into your husband on the coast, madam. He seemed to be busy setting up some trap, so we didn't talk to him much.
Zh: “我想我们在海岸遇见你丈夫了，夫人。他好像在忙着布置陷阱，所以我们没跟他说太多。”

En: Are you sure it was him? Oh, thank heavens! Silly me. She smiles, though there's still concern in her features.
Zh: “你确定是他吗？哦，谢天谢地！我真傻。”她笑了，不过脸上还是有些忧虑。

En: Still, I should have *known* he'd be returning late this time around. Finding the Insulindian phasmid -- it's *very* important to him. She glances out the window toward the bay.
Zh: “不过，我应该*知道*他这次回来地会很晚。找到伊苏林迪竹节虫——对他来说*非常*重要。”她透过窗户望向海湾。

En: Hello, dear. It's good to see a familiar face. The elderly woman smiles up at you hopefully.
Zh: “你好，亲爱的。很高兴能看到一张相熟的面孔。”老夫人满怀希望地看着你微笑。

En: Oh, hello, sweetie. So nice to see you again.
Zh: “哦，你好啊，甜心。再见到你真是太好了。”

En: Oh, hello, dear. I didn't notice you at first. She smiles up at you distractedly.
Zh: “哦，你好啊，亲爱的。我一开始都没注意到你。”她心烦意乱地笑望着你。

En: Here, I want to give you a small token of my gratitude. She hands you a thin ribbon held together by a silver bird skull. "It's a tie, Mesque in origin. The pin is an antique... quite special."
Zh: “来，我想送你个小礼物以示感激。”她递给你一条用银色鸟骨系在一起的细缎带。“这是一条领带，起源于梅斯克。这个别针很古老……非常特别。”

En: I ran into your husband on the coast.
Zh: “我在海岸见到你丈夫了。”

En: Goodness! How is he? Did he say why he hasn't returned yet? The old woman clasps her hands together over her blanket.
Zh: 老妇人的双手在毛毯上扣紧。“天啦！他怎么样了？他有没有说为什么还不回来？”

En: He's fine. As I had suspected, he couldn't get back before because the water lock on the canal was broken. I'm sure he'll be back soon.
Zh: “他很好。跟我猜的一样，他没回来是因为运河的水闸坏了。我肯定他很快就会回来。”

En: He's fine. He couldn't get back before because the water lock on the canal was broken. I'm sure he'll be back soon.
Zh: “他很好。他没回来是因为运河的水闸坏了。我肯定他很快就会回来。”

En: He's fine, ma'am. As I had suspected, he couldn't get back earlier because the water lock on the canal was broken. Now he's just finishing up some work...
Zh: “他很好，夫人。跟我猜的一样，他没能早点回来是因为运河的水闸坏了。现在他只要处理完手头上的一些工作……”

En: He's fine, ma'am. He couldn't get back earlier because the water lock on the canal was broken. Now he's just finishing up some work...
Zh: “他很好，夫人。他没能早点回来是因为运河的水闸坏了。现在他只要处理完手头上的一些工作……”

En: Oh, yes, that's my Morell -- he's bound to catch a cold, staying out there for so long...
Zh: “哦，是的，那就是我的莫雷尔——在那里待了那么久，他一定是感冒了……”

En: But I am so relieved to hear that he's okay. Thank you for putting an old woman's heart at ease, if even a little.
Zh: “不过听到他没事，我真是放心了。感谢你让一个老妇人安心了，哪怕只有那么一点点。”

En: Now maybe she'll open up about those *fascinating cryptids*. Take her mind off all this...
Zh: 现在她也许会开口说说那些*迷人的神秘动物*了。把她的注意力从这些事情上移开……

En: I hope so! He's going to catch a cold, staying out there for so long...
Zh: “希望如此！在那里待了那么久，他一定是感冒了……”

En: He's fine, ma'am. As we had suspected, he couldn't get back earlier because the water lock on the canal was broken. He should be back soon.
Zh: “他很好，夫人。跟我们猜测的一样，他没能早点回来是因为运河的水闸坏了。他应该很快就能回来。”

En: He's fine, ma'am. He couldn't get back earlier because the water lock on the canal was broken. He should be back soon.
Zh: “他很好，夫人。他没能早点回来是因为运河的水闸坏了。他应该很快就能回来。”

En: He's fine, ma'am. As we had suspected, he couldn't get back earlier because the water lock on the canal was broken. Now he's just finishing up some work.
Zh: “他很好，夫人。跟我们猜测的一样，他没能早点回来是因为运河的水闸坏了。现在他在处理手头上的一点工作。”

En: He's fine, ma'am. He couldn't get back earlier because the water lock on the canal was broken. Now he's just finishing up some work.
Zh: “他很好，夫人。他没能早点回来是因为运河的水闸坏了。现在他在处理手头上的一点工作。”

En: Why do *I* have to play Rescueman?
Zh: *我*为什么要玩人力救援的游戏呢？

En: To redeem yourself before the hoods of Law. Now start asking questions, like a real law enforcement official.
Zh: 为了在法律之神面前完成你自己的救赎。现在开始问问题吧，像个真正的执法人员一样。

En: This sounds more like a side thing. I need to take care of my *main thing* -- then I'll get back to this. [Leave.]
Zh: “这个听起来更像是一件次要的事情。我需要处理好手上*主要的事情*——然后再回来。”[离开。]

En: You seem busy. I'll come back later. [Leave.]
Zh: “你好像很忙，我之后再回来。”[离开。]

En: Of course, sweetie. I'm probably working myself up over nothing anyway. I'll be right here. She glances out the window toward the bay.
Zh: “当然了，甜心。我可能只是在无中生有。我会一直待在这里。”她透过窗户望向海湾。

En: Just doing my job, ma'am.
Zh: “我只是在做我的工作，夫人。”

En: It was a truly epic long distance trek.
Zh: “这真是一场史诗般地长途艰苦跋涉啊。”

En: It was just on my way while I was working the case.
Zh: “我只是调查案子的半路上碰到的。”

En: Oh, hello, dear. There you are again. She sounds glad to see you.
Zh: “哦，你好啊，亲爱的。又见到你了。”见到你她好像很高兴。

En: The Union office probably has a phone, but I can't really get there, or to the phone booth down the coast... And Gary's phone is dead, too... She sighs.
Zh: “工会办公室可能有电话，不过我又不能去那里，或者是海岸边的电话亭……而且盖理的电话也没坏了……”她叹了口气。

En: Please don't trouble yourself about me, sweetie. I was just hoping to make a call, but the Whirling's phone line isn't working.
Zh: “请不要为我操心了，甜心。我只是希望能打个电话，不过飞旋旅社电话线不通。”

En: You seem busy. I'll come back later. [Leave.]
Zh: “你好像很忙，我之后再回来。”[离开。]

En: Sorry, ma'am, I didn't mean to eavesdrop on your conversation. Tell me how I might make it up to you?
Zh: “抱歉，夫人。我不是有意要偷听你们的谈话。告诉我怎样才能补偿你呢？”

En: Good day, ma'am. Everything alright?
Zh: “日安，夫人。一切都还好吧？”

En: The lady is distressed. Perhaps something more up-beat might cheer her up?
Zh: 这位夫人很焦虑。也许更积极一些的东西能让她开心起来？

En: I was also hoping she'd heard from Morell... She looks down.
Zh: 她低下头。“希望她收到了莫雷尔的消息……”

En: Oh, hello, dear. I didn't notice you at first. She smiles up at you distractedly.
Zh: “哦，你好啊，亲爱的。我一开始都没注意到你。”她心烦意乱地笑望着你。

En: So, about your husband... Does he often go missing?
Zh: “所以，关于你的丈夫……他经常失踪吗？”

En: So why did you need to make that call?
Zh: “那你为什么要打电话呢？”

En: Is everything alright?
Zh: “一切都还好吧？”

En: So, about your husband... Does he often go missing?
Zh: “所以，关于你的丈夫……他经常失踪吗？”

En: Hello again, ma'am. So why did you need to make that call?
Zh: “又见面了，夫人。所以你为什么要打电话呢？”

En: Is everything alright, ma'am?
Zh: “一切都还好吧，夫人？”

En: You see, Morell, my husband, and Gary were supposed to get back Monday night, but they're still missing and I haven't heard from them...
Zh: “是这样的，莫雷尔，我的丈夫，还有盖理本来应该周一晚上回来的，不过现在还不见人影，而且我也没收到他们的消息……”

En: Truly -- a lot of legwork. My partner *adores* these kinds of things... The lieutenant is still catching his breath.
Zh: “真的是——很多跑腿工作。我的搭档很*喜欢*这类事情……”警督还在喘气。

En: You see, Morell, my husband, and Gary were supposed to get back Monday night, but they're still missing and I haven't heard from them...
Zh: “你看，莫雷尔，我的丈夫，还有盖理本来应该周一晚上回来，不过现在还没见到人影，而且我没还收到他们的消息……”

En: No mystery here -- this is Klaasje's handiwork. She cut the line.
Zh: 这里没什么秘密——这是卡拉洁的杰作。她把线路切断了。

En: It must have been the Whirling landline that Klaasje "nicked."
Zh: 一定是卡拉洁“切断”了飞旋旅社的电话陆线。

En: Why would he be *vague* about phone problems? This is something to look into later -- ask Garte maybe.
Zh: 他为什么对电话的问题这么*含糊*呢？这个问题需要稍后调查——也许要询问一下加尔特。

En: What does your husband look like?
Zh: “你的丈夫看起来什么样？”

En: Oh! Well, he's a bit shorter than you but with a larger frame. And he has longish white hair -- usually a bit uncombed, you might say *wild* even...
Zh: “哦！他比你稍矮一些，带着一副大眼镜。而且他留着略长的白头发——一般有些蓬乱，甚至可以说有些*狂乱*……”

En: It's always a challenge to describe the person you know best in the world.
Zh: 描述你在这个世界上最了解的人总是很有挑战性的。

En: One other thing -- he'll likely have all kinds of *field gear* on him, even if he's not out in the reeds. You know, just in case.
Zh: “还有一件事——他身上很可能有各种各样的*户外装备*，就算他不是真的待在芦苇丛里。你知道的，只是以防万一。”

En: Tell me about seeing the phasmid again.
Zh: “再跟我说说看见竹节虫的事。”

En: You never told me you've *seen* the phasmid!
Zh: “你都没告诉过我你*见过*竹节虫！”

En: It must have been the Whirling landline that Klaasje "nicked."
Zh: 一定是卡拉洁“切断”了飞旋旅社的电话陆线。

En: Why would he be vague? This is something to look into later. Ask Garte maybe.
Zh: 为什么这么含糊？这个问题之后需要调查一下。可能要问问加尔特。

En: No mystery here -- this is Klaasje's handiwork.
Zh: 这里没什么秘密——这是卡拉洁的杰作。

En: It's all about money, you know.
Zh: “这一切都是关于钱，你知道的。”

En: You gotta spend money to make money.
Zh: “你要通过花钱来赚钱。”

En: Money is what *really* matters.
Zh: “钱才是*真正*重要的东西。”

En: They say money can't buy you happiness...
Zh: “他们说金钱买不来欢乐……”

En: ...but that just means you don't have enough money.
Zh: “……但是那只不过意味着，你的钱还不够。”

En: You have to invest your money wisely.
Zh: “你必须更明智地投资你的钱。”

En: You can buy stocks with money.
Zh: “你可以用钱买股票。”

En: And then use those stocks to make even more money.
Zh: “然后用那些股票去赚更多的钱。”

En: That way you'll have enough when you grow too old to make new money.
Zh: “这样一来，当你老得赚不动新钱的时候，也能有足够的钱花了。”

En: Money is what literally makes the world turn.
Zh: “钱才是让这个世界转动的东西。”

En: The laws of money are just like the laws of nature.
Zh: “金钱法则就像自然规律一样。”

En: Money is all about numbers.
Zh: “金钱就是数字。”

En: Money is actually all about trust.
Zh: “金钱完全是关乎于信任的！”

En: Unpaid labour doesn't net you any money.
Zh: “无偿劳动不会为你赚来一分钱。”

En: There is no such thing as free money.
Zh: “根本就没有白给的钱这种东西。”

En: Real estate is the graveyard where tired, old money goes to rest.
Zh: “房地产是墓地，只有那些疲倦的古钱才会在那里安息。”

En: Zzzz...
Zh: “Zzzz……”

En: Zzz...  zzzzz... zzz...
Zh: “Zzz……zzzzz……zzz……”

En: Zzzzzzzz...
Zh: “Zzzzzzzz……”

En: What trouble?
Zh: “什么麻烦？”

En: On the counter -- rolled out of his open hand -- you see a blister pack of headache medicine.
Zh: 你看见柜台上有一包头痛药，应该是从他张开的手里滚落出来的。

En: Pick up the pills.
Zh: 拿起药丸。

En: The man does not mind. You probably need them more than he does.
Zh: 这个男人不会介意的。你可能比他更需要这东西。

En: You’ve just picked up some MAGNESIUM. This item is stored in the bottom left corner of the screen, above your character portrait. Use MAGNESIUM to heal your Morale, if you have Morale damage.
Zh: 你刚刚捡到了一些镁。这个物品被存放在屏幕的左下角，位于你头像的上方。使用镁可以治疗你受损的士气。

En: The lieutenant gives you a little nod -- then makes a note in his blue notebook.
Zh: 警督朝你微微点点头——然后在蓝色笔记本里做了点笔记。

En: So Union people think he was a killer, he thinks -- even Sleepy here? This doesn't help a *lot*, but it's something. Good work, detective.
Zh: 所以工会的人觉得他是个杀人犯，他这么想——哪怕已经睡得云里雾里？这个并没有*多大*的帮助，不过也算是个信息。做得好，警探。

En: Was it pure nothing, your consciousness no larger than a single grain of malt?
Zh: “它是不是纯粹的无，你的意识比一粒麦芽大不了多少？”

En:
Tell me -- was it a bliss free of ex-somethings?	“告诉我——它是不是没有什么*旧日之物*的极乐之境呢？”
0x0100000600010A3F	Dialogue Text	WHIRLING / SLEEPING DOCKWORKER	Sleeping Dockworker	I don't know what you're talking about, kind sir, but when I'm out, then I'm really *out*. No malt grains or whatever. No poetry-stuff. Just *quality-time*."
Zh: “我不知道你在说什么，好好先生，但是我晕过去的时候，就是真的*晕过去*了。没什么尸体，没什么陵墓。只有*休闲时光*。”

En: The worker stares at you, his eyes dry from sleep. A web of wrinkles covers his tanned forehead.
Zh: 工人盯着你，眼睛因为睡眠有些干涩。他棕褐色的前额上压出了好多皱纹。

En: I don't know what you're talking about, kind sir, but when I'm out, then I'm really *out*. No ex-anything. Just *quality time*.
Zh: “我不知道你在说什么，好好先生，但是我晕过去的时候，就是真的*晕过去*了。没有前任什么的。只有*休闲时光*。”

En: The worker stares at you, his eyes dry from sleep. A web of wrinkles covers his tanned forehead.
Zh: 工人盯着你，眼睛因为睡眠有些干涩。他棕褐色的前额上压出了好多皱纹。

En: A little *me*-time in the abyssopelagic zone... you can never return to it now. Only detective-work remains. Wakefulness and detection.
Zh: 在深渊地带里一段小小的*自我*时间……现在你再也不可能回去了。只剩下警探的工作。清醒和侦察。

En: Officer? Even after a rude awakening -- this dockworker respects the police. More than they usually do at least.
Zh: 警官？就算被粗鲁地唤醒——这个码头工人还是很尊敬警察。至少比平常表现的要多。

En: And the man?
Zh: 而这个男人呢？

En: Does the lieutenant see me?
Zh: 警督看见我了吗？

En: No. He's looking the other way -- perhaps purposefully.
Zh: 不。他在看别的方向——也许是故意的。

En: Like fucking sleigh bells! What are you doing, you're gonna get caught!
Zh: 动静就像摇一串铃铛一样！你在做什么，你会被抓住的！

En: It was just an *idea*, c'mon. Relax, everyone. It didn't turn out well -- doesn't mean it was a bad idea. Or that you shouldn't *appropriate* stuff...
Zh: 这只是一个*主意*，拜托。大家都放松点。结果并不是太好——也不代表这就是个坏主意吧。或者你不应该*占用*那玩意……

En: A colourful piece of plastic is dangling from his carabiner... hmm. Makes your fingers itch.
Zh: 一片彩色的塑料在他的安全扣上悬挂着……嗯。让你的手指有些发痒。

En: Looks like a Sao descendant. A lot of Sao dockworkers around.
Zh: 看起来像是圣艾尔人的后裔。这里有很多圣艾尔裔的码头工人。

En: Wait -- *who* says that?
Zh: “等等——*谁*说的？”

En: Everyone. It's a local rumour. He yawns suddenly.
Zh: “所有人都这么说，这是本地的一个传闻。”他突然打了个哈欠。

En: Meaning: you're not gonna get more out of me.
Zh: 意思是说：你不会再从我这里得到更多消息了。

En: It was probably his colleagues from the Union.
Zh: 可能是他在工会的同事。

En: Fuck sleep. Despite the headache -- and the queasiness -- you feel like you've got at least a ten kilometre run in you.
Zh: 睡他妈的鬼觉。尽管有头痛——还有恶心——你感觉自己至少跑了10公里。

En: I've almost never been outside of this building.
Zh: “我几乎从没出过这栋楼。”

En: Good. He doesn't dwell on the particulars of your existence. "We're in the middle of a strike down at the harbour. Trying to force some sense into the executive board of Wild Pines."
Zh: “很好。”根本不在意你的存在。“港口那边正在罢工。我们想让野松公司的执行董事会明白一些道理。”

En: The way he says "Measurehead" implies ultimate trust in this head-reckoner's physical prowess.
Zh: 他说出“测颅先生”的方式，暗示了他对这位‘碎颅者’身体力量的极端信任。

En: The way he says "Measurehead" implies ultimate trust in this head-reckoner's physical prowess. He seems like a worthy rival. You should *slay* him.
Zh: 他说出“测颅先生”的方式，暗示了他对这位‘碎颅者’身体力量的极端信任。对于你来说，他好像是个实力相称的对手。你应该*干掉*他。

En: A man is sleeping at the table, wearing mud-caked boots and rolled down overalls. The back of his shirt reads "WILD PINES", encircled by a logo with a tree.
Zh: 一个男人趴在桌子上睡着了，他穿着泥泞的靴子，工作服落在地上。衬衫背后印有一个树形的标志，以及“野松”的字样。

En: He looks one with the table. It would take quite some effort to wake him up.
Zh: 他看起来和桌子合为一体了。要想把他弄醒需要费不少力气。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Wake him up.
Zh: 把他叫醒。

En: Anti-Dream Task Force.
Zh: 反做梦特遣小队。

En: Shout like the Coach.
Zh: 像教练一样大声叫喊。

En: Ruckus talking to Garte.
Zh: 嚷嚷着跟加尔特说话。

En: Crashed into wheel-chair.
Zh: 撞到轮椅。

En: You gently shake his shoulder, but nothing happens. This man could probably sleep soundly in a ship's engine room.
Zh: 你轻轻摇了摇他的肩膀，但是什么也没发生。这个男人就算是在船舶机舱里也能睡的很香。

En: Perhaps he's on his way to where you just came from -- into the primordial darkness?
Zh: 他也许正走在你刚才来的那条路上——在那片最初的黑暗深处？

En: No idea. Looks like he works for Wild Pines, the logistics company who owns and operates the harbour.
Zh: “不知道。看起来他像是为野松公司工作的，那是一家物流公司，是港口的所有者和运营商。”

En: Lieutenant, who is this?
Zh: “警督，这是谁？”

En: Yours? (Point at the bottle and the spilt rum.)
Zh: “这是你的吗？”（指着酒瓶和洒出来的朗姆酒。）

En: Indeed. Help yourself to some. He blinks. "Wait, no, it's empty. Sorry about that, pal."
Zh: “没错，你请便吧。”他眨眨眼。“等一下，不对，这是空的。很抱歉，老兄。”

En: I just wanted to see what would happen if I yelled.
Zh: “我只想看看如果我大喊的话会发生什么。”

En: I already had some. It's a fine rum.
Zh: “我已经尝过了。这瓶朗姆酒挺不错的。”

En: Knocks you right out when you drown a bottle or two. He rubs his face, then buries it in his hands. You can hear a faint snore.
Zh: “灌个一两瓶左右，你就会晕倒的。”他摸了摸脸，然后把脸埋进手掌。你可以听到一阵微弱的鼾声。

En: Congratulations, you have seen the future. Now if you excuse me again, officer... The worker sinks back onto the table, ready to continue his nap.
Zh: “恭喜，你看见未来了。现在请让我再失陪一下，警官……”工人又把头埋向桌子，准备继续打盹。

En: What about the dead body in the yard?
Zh: “聊聊院子里的那具尸体吧？”

En: Yeah, what about it?
Zh: “可以啊，那玩意怎么了？”

En: You seem cool with it. Do you have many of those hanging from the trees here?
Zh: “你好像不太在意啊。你们这里的树上经常挂着那种玩意吗？”

En: You know, people die here every day. Someone's found in a ditch, another one falls in a manhole, a third one gets eaten by stray dogs. He respites.
Zh: “你知道的，这里每天都有人死。一个掉沟里了，另一个掉进了检修孔，第三个被野狗吃了。”他缓了一会儿。

En: Someone has to look into it. Looks like I'm that someone. Tell me what you know.
Zh: “必须有人调查一下，看起来我就是那个人。把你知道的都告诉我吧。”

En: I can tell you this -- trouble's ahead.
Zh: “我可以告诉你这个——你要有麻烦了。”

En: The trouble is already here. Or how else would you call a dead man hanging from a tree?
Zh: “麻烦不是已经来了吗？不然你把树上吊着个死人叫什么？”

En: They say your guy was a killer. Your tree-man. He has friends, same as him. Killers. There's only one thing they know well -- how to wreak havoc.
Zh: “他们说那个吊在树上的人是个杀人犯。他还有同伙，跟他一样，也都是杀人犯。他们只知道一件事——怎么搞破坏。”

En: You have a pretty solid feeling that the man's *not* just mouthing off. He's strong, but even he has reasons to be cautious here.
Zh: 你有一种很实际的感觉，这个男人*不是*在信口开河。他很强壮，不过即便是他，在这里也得小心行事。

En: You're saying things might get violent around here?
Zh: “你是说事情可能会朝暴力的方向发展？”

En: You heard what I said. Draw your own conclusions. That's all I know, and I prefer to keep it this way.
Zh: “我说的你都听到了。你自己下结论吧。我就知道这么多，而且我也不想知道更多了。”

En: The man emits a loud snore. Is he about to wake up? Doesn't look like it, but you never know. Better be quick!
Zh: 男人发出一阵响亮的鼾声。他要醒了吗？看起来不像，不过你永远也不会知道。动作最好快一点！

En: Are you positive that what you're doing here is proper police conduct? You can still leave and do *nothing*.
Zh: 你确定你在这里做的是正当的警察行为吗？你还是可以离开，然后*什么也不做*。

En: That's the name of my employer. I work in logistics.
Zh: “那是我雇主的名字。我在物流公司上班。”

En: If someone has decided to die on top of a tree, then how is it my concern?
Zh: “如果有人决定死在树上，那又关我什么事呢？”

En: Why the yelling, man? The 30-something man slowly lifts his head and gives you a pensive look.
Zh: 这个30多岁的男人慢慢抬起头，朝你露出一副哀怨的样子。“你喊什么，老兄？”

En: You're *alive*? You looked out for the long count there...
Zh: “你还*活着*吗？你看起来一点反应都没有……”

En: Wait, what? I was just sleeping. He seems pissed off. "What do you want, officer?"
Zh: “等等，什么？我只是在睡觉。”他似乎有些生气。“你想干什么，警官？”

En: Why are you sleeping here while the others are out striking?
Zh: “你为什么在这里睡觉，其他人都在罢工呢？”

En: The worker continues to nap, undisturbed despite you shaking him. This guy's a real juggernaut of sleep.
Zh: 工人继续沉睡，没有被你摇醒。这家伙真是睡的有够沉的。

En: Tell me about Wild Pines. (Point to the writing on his overalls.)
Zh: “跟我说说野松公司。”（指向他工作服上的字样。）

En: The worker is in a deep slumber.
Zh: 工人正在酣睡。

En: Alright, I'll let you sleep. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，那你就继续睡吧。”[离开。]

En: The dockworker doesn't answer, his head is already back down on the table. In sweet sleep.
Zh: 码头工人没有回答。他的脑袋已经靠在桌子上香甜地睡着了。

En: Steal the document.
Zh: 偷走证件。

En: Swipe through the pocketbook and take what's inside.
Zh: 翻一下钱包，把里面的东西拿出来。

En: You don't really know what it's for, but you get the feeling notes like these are *super* important -- you should keep it.
Zh: 你真不知道它是干什么的，但你直觉这种纸币*非常*重要——你应该留着它。

En: You find a black paper note with a woman's profile on it. Seems like this woman lived centuries ago. The note says *five* reál. It feels nice and greasy.
Zh: 你找到一张黑色纸币，上面印着一个女人的轮廓。女人看起来生活在几个世纪以前。纸条上写着*5*雷亚尔。感觉很好，很油腻。

En: You slip the plastic ID card out of the loop and pocket it. The man continues to snore.
Zh: 你把塑料身份证从钥匙圈里取了出来，塞进口袋。男人还在打鼾。

En: Inspect the piece of plastic on his carabiner.
Zh: 检查他安全扣上的塑料卡。

En: It's a dockworker's ID, doubling as a shift card and a job permit. A young, able-bodied man stares back at you from the photo: Santiago S. John.
Zh: 这是一张码头工人的身份证，既是班次卡，又是工作许可。照片上的那个年轻力壮的小伙子正回望着你：圣地亚哥·S·约翰。

En: But why is he sleeping during the day?
Zh: “但是他为什么要白天睡大觉呢？”

En: Possibly because there's a strike going on in the harbour. There's not much to do -- aside from drinking and sleeping.
Zh: “可能是因为港口正在罢工吧。他们没什么可做的——除了喝酒和睡觉。”

En: There is only one way to awake this bone idle from his slumber. Roar like a hurricane, rip the buildings from the Earth!
Zh: 只有一种办法才能把这个懒骨头从沉睡中唤醒。像飓风一样咆哮，把整个建筑物从地上连根拔起！

En: Yum, yum. Money. Think of all the things you can buy with this. The synapses in your brain fire with glee.
Zh: 哎哟哟。钱。想想你能用它买多少东西。你的大脑突触兴奋地燃烧起来了。

En: Close the carabiner.
Zh: 关上安全扣。

En: You're not 100% clear what you did here was *right*, but to hell with it...
Zh: 你并没有100%地弄清楚你的行为是不是*正确*的，不过管它的呢……

En: The sleeping dockworker has little to say about your actions. He remains silent.
Zh: 沉睡的码头工人对你的行为没什么好说的。他依然沉默。

En: WAKE UP! THIS IS AN ORDER, I AM THE LAW.
Zh: “快醒醒！这是命令，我是执法人员。”

En: (No words, just a primordial scream.)
Zh: （不说话，只是发出一声原始的尖叫。）

En: See anything interesting in your dreams?
Zh: “梦里见到什么有趣的东西了吗？”

En: He doesn't sound too enthusiastic about this.
Zh: 他听起来并不是特别热情。

En: How's that going?
Zh: “你的工作怎么样？”

En: That's wonderful.
Zh: “那很好啊。”

En: How's it going? The dockworker lets out a big yawn, then stares at the cafeteria's terrace doors. Some fingerprints glisten on the glass.
Zh: “怎么样？”码头工人打了个大哈欠，然后盯着餐厅的露台大门。有些指纹在玻璃上闪闪发亮。

En: Haven't you noticed what's going on outside?
Zh: “你难道没注意到外面出什么事了吗？”

En: Of course. It's a total *madhouse*.
Zh: “当然注意到了，这里完全就是个*精神病院*。”

En: I'm not sure what you mean.
Zh: “我不确定你是什么意思。”

En: We're in the middle of a strike down at the harbour. Trying to force some sense into the executive board of Wild Pines.
Zh: “港口正在进行罢工呢。想让野松公司的执行董事会明白一些道理。”

En: Good, then you understand the gist of it. We would all be better off without the employers and their employment.
Zh: “很好，那你已经理解它的要旨了。如果没有那些雇主和雇佣关系的话，我们都会过得更好。”

En: For one -- I could use some more shut-eye in the mornings... He rubs his eyes and sighs. "Right to work? Right to *sleep*, I say."
Zh: “举个例说——早晨我可以多睡一会儿……”他揉了揉眼睛，叹了口气。“工作权？要我说，*睡觉*权还差不多。”

En: For one -- I could get some god damn shut-eye. He rubs his eyes, then lets his head fall back on the table. "Right to *sleep*, I say."
Zh: “举个例子——我可以好好多睡一会儿。”他揉了揉眼睛，然后又把脑袋埋在了桌子上。“要我说，*睡觉*权还差不多。”

En: Logistics? No, it's a nightmare. He lets his head fall back on the table.
Zh: “物流公司好？不，那是个噩梦。”他把脑袋又埋在了桌子上。

En: Thank god, no.
Zh: “谢天谢地，没有。”

En: Was it a fathomless darkness into which we ought to be deferred, like corpses to a mausoleum?
Zh: “你难道不是应该像陵墓里的尸体一样，置身于深不可测的黑暗之中吗？”

En: You're right. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “你说得对。我们说点别的吧。”

En: They got it. Measurehead's got it, he's guarding the gate. I'm just getting some sleep. Or *was*...
Zh: “有他们就好。测颅先生能搞定，他正在看守大门，我只想睡觉。或者说，我*本来*只想睡觉 ……”

En: The worker stares at you, his eyes dry from sleep. A web of wrinkles covers his tanned forehead.
Zh: 工人盯着你，眼睛睡的有些干涩。他棕褐色的前额上压出了好多皱纹。

En: Brilliant. The worker blinks, as if trying to scare away the sleep.
Zh: “太好了。”工人眯起眼睛，似乎想要把睡魔吓跑。

En: I'm just trying to find out what you know about it.
Zh: “我只想知道你都知道些什么。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: He rubs his face, before burying it in his hands. You can hear a faint snore.
Zh: 他摸了摸脸，然后一头埋进双手里。你可以听见一阵微弱的鼾声。

En: I don't know what you're talking about, kind sir, but when I'm out, then I'm really *out*. No corpses. No mausoleums. Just *quality-time*.
Zh: “我不知道你在说什么，好好先生，但是我晕过去的时候，就是真的*晕过去*了。没什么尸体。没什么陵墓。只有*休闲时光*。”

En: Just for the record, you look nothing like the man on the document.
Zh: 顺便提一句，你跟文件上的男人长的一点也不像。

En: Steal the dockworker's ID.
Zh: 偷走码头工人的身份证。

En: I need you to answer some questions right now.
Zh: “现在我需要你回答几个问题。”

En: Really? You woke me up for that? The worker gives you a vacant stare, then groans. "Fine, what is it, officer?"
Zh: “真的吗？你把我叫醒就为了这个？”工人茫然地注视着你，然后嘟囔到。“好吧，什么事，警官？”

En: Your fingers find their way into the shackle, flicking open its spring-loaded gate. The metal loop holds the plastic ID, and a leather-bound pocketbook...
Zh: 你的手指巧妙地伸进了钩环，轻轻打开了弹簧塞。金属圈上挂着一张塑料身份证，还有一个皮革包边的钱包……

En: You fumble with the spring-loaded gate of the carabiner, but to no avail... the dangling items refuse to come loose, they just jingle in an ever louder manner.
Zh: 你笨手笨脚地摆弄着安全扣的弹簧塞，但是完全不起作用……上面悬挂的东西拒绝放松，发出的叮当声更响了。

En: Stop *finger-fucking* him, officer! The cafeteria manager shouts from behind the counter. "I'm responsible for these oafs and their *stuff*. Even Sleepy there..."
Zh: “别再*乱动*他了，警官！”柜台后面的餐厅经理大叫起来。“我要为这些白痴和他们的*东西*负责。甚至是睡着的时候……”

En: What, no, I wasn't.... I'm just checking if the man is feeling okay.
Zh: “什么，不，我没有……我只是想看看他是不是还好。”

En: You stay out of this, bartender!
Zh: “你少插手，酒保！”

En: We might be dealing with a *criminal* here. Let me work.
Zh: “我们可能在对付一个*罪犯*呢。别打扰我工作。”

En: Yes. Sure. A *criminal*. He rolls his eyes, then turns back to his activities.
Zh: “是啊，当然了。一个*罪犯*。”他翻了个白眼，然后继续手中的工作。

En: Fine -- fine! Finger-bang him then, I don't care. He snorts angrily, then turns back to his activities.
Zh: “好吧——好吧！那就乱动他吧，我不在乎。”他愤怒地哼了一声，然后继续手中的工作。

En: Yeah, sure! I don't care. Just don't finger-bang his stuff, okay! He snorts angrily, then turns back to his activities.
Zh: “是啊，当然了！我不在乎。只要别瞎碰他的东西，好吗！”他愤怒地哼了一声，然后继续手中的工作。

En: To be honest, he is correct. Police officers generally don't go around *appropriating* possessions from people. Let's go.
Zh: “老实说，他是对的。一般来说，警察不会到处*占用*别人的东西。我们走吧。”

En: Who's behind organizing the strike?
Zh: “这次罢工背后的组织者是谁？”

En: We are... the workers. The Union. We know what we need, what's right for us.
Zh: “是我们……工人。工会。我们知道自己需要什么，知道什么对我们来说是对的。”

En: You mean there's no leader?
Zh: “你的意思是没有领导吗？”

En: Huh, alright. I guess that makes sense... somehow.
Zh: “啊，好吧。我猜总是……有点道理吧。”

En: Okay, I guess there's also Evrart, he's in charge of the Union. He's smart. Knows how to negotiate. He's got our back.
Zh: “嗯，我想应该是艾弗拉特，他是工会的负责人。他很聪明，知道怎么谈判，他一直在背后支持我们。”

En: Well... little point asking you about the body, then. I got other questions.
Zh: “呃……那问你尸体的事也没什么意义。我还有其他问题。”

En: If you get lucky, I might even have answers for them.
Zh: “如果你走运的话，我也许能有答案呢。”
